# SOURCE
-- Core IR -> textual LLVM IR — Stage 2.4 NATIVE BACKEND (slices 1–8+).
--
-- This IS the canonical LLVM backend (STAGE2-DESIGN.md §2.4). Originally
-- developed as a de-risking spike AFTER the bytecode VM (§2.2, removed
-- 2026-06-10). It emits textual LLVM IR + shells out to clang (no llc/opt,
-- no C++ bindings). Consumes the Core IR (core_ir.mdk) the tree-walker-
-- validated lowering produces, and its emitted
-- binary's stdout is diffed byte-for-byte against the tree-walker oracle
-- (test/diff_compiler_llvm.sh) over prelude-free fixtures — the usual
-- equivalence-gate shape, exactly as core_ir_main.mdk gates the lowering itself.
--
-- SCOPE DISCIPLINE (matches runtime/medaka_rt.c):
--   • slice 1 — integer/float arithmetic, comparisons, unary -/!, let, if,
--     top-level value bindings, and a type-directed print of `main`.
--   • slice 2 — top-level FUNCTIONS (`name p… = …` → `define i64 @mdk_<name>`)
--     and saturated direct CALLS (`CApp` spine with a known-function head).
--     Self-recursive tail calls lower to `musttail call` + `ret` (the
--     calling-convention proof: self-recursion ⇒ caller/callee prototypes match,
--     which musttail requires; empirically TCO-correct under clang -O0).
--   • slice 2b — Bool/Float FUNCTION BOUNDARIES.  Core IR is type-erased, so a
--     two-pass signature inference (`inferSigs`/`typeOf`/`paramUseTy`) RECOVERS
--     each function's param + return LTy: a param's type comes from its first
--     typed use (an `if`-condition/`!` ⇒ Bool, an arith/compare operand shares its
--     sibling, an argument to a known fn takes that fn's param type), the return
--     type follows structurally.  This is the pure twin of the type-recovery
--     `emitExpr` already does as it emits.  The ABI is UNCHANGED — every value is
--     a uniform i64 word regardless (Int immediate, Bool immediate, Float
--     boxed-pointer-as-i64), so no `define`/`call`/musttail prototype changes; the
--     recovered type only selects int-vs-float instructions in the body and the
--     print routine for the result (so a Bool-returning function prints
--     `true`/`false`, a Float param unboxes/reboxes).
--   • slice 3 — ALGEBRAIC DATA TYPES + PATTERN MATCHING.  A constructor lowers to
--     a BOXED heap cell (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §8.4 Option A): a one-word header (the
--     constructor name string-hashed to an i64 tag) followed by the field words,
--     allocated via @mdk_alloc (the same boxing path slice 1 uses for Float).  A
--     `match` lowered to a decision tree (`CDecision`, the Maranget tree from
--     core_ir_lower) becomes an LLVM CFG: each `CTSwitch` tests the focus value's
--     head (load tag → icmp → br for constructors; icmp on the immediate for int
--     literals) and descends into the matched cell's fields as the new focus
--     columns; a leaf re-matches its arm's pattern against the scrutinee to bind
--     variables (getelementptr loads), evaluates the body into an alloca result
--     slot, and branches to the decision's end block.  Mirrors core_ir_eval's
--     cevalDecision/cevalTree/cevalSwitch one-to-one, value-walk → block-emit.
--   • slice 4 — CLOSURES + HIGHER-ORDER FUNCTIONS.  A `CLam` is lambda-lifted to a
--     top-level `define @mdk_lamN(i64 %clos, i64 %arg…)` and, at its site, a BOXED
--     closure cell {header, code_ptr, captured…} is allocated (the slice-3 ctor
--     alloc, reused verbatim with "fields" = [code_ptr, capture…]).  The CAPTURED
--     set is freeVars(body) − params, intersected with the live emit env.  An
--     application whose head is NOT a known fn/ctor (a closure-valued param /
--     let-binding / returned closure / immediately-applied lambda) lowers to an
--     INDIRECT call: load code_ptr, `call` it passing the closure word as the
--     leading argument (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §8.5).  A named top-level fn used as a
--     VALUE is eta-wrapped into a captureless static closure forwarding to
--     `@mdk_<name>`.  SATURATED calls only — the type-erased Core IR can't see a
--     closure's arity at the call site; partial / over-application are OUT (the
--     real backend will carry arity in the cell).
--   • slice 5a — RECORDS, TUPLES, and MUTABLE REFS.  Records lower to boxed cells
--     exactly like ADT constructors (emitCtorAlloc/storeFields/loadField verbatim):
--     header = hashName(record-type-name), then one word per field in source
--     declaration order.  Field access looks up the positional index from a record-
--     field-order table built by scanning CRecord nodes in the program up front.
--     Record update copies all fields from the base then overwrites the named ones.
--     Tuples use the same cell layout with a fixed header (hashName "$tuple"); tuple
--     destructuring via `let (a, b, c) = t` binds elements by loadField.  Mutable
--     refs are lowered INLINE (no new runtime functions): `Ref x` allocates a 1-field
--     cell; `setRef r x` stores into its field; `r.value` loads from it.
--     SPIKE-REP NOTES (filed here per the slice-3/4 convention):
--       • Records: positional-by-declaration-order.  Name-keyed access at runtime
--         would need a name→offset map; the spike sidesteps this by reading the
--         declaration (from scanned CRecord nodes) at emit time.
--       • Tuples: fixed header hashName "$tuple".  Headerless tuples would save one
--         word but break the uniform cell discipline used for tag-testing in switches.
--       • Refs: 1-field cells over @mdk_alloc (malloc-and-leak).  The real GC will
--         need a write barrier on the `setRef` store — this is the FIRST mutation
--         site in the spike and the first place a GC would need to act.
--   • slice 5b — BUILT-IN LIST/TUPLE MATCH HEADS + RECURSIVE CLOSURES.  The
--     decision tree's built-in list heads (HCons/HNil) and tuple head (HTuple n)
--     are now treated as constructor tests: `conHeadInfo` maps HCon/HCons/HNil/
--     HTuple to a (name, arity) pair — HCons → ("Cons", 2), HNil → ("Nil", 0),
--     HTuple n → ("$tuple", n) — so emitSwitch/emitConChain compare the loaded tag
--     against `cellTag e name`, the SAME tag the constructor/tuple alloc site stamps
--     (so construction and match agree by construction).  `bindPattern` gains
--     `PCons h t` (a 2-field [head, tail] extraction) and `PList []` (binds nothing).
--     A recursive function-`let` (`let f p… = … in e2` → CLet True (PVar f) (CLam …))
--     lambda-lifts like any CLam but EXCLUDES `f` from the capture set (it is the
--     closure cell itself) and binds `f → %clos` inside the lifted body, so the
--     self-call re-enters the same cell.
--   • slice 6 — TYPECLASS DISPATCH.  The Core IR's two dispatch nodes lower here:
--     `CMethod` (a method occurrence) and `CDict` (a `=>`-constrained-fn occurrence),
--     each carrying the typechecker's resolved `Route`s.  An `RKey` route is
--     STATICALLY resolved (the concrete impl is known at compile time) → a DIRECT
--     call to that impl's lifted `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>`; `RDict`/`RDictFwd` read
--     a runtime dict-parameter witness word → a CALL to the method's SHARED
--     dispatcher `@mdk_disp_<method>_<nMeth>_<nArgs>`, which holds the impl if-chain
--     (issue #118 — the chain is OUTLINED, not inlined at the site; look for it
--     there, not here).  Mirrors core_ir_eval's methodAtNarrow/applyDicts/
--     narrowMethod.  See the "typeclass dispatch (slice 6)" section below for the
--     (j)/(k)/(l) rep notes.
--   • slice 8 — ARRAYS + RANGES.  CArray allocates a length-prefixed boxed cell
--     (layout: [ i64 raw_len | elem0 | elem1 … ], reusing the slice-3 cell shape
--     but with the header word = raw element count rather than a constructor hash).
--     CIndex loads the length, bounds-checks (calls @mdk_oob() on failure), then
--     GEPs to field (idx+1) dynamically.  CRangeArray and CSlice both emit an
--     alloca-counter loop (the spike's first non-recursion loop): CRangeArray fills
--     i ↦ lo+i (tagged Int word); CSlice copies elements from the source array.
--   • slice 9 — LISTS (CList + CRangeList).  CList [e0…en] right-folds inline into
--     Cons/Nil boxed cells (the same cells the slice-5b HCons/HNil match heads test,
--     so construction and match agree).  CRangeList lo hi incl builds an ascending
--     list via an alloca-counter back-to-front loop (initialise Nil, prepend
--     Cons(lo+i,acc) for i from count-1 downto 0) — avoids stack overflow and
--     mirrors the slice-8 loop discipline.  See the "lists (slice 9)" section and
--     §2.4a-6 in STAGE2-DESIGN.md for the (u)/(v) rep notes.
--   • native extern catalog slice 1 — STRINGS + minimal string/IO externs (NOT a
--     Core IR-surface slice; the first leg of the real-backend extern catalog,
--     unblocked by Boehm GC).  Locks the String rep (RUNTIME-DESIGN §4/§7 decision
--     2: UTF-8 bytes + cached codepoint count) as a boxed cell
--     [ header | byte_len | cp_count | bytes… | NUL ].  `CLit (LString _)` emits a
--     private `@.str.N` byte-array global (`\HH`-escaped, codepoints re-encoded to
--     UTF-8 via utf8Bytes) + a run-time @mdk_str_lit box; `intToString` lowers to a
--     direct @mdk_int_to_string call (first extern RETURNING a heap String — the
--     §2a GC-alloc contract); a String-typed `main` auto-prints via @mdk_print_str
--     (raw bytes + newline, matching pp_value (VString s) = s).  See the "string
--     literals + string externs" section and §2.4a-7 for the (w)–(z) rep notes.
-- Still OUT of scope: arg-tag dispatch on non-ADT args; nested requires dicts;
-- further string/IO externs (charToStr/stringConcat/putStr/file-IO/unicode); the
-- Char rep (gates charCode).
-- An unsupported Core IR node panics with its tag (the spike refuses to emit wrong
-- code rather than silently mis-lowering).
--
-- VALUE REPRESENTATION (PROVISIONAL — revisable; see RUNTIME-DESIGN.md "Value
-- representation & calling convention").  A value is a uniform 64-bit word:
--   • Int  — immediate, (n << 1) | 1   (low bit 1).  Arithmetic untags/retags,
--            so the tagging cost is VISIBLE in the emitted IR (the point).
--   • Bool — the immediate-int encoding 0/1 (slice 1 is statically typed, so the
--            print routine is chosen by static type; the rep need not yet tell a
--            Bool from an Int — a limitation this spike deliberately exposes).
--   • Float — BOXED: word is a pointer to a 16-byte cell {i64 header, double},
--            allocated via @mdk_alloc.  Surfaces "floats don't fit a tagged-int
--            word", which is the core input to the rep proposal.
--   • ADT  — a constructor with FIELDS is BOXED (slice 3): word is a pointer (low
--            bit 0) to a heap cell [ i64 tag | field0 | field1 … ], one 8-byte word
--            each; tag = the composite (typeId<<32 | ctor-ordinal) `cellTag` stamps.
--            The same boxed-pointer discipline as Float, extended to n fields.  A
--            NULLARY constructor is IMMEDIATE (low bit 1, RUNTIME-DESIGN §8.1/§8.4):
--            the word is `(cellTag<<1)|1` carrying that same composite tag, NO
--            allocation — the Bool immediate (True→3/False→1) generalised to every
--            nullary ctor.  A `match` head recovers the tag via `loadDiscriminant`
--            (branch on the low bit: immediate ⇒ `ashr 1`, boxed ⇒ load header), so
--            a type mixing both (Option = None immediate + Some boxed) discriminates
--            without ever dereferencing an immediate.
--   • Closure — BOXED (slice 4): word is a pointer to a heap cell
--            [ i64 header | i64 code_ptr | capt0 | capt1 … ] (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md
--            §8.5).  The same cell shape as an ADT (header + fields), so it reuses
--            the slice-3 alloc; calling loads code_ptr and passes the cell pointer
--            as the leading argument.
--   • Record — BOXED (slice 5a): word is a pointer to a heap cell
--            [ i64 header | field0 | field1 … ], fields in declaration order.
--            header = hashName(record-type-name), same hashing as ADT constructors.
--   • Tuple  — BOXED (slice 5a): same cell shape with header = hashName "$tuple".
--   • Ref    — BOXED (slice 5a): 1-field cell [ i64 header | stored_value ].
--            header = hashName "$ref".  .value loads field 0; setRef stores into
--            field 0.  Write barrier absent (malloc-and-leak, same as @mdk_alloc).
--   • String — BOXED (native extern catalog slice 1): word is a pointer to a heap
--            cell [ i64 header | i64 byte_len | i64 cp_count | UTF-8 bytes… | NUL ]
--            (RUNTIME-DESIGN §4, rep LOCKED 2026-06-07).  Built by @mdk_str_lit
--            (literals + intToString); cp_count cached so stringLength is O(1).
--            GC-managed under Boehm for free (aligned pointer, low bit 0).

import frontend.ast.{Lit(..), Pat(..), Addr(..), Route(..)}
import ir.core_ir.{
  CExpr(..),
  CField(..),
  CBind(..),
  CClause(..),
  CStmt(..),
  CProgram(..),
  CArm(..),
  CGuard(..),
  CTree(..),
  CTBranch(..),
  CHead(..),
  CImplEntry(..),
  CImplBody(..),
}
import ir.core_ir_lower.{compileTree, canonPat}
import support.util.{
  reverseL,
  joinNl,
  lookupAssoc,
  listLen,
  contains,
  startsWith,
  fallthroughName,
}
import backend.llvm_preamble.{preambleLines}
import support.ordmap.{
  OrdMap,
  omInsert,
  omLookup,
  omFromNames,
  omFromPairs,
  omEmpty,
}
-- backend-agnostic TRMC eligibility analysis (lifted out per WASMGC-TRMC-DESIGN §5/§7-Stage-0;
-- also consumed by wasm_emit.mdk from Stage 1).  The structural CExpr helpers (freeVars/
-- flattenApp/lengthS/pattern + bind helpers) live there too so the analysis is self-contained.
import backend.trmc_analysis.{
  SelfRef(..),
  flattenApp,
  lengthS,
  freeVars,
  routeDictNames,
  bindName,
  bindNames,
  patVars,
  patVarsList,
  patVarNames,
  trmcEligible,
  isCtorTail,
  isSelfSatApp,
  consTailArgs,
  ctorTailName,
  ctorTailLeadFields,
  ctorTailSelfIdx,
  splitLastF,
  selfFree,
  mentionsSelfMethod,
  DispGroup(..),
  dispRootOf,
  dispMembersOf,
  dispGroupOf,
  dispFindBind,
  detectDispatchGroups,
  dispSpineParts,
  dictUniformPairs,
  dropFirstN,
  clauseArityOf,
}
import backend.private_mangle.{hashName, safeChar}
import backend.emit_support.{
  eagerVars,
  methodIfaceTableRef,
  methodIfaceOf,
  methodArityOf,
  isDictParamName,
  ftPrefix,
  ftLabelOf,
  labelFallthrough,
  rngBound,
}

-- ── D4 emitter-completeness inventory (record-and-skip) ──────────────────────
-- A MEASUREMENT scaffold for STAGE D4 (compiler/EMITTER-GAPS.md).  OFF by default:
-- when `gapRecordEnabled` is False every gap site below still `panic`s, exactly as
-- before, so normal emit output and all four diff_compiler_* gates stay
-- byte-identical.  The D4 gaps probe (llvm_emit_gaps_main.mdk) flips it ON to
-- COLLECT every gap in one pass instead of aborting on the first: each gap is
-- recorded as "<binding>\t<reason>" and the offending construct returns a benign
-- placeholder so emission of the rest of the program proceeds.  The placeholder
-- poisons only that one sub-expression's output (which the probe discards — it
-- never compiles/links the .ll), so it surfaces ALL gaps rather than one.
gapRecordEnabled : Ref Bool
gapRecordEnabled = Ref False

gapLog : Ref (List String)
gapLog = Ref []

curGapBind : Ref String
curGapBind = Ref "?"

-- The CTFail target for a multi-clause function emitted as a CHAIN of single-clause
-- decision trees: when clause K's PATTERN fails to match, control must resume at
-- clause K+1's entry block — the interpreter's "try next clause" semantics.  Holds
-- the current next-clause LLVM label; "" means "no next clause" → a genuine
-- non-exhaustive-match abort.  Set per-clause by emitClauseChain; read ONLY by
-- emitTree's CTFail arm.  A module-level Ref (mirrors gapLog) to avoid threading
-- the label through every emitTree arm.
--
-- ⚠️ This Ref is for CTFail and NOTHING ELSE.  emitDecision/emitTrmcBody/emitGDispBody
-- save+NULL it across a body-level `match` precisely because such a match's own CTFail
-- is a genuine abort, not the enclosing chain's fallthrough.  The `__fallthrough__ ()`
-- SENTINEL — desugar's "this clause's guard chain fell through" marker — is a
-- different thing and used to read this same Ref, which is why a refutable
-- pattern-guard on a multi-clause function MISCOMPILED (it desugars to a body-level
-- CDecision, so the null erased its target and the sentinel became `@mdk_oob`).  The
-- sentinel now carries its own target in the node (`__ft__<label>`, stamped by
-- `labelFallthrough`) — see backend/emit_support.mdk.  Do NOT re-merge the two.
fallthroughLabelRef : Ref String
fallthroughLabelRef = Ref ""

-- compile-time closure-arity table, keyed by the closure cell's SSA register
-- (`%tN`).  ⚠️ `%tN` is only unique WITHIN a define (issue #118 restarts the
-- register counter at each one), so this table is PER-DEFINE: `beginDefine`
-- clears it and `endDefine` restores the enclosing define's — otherwise `%t5` in
-- one function would answer a lookup for `%t5` in another and the indirect call
-- would saturate with the WRONG arity.  Sound because an entry can only be read
-- in the define that wrote it (the key is used verbatim as an SSA operand of the
-- emitted `call`, which LLVM requires to be defined in the same function).
--   Recorded when a user lambda's closure cell is allocated
-- (emitClosureAllocA); read at an INDIRECT call site (emitIndirect) so a let-bound
-- closure applied to MORE args than its arity (`(g 7) 9` where `g = (x => (y => …))`)
-- saturates with the first `arity` args then applies the surplus to the returned
-- closure (emitApplyAny) — mirroring emitOverApp for known fns.  Without this the
-- indirect call passed ALL args to the arity-1 lifted code → arity mismatch →
-- garbage.  A miss (closure word not a recorded `%tN` — e.g. a captured/param
-- closure) falls to emitApplyAny, which reads the closure's RUNTIME arity from the
-- cell header (field 0) and dispatches exact / partial (PAP) / over application via
-- the runtime @mdk_apply — so an opaque under-application no longer miscompiles.
closureArityRef : Ref (List (String, Int))
closureArityRef = Ref []

-- compile-time closure RETURN-TYPE table, keyed by the closure cell's SSA register.
-- ⚠️ Same `%tN` scoping hazard as closureArityRef above — also PER-DEFINE.
-- Records LTFloat for a user lambda whose body EVIDENTLY returns a Float (a float
-- literal or known-float capture flows to the result through arith/if/block — see
-- bodyFloatRet, conservative).  Read at an indirect call site (indirectRetTy) so a
-- saturated call to a float-returning closure types its result LTFloat — fixing
-- `f 1.0 + f 2.0` (each `f _` result was LTInt → integer arith on boxed-float
-- pointers → garbage).  Only LTFloat is ever recorded, so non-float closures keep
-- the historical LTInt default → zero blast radius for them.
closureRetTyRef : Ref (List (String, LTy))
closureRetTyRef = Ref []

-- is this CVar name the fall-through sentinel — either BARE (`__fallthrough__`, no
-- enclosing clause chain) or LABELLED by emit_support's `labelFallthrough`
-- (`__ft__<block>`, "resume at that clause's block")?
isFallthroughVar : String -> Bool
isFallthroughVar x = x == fallthroughName || startsWith ftPrefix x

-- lower the fall-through sentinel: a LABELLED one branches to the next clause's
-- block; a BARE one (single-clause fn, or a `__fallthrough__` under a CLam, which
-- labelFallthrough deliberately does not enter) has no next clause → the @mdk_oob
-- non-exhaustive abort.  `@mdk_oob` is not a terminator, so the caller's
-- store+branch stays well-formed; the `br` path leaves them in a dead anonymous
-- block, which LLVM discards.
emitFallthrough : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitFallthrough e fname = match ftLabelOf fname
  Some lbl =>
    let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ lbl)
    ("0", LTInt)
  None =>
    let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_oob()"
    ("0", LTInt)

-- ── TRMC (tail-recursion-modulo-cons) context (PLAN #56, TRMC-DESIGN.md) ─────
-- When a top-level NON-DICT self-recursive define is `trmcEligible` — its tail
-- leaves are `head :: self-call` cons-tails (`upto`/`replicate`/`myMap`) — it is
-- emitted in destination-passing form: an O(1)-stack loop with a MUTABLE
-- destination slot (`%trmc_dest`, an `alloca ptr` holding the address of the i64
-- to fill next).  A cons leaf allocates the cell, links it into `*dest`, advances
-- `dest` to the cell's tail slot, recomputes the recursion args into the param
-- slots, and branches back to the loop head; a base leaf stores its value into
-- `*dest` and branches to the exit, which loads `%trmc_hole` (the final list head)
-- and rets.  The self-call is NEVER emitted as a `call` — only its arg
-- recomputation feeds the loop, which is what removes the stack-growing call.
--
-- A module-level Ref (mirroring fallthroughLabelRef) carries the active context
-- so the decision-tree leaf (emitLeaf) and the single-clause CIf-tail descent
-- (emitTrmcBody) both route into it without threading it through every emit arm.
-- TrmcOff = ordinary (non-TRMC) codegen; TrmcOn self arity destSlot loopL exitL
-- paramSlots = the live context.  `self` is the SelfRef the loop recurses on (a
-- top-level CVar name, or a dispatched CMethod method+tag — Phase 2 B-dispatch);
-- destSlot is the `alloca ptr` register (the mutable destination); loopL/exitL the
-- loop-head / exit blocks; paramSlots the mutable `alloca i64` param slots the cons
-- leaf recomputes the recursion args into.
data TrmcCtx = TrmcOff | TrmcOn SelfRef Int String String String (List String)

trmcCtxRef : Ref TrmcCtx
trmcCtxRef = Ref TrmcOff

-- ── dispatch-graph (b′) TMC context (TMC-parity arc; TRMC-DESIGN §Phase 3) ────
-- The active group context during a member-body emit: `GDispOn root members
-- uslots destSlot exitL` — root/member names (the leaf redirect recognizes
-- sanctioned tail edges), the U shared `alloca i64` param slots, the mutable
-- destination slot (the SAME local-alloca protocol as TrmcOn — dest seeded to
-- &hole, no globals), and the group define's shared exit label.  GDispOff =
-- ordinary codegen.  Mirrors trmcCtxRef (saved+cleared by emitDecision for an
-- expression-position match; kept LIVE across a tail-position CDecision).
data GDispCtx =
  | GDispOff
  | GDispOn String (List String) (List String) String String

gDispCtxRef : Ref GDispCtx
gDispCtxRef = Ref GDispOff

-- the detected (b′) groups (shared detection, backend/trmc_analysis.mdk), the fn
-- binds they were detected over, and the fn-arity table with wasm's `progFnArity`
-- contract (clause arity; -1 when not a top-level fn — an 0-when-unknown lookup
-- would let a 0-arg call "saturate").  Computed once per emitProgram; reset (to
-- empty) by emitProgramGaps so a prior program's groups can never leak into the
-- gap-census path.
gDispGroupsRef : Ref (List DispGroup)
gDispGroupsRef = Ref []

gDispBindsRef : Ref (List CBind)
gDispBindsRef = Ref []

gDispArsRef : Ref (List (String, Int))
gDispArsRef = Ref []

gDispIsFn : String -> Bool
gDispIsFn n = match lookupAssoc n gDispArsRef.value
  Some _ => True
  None => False

gDispFnArity : String -> Int
gDispFnArity n = match lookupAssoc n gDispArsRef.value
  Some a => a
  None => 0 - 1

-- is `n` a member (root included) of a detected group?
gDispIsMember : String -> Bool
gDispIsMember n = match dispGroupOf gDispGroupsRef.value n
  Some _ => True
  None => False

export enableGapRecord : Unit -> Unit
enableGapRecord _ = setRef gapRecordEnabled True

-- clear the gap log (so one process can run several independent gap censuses).
export resetGaps : Unit -> Unit
resetGaps _ =
  let _ = setRef gapLog []
  let _ = resetEmittedDefaults ()
  setRef curGapBind "?"

-- the recorded gap events, in first-recorded order ("<binding>\t<reason>").
export gapEvents : Unit -> List String
gapEvents _ = reverseL gapLog.value

-- set the binding name attributed to subsequent gaps (the probe sets this per
-- top-level binding / impl entry before emitting it).
export setGapBind : String -> Unit
setGapBind name = setRef curGapBind name

noteGap : String -> Unit
noteGap reason = setRef gapLog ("\{curGapBind.value}\t\{reason}"::gapLog.value)

-- ── method-call RESULT-type inference (native backend) ──────────────────────
-- The (interface, method) -> returns-self table, computed by core_ir_lower's
-- `returnsSelfTable` and installed once at the start of emitProgram/
-- emitProgramGaps (a module-level Ref, mirroring gapLog — avoids threading a
-- field through every Emit pattern).  A method whose RESULT type mentions the
-- interface/self type variable (`map`/`ap`/`andThen`: f a -> … -> f b) returns
-- the CONTAINER; the emitter types its RKey-dispatched call result as the impl's
-- own type (`tagToLTy tag` → LTCon for List), so a downstream `++`/`::` on that
-- result picks list/string append instead of falling to the LTInt default.  This
-- closes EMITTER-GAPS #3's last residual (`ap@List` / `andThen@List`, whose `++`
-- operand is a CALL result like `map f xs` / the recursive `ap fs xs`, not a
-- param the static E2c typing could reach).
returnsSelfTableRef : Ref (List ((String, String), Bool))
returnsSelfTableRef = Ref []

-- install the (interface, method) -> returns-self table (from core_ir_lower's
-- `returnsSelfTable <decls>`).  A consumer that emits typeclass methods calls
-- this BEFORE emitProgram/emitProgramGaps; a method/interface-free consumer may
-- skip it (the empty default ⇒ returns-self False ⇒ unchanged behaviour).
export installReturnsSelf : List ((String, String), Bool) -> Unit
installReturnsSelf t = setRef returnsSelfTableRef t

-- does interface method (iface, method) return the self/container type?
-- (False when absent — the conservative default keeps the pre-change behaviour.)
methodReturnsSelf : String -> String -> Bool
methodReturnsSelf iface method =
  lookupReturnsSelf iface method returnsSelfTableRef.value

lookupReturnsSelf : String -> String -> List ((String, String), Bool) -> Bool
lookupReturnsSelf _ _ [] = False
lookupReturnsSelf iface method (((i, m), b)::rest)
  | i == iface && m == method = b
  | otherwise = lookupReturnsSelf iface method rest

-- ── self-returning function-PARAM typing (native backend) ───────────────────
-- The (interface, method) -> [function-param positions returning self] table,
-- from core_ir_lower's `selfFnParamTable`.  When emitting an impl method's body,
-- the params at those positions are callbacks that yield the container (e.g.
-- `andThen : m a -> (a -> m b) -> m b`, param 1 = `a -> m b`).  Before emitting a
-- group body, `curSelfFnParamsRef` is set to the NAMES of those params and
-- `curImplTagRef` to the impl's type-head tag; `emitIndirect` then types the
-- APPLICATION of such a param (`f x`) as `tagToLTy curImplTagRef` instead of the
-- LTInt indirect-call default.  This closes `andThen@List`, whose `++` LEFT
-- operand `f x` is an indirect call the method-call inference can't reach.
selfFnParamTableRef : Ref (List ((String, String), List Int))
selfFnParamTableRef = Ref []

export installSelfFnParams : List ((String, String), List Int) -> Unit
installSelfFnParams t = setRef selfFnParamTableRef t

selfFnParamPositions : String -> String -> List Int
selfFnParamPositions iface method =
  lookupSelfFnParams iface method selfFnParamTableRef.value

lookupSelfFnParams : String -> String -> List ((String, String), List Int) -> List Int
lookupSelfFnParams _ _ [] = []
lookupSelfFnParams iface method (((i, m), p)::rest)
  | i == iface && m == method = p
  | otherwise = lookupSelfFnParams iface method rest

-- ── interface-default-method dispatch (native backend, E19) ──────────────────
-- The method → (interface, full-arity) table from core_ir_lower's
-- `methodIfaceTable`.  An interface DEFAULT method (a `CImplDefault` body the
-- type's `impl` did not override — e.g. `filter` on a `Filterable List` that only
-- defines `filterMap`, or `max`/`min` on an `Ord` instance) is dispatched by
-- emitting the default body as a lifted `@mdk_default_<method>[_<tag>]` define.
-- Two facts this table supplies:
--   • ARITY — eta-expand a point-free default (`filter p = filterMap …` is
--     arity-1 in source but the method is arity-2) to its full arity so the
--     direct RKey call passes every call-site argument.
--   • INTERFACE — recognise inner SAME-interface method occurrences in the default
--     body (the `filterMap` in `filter`'s body) and re-stamp them to the concrete
--     dispatch tag, so a partially-applied inner method lowers to a direct
--     `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>` call instead of an un-dispatchable arg-tag site.
-- Keyed by BARE method name (interface method names are distinct in the prelude).
-- The table Ref + the `methodIfaceOf`/`methodArityOf` readers live in
-- `backend.emit_support` (shared with the WasmGC emitter); `installMethodIface`
-- stays here as the LLVM-side install entry point the emit drivers import.
export installMethodIface : List (String, (String, Int)) -> Unit
installMethodIface t = setRef methodIfaceTableRef t

-- ── method → method-level constraint INTERFACE names (native backend, G7) ────
-- Per interface method that carries a method-level `=>` constraint, the interface
-- names of those constraints IN SLOT ORDER (the order typecheck fills the method
-- occurrence's `methRoutes`).  The default-body emitter reads this to map a
-- cross-interface method in the body (foldMap's `empty`, a Monoid method) to the
-- threaded method-dict param at its slot, so it dispatches the right impl at run
-- time without baking a single Monoid tag into the shared default define.
methodConstraintIfacesRef : Ref (List (String, List String))
methodConstraintIfacesRef = Ref []

export installMethodConstraintIfaces : List (String, List String) -> Unit
installMethodConstraintIfaces t = setRef methodConstraintIfacesRef t

-- the method-level constraint interface names for [method], in slot order ([]=none).
methodConstraintIfacesOf : String -> List String
methodConstraintIfacesOf method = match lookupAssoc method methodConstraintIfacesRef.value
  Some ifaces => ifaces
  None => []

-- ── constructor → DECLARED field type-head names (native backend, Gap E2) ────
-- The ctor → declared-field-type-head-name table from core_ir_lower's
-- `ctorFieldTypeNames` (e.g. `Rect Float Float` → ("Rect", ["Float","Float"])).
-- `bindFields` reads it to type a match-bound field VARIABLE from the ctor's
-- DECLARED field type rather than guessing from body-use (`paramUseTy`, which
-- defaults a Float field to LTInt → integer arith on a boxed-Float pointer-word →
-- SIGSEGV; Gap E2).  Installed before emitProgram/emitProgramGaps (module-level
-- Ref, mirroring returnsSelfTableRef); empty default ⇒ no field is overridden ⇒
-- unchanged pre-change behaviour.
ctorFieldTypesRef : Ref (List (String, List String))
ctorFieldTypesRef = Ref []

export installCtorFieldTypes : List (String, List String) -> Unit
installCtorFieldTypes t = setRef ctorFieldTypesRef t

-- the declared field type-head names of a constructor, or [] if unknown.
ctorFieldTypeNamesOf : String -> List String
ctorFieldTypeNamesOf con = match lookupAssoc con ctorFieldTypesRef.value
  Some names => names
  None => []

-- map a declared field type-head NAME to a definite scalar LTy, or None when it
-- is not a known scalar (polymorphic / pointer-typed / "" unknown) — None ⇒ the
-- caller falls back to paramUseTy/LTInt, so the override is ADDITIVE: only
-- known-scalar fields (esp. Float/Bool) change typing.
fieldNameToLTy : String -> Option LTy
fieldNameToLTy "Float" = Some LTFloat
fieldNameToLTy "Bool" = Some LTBool
fieldNameToLTy "Int" = Some LTInt
fieldNameToLTy "Char" = Some LTChar
fieldNameToLTy "String" = Some LTStr
fieldNameToLTy _ = None

-- ── function → DECLARED param/return type-head names (native backend, Gap E1) ─
-- The fn → (declared-param-type-head-names, declared-return-type-head-name) table
-- from core_ir_lower's `declSigTypeNames` (e.g. `double : Float -> Float` →
-- ("double", (["Float"], "Float"))).  Signature inference (`inferSigs` →
-- `inferOneSig`/`inferParamTys`) SEEDS each param/return LTy from this declared
-- type when it is a known scalar, winning over the body-use guess (`paramUseTy`,
-- which for a literal-free annotated body like `double x = x + x` finds no Float
-- anchor → LTInt → integer arith on a boxed-Float word → garbage; Gap E1).
-- Installed before emitProgram/emitProgramGaps (module-level Ref, mirroring
-- ctorFieldTypesRef); empty default ⇒ no fn is seeded ⇒ unchanged pre-change
-- behaviour.  ADDITIVE: only known-scalar declared params/returns override; an
-- unannotated fn / non-scalar declared type falls through to the existing guess.
declSigTypesRef : Ref (List (String, (List String, String)))
declSigTypesRef = Ref []

export installDeclSigTypes : List (String, (List String, String)) -> Unit
installDeclSigTypes t = setRef declSigTypesRef t

-- the declared (param-type-head-names, return-type-head-name) of a function, or
-- None when it has no annotation.
declSigOf : String -> Option (List String, String)
declSigOf name = lookupAssoc name declSigTypesRef.value

nthName : List String -> Int -> Option String
nthName (x::_) 0 = Some x
nthName (_::rest) i = nthName rest (i - 1)
nthName [] _ = None

-- the set of default fn names already lifted (one define per (method, tag)),
-- reset at the start of each emit run so the two censuses don't share state.
emittedDefaultsRef : Ref (List String)
emittedDefaultsRef = Ref []

resetEmittedDefaults : Unit -> Unit
resetEmittedDefaults _ = setRef emittedDefaultsRef []

markDefaultEmitted : String -> Unit
markDefaultEmitted name =
  setRef emittedDefaultsRef (name::emittedDefaultsRef.value)

defaultAlreadyEmitted : String -> Bool
defaultAlreadyEmitted name = contains name emittedDefaultsRef.value

-- the impl currently being emitted: its type-head tag, and the NAMES of its
-- params that are self-returning function callbacks (set in emitGroupBody).
curImplTagRef : Ref String
curImplTagRef = Ref ""

curSelfFnParamsRef : Ref (List String)
curSelfFnParamsRef = Ref []

-- set the current-impl context for self-returning-fn-param typing; cleared after
-- the group body so a following top-level fn body isn't mis-typed.
setCurImplSelfFns : String -> List String -> Unit
setCurImplSelfFns tag names =
  let _ = setRef curImplTagRef tag in setRef curSelfFnParamsRef names

clearCurImplSelfFns : Unit -> Unit
clearCurImplSelfFns _ =
  let _ = setRef curImplTagRef "" in setRef curSelfFnParamsRef []

-- is `name` a self-returning function param of the impl currently being emitted?
isCurSelfFnParam : String -> Bool
isCurSelfFnParam name = contains name curSelfFnParamsRef.value

-- gap helpers: when recording is on, log the reason + return a placeholder so
-- emission continues; otherwise `panic` (the unchanged normal-path behaviour).
-- One per return shape the panic sites need.
gapE : String -> (String, LTy)
gapE reason =
  if gapRecordEnabled.value then
    let _ = noteGap reason in ("0", LTInt)
  else
    panic reason

gapU : String -> Unit
gapU reason = if gapRecordEnabled.value then noteGap reason else panic reason

-- gap that returns the unchanged emit env (the bindPattern accumulator).
gapEnv : String -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List (String, (String, LTy))
gapEnv reason env =
  if gapRecordEnabled.value then
    let _ = noteGap reason in env
  else
    panic reason

-- gap that returns a placeholder operand word (an unbound dict witness).
gapStr : String -> String
gapStr reason =
  if gapRecordEnabled.value then
    let _ = noteGap reason in "0"
  else
    panic reason

-- the static type of a slice-1/3 value, used to pick the print routine and the
-- int-vs-float instruction (Core IR is type-erased; for this subset the type is
-- recoverable structurally as we emit).  `LTCon` (slice 3) is an ADT value — a
-- boxed heap-cell pointer word; it never reaches an arithmetic/print site in the
-- supported subset (constructor values flow only as scrutinees and call args,
-- which are LTy-agnostic uniform i64 words), so it carries no payload type.
-- `LTClosure` (slice 4) is a first-class function value — a boxed closure cell
-- pointer word, like `LTCon`.  It flows only as a call argument and as the head of
-- an indirect call (both LTy-agnostic uniform i64 words); it never reaches an
-- arithmetic or print site in the supported subset, so it carries no payload type.
-- `LTRef` (slice 5a) is a mutable ref cell pointer word.  Like `LTCon`/`LTClosure`,
-- it never reaches an arithmetic or print site directly — so it carries no payload.
-- `LTStr` (native extern catalog slice 1) is a heap String value — a boxed cell
-- pointer word (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §4: UTF-8 bytes + cached codepoint count).  It
-- flows as a call argument and reaches the top-level print site (mdk_print_str);
-- it never reaches an arithmetic site, so it carries no payload type.
-- `LTUnit` (native extern catalog slice 3) is the Unit return type of IO-output
-- externs (putStr/putStrLn/ePutStr/ePutStrLn).  Its operand word is never
-- inspected — only auto-printed as "()" by emitPrint.
-- `LTUnknown` tags the result of a `++` whose operand static LTy could NOT be
-- recovered (the runtime-dispatched @mdk_append fallback below).  Its VALUE is
-- always correct (mdk_append inspects the cell header: String → string append,
-- list → list append), but its static kind is genuinely unknown — it may be a
-- String (the dominant `scanTriple`/`splitLines` string-builder case) OR a list
-- (e.g. typecheck.mdk's `freeEffvars a ++ rowFreeEffvars r ++ freeEffvars b`,
-- `List Int`).  Crucially it must NOT be treated as a guaranteed String: a
-- CHAINED `++` over an LTUnknown left operand re-routes through @mdk_append
-- (runtime dispatch) rather than a DIRECT @mdk_string_append, which would read a
-- list cell as a String header and segfault.  Everywhere a guaranteed String is
-- treated specially (print, `==`/`!=`) LTUnknown follows LTStr, because those
-- sites are only reached for the string-builder case in practice.
-- LTNum (G3): a POLYMORPHIC numeric operand — a `Num a =>`-constrained param
-- whose declared type head is a type variable, so it could be an Int OR a Float
-- at runtime.  The emitter can't pick int- vs float-instructions statically, so
-- LTNum arithmetic/print route through the runtime tag-dispatched @mdk_num_*
-- helpers (low-bit discriminator).  EVERYWHERE ELSE LTNum behaves like LTInt
-- (its word rep IS the tagged-int/boxed-float word, default-handled like a word),
-- so concrete Int/Float paths are byte-unchanged; only emitArith/emitPrint
-- special-case it.
data LTy =
  | LTInt
  | LTFloat
  | LTBool
  | LTCon
  | LTClosure
  | LTStr
  | LTUnit
  | LTChar
  | LTUnknown
  | LTNum
  -- LTFloatU: the register holds an UNBOXED `double`, not an i64 word.  Used ONLY
  -- for a let-bound float that is not captured by any lambda (so it never reaches
  -- the pure `lookupVar`/capWords path).  Every read through `lookupVarG` boxes it
  -- back to an i64 word (escape coercion); only `emitFloatLeaf` consumes it raw.
  | LTFloatU

-- emitter state: a fresh-name counter, the output lines (accumulated in REVERSE
-- — prepend is O(1); reverseL'd once at the end restores program order), the
-- set of known top-level FUNCTION names (slice 2), so a `CApp` head can be
-- distinguished as a direct call to `@mdk_<name>` vs an unsupported higher-order
-- application, and the function SIGNATURE table (slice 2b — `name ->
-- (param types, return type)`), which lets a `CApp` recover the callee's return
-- type and a `define` bind each parameter at its inferred type instead of
-- assuming Int.  Both the function set and the signature table are populated
-- once, up front, in emitProgram.  The fifth ref is the CONSTRUCTOR table
-- (slice 3 — `name -> arity`, read out of `CProgram`): it tells a `CVar`/`CApp`
-- head apart as an ADT constructor (→ heap-cell allocation) vs a function call /
-- local variable, and supplies the arity for the alloc + field stores.  The SIXTH
-- ref (slice 4) is a SEPARATE output buffer for LIFTED LAMBDA definitions: a CLam
-- is lambda-lifted to its own top-level `define` (LLVM has no nested functions),
-- but it is DISCOVERED mid-body while the main buffer holds the enclosing
-- function — so its `define` is emitted into this side buffer (via a save/restore
-- swap of the main buffer) and concatenated after `@main` at module end (LLVM
-- define order is irrelevant).  The SEVENTH ref (slice 5a) is the RECORD FIELD
-- ORDER table (`name -> [label_in_order]`, built by scanning CRecord nodes in the
-- program up front): it lets CFieldAccess recover a label's positional index so
-- the field can be reached by loadField without carrying type information.  The
-- EIGHTH ref (slice 6 — typeclass dispatch) is the IMPL TABLE: the program's
-- `CImplEntry` list (read out of `CProgram`).  It tells a `CMethod` site which
-- concrete impl body an `RKey`-resolved route names (→ a direct call to that
-- impl's lifted `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>`), and supplies the full impl set a
-- runtime `RDict`/`RDictFwd` site switches over by the dict's head tag.  The
-- NINTH ref (slice 7 — arg-position dispatch) is the CTOR→TYPE map (read out of
-- `CProgram`'s 3rd field): a method dispatched on its argument's runtime tag
-- (`eq`/`compare`/`show`) lowers to a bare `CVar` applied to args; the call site
-- loads the discriminating argument's constructor tag and selects the impl whose
-- TYPE owns that constructor — this map turns the loaded ctor tag into its type.
-- The TENTH ref (slice E1b — top-level value/`Ref` globals) is the GLOBALS
-- REGISTRY: `name -> (initialised?, LTy)`.  Each top-level non-`main` non-function
-- binding is emitted as a module global `@mdk_g_<name>` and computed once in an
-- `@main` init prologue (source order).  The registry lets `emitVar` recognise such
-- a name and lower it to a `load i64, ptr @mdk_g_<name>` referenceable from ANY
-- `define` (the gap #7 fix: a value binding referenced from a sibling function is
-- no longer an unbound local).  The `initialised?` flag is set as the prologue
-- computes each rhs in source order, so a reference to a NOT-yet-computed global
-- inside the prologue (a forward / mutual init dependency) is caught precisely
-- instead of loading an uninitialised 0; after the prologue every entry is `True`,
-- so any function body may reference any global.  MULTI-MODULE init (each module's
-- globals initialised in cross-module dependency order) is a D4 concern — NOT built
-- here; this is the single-program (`@main` + prologue) milestone.
data Emit =
  | Emit {
      counter : Ref Int,
      buf : Ref (List String),
      fnNames : Ref (List String),
      sigs : Ref (List (String, FnSig)),
      ctors : Ref (List (String, Int)),
      lams : Ref (List String),
      recFields : Ref (List (String, List String)),
      implEntries : Ref (List CImplEntry),
      ctorTypes : Ref (List (String, String)),
      globalsReg : Ref (List (String, (Bool, LTy))),
    }

-- a function's inferred signature: its parameter types (in order) and its return
-- type.  At the LLVM ABI every value is a uniform i64 word regardless (Int
-- immediate, Bool immediate, Float boxed-pointer-as-i64), so a signature changes
-- NO `define`/`call` LLVM type — it only drives the static LTy bookkeeping that
-- picks int-vs-float instructions in the body and the print routine for a result.
data FnSig = FnSig (List LTy) LTy

-- an emit-time environment: source name -> (its LLVM operand, its static type).
-- A "register" is any LLVM operand text: a `%tN`, or an immediate like `5` for a
-- tagged-int constant (constants are used inline, emitting no instruction).

-- ── emitter plumbing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TWO gensym namespaces (issue #118, "per-define deterministic numbering"):
--
--   * `freshId` — the MODULE-GLOBAL counter (`e.counter`).  Feeds every name that
--     must be unique across the whole LLVM module: `@.strc.N` string constants,
--     `@mdk_dc_N` constant dict cells, and every lifted-define name
--     (`@mdk_lamN`, `@mdk_eta_*`, `@mdk_pap_*`, `@mdk_methval_*`, …).  NEVER reset.
--
--   * `freshLocal` — the PER-DEFINE counter (`defineCounterRef`).  Feeds `%tN` SSA
--     registers (`freshReg`) and block labels (`then{N}`, `divok{N}`, `clause{N}`,
--     …).  Both are FUNCTION-scoped in LLVM, so restarting them at 0 in each
--     `define` is legal — and it is what makes a define's text depend only on that
--     define, not on how much code was emitted before it.
--
-- Reset by `beginDefine` at the top of every `define`; saved+restored by
-- `endDefine` around the side-buffer swap that emits a lifted define INSIDE
-- another define's emission (see `defineScope` below).
freshId : Emit -> Int
freshId e =
  let c = e.counter
  let n = c.value
  let _ = setRef c (n + 1)
  n

defineCounterRef : Ref Int
defineCounterRef = Ref 0

freshLocal : Emit -> Int
freshLocal _ =
  let n = defineCounterRef.value
  let _ = setRef defineCounterRef (n + 1)
  n

freshReg : Emit -> String
freshReg e = "%t" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)

-- ── the per-define gensym scope (issue #118) ────────────────────────────────
-- Everything keyed on, or counting, a FUNCTION-SCOPED name: the `%tN`/label
-- counter plus the two tables keyed BY a `%tN` register — `closureArityRef` and
-- `closureRetTyRef`.  Once `%tN` restarts per define, a register name is only
-- unique WITHIN a define, so those tables must be scoped the same way or `%t5` in
-- one function would answer a lookup for `%t5` in another (a wrong closure arity
-- at an indirect call = silent miscompile).  Scoping them per define is sound
-- because a table entry can only ever be READ inside the define that WROTE it:
-- the key is used verbatim as an SSA operand of the emitted `call`, and LLVM
-- requires an operand to be defined in the same function.
--
-- It also fixes a latent leak — both tables previously grew for the whole program.
data DefScope = DefScope Int (List (String, Int)) (List (String, LTy))

-- start a fresh `define` scope, returning the enclosing one (for `endDefine`).
beginDefine : Emit -> DefScope
beginDefine _ =
  let saved = DefScope defineCounterRef.value closureArityRef.value closureRetTyRef.value
  let _ = setRef defineCounterRef 0
  let _ = setRef closureArityRef []
  let _ = setRef closureRetTyRef []
  saved

-- restore the enclosing `define` scope (only needed where a define NESTS inside
-- another's emission, i.e. at each bufRef save/restore swap).
endDefine : Emit -> DefScope -> Unit
endDefine _ (DefScope n ar rt) =
  let _ = setRef defineCounterRef n
  let _ = setRef closureArityRef ar
  setRef closureRetTyRef rt

emit : Emit -> String -> Unit
emit e line = let buf = e.buf in setRef buf (line::buf.value)

-- the main output buffer ref (slice 4: needed for the save/restore swap that
-- redirects emission of a lifted lambda's `define` into the side buffer).
bufRef : Emit -> Ref (List String)
bufRef e = e.buf

-- the side buffer holding lifted-lambda `define`s, in REVERSE order (newest
-- first): each append prepends its chunk (O(chunk), amortized O(total)) instead
-- of the old O(N²) `acc ++ forward`; the consumer `reverseL`s it once at module
-- end (slice 4).
lamsRef : Emit -> Ref (List String)
lamsRef e = e.lams

-- the known-function table (set once in emitProgram); membership decides whether
-- a `CApp` head names a callable top-level function.
fnNames : Emit -> List String
fnNames e = e.fnNames.value

-- ── known-function index (perf) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
-- `fnNames` is set ONCE at Emit construction and never mutated (unlike `sigs`,
-- which the two-pass float propagation rewrites — that is why only THIS table,
-- not the sig table, is safely indexable). Index it into the shared `OrdMap`
-- (support.ordmap, a String-keyed weight-balanced tree → self-compiles in
-- llvm_emit's own graph) installed at emitProgram, so the per-CApp/CVar
-- `isKnownFn` membership test is O(log n) not O(fns).  `omFromPairs` inserts the
-- list REVERSED at the call sites below so the FIRST list entry wins on a
-- duplicate key (matches lookupAssoc's first-match). For CONSTANT tables only.

knownFnMapRef : Ref (Option (OrdMap Unit))
knownFnMapRef = Ref None

installKnownFnMap : List String -> Unit
installKnownFnMap fns = setRef knownFnMapRef (Some (omFromNames fns omEmpty))

-- ctor-arity table index (ct field is set once at Emit construction, constant).
ctorMapRef : Ref (Option (OrdMap Int))
ctorMapRef = Ref None

installCtorMap : List (String, Int) -> Unit
installCtorMap t = setRef ctorMapRef (Some (omFromPairs (reverseL t) omEmpty))

-- sig table index — ONLY for the GLOBAL-`sigs` users (fnRetTy/fnArity), which read
-- the Emit's `sigs` field. That field is set once at construction and never
-- mutated (constant), so a memo built from `sigTable e` is byte-identical to the
-- old `lookupAssoc name sigs.value`. NOTE: callRetTy/nthParamTy use a DIFFERENT,
-- THREADED `sigs` augmented with locally-inferred Float-propagation types — those
-- are NOT covered here (see the reverted sig-table attempt + fn_float_chain).
sigMapRef : Ref (Option (OrdMap FnSig))
sigMapRef = Ref None

installSigMap : List (String, FnSig) -> Unit
installSigMap t = setRef sigMapRef (Some (omFromPairs (reverseL t) omEmpty))

-- E22: the EMITTED define arity per top-level fn (post eta-saturate), the truth a
-- CDict call site needs to decide under-application.  Computed by replaying
-- etaSaturateMethodBody (deterministic, side-effect-free reads only) — the
-- SIGNATURE arity is NOT a safe proxy: a closure-returning constrained fn's define
-- has `nDicts + sigValueArity` params (eta-saturated), while a fn whose body is a
-- non-CLam expression keeps its bare clause arity (eta-saturate does not fire), so
-- the two diverge and only the replayed arity tracks the real `@mdk_<fn>` define.
defArityMapRef : Ref (Option (OrdMap Int))
defArityMapRef = Ref None

installDefArityMap : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
installDefArityMap e binds =
  setRef defArityMapRef (Some (omFromPairs (defArityPairs e binds) omEmpty))

defArityPairs : Emit -> List CBind -> List (String, Int)
defArityPairs _ [] = []
defArityPairs e (b::rest) = defArityPair e b :: defArityPairs e rest

defArityPair : Emit -> CBind -> (String, Int)
defArityPair e (CBind name [CClause pats body]) =
  let (pats2, _) = etaSaturateMethodBody e name pats body
  (name, listLen pats2)
defArityPair e (CBind name (c::rest)) = (name, clauseArityC (c::rest))
defArityPair _ (CBind name []) = (name, 0)

-- the recorded emitted define arity of `name` (None when the table is uninstalled
-- or the name is absent — fall back to the unconditional direct call).
defArityOf : String -> Option Int
defArityOf name = match defArityMapRef.value
  Some m => omLookup name m
  None => None

isKnownFn : Emit -> String -> Bool
isKnownFn e name = match knownFnMapRef.value
  Some m => match omLookup name m
    Some _ => True
    None => False
  None => contains name (fnNames e)

-- Canonicalize a referenced function name to the actual emitted symbol.  The emit
-- drivers run `mangleUnits` BEFORE `elaborateModules`, so every prelude definition
-- is renamed `core__<name>` (and uses present at mangle time are rewritten to
-- match).  But `elaborateModules` SYNTHESIZES bare references that never passed
-- through mangling — notably the `!=` rewrite (`typecheck.mdk` binopMethodApp)
-- which builds `EApp (EVar "not") …`.  Such a bare prelude name is absent from the
-- `core__`-keyed known-fn table → it would fall through to a closure-load and the
-- emitter aborts `unbound variable 'not'`.  GENERAL FIX: when a bare name is not a
-- known fn but its `core__`-mangled form IS, resolve to the mangled symbol.  This
-- covers ANY post-mangle-synthesized prelude reference, not just `not`.  A name
-- already known (incl. a user `core__`-collision) is returned unchanged, and a
-- genuinely-unbound name still falls through to the original gap.
canonFnName : Emit -> String -> String
canonFnName e name =
  if isKnownFn e name then name
  else
    let mangled = "core__" ++ name
    if isKnownFn e mangled then mangled else name

-- O(log n) lookup into the CONSTANT global sig table (the `sigMapRef` index built
-- from `sigTable e` at emitProgram). Byte-identical to `lookupAssoc name (sigTable
-- e)`: the map is built with `reverseL` so the FIRST list entry wins on a duplicate
-- key, matching lookupAssoc's first-match. Falls back to the linear scan only when
-- the map is uninstalled. Use ONLY for GLOBAL-sigs sites (`sigTable e`), never a
-- threaded/augmented `sigs` parameter (those carry local Float-propagation types).
sigLookup : Emit -> String -> Option FnSig
sigLookup e name = match sigMapRef.value
  Some m => omLookup name m
  None => lookupAssoc name (sigTable e)

-- the inferred return type of a known function (Int if unknown — keeps the
-- pre-signature behaviour for any head the table somehow misses).
fnRetTy : Emit -> String -> LTy
fnRetTy e name = match sigLookup e name
  Some (FnSig _ rty) => rty
  None => LTInt

-- ── constructor table (slice 3) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
-- the constructor arity table (set once in emitProgram); membership decides
-- whether a `CVar`/`CApp` head names an ADT constructor (→ heap allocation).
ctorTable : Emit -> List (String, Int)
ctorTable e = e.ctors.value

isCtor : Emit -> String -> Bool
isCtor e name = match ctorMapRef.value
  Some m => match omLookup name m
    Some _ => True
    None => False
  None => match lookupAssoc name (ctorTable e)
    Some _ => True
    None => False

-- ── record field-order table (slice 5a) ─────────────────────────────────────
-- maps record-type-name -> [field-names-in-declaration-order].  Built up front
-- in emitProgram by scanning all CRecord nodes in the program's CBind groups.
-- CFieldAccess uses it to convert a label name to a positional index so the
-- field can be reached by loadField without carrying type information.
recFieldTable : Emit -> List (String, List String)
recFieldTable e = e.recFields.value

-- ── impl table (slice 6) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- the program's CImplEntry list (set once in emitProgram).  A CMethod site reads
-- it two ways: `implFor` resolves an RKey-narrowed route to the ONE concrete impl
-- it names (a direct call to that impl's lifted fn); `implsOf` returns every
-- tagged impl of a method (the runtime RDict/RDictFwd dispatch-switch arms).
implEntriesOf : Emit -> List CImplEntry
implEntriesOf e = e.implEntries.value

-- the impls of a method, in program order — only the CImplTagged ones (an
-- untagged CImplDefault is the arg-tag fallback, never a static-dispatch target).
implsOf : Emit -> String -> List CImplEntry
implsOf e method = filterTagged method (implEntriesOf e)

filterTagged : String -> List CImplEntry -> List CImplEntry
filterTagged _ [] = []
filterTagged method ((CImplEntry n s (CImplTagged tag key iface ps pats body))::rest)
  | n == method = CImplEntry n s (CImplTagged tag key iface ps pats body) :: filterTagged method rest
  | otherwise = filterTagged method rest
filterTagged method (_::rest) = filterTagged method rest

-- the single tagged impl an RKey route names: the CImplTagged of `method` whose
-- type-head tag `t` OR canonical full-type key `k` equals `tag`.  The typechecker
-- stamps the canonical key (TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7) when ≥2 impls share a head tycon
-- but differ in type args; otherwise the bare head tag.  Mirrors eval.mdk's hasTag
-- (`t == tag || k == tag`).  None ⇒ the route named an impl not in the program
-- (an internal error — the typechecker only stamps tags it resolved an impl for).
implFor : Emit -> String -> String -> Option CImplEntry
implFor e method tag = findByTag tag (implsOf e method)
findByTag : String -> List CImplEntry -> Option CImplEntry
findByTag _ [] = None
findByTag tag ((CImplEntry n s (CImplTagged t k iface ps pats body))::rest)
  | t == tag || k == tag =
    Some (CImplEntry n s (CImplTagged t k iface ps pats body))
  | otherwise = findByTag tag rest
findByTag tag (_::rest) = findByTag tag rest

-- the interface DEFAULT entry (CImplDefault) for a method: the untagged body the
-- interface declared and the type's `impl` did not override.  None ⇒ no default
-- (the method has no default body — a genuine missing-impl error).
defaultFor : Emit -> String -> Option CImplEntry
defaultFor e method = findDefault method (implEntriesOf e)

findDefault : String -> List CImplEntry -> Option CImplEntry
findDefault _ [] = None
findDefault method ((CImplEntry n s (CImplDefault pats body))::rest)
  | n == method = Some (CImplEntry n s (CImplDefault pats body))
  | otherwise = findDefault method rest
findDefault method (_::rest) = findDefault method rest

-- the lifted LLVM name of an interface default method (E19): `mdk_default_<method>_<tag>`.
-- A default is emitted only on the CONCRETE RKey path (`filter`@List), keyed per
-- (method, tag) — its inner same-interface calls are re-stamped to that tag, so
-- distinct dispatch types need distinct defines.
defaultFnName : String -> String -> String
defaultFnName tag method = "mdk_default_\{method}_\{tag}"

-- the lifted LLVM name of a tagged impl method: `mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>`.  Type
-- heads and method names are alphanumeric identifiers (an operator like `+` is a
-- `CBinPrim`, never a method name here), so the concatenation is always a legal
-- LLVM symbol — clang would reject it loudly otherwise, which the gate catches.
implFnName : String -> String -> String
implFnName tag method = "mdk_impl_\{tag}_\{method}"

-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7 (native backend): the SYMBOL tag a same-head-tycon impl is
-- emitted/dispatched under.  Two impls of one method that share a head tycon but
-- differ in type args (`Def (MyPair Int Bool)` vs `Def (MyPair Bool Int)`) must NOT
-- collide on `mdk_impl_<head>_<method>` — they need distinct bodies.  When the head
-- `tag` is the SOLE impl of `(method, head)` (the common case) → the bare head tag,
-- so every existing impl's symbol stays byte-identical (fixpoint-safe).  On a
-- collision → the sanitized canonical full-type `key`, byte-distinct per impl.
-- The typechecker stamps the SAME canonical key into the RKey route (keyForSite),
-- so emitMethod's `implFnSymOf` and emitGroup's name agree.
implFnSymTag : List CImplEntry -> String -> String -> String -> String
implFnSymTag entries method tag key =
  if headTagUnique entries method tag then
    tag
  else
    safeIdent key

-- does the head tycon [tag] of [method] have a single impl, or several distinct
-- ones (C7 collision)?  Count DISTINCT canonical keys at this head — NOT raw
-- entries: the joint prelude+module entry list duplicates each prelude impl, and a
-- multi-clause impl contributes several entries that share ONE key.  A genuine
-- collision is ≥2 DIFFERENT keys at the same (method, head).
headTagUnique : List CImplEntry -> String -> String -> Bool
headTagUnique entries method tag =
  lengthS (distinctKeysAtHead entries entries method tag []) <= 1

distinctKeysAtHead : List CImplEntry -> List CImplEntry -> String -> String -> List String -> List String
distinctKeysAtHead _ [] _ _ acc = acc
distinctKeysAtHead full ((CImplEntry n _ (CImplTagged t k _ _ _ _))::rest) method tag acc
  | n == method && t == tag && not (contains k acc) =
    distinctKeysAtHead full rest method tag (k::acc)
  | otherwise = distinctKeysAtHead full rest method tag acc
distinctKeysAtHead full (_::rest) method tag acc =
  distinctKeysAtHead full rest method tag acc

-- the symbol tag for the impl an RKey route resolved to (call-site side).  [tag] is
-- the route's stamped tag — the bare head when unique, the canonical key on
-- collision; [entry] is the matched CImplEntry (carries the real head + key).
implFnSymOf : List CImplEntry -> String -> CImplEntry -> String
implFnSymOf entries method (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged t k _ _ _ _)) =
  implFnSymTag entries method t k
implFnSymOf _ _ _ = ""

-- sanitize a canonical impl key to a valid LLVM identifier (mirrors
-- private_mangle.mdk's sanitizeId: anything outside [A-Za-z0-9_] → `_`).
safeIdent : String -> String
safeIdent s = safeIdentGo s 0 (stringLength s) ""

safeIdentGo : String -> Int -> Int -> String -> String
safeIdentGo s i len acc =
  if i >= len then acc
  else
    let c = stringSlice i (i + 1) s
    let c2 = if safeChar c then c else "_"
    safeIdentGo s (i + 1) len (acc ++ c2)

-- the distinct method names of the program's tagged impls (slice 7).  A bare CVar
-- naming one of these, applied to args, is an arg-position dispatch site (return-
-- position methods are CMethod nodes, never a bare applied CVar — so this set is
-- exactly the arg-dispatched methods at a call site).
implMethodNames : Emit -> List String
implMethodNames e = dedupS (taggedMethodNames (implEntriesOf e))

taggedMethodNames : List CImplEntry -> List String
taggedMethodNames [] = []
taggedMethodNames ((CImplEntry m _ (CImplTagged _ _ _ _ _ _))::rest) =
  m :: taggedMethodNames rest
taggedMethodNames (_::rest) = taggedMethodNames rest

isImplMethod : Emit -> String -> Bool
isImplMethod e name = contains name (implMethodNames e)

-- the parameter patterns + body of an impl entry (CImplTagged or CImplDefault).
implPats : CImplEntry -> List Pat
implPats (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged _ _ _ _ pats _)) = pats
implPats (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplDefault pats _)) = pats

implBody : CImplEntry -> CExpr
implBody (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged _ _ _ _ _ body)) = body
implBody (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplDefault _ body)) = body

implEntryTag : CImplEntry -> String
implEntryTag (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged tag _ _ _ _ _)) = tag
implEntryTag (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplDefault _ _)) = ""

-- the ROUTE key the typechecker stamps for this impl — the bare head tag when the
-- head is unique (byte-identical to the pre-C7 dict header), else the canonical
-- key (C7).  Matches dictWordOfRoute's `hashName key`, so emitDispatchChain's
-- runtime `icmp` compares against the right word.
implEntryRouteKey : List CImplEntry -> CImplEntry -> String
implEntryRouteKey entries (CImplEntry n _ (CImplTagged t k _ _ _ _)) =
  if headTagUnique entries n t then
    t
  else
    k
implEntryRouteKey _ _ = ""

-- ── ctor→type map (slice 7) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- the program's constructor→type-name map (CProgram's 3rd field).  An arg-tag
-- dispatch loads the discriminating argument's constructor tag at run time; this
-- map names the constructors each candidate TYPE owns, so the dispatch chain can
-- test "is the loaded ctor tag one of type T's constructors?" by OR-ing the
-- hashName of each.
ctorTypeTable : Emit -> List (String, String)
ctorTypeTable e = e.ctorTypes.value

-- ── globals registry (slice E1b) ────────────────────────────────────────────
-- the top-level value/`Ref` globals registry ref: `name -> (initialised?, LTy)`.
-- `emitVar` consults it (via lookupVarG) to lower a value-binding reference to a
-- `load i64, ptr @mdk_g_<name>`; the `@main` init prologue computes each rhs in
-- source order and flips its flag, so a forward/mutual init dependency is caught.
globalsRegRef : Emit -> Ref (List (String, (Bool, LTy)))
globalsRegRef e = e.globalsReg

-- mark a global initialised, recording the static type its rhs computed to.  Drops
-- any prior (uninitialised) entry for the name and prepends the resolved one.
markGlobalInit : Emit -> String -> LTy -> Unit
markGlobalInit e name ty =
  let gr = globalsRegRef e
  setRef gr ((name, (True, ty)) :: dropAssoc name gr.value)

-- drop the first entry keyed `k` from an assoc list (keeps the rest in order).
dropAssoc : String -> List (String, (Bool, LTy)) -> List (String, (Bool, LTy))
dropAssoc _ [] = []
dropAssoc k ((n, v)::rest) = if n == k then rest else (n, v) :: dropAssoc k rest

-- the constructor names owned by a type, in program order.  The runtime ADTs
-- (List/Option/Result/Ordering) carry RESERVED built-in constructors that are not
-- user-declared, so they are absent from the ctor→type table (`ctorTypeTable`); an
-- arg-tag dispatch over such a type (e.g. an UNANNOTATED `fold`/`Foldable List`
-- caller whose route stays RNone — `total xs = fold (+) 0 xs`) would otherwise see
-- an empty ctor list and hit the "owns no constructors" gap.  Fall back to the
-- reserved ctors so the tag-match discriminates on the built-in cell tags.
ctorsOfType : Emit -> String -> List String
ctorsOfType e ty = match ctorsOfTypeIn ty (ctorTypeTable e)
  [] => reservedCtorsOfType ty
  cs => cs

-- the reserved built-in constructors of a runtime ADT, mirroring `reservedTag`'s
-- ordinal layout (List = Cons|Nil, Option = Some|None, Result = Ok|Err,
-- Ordering = Lt|Eq|Gt).  Empty for any other (user) type.
reservedCtorsOfType : String -> List String
reservedCtorsOfType "List" = ["Cons", "Nil"]
reservedCtorsOfType "Option" = ["Some", "None"]
reservedCtorsOfType "Result" = ["Ok", "Err"]
reservedCtorsOfType "Ordering" = ["Lt", "Eq", "Gt"]
reservedCtorsOfType _ = []

ctorsOfTypeIn : String -> List (String, String) -> List String
ctorsOfTypeIn _ [] = []
ctorsOfTypeIn ty ((c, t)::rest)
  | t == ty = c :: ctorsOfTypeIn ty rest
  | otherwise = ctorsOfTypeIn ty rest

-- ── tag / box helpers (the value-representation, made concrete) ──────────────
-- untag an immediate int word -> raw i64 (arithmetic shift, preserves sign).
untagInt : Emit -> String -> String
untagInt e v =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = ashr i64 \{v}, 1"
  r

-- tag a raw i64 -> immediate int word ((x << 1) | 1).
tagInt : Emit -> String -> String
tagInt e v =
  let s = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{s} = shl i64 \{v}, 1"
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = or i64 \{s}, 1"
  r

-- Normalise a float lexeme so it always contains a decimal point in the mantissa,
-- which LLVM requires to distinguish a floating-point constant from an integer.
-- Examples: "1e-05" → "1.0e-05", "1e+12" → "1.0e+12", "100" → "100.0".
-- Lexemes that already contain "." are returned unchanged.
-- Special values "nan", "inf", "-inf" are returned unchanged (adding ".0" would
-- corrupt them — LLVM accepts these bare).
ensureFloatDot : String -> String
ensureFloatDot s =
  if startsWith "nan" s || startsWith "inf" s || startsWith "-inf" s then s
  else match stringIndexOf "." s
    Some _ => s
    None => match stringIndexOf "e" s
      Some i =>
        "\{stringSlice 0 i s}.0e\{stringSlice (i + 1) (stringLength s) s}"
      None => match stringIndexOf "E" s
        Some i =>
          "\{stringSlice 0 i s}.0E\{stringSlice (i + 1) (stringLength s) s}"
        None => s ++ ".0"

-- box a double -> a heap cell {i64 header, double}; word = pointer-as-i64.
-- The cell is POINTER-FREE (tag + raw double), so allocate it atomic — Boehm
-- never scans its 8 payload bytes for pointers during mark (sound; no false
-- retention from float bit patterns).
boxFloat : Emit -> String -> String
boxFloat e d =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ p ++ " = call ptr @mdk_alloc_atomic(i64 16)")
  let _ = emit e ("  store i64 2, ptr " ++ p)
  let dp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{dp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 8"
  let _ = emit e "  store double \{d}, ptr \{dp}"
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  w

-- unbox a float word -> the stored double.
unboxFloat : Emit -> String -> String
unboxFloat e v =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{v} to ptr"
  let dp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{dp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 8"
  let d = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{d} = load double, ptr \{dp}"
  d

-- ── expressions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
emitExpr : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitExpr e _ (CLit l) = emitLit e l
emitExpr e env (CVar x _) = emitVar e env x
emitExpr e env (CBinPrim op l r tag) = emitBin e env op l r tag
emitExpr e env (CUnOp op x) = emitUn e env op x
emitExpr e env (CIf c t f) = emitIf e env c t f
emitExpr e env (CLet recF pat e1 e2) = emitLet e env recF pat e1 e2
emitExpr e env (CLetGroup binds body) = emitLetGroup e env binds body
emitExpr e env (CBlock stmts) = emitBlock e env stmts
emitExpr e env (CApp f a) = emitApp e env (CApp f a)
emitExpr e env (CLam pats body) = emitLam e env pats body
emitExpr e env (CDecision scrut arms tree) = emitDecision e env scrut arms tree
emitExpr e env (CTuple es) = emitTuple e env es
emitExpr e env (CRecord name fields) = emitRecordCreate e env name fields
emitExpr e env (CFieldAccess ex label recName) =
  emitFieldAccess e env ex label recName
emitExpr e env (CRecordUpdate name base updates) =
  emitRecordUpdate e env name base updates
emitExpr e env (CVariantUpdate con base updates) =
  emitVariantUpdate e env con base updates
emitExpr e env (CMethod name route implRoutes methRoutes) =
  emitMethodValue e env name route implRoutes methRoutes
emitExpr e env (CDict name routes) = emitDictValue e env name routes
emitExpr e env (CArray es) = emitArrayCreate e env es
emitExpr e env (CRangeArray lo hi incl) = emitRangeArray e env lo hi incl
emitExpr e env (CIndex a i) = emitArrayIndex e env a i
emitExpr e env (CSlice a lo hi incl) = emitArraySlice e env a lo hi incl
emitExpr e env (CStringIndex a i) = emitStringIndex e env a i
emitExpr e env (CStringSlice a lo hi incl) = emitStringSlice e env a lo hi incl
emitExpr e env (CListIndex a i) = emitListIndex e env a i
emitExpr e env (CListSlice a lo hi incl) = emitListSlice e env a lo hi incl
emitExpr e env (CList es) = emitList e env es
emitExpr e env (CRangeList lo hi incl) = emitRangeList e env lo hi incl
emitExpr _ _ other = gapE ("unsupported Core IR node " ++ ctag other)

-- a variable occurrence: a bare constructor name (slice 3) is a NULLARY ADT value
-- → allocate its heap cell; a bare KNOWN top-level FUNCTION name used as a VALUE
-- (slice 4 — not the head of a saturated call) must become a closure, so eta-wrap
-- it into a captureless static closure forwarding to `@mdk_<name>`; anything else
-- is a let/param-bound local operand.
--
-- The math/bound constants (`pi`/`e`/`intMaxBound`/…) are ordinary PRELUDE
-- bindings, so a LOCAL of the same name must shadow them — check the local env
-- FIRST for those.  (`True`/`False`/`otherwise` and constructors are reserved
-- value keywords a local can never rebind, so they stay ahead of the env check.)
-- This matters for real code: the self-hosted lexer uses `e` as a parameter name
-- pervasively (`let e = identEnd …`); intercepting it as Euler's number wrecked
-- every `substr … e` call (B1 bootstrap bug).
emitVar : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitVar e env x =
  if x == "True" then
    ("3", LTBool)
  else if x == "False" then
    ("1", LTBool)
  else if x == "otherwise" then
    ("3", LTBool)
  else if isLocal env x then
    lookupVarG e env x
  else if x == "pi" then
    (boxFloat e "3.141592653589793", LTFloat)
  else if x == "e" then
    (boxFloat e "2.718281828459045", LTFloat)
  else if x == "intMaxBound" then
    ("9223372036854775807", LTInt)
  else if x == "intMinBound" then
    ("-9223372036854775807", LTInt)
  else if x == "charMinBound" then
    ("1", LTChar)
  else if x == "charMaxBound" then
    ("2228223", LTChar)
  else if isCtor e x then
    if ctorArity e x == 0 then emitCtorAlloc e x [] else emitCtorEtaClosure e x
  else if isKnownFn e x then
    emitEtaClosure e x
  else if isKnownFn e (canonFnName e x) then
    emitEtaClosure e (canonFnName e x)
  else if isAnyExtern x then
    emitExternEtaClosure e x
  else if isImplMethod e x then
    emitMethodEtaClosure e x
  else
    lookupVarG e env x

-- is `x` a local (let/param) binding in the current emit env?  Used to let a local
-- shadow the prelude math/bound constants (pi/e/intMaxBound/…) above.
isLocal : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> Bool
isLocal env x = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some _ => True
  None => False

-- a Mut-aware variable lookup used at the emit site (emitVar): a local (let/param)
-- operand shadows everything, so the env is checked FIRST; otherwise the name may
-- be a top-level value/`Ref` global (slice E1b) → emit a `load i64, ptr @mdk_g_<x>`
-- (referenceable from any `define`).  A global that the source-order init prologue
-- has NOT yet computed (a forward/mutual init dependency) records a precise gap
-- rather than loading an uninitialised 0.  When gap-recording is on, a genuinely
-- unbound variable records a gap and returns a placeholder instead of aborting (the
-- dict/internal callers keep the pure `lookupVar` panic).
lookupVarG : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> (String, LTy)
lookupVarG e env x = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some (reg, LTFloatU) => (boxFloat e reg, LTFloat)
  Some vt => vt
  None => match lookupAssoc x (globalsRegRef e).value
    Some (True, ty) =>
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr @mdk_g_\{x}"
      (r, ty)
    Some (False, _) =>
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr @mdk_g_\{x}"
      (r, LTInt)
    None =>
      gapE ("unbound variable '" ++ x ++ "' (not a local, ctor, or known fn)")
-- A not-yet-initialised global is still loaded from its `@mdk_g_<name>` cell:
-- the `load` resolves at the instruction's RUNTIME, not emit time.  Two cases
-- reach here, both correct as a deferred load.  (1) A reference INSIDE A LAMBDA
-- body emitted during an enclosing global's init (`stmtsCons`'s continuation
-- closes over `stmtsLoop`, defined later): the closure runs only at parse time,
-- long after every global is initialised, so the load sees the real value — a
-- gap-`0` here would bake a permanent null into the closure and crash.  (2) An
-- eager same-init forward ref: orderedValBinds topologically sorts eager
-- dependencies, so a surviving eager forward ref is either a genuine value cycle
-- or an edge the sort MISSED, and the cell still holds 0.
-- ⚠️ This comment used to claim such refs are cycles that "none arise in
-- practice" and that reading 0 "is exactly the interpreter's recursive-value
-- semantics".  BOTH HALVES ARE FALSE (#553; corrected 2026-07-17).  `x = x + 1`
-- is a two-line counterexample: the interpreter BLACK-HOLES and raises
-- E-CYCLIC-VALUE (exit 1), while native silently prints 1 at exit 0 — so the
-- zero is a DEVIATION from eval, not a shared semantics.  And missed edges are
-- routine, not absent: `eagerVars` follows no calls and no dispatch, so a global
-- reached through either is invisible to the sort and lands here (silent 0 for
-- an unboxed Int, SIGSEGV for a boxed value).  See compiler/EMITTER-GAPS.md
-- "NATIVE-EAGER-INIT" for the law and the live divergence table; #561 removes
-- the approximation by making these bindings actually lazy.  Either way
-- the cell exists (declared `@mdk_g_<name> = global i64 0` up front), so emit
-- the load.  LTy is unknown until init computes it; default LTInt (the loaded
-- word is a uniform i64 — a downstream container op re-derives its shape).

emitLit : Emit -> Lit -> (String, LTy)
-- Tag boundary: n*2+1 overflows the emitter's own 63-bit Medaka Int when |n| >= 2^61.
-- For those literals, emit 64-bit shl+or instructions so tagging is done at full
-- i64 width by LLVM rather than in Medaka arithmetic (which would wrap).
-- tagSafeMax = 2^61 - 1 = 2305843009213693951: largest n where n*2+1 stays in range.
emitLit e (LInt n) =
  let tagSafeMax = 2305843009213693951
  if n >= -tagSafeMax - 1 && n <= tagSafeMax then
    (intToString (n * 2 + 1), LTInt)
  else
    (tagInt e (intToString n), LTInt)
emitLit _ (LBool b) = (if b then "3" else "1", LTBool)
emitLit e (LFloat f) =
  let w = boxFloat e (ensureFloatDot (floatToString f))
  (w, LTFloat)
-- a String literal (native extern catalog slice 1): emit a private module-scope
-- byte-array global for the UTF-8 bytes and box it at run time via @mdk_str_lit
-- (which caches the codepoint count, RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §4).  Bytes are escaped
-- one-per-`\HH` so the LLVM `c"…"` constant is byte-faithful to the source.
emitLit e (LString s) =
  let bytes = strBytes s
  let n = lengthS bytes
  let cpc = arrayLength (stringToChars s)
  let gid = ".strc." ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let _ = emitGlobal e "@\{gid} = private unnamed_addr constant { i64, i64, i64, [\{intToString (n + 1)} x i8] } { i64 1, i64 \{intToString n}, i64 \{intToString cpc}, [\{intToString (n + 1)} x i8] c\"\{escBytes bytes}\\00\" }"
  ("ptrtoint (ptr @" ++ gid ++ " to i64)", LTStr)
emitLit _ (LChar c) =
  let cp = charCode (arrayGetUnsafe 0 (stringToChars c))
  (intToString (cp * 2 + 1), LTChar)
emitLit _ LUnit = ("1", LTUnit)
emitLit _ _ = gapE "non-Int/Float/Bool/String/Char/Unit literal (slice 1)"

-- ── string literals + string externs (native extern catalog slice 1) ─────────
-- emit a module-scope line (a private string-literal global) into the side buffer
-- the lifted-lambda defines also use — LLVM module-item order is irrelevant, so a
-- `@.str.N = … constant …` appended after @main is sound (same discipline as the
-- lifted `define`s).
emitGlobal : Emit -> String -> Unit
emitGlobal e line = setRef (lamsRef e) (line::(lamsRef e).value)

-- the raw UTF-8 bytes of a string literal, in order.  `stringToChars` yields the
-- source codepoints; `utf8Bytes` re-encodes each to its UTF-8 byte sequence, so the
-- emitted global is byte-faithful to the source whatever the codepoints' width.
strBytes : String -> List Int
strBytes s = strBytesGo (stringToChars s) 0

strBytesGo : Array Char -> Int -> List Int
strBytesGo cs i
  | i >= arrayLength cs = []
  | otherwise = utf8Bytes (charCode (arrayGetUnsafe i cs))
    ++ strBytesGo cs (i + 1)

-- encode one Unicode codepoint to its UTF-8 bytes (0..255).  The bit fields are
-- disjoint, so `or` is `+` and the masks are `% 64`; no bitwise primitive needed.
utf8Bytes : Int -> List Int
utf8Bytes cp
  | cp < 128 = [cp]
  | cp < 2048 = [192 + cp / 64, 128 + cp % 64]
  | cp < 65536 = [224 + cp / 4096, 128 + (cp / 64) % 64, 128 + cp % 64]
  | otherwise = [
    240 + cp / 262144,
    128 + (cp / 4096) % 64,
    128 + (cp / 64) % 64,
    128 + cp % 64,
  ]

-- escape a byte list for an LLVM `c"…"` constant: EVERY byte as `\HH` (uppercase
-- hex).  Always valid (no quoting edge cases) and exact — the constant's declared
-- length is the byte count, and mdk_str_lit copies exactly that many bytes.
escBytes : List Int -> String
escBytes [] = ""
escBytes (b::rest) = "\\\{hex2 b}\{escBytes rest}"

hex2 : Int -> String
hex2 b = hexDigit (b / 16) ++ hexDigit (b % 16)

hexDigit : Int -> String
hexDigit d
  | d < 10 = intToString d
  | d == 10 = "A"
  | d == 11 = "B"
  | d == 12 = "C"
  | d == 13 = "D"
  | d == 14 = "E"
  | otherwise = "F"

-- the string-returning leaf externs the spike lowers to direct C-ABI calls
-- (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §5 LEAF).  `intToString` is the first: it proves the §2a
-- contract that an extern returning a Medaka value allocates it in the GC heap
-- (mdk_int_to_string -> mdk_str_lit -> mdk_alloc).  charToStr/stringConcat/putStr
-- are the obvious next additions (a follow-on slice).
isStrExtern : String -> Bool
isStrExtern name = contains name ["intToString", "stringConcat", "stringLength"]

emitStrExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitStrExtern e env "intToString" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_int_to_string(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: intToString takes exactly one argument (slice 1)"
emitStrExtern e env "stringConcat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_concat(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
