  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringConcat takes one argument (slice 5)"
emitStrExtern e env "stringLength" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let p = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{a} to ptr"
    let cpp = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{cpp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 16"
    let n = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{n} = load i64, ptr \{cpp}"
    (tagInt e n, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringLength takes one argument (slice 5)"
-- INTRINSIC: load cp_count@16, tag it

emitStrExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported string extern " ++ name)

-- numeric conversion externs (native extern catalog slice 2).
-- intToFloat / floatToInt are INTRINSIC: inline sitofp / fptosi + box/untag.
-- floatToString is a LEAF: delegates to mdk_float_to_string (mirrors mdk_print_float's
-- %.12g+dot logic but returns a mdk_str_lit box instead of printf-ing).
isNumExtern : String -> Bool
isNumExtern name = contains name [
    "intToFloat",
    "floatToInt",
    "floatToString",
    "intBitsToFloat",
    "bytesToFloat64",
    "floatRem",
    "floatToBytes64",
  ]
  || isMathUnary name
  || isMathBinary name

-- libm one-arg Float -> Float externs (native/LLVM only).  Each emits a
-- direct `call double @mdk_<name>(double x)`, unbox in / rebox out.
isMathUnary : String -> Bool
isMathUnary name = contains
  name
  [
    "sqrt",
    "cbrt",
    "exp",
    "log",
    "log2",
    "log10",
    "sin",
    "cos",
    "tan",
    "asin",
    "acos",
    "atan",
    "sinh",
    "cosh",
    "tanh",
    "floor",
    "ceil",
    "round",
    "trunc",
  ]

-- libm two-arg Float -> Float -> Float externs (native/LLVM only).
isMathBinary : String -> Bool
isMathBinary name = contains name ["pow", "atan2", "hypot"]

emitNumExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitNumExtern e env "intToFloat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let i = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = sitofp i64 \{i} to double"
    (boxFloat e r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: intToFloat takes exactly one argument (slice 2)"
-- #346 (S0): floatToInt SATURATES — NaN -> 0, +inf/over -> intMaxBound,
-- -inf/under -> intMinBound.  A raw `fptosi` on an out-of-range/NaN double is
-- LLVM POISON, and on x86 it did not merely return a wrong number: the poison
-- register was read back as a LIVE POINTER, so `println (floatToInt 1.0e19)`
-- printed an ASLR-randomized address (stable under `setarch -R`, different every
-- run with ASLR on) — an address-disclosure primitive reachable from safe,
-- non-`internal` surface.  `llvm.fptosi.sat` makes the conversion total.
--
-- The clamp is NOT redundant: fptosi.sat saturates to *i64* bounds (+-2^63), but
-- Medaka's Int is the 63-bit payload of the tagged word (RUNTIME-DESIGN §8), so
-- its domain is [-2^62, 2^62-1].  Saturating to i64 and then tagging would shift
-- INT64_MAX/MIN straight out the top and decode to -1/0 — so we must clamp to the
-- Int bounds BEFORE tagInt.  Those literals are the UNTAGGED bounds; the tagged
-- words emitVar uses for `intMaxBound`/`intMinBound` are these << 1 | 1.
-- icmp+select (not llvm.smin/smax) keeps the preamble to a single new declare.
emitNumExtern e env "floatToInt" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let d = unboxFloat e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @llvm.fptosi.sat.i64.f64(double \{d})"
    let hi = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{hi} = icmp sgt i64 \{r}, 4611686018427387903"
    let rhi = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{rhi} = select i1 \{hi}, i64 4611686018427387903, i64 \{r}"
    let lo = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{lo} = icmp slt i64 \{rhi}, -4611686018427387904"
    let rlo = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{rlo} = select i1 \{lo}, i64 -4611686018427387904, i64 \{rhi}"
    (tagInt e rlo, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: floatToInt takes exactly one argument (slice 2)"
emitNumExtern e env "floatToString" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let d = unboxFloat e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_float_to_string(double \{d})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: floatToString takes exactly one argument (slice 2)"
emitNumExtern e env "intBitsToFloat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let i = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_int_bits_to_float(i64 \{i})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: intBitsToFloat takes one argument"
emitNumExtern e env "bytesToFloat64" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [arr, off] =>
    let oi = untagInt e off
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_bytes_to_float64(i64 \{arr}, i64 \{oi})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: bytesToFloat64 takes two arguments"
-- floatToBytes64 d — encode a Float as 8 big-endian IEEE 754 bytes → Array Int.
-- The Float is unboxed to `double` then passed to mdk_float_to_bytes64; the result
-- is a freshly-allocated Array cell (LTCon, a heap-pointer word).
emitNumExtern e env "floatToBytes64" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let d = unboxFloat e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_float_to_bytes64(double \{d})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: floatToBytes64 takes one argument"
-- floatRem a b = fmod(a, b); both operands unbox to double, result reboxes.
-- Same value as the inline `frem` the `Float % Float` operator emits.
emitNumExtern e env "floatRem" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a, b] =>
    let da = unboxFloat e a
    let db = unboxFloat e b
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call double @mdk_float_rem(double \{da}, double \{db})"
    (boxFloat e r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: floatRem takes two arguments"
-- libm one-arg math externs: unbox one double, call mdk_<name>, rebox.
emitNumExtern e env name args
  | isMathUnary name = match emitArgs e env args
    [a] =>
      let d = unboxFloat e a
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call double @mdk_\{name}(double \{d})"
      (boxFloat e r, LTFloat)
    _ => panic "llvm spike: unary math extern takes one argument"
-- libm two-arg math externs: unbox two doubles, call mdk_<name>, rebox.
emitNumExtern e env name args
  | isMathBinary name = match emitArgs e env args
    [a, b] =>
      let da = unboxFloat e a
      let db = unboxFloat e b
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call double @mdk_\{name}(double \{da}, double \{db})"
      (boxFloat e r, LTFloat)
    _ => panic "llvm spike: binary math extern takes two arguments"
emitNumExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported numeric extern " ++ name)

-- IO-output externs (native extern catalog slice 3).
-- putStr/putStrLn write to stdout; ePutStr/ePutStrLn write to stderr.
-- All return Unit (LTUnit); the operand "1" is an arbitrary immediate never read.
isIoExtern : String -> Bool
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
isIoExtern name = contains
  name
  [
    "putStr",
    "putStrLn",
    "ePutStr",
    "ePutStrLn",
    "flushStdout",
    "stashRunStdout",
    "enableRunStdoutFlush",
  ]

emitIoExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitIoExtern e env "putStr" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_putstr(i64 " ++ s ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: putStr takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern e env "putStrLn" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_putstrln(i64 " ++ s ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: putStrLn takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern e env "ePutStr" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_eputstr(i64 " ++ s ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: ePutStr takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern e env "ePutStrLn" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_eputstrln(i64 " ++ s ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: ePutStrLn takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern e env "flushStdout" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_flushstdout(i64 " ++ u ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: flushStdout takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
-- "run drops stdout on panic" fix: stashRunStdout snapshots the interpreter's
-- currently-buffered stdout (a pointer+length store; no allocation) so an
-- abort can flush it; enableRunStdoutFlush arms that flush.  See
-- runtime/medaka_rt.c's mdk_flush_run_stdout_on_abort and eval.mdk's
-- appendOutput/evalModulesOutputRun.
emitIoExtern e env "stashRunStdout" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_stash_run_stdout(i64 " ++ s ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stashRunStdout takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern e env "enableRunStdoutFlush" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_enable_run_stdout_flush(i64 " ++ u ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: enableRunStdoutFlush takes exactly one argument (slice 3)"
emitIoExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported IO extern " ++ name)

-- abort externs (native extern catalog slice 4).
-- panic/exit terminate the process; the call itself never returns, so the
-- auto-print emitted downstream is dead code.  Return ("1", LTUnit) so
-- emitPrint sees LTUnit (safe) rather than LTCon/LTClosure (compile panic).
isAbortExtern : String -> Bool
isAbortExtern name = contains name ["panic", "exit", "indexError"]

emitAbortExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitAbortExtern e env name args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    if name == "indexError" then
      -- indexError reuses the existing coded E-INDEX-OOB abort (@mdk_oob); the
      -- message argument is evaluated above (for consistency with the other
      -- abort externs) but ignored -- @mdk_oob takes no args and prints the
      -- fixed "runtime error [E-INDEX-OOB]: index out of bounds" text.
      let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_oob()"
      ("1", LTUnit)
    else
      let cname = if name == "panic" then "panic" else "exit"
      let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_\{cname}(i64 \{a})"
      ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic ("llvm spike: emitAbortExtern: " ++ name ++ " takes exactly one argument (slice 4)")

-- array intrinsics (native extern catalog slice 6).
-- pure-inline: no C helper; all three reuse loadTag/loadFieldDyn/storeFieldDyn.
-- arrayLength: header word = raw element count; tag it as a Medaka Int.
-- arrayGetUnsafe: CIndex path minus bounds-check.
-- arraySetUnsafe: store to element slot; returns Unit.
isArrIntrinsic : String -> Bool
isArrIntrinsic name =
  contains name ["arrayLength", "arrayGetUnsafe", "arraySetUnsafe"]

emitArrIntrinsic : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArrIntrinsic e env "arrayLength" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [arr] => (tagInt e (loadTag e arr), LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayLength takes one argument (slice 6)"
emitArrIntrinsic e env "arrayGetUnsafe" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [i, arr] =>
    let idx_raw = untagInt e i
    (loadFieldDyn e arr idx_raw, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayGetUnsafe takes two arguments (slice 6)"
emitArrIntrinsic e env "arraySetUnsafe" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [i, x, arr] =>
    let idx_raw = untagInt e i
    let _ = storeFieldDyn e arr idx_raw x
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arraySetUnsafe takes three arguments (slice 6)"
emitArrIntrinsic _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported array intrinsic " ++ name)

-- array leaf externs (native extern catalog slice 7).
-- C helpers in runtime/medaka_rt.c; array cell = [i64 count | elem@8 | ...].
-- arrayMake/arrayCopy/arrayFromList return Array (LTCon); arrayBlit/arrayFill return Unit.
-- Do NOT end a fixture with an array-returning call: emitPrint LTCon panics.
-- Project to scalar via .[i] (CIndex) in every fixture.
isArrLeafExtern : String -> Bool
isArrLeafExtern name = contains
  name
  ["arrayMake", "arrayCopy", "arrayBlit", "arrayFill", "arrayFromList"]

emitArrLeafExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArrLeafExtern e env "arrayMake" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n, x] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_array_make(i64 \{n}, i64 \{x})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayMake takes two arguments (slice 7)"
emitArrLeafExtern e env "arrayCopy" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_array_copy(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayCopy takes one argument (slice 7)"
emitArrLeafExtern e env "arrayFromList" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [l] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_array_from_list(i64 \{l})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayFromList takes one argument (slice 7)"
emitArrLeafExtern e env "arrayBlit" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [src, so, dst, dof, len] =>
    let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_array_blit(i64 \{src}, i64 \{so}, i64 \{dst}, i64 \{dof}, i64 \{len})"
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayBlit takes five arguments (slice 7)"
emitArrLeafExtern e env "arrayFill" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [x, arr] =>
    let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_array_fill(i64 \{x}, i64 \{arr})"
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: arrayFill takes two arguments (slice 7)"
emitArrLeafExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported array leaf extern " ++ name)

-- char scalar externs (native extern catalog slice 8).
-- charCode is INTRINSIC: Char and Int share the same tagged word encoding, so
-- charCode is a pure re-type — emit the operand as-is and return LTInt.
-- charToStr delegates to mdk_char_to_str (runtime/medaka_rt.c).
isCharExtern : String -> Bool
isCharExtern name = contains name ["charCode", "charToStr"]

emitCharExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitCharExtern e env "charCode" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] => (a, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charCode takes one argument (slice 8)"
emitCharExtern e env "charToStr" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_to_str(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charToStr takes one argument (slice 8)"
emitCharExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported char extern " ++ name)

-- slice 9: string↔char conversions + codepoint slicing.
isStrCharExtern : String -> Bool
isStrCharExtern name = contains
  name
  [
    "stringToChars",
    "stringFromChars",
    "stringSlice",
    "stringToUtf8Bytes",
    "stringFromUtf8Bytes",
  ]

emitStrCharExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitStrCharExtern e env "stringToChars" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_chars(i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringToChars takes one argument (slice 9)"
emitStrCharExtern e env "stringFromChars" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_from_chars(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringFromChars takes one argument (slice 9)"
emitStrCharExtern e env "stringSlice" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [lo, hi, s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_slice(i64 \{lo}, i64 \{hi}, i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringSlice takes three arguments (slice 9)"
-- UTF-8 codec: stringToUtf8Bytes -> Array Int ($arr-shaped, LTCon); stringFromUtf8Bytes -> String.
emitStrCharExtern e env "stringToUtf8Bytes" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_utf8_bytes(i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringToUtf8Bytes takes one argument"
emitStrCharExtern e env "stringFromUtf8Bytes" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_from_utf8_bytes(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringFromUtf8Bytes takes one argument"
emitStrCharExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported str-char extern " ++ name)

-- slice 14: ASCII char classification + case mapping (unicode subset).
-- Predicate externs return raw 0/1 from C; emitter wraps with tagInt to Bool.
-- charIsPunct matches Unicode Pc/Pd/Pe/Pf/Pi/Po/Ps for ASCII (NOT C ispunct —
-- symbols like + $ = ^ ` | ~ are excluded).
isUnicodeExtern : String -> Bool
isUnicodeExtern name = contains
  name
  [
    "charIsAlpha",
    "charIsSpace",
    "charIsUpper",
    "charIsLower",
    "charIsPunct",
    "charToUpper",
    "charToLower",
    "stringToUpper",
    "stringToLower",
  ]

emitUnicodeExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charIsAlpha" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_is_alpha(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charIsAlpha takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charIsSpace" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_is_space(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charIsSpace takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charIsUpper" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_is_upper(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charIsUpper takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charIsLower" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_is_lower(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charIsLower takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charIsPunct" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_is_punct(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charIsPunct takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charToUpper" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_to_upper(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTChar)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charToUpper takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "charToLower" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_to_lower(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTChar)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charToLower takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "stringToUpper" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_upper(i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringToUpper takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern e env "stringToLower" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_lower(i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringToLower takes one argument (slice 14)"
emitUnicodeExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported unicode extern " ++ name)

-- slice 10: ADT-RETURNING externs (precursor canary).  The C side constructs the
-- result cell with the reserved tags (see `reservedTag` / runtime mdk_some etc.), so
-- a later `match` agrees.  `charFromCode` is the canary — it proves both the boxed
-- (`Some`) and immediate (`None`) reserved-tag round-trips end-to-end.  Slices 11/12/
-- 13 extend this family (stringToFloat, args, readFile, …).  Result is LTCon (an ADT
-- value): never auto-printed — fixtures `match` it down to a scalar.
isAdtExtern : String -> Bool
isAdtExtern name = contains
  name
  ["charFromCode", "stringToFloat", "stringIndexOf", "stringCompare"]

emitAdtExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitAdtExtern e env "charFromCode" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_char_from_code(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: charFromCode takes one argument (slice 10)"
emitAdtExtern e env "stringToFloat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [s] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_float(i64 \{s})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringToFloat takes one argument (slice 11)"
emitAdtExtern e env "stringIndexOf" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n, h] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_index_of(i64 \{n}, i64 \{h})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringIndexOf takes two arguments (slice 11)"
emitAdtExtern e env "stringCompare" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a, b] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_compare(i64 \{a}, i64 \{b})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: stringCompare takes two arguments (slice 11)"
emitAdtExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported adt extern " ++ name)

-- slice 12: args + env externs.  `args ()` → List String of argv[1..];
-- `getEnv name` → Option String.  Results are LTCon — never auto-printed.
isEnvExtern : String -> Bool
isEnvExtern name = contains name ["args", "getEnv"]

emitEnvExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitEnvExtern e env "args" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_args(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: args takes one (Unit) argument (slice 12)"
emitEnvExtern e env "getEnv" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_get_env(i64 \{n})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: getEnv takes one argument (slice 12)"
emitEnvExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported env extern " ++ name)

-- slice 13: file IO + stdin externs.  Result/Option/List returns are LTCon;
-- fileExists returns raw 0/1 which the emitter tags (LTBool); readLine/readAll
-- return heap Strings (LTStr).  readLine/readLineOpt/readAll are implemented
-- but NOT fixtured — the gate does not pipe stdin.
isFileExtern : String -> Bool
isFileExtern name = contains
  name
  [
    "readFile",
    "readFileBytes",
    "writeFile",
    "writeFileBytes",
    "appendFile",
    "fileExists",
    "canonicalizePath",
    "listDir",
    "readLine",
    "readLineOpt",
    "readAll",
    "readExactly",
    "makeDir",
    "removeFile",
    "rename",
    "removeDir",
    "statFile",
    "runCommand",
    "executablePath",
    "buildFingerprint",
  ]

emitFileExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitFileExtern e env "readFile" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_file(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readFile takes one argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "readFileBytes" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_file_bytes(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readFileBytes takes one argument"
emitFileExtern e env "writeFile" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p, c] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_write_file(i64 \{p}, i64 \{c})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: writeFile takes two arguments (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "writeFileBytes" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p, a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_write_file_bytes(i64 \{p}, i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: writeFileBytes takes two arguments"
emitFileExtern e env "appendFile" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p, c] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_append_file(i64 \{p}, i64 \{c})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: appendFile takes two arguments (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "fileExists" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_file_exists(i64 \{p})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: fileExists takes one argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "canonicalizePath" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_canonicalize_path(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: canonicalizePath takes one argument"
emitFileExtern e env "listDir" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_list_dir(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: listDir takes one argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "readLine" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_line(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readLine takes one (Unit) argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "readLineOpt" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_line_opt(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readLineOpt takes one (Unit) argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "readAll" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_all(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readAll takes one (Unit) argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "readExactly" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_read_exactly(i64 \{n})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: readExactly takes one (Int) argument"
emitFileExtern e env "makeDir" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_make_dir(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: makeDir takes one argument (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "removeFile" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_remove_file(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: removeFile takes one argument"
emitFileExtern e env "rename" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [old, new] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_rename(i64 \{old}, i64 \{new})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: rename takes two arguments"
emitFileExtern e env "removeDir" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_remove_dir(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: removeDir takes one argument"
emitFileExtern e env "statFile" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_stat_file(i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: statFile takes one argument"
emitFileExtern e env "runCommand" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [prog, argv] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_run_command(i64 \{prog}, i64 \{argv})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: runCommand takes two arguments (slice 13)"
emitFileExtern e env "executablePath" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_executable_path(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: executablePath takes one (Unit) argument"
emitFileExtern e env "buildFingerprint" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_build_fingerprint(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: buildFingerprint takes one (Unit) argument"
emitFileExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported file extern " ++ name)

-- Net externs (NET-DESIGN.md).  A SEPARATE family from isFileExtern — deliberately,
-- so the wasm backend can reject the whole net set in one guard.  All return a
-- Result (LTCon) traffic'ing raw tagged-Int fds; blocking BSD-socket shims in
-- runtime/medaka_rt.c.
isNetExtern : String -> Bool
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
isNetExtern name = contains
  name
  [
    "netResolve",
    "netTcpConnect",
    "netTcpListen",
    "netListenPort",
    "netTcpAccept",
    "netSend",
    "netRecv",
    "netShutdown",
    "netClose",
    "netSetTimeout",
  ]

emitNetExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitNetExtern e env "netResolve" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [h] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_resolve(i64 \{h})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netResolve takes one argument"
emitNetExtern e env "netTcpConnect" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [h, p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_tcp_connect(i64 \{h}, i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netTcpConnect takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern e env "netTcpListen" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a, p] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_tcp_listen(i64 \{a}, i64 \{p})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netTcpListen takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern e env "netListenPort" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_listen_port(i64 \{fd})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netListenPort takes one argument"
emitNetExtern e env "netTcpAccept" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_tcp_accept(i64 \{fd})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netTcpAccept takes one argument"
emitNetExtern e env "netSend" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd, bs] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_send(i64 \{fd}, i64 \{bs})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netSend takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern e env "netRecv" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd, n] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_recv(i64 \{fd}, i64 \{n})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netRecv takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern e env "netShutdown" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd, how] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_shutdown(i64 \{fd}, i64 \{how})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netShutdown takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern e env "netClose" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_close(i64 \{fd})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netClose takes one argument"
emitNetExtern e env "netSetTimeout" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [fd, ms] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_net_set_timeout(i64 \{fd}, i64 \{ms})"
    (r, LTCon)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: netSetTimeout takes two arguments"
emitNetExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported net extern " ++ name)

-- Tier D: RNG (deterministic SplitMix64).  The C runtime holds the state; the
-- algorithm matches lib/eval.ml splitmix64_next, seeded identically, so output is
-- byte-identical per seed.  randomInt/Bool/Char return RAW words tagged here;
-- randomFloat returns a boxed Float word; setSeed is Unit.
isRngExtern : String -> Bool
isRngExtern name = contains
  name
  ["randomInt", "randomBool", "randomFloat", "randomChar", "setSeed"]

emitRngExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitRngExtern e env "randomInt" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [lo, hi] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_random_int(i64 \{lo}, i64 \{hi})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: randomInt takes two arguments (Tier D)"
emitRngExtern e env "randomBool" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_random_bool(i64 \{u})"
    (tagInt e r, LTBool)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: randomBool takes one (Unit) argument (Tier D)"
emitRngExtern e env "randomFloat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_random_float(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: randomFloat takes one (Unit) argument (Tier D)"
emitRngExtern e env "randomChar" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_random_char(i64 \{u})"
    (tagInt e r, LTChar)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: randomChar takes one (Unit) argument (Tier D)"
emitRngExtern e env "setSeed" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_set_seed(i64 " ++ n ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: setSeed takes one argument (Tier D)"
emitRngExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported rng extern " ++ name)

-- wallTimeSec / allocBytes: both take Unit and return a boxed Float (same ABI
-- as randomFloat).  C: mdk_wall_time_sec / mdk_alloc_bytes in medaka_rt.c.
isPerfExtern : String -> Bool
isPerfExtern name =
  contains name ["wallTimeSec", "monotonicSec", "sleepMs", "allocBytes"]

emitPerfExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitPerfExtern e env "wallTimeSec" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_wall_time_sec(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: wallTimeSec takes one (Unit) argument"
emitPerfExtern e env "monotonicSec" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_monotonic_sec(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: monotonicSec takes one (Unit) argument"
emitPerfExtern e env "sleepMs" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [n] =>
    let _ = emit e ("  call void @mdk_sleep_ms(i64 " ++ n ++ ")")
    ("1", LTUnit)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: sleepMs takes one (Int) argument"
emitPerfExtern e env "allocBytes" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [u] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_alloc_bytes(i64 \{u})"
    (r, LTFloat)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: allocBytes takes one (Unit) argument"
emitPerfExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported perf extern " ++ name)

-- Hashable per-type hashers (specified, byte-identical to lib/eval.ml hash_*).
-- Each unpacks the native arg the C helper wants (untagged int / codepoint /
-- unboxed double / String cell pointer) and tags the RAW Int result.  hashBool
-- untags the 0/1 bool word.  See runtime/medaka_rt.c mdk_hash_*.
-- Bitwise / shift externs (PURE).  Args are tagged ints — untag each operand,
-- call the C helper on the raw values, retag the result.  shiftRight is LOGICAL
-- (C >> on the untagged non-negative value == OCaml lsr for non-negative);
-- bitNot's retag (shl/or) drops the top bit so it matches OCaml lnot on the
-- 63-bit rep.  See runtime/medaka_rt.c mdk_bit_*/mdk_shift_*.
isBitExtern : String -> Bool
isBitExtern name = contains
  name
  ["bitAnd", "bitOr", "bitXor", "shiftLeft", "shiftRight", "bitNot"]

emitBitBin : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitBitBin e env sym args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a, b] =>
    let x = untagInt e a
    let y = untagInt e b
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @\{sym}(i64 \{x}, i64 \{y})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic ("llvm spike: " ++ sym ++ " takes two arguments")

emitBitExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitBitExtern e env "bitAnd" args = emitBitBin e env "mdk_bit_and" args
emitBitExtern e env "bitOr" args = emitBitBin e env "mdk_bit_or" args
emitBitExtern e env "bitXor" args = emitBitBin e env "mdk_bit_xor" args
emitBitExtern e env "shiftLeft" args = emitBitBin e env "mdk_shift_left" args
emitBitExtern e env "shiftRight" args = emitBitBin e env "mdk_shift_right" args
emitBitExtern e env "bitNot" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let x = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_bit_not(i64 \{x})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: bitNot takes one argument"
emitBitExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported bit extern " ++ name)

isHashExtern : String -> Bool
isHashExtern name =
  contains name ["hashInt", "hashChar", "hashBool", "hashFloat", "hashString"]

emitHashExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitHashExtern e env "hashInt" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let i = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_hash_int(i64 \{i})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: hashInt takes one argument"
emitHashExtern e env "hashChar" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let cp = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_hash_char(i64 \{cp})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: hashChar takes one argument"
emitHashExtern e env "hashBool" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let b = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_hash_bool(i64 \{b})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: hashBool takes one argument"
emitHashExtern e env "hashFloat" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let d = unboxFloat e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_hash_float(double \{d})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: hashFloat takes one argument"
emitHashExtern e env "hashString" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_hash_string(i64 \{a})"
    (tagInt e r, LTInt)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: hashString takes one argument"
emitHashExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported hash extern " ++ name)

-- Debug-literal externs (byte-exact quoted/escaped rendering; no oracle change).
-- The String/Char arg word passes straight to the C helper (mdk_debug_*_lit
-- reads the cell / untags the codepoint); result is a heap String (LTStr).
isDebugLitExtern : String -> Bool
isDebugLitExtern name = contains name ["debugStringLit", "debugCharLit"]

emitDebugLitExtern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitDebugLitExtern e env "debugStringLit" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_debug_string_lit(i64 \{a})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: debugStringLit takes one argument"
emitDebugLitExtern e env "debugCharLit" args = match emitArgs e env args
  [a] =>
    let cp = untagInt e a
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_debug_char_lit(i64 \{cp})"
    (r, LTStr)
  _ => panic "llvm spike: debugCharLit takes one argument"
emitDebugLitExtern _ _ name _ = gapE ("unsupported debug-lit extern " ++ name)

-- ── extern dispatch: applied form ───────────────────────────────────────────
-- Is `name` a KNOWN extern of any family the emitter handles?  The OR of every
-- family predicate (plus the `arrayMakeWith` intrinsic, which has no `isXxx`
-- predicate — it is dispatched by exact name).  Drives both the saturated CApp
-- head path (emitExternApplied) and the value-position eta-wrap (emitVar →
-- emitExternEtaClosure); keeping the membership in ONE place means the two paths
-- can never disagree about what counts as an extern.
isAnyExtern : String -> Bool
isAnyExtern name = isStrExtern name
  || isNumExtern name
  || isIoExtern name
  || isAbortExtern name
  || isArrIntrinsic name
  || name == "arrayMakeWith"
  || isArrLeafExtern name
  || isCharExtern name
  || isStrCharExtern name
  || isUnicodeExtern name
  || isAdtExtern name
  || isEnvExtern name
  || isFileExtern name
  || isNetExtern name
  || isRngExtern name
  || isHashExtern name
  || isBitExtern name
  || isDebugLitExtern name
  || isPerfExtern name

-- SATURATED extern application: route a known extern + its (non-empty) argument
-- list to the right family emitter.  Factored out of emitApp's CVar-head ladder
-- so that the VALUE-position eta-wrap (emitExternEtaClosure) can REPLAY the exact
-- applied-form codegen on synthetic `%argK` operands — value/applied byte-
-- consistency by construction, no parallel codegen to drift.  The family order is
-- the ladder's original order (behaviour-preserving; `arrayMakeWith` keeps its
-- exact-name slot ahead of the arrayLeaf family).  Caller guards `hasArgs args`.
emitExternApplied : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitExternApplied e env fname args =
  if isStrExtern fname then
    emitStrExtern e env fname args
  else if isNumExtern fname then
    emitNumExtern e env fname args
  else if isIoExtern fname then
    emitIoExtern e env fname args
  else if isAbortExtern fname then
    emitAbortExtern e env fname args
  else if isArrIntrinsic fname then
    emitArrIntrinsic e env fname args
  else if fname == "arrayMakeWith" then
    emitArrayMakeWith e env args
  else if isArrLeafExtern fname then
    emitArrLeafExtern e env fname args
  else if isCharExtern fname then
    emitCharExtern e env fname args
  else if isStrCharExtern fname then
    emitStrCharExtern e env fname args
  else if isUnicodeExtern fname then
    emitUnicodeExtern e env fname args
  else if isAdtExtern fname then
    emitAdtExtern e env fname args
  else if isEnvExtern fname then
    emitEnvExtern e env fname args
  else if isFileExtern fname then
    emitFileExtern e env fname args
  else if isNetExtern fname then
    emitNetExtern e env fname args
  else if isRngExtern fname then
    emitRngExtern e env fname args
  else if isHashExtern fname then
    emitHashExtern e env fname args
  else if isBitExtern fname then
    emitBitExtern e env fname args
  else if isPerfExtern fname then
    emitPerfExtern e env fname args
  else
    emitDebugLitExtern e env fname args

emitBin : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitBin e env op l r tag
  | isCmpOp op = emitCmp e env op l r
  | isArithOp op = emitArith e env op l r tag
  | op == "::" =
    let (lv, _) = emitExpr e env l
    let (rv, _) = emitExpr e env r
    emitCtorAlloc e "Cons" [lv, rv]
  | op == "++" =
    let (lv, lty) = emitExpr e env l
    let (rv, _) = emitExpr e env r
    let r2 = freshReg e
    match lty
      LTStr =>
        let _ = emit e "  \{r2} = call i64 @mdk_string_append(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
        (r2, LTStr)
      LTCon =>
        let _ = emit e "  \{r2} = call i64 @mdk_list_append(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
        (r2, LTCon)
      LTUnknown =>
        let _ = emit e "  \{r2} = call i64 @mdk_append(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
        (r2, LTUnknown)
      _ =>
        let _ = emit e "  \{r2} = call i64 @mdk_append(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
        (r2, LTUnknown)
  | otherwise = gapE ("unsupported binary operator '" ++ op ++ "'")
-- LTUnknown: the left operand is itself a runtime-dispatched-append result
-- (a CHAINED `++`, e.g. `a ++ b ++ c`).  Its kind is genuinely unknown — it
-- may be a String OR a list (typecheck.mdk's `freeEffvars` builds `List Int`
-- this way).  Re-route through @mdk_append (NOT a direct @mdk_string_append,
-- which would crash on a list cell), keeping the result LTUnknown so any
-- further chaining stays runtime-dispatched.  Value-identical to the direct
-- path for the string case (mdk_append → mdk_string_append on a String
-- header), so every byte-diff gate is unaffected.

-- E8 (#3 census-B): operand static LTy unknown (E2c/E7 couldn't recover
-- it) — the ~328 scanTriple/splitLines string-builder residual.  Instead
-- of gapE, dispatch at RUNTIME via @mdk_append, which inspects the left
-- cell header (odd => Nil/list; even header==MDK_STR_TAG => string; else
-- => Cons/list) and calls the right append helper.  The result VALUE is
-- always correct.  Result LTy is statically unknowable here, so we tag it
-- LTUnknown (NOT LTStr): LTUnknown follows LTStr at print/`==` sites (the
-- dominant String-builder downstream) but a CHAINED `++` over it re-routes
-- through @mdk_append instead of a direct @mdk_string_append — closing the
-- segfault when the value is actually a list.  emitPrint LTCon already
-- panics on lists, and fixtures project String results through
-- stringLength (a type-forcing String read) so validation is
-- byte-identical and independent of this default.

-- the arithmetic operators the spike lowers to int/float instructions.  `::` and
-- `++` survive lowering as CBinPrim but are NOT arithmetic — they fall to the gap.
isArithOp : String -> Bool
isArithOp op = op == "+" || op == "-" || op == "*" || op == "/" || op == "%"

-- ── float-expression fusion (unboxed double arithmetic) ─────────────────────
-- Float arith/compare otherwise boxes a 16B cell at EVERY intermediate node and
-- round-trips each result through a box even when the parent immediately
-- re-consumes it.  Fusion computes a maximal float-arith subtree in unboxed
-- `double` SSA registers and boxes ONCE at the boundary (and ZERO times when the
-- consumer is a comparison).  Bit-identical results — box/unbox is a pure memory
-- round-trip of the same double.  No ABI change; only the statically-Float path
-- (the polymorphic LTNum path is untouched).

-- Statically Float?  CONSERVATIVE (no false positives vs emitExpr's emitted LTy):
-- a float literal; a local typed LTFloat; an arith node whose left operand is
-- statically float (arith preserves operand type).  A false negative only loses
-- the optimization (falls to the boxing path); it can never miscompile.
staticIsFloat : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Bool
staticIsFloat env (CLit (LFloat _)) = True
staticIsFloat env (CVar x _) = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some (_, LTFloat) => True
  Some (_, LTFloatU) => True
  _ => False
staticIsFloat env (CBinPrim op l _ _) = isArithOp op && staticIsFloat env l
staticIsFloat env _ = False

-- CONSERVATIVE "does this expression evidently evaluate to a Float?" check, used to
-- record a lambda's return type.  Unlike staticIsFloat (which checks only the LEFT
-- arith operand, for the unbox-fusion fast path) this is SYMMETRIC over arith (a
-- float on EITHER side ⇒ the homogeneous `+`/`-`/`*`/`/` result is float) and
-- descends if/let/block tails.  Only a float LITERAL or a known-float var (capture)
-- makes it True, so it never false-positives a non-float body.
bodyFloatRet : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Bool
bodyFloatRet env (CLit (LFloat _)) = True
bodyFloatRet env (CVar x _) = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some (_, LTFloat) => True
  Some (_, LTFloatU) => True
  _ => False
bodyFloatRet env (CBinPrim op l r _) = isArithOp op
  && (bodyFloatRet env l || bodyFloatRet env r)
bodyFloatRet env (CIf _ t f) = bodyFloatRet env t || bodyFloatRet env f
bodyFloatRet env (CLet _ _ _ b) = bodyFloatRet env b
bodyFloatRet env (CBlock stmts) = blockFloatRet env stmts
bodyFloatRet env _ = False

-- a block evaluates to its LAST statement's value (a trailing CSLet yields Unit).
blockFloatRet : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CStmt -> Bool
blockFloatRet _ [] = False
blockFloatRet env [CSExpr e] = bodyFloatRet env e
blockFloatRet _ [_] = False
blockFloatRet env (_::rest) = blockFloatRet env rest

-- record a closure word's return type as LTFloat when its body evidently returns a
-- Float (so a saturated indirect call to it types its result LTFloat).
recordFloatRet : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> Unit
recordFloatRet e env w body =
  if bodyFloatRet env body then
    setRef closureRetTyRef ((w, LTFloat)::closureRetTyRef.value)
  else
    ()

-- Emit `ex` as an UNBOXED double register (no heap box).  Recurses through float
-- arith nodes; a non-arith leaf is emitted normally to a word then unboxed once
-- (matching emitArith's existing LTFloat branch, which already unboxes operands on
-- the strength of the left's type).  Only called in statically-Float context.
emitFloatD : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String
emitFloatD e env (CLit (LFloat f)) = ensureFloatDot (floatToString f)
emitFloatD e env (ex@(CBinPrim op l r _)) =
  if isArithOp op then
    let ld = emitFloatD e env l
    let rd = emitFloatD e env r
    let res = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{floatOp op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
    res
  else emitFloatLeaf e env ex
emitFloatD e env ex = emitFloatLeaf e env ex

emitFloatLeaf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String
emitFloatLeaf e env ex = match floatFromIntArg ex
  Some a =>
    let (aw, _) = emitExpr e env a
    let i = untagInt e aw
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = sitofp i64 \{i} to double"
    r
  None => emitFloatLeafN e env ex

-- a saturated call to a float-fw fn → call its `$fw` (double-ABI) directly (float-pos
-- args as doubles, others i64) and use the returned double — no box/unbox round-trip.
emitFloatLeafN : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String
emitFloatLeafN e env ex = match flattenApp ex []
  (CVar f _, args) => match floatFwHas f (lengthS args) && not (isLocal env f)
    True =>
      let ops = emitWorkerArgs e env (floatFwFp f) 0 args
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call double @mdk_\{f}__fw(\{ops})"
      r
    False => emitFloatLeafVar e env ex
  _ => emitFloatLeafVar e env ex

emitFloatLeafVar : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String
emitFloatLeafVar e env ex = match floatVarReg env ex
  Some reg => reg
  None =>
    let (w, _) = emitExpr e env ex
    unboxFloat e w

-- floatFwSetRef accessors: does `f` have a `$fw` of arity `n`, and its float positions.
floatFwHas : String -> Int -> Bool
floatFwHas f n = match lookupAssoc f floatFwSetRef.value
  Some (ar, _) => ar == n
  None => False

floatFwFp : String -> List Int
floatFwFp f = match lookupAssoc f floatFwSetRef.value
  Some (_, fp) => fp
  None => []

-- in a statically-Float context, `intToFloat x` / `fromInt x` (@ Float) is just an
-- int→double conversion: emit `sitofp` directly instead of the extern/impl call's
-- box-then-unbox. Returns the Int arg if `ex` is such a call (head name match, arity 1).
floatFromIntArg : CExpr -> Option CExpr
floatFromIntArg ex = match flattenApp ex []
  (h, a::[]) =>
    if headNameIs h "intToFloat" || headNameIs h "fromInt" then
      Some a
    else
      None
  _ => None

headNameIs : CExpr -> String -> Bool
headNameIs (CVar n _) name = n == name
headNameIs (CMethod n _ _ _) name = n == name
headNameIs _ _ = False

-- the unboxed-double register for an LTFloatU let-bound CVar (a float-let kept
-- unboxed in the env), else None — then emitFloatLeaf unboxes the boxed word.
floatVarReg : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Option String
floatVarReg env (CVar x _) = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some (reg, LTFloatU) => Some reg
  _ => None
floatVarReg _ _ = None

-- arithmetic preserves operand type (well-typed: both operands share a type).
-- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): [tag] is the typecheck-stamped scalar type of this
-- arithmetic node ("Float"/"Int"/""; from CBinPrim's RScalar route).  When it is
-- "Float" (fieldNameToLTy → LTFloat) route to the float path even when the
-- structural staticIsFloat can't see through a type-lost operand (e.g. a value
-- bound from a polymorphic HOF, #9) — additive: an absent/non-Float tag falls back
-- to today's structural detection, so the tag only ever ADDS Float knowledge.
scalarTagIsFloat : String -> Bool
scalarTagIsFloat tag = match fieldNameToLTy tag
  Some LTFloat => True
  _ => False

emitArith : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitArith e env op l r tag =
  if scalarTagIsFloat tag || staticIsFloat env l || isFloatFieldAccess e l || isFloatFieldAccess e r then
    emitFloatArith e env op l r
  else
    emitArithW e env op l r

-- True when `ex` is a record field ACCESS of a declared-Float field.  Used ONLY to
-- route arith to the float path (emitFloatArith unboxes the boxed-float field word
-- via emitFloatLeafVar) — `r.dx + r.dy` over Float fields otherwise defaulted both
-- operands to LTInt (emitFieldAccess always returns LTInt) and did INTEGER arith on
-- boxed-float pointers → garbage.  Scoped to the arith ROUTING decision (no change
-- to emitFieldAccess's returned LTy), so field accesses flowing elsewhere — e.g.
-- arg-tag dispatch — keep LTInt and the emitter's own self-compile is unperturbed.
isFloatFieldAccess : Emit -> CExpr -> Bool
isFloatFieldAccess e (CFieldAccess _ label recName) =
  isFloatFieldLabel e recName label
isFloatFieldAccess _ _ = False

isFloatFieldLabel : Emit -> String -> String -> Bool
isFloatFieldLabel e recName label = match floatFieldRecord e recName label
  Some (name, labels) => match indexOfStr label labels
    Some pos =>
      let tns = ctorFieldTypeNamesOf name
      if pos < listLen tns then nthStr tns pos == "Float" else False
    None => False
  None => False

-- Pick the record owning `label` for float-ness: by the typecheck-stamped name
-- when known + present, else the label-only search (same fallback rule as
-- fieldIdxByName, so an Int/Float collision on the same field name routes float
-- arithmetic by the receiver's actual record).
floatFieldRecord : Emit -> String -> String -> Option (String, List String)
floatFieldRecord e recName label =
  if recName == "" then findRecordByLabel (recFieldTable e) label
  else match lookupAssoc recName (recFieldTable e)
    Some labels =>
      if contains label labels then
        Some (recName, labels)
      else
        findRecordByLabel (recFieldTable e) label
    None => findRecordByLabel (recFieldTable e) label

-- fused statically-Float arithmetic: both operands as unboxed doubles, one float
-- op, box ONCE (replaces N intermediate boxes with 1).
emitFloatArith : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitFloatArith e env op l r =
  let ld = emitFloatD e env l
  let rd = emitFloatD e env r
  let res = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{floatOp op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
  (boxFloat e res, LTFloat)

-- the boxing fallback path (left operand not statically float): unchanged from the
-- original emitArith.  Still reached for float values the static check misses (e.g.
-- a float-returning CALL on the left) — its LTFloat branch boxes as before.
emitArithW : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArithW e env op l r =
  let (lv, lty) = emitExpr e env l
  let (rv, rty) = emitExpr e env r
  match lty
    LTFloat =>
      let ld = unboxFloat e lv
      let rd = unboxFloat e rv
      let res = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{floatOp op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
      (boxFloat e res, LTFloat)
    LTNum =>
      let res = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{res} = call i64 @\{numOp op}(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
      (res, LTNum)
    _ => match rty
      LTFloat =>
        let ld = unboxFloat e lv
        let rd = unboxFloat e rv
        let res = freshReg e
        let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{floatOp op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
        (boxFloat e res, LTFloat)
      LTNum =>
        let res = freshReg e
        let _ = emit e "  \{res} = call i64 @\{numOp op}(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
        (res, LTNum)
      _ =>
        let li = untagInt e lv
        let ri = untagInt e rv
        if op == "/" || op == "%" then emitIntDivZeroChecked e op li ri
        else
          let res = freshReg e
          let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{intOp op} i64 \{li}, \{ri}"
          (tagInt e res, LTInt)

-- Integer `/` or `%`: guard the divisor against zero before the hardware
-- sdiv/srem.  A zero divisor is UB in LLVM (the div is elided / yields garbage
-- and the program silently exits 0), so branch to a runtime abort
-- (@mdk_div_zero / @mdk_mod_zero: prints `runtime error [E-DIV-ZERO]` /
-- `[E-MOD-ZERO]` to stderr, exits nonzero), matching the interpreter's evalArith
-- guard.  No source location is baked in — Core IR carries none, so the native
-- trap is the interpreter's message MINUS `file:L:C:` (a located trap is a
-- follow-on).  On a nonzero divisor the ok-branch runs the original sdiv/srem
-- unchanged, so valid-division output is byte-identical.
emitIntDivZeroChecked : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitIntDivZeroChecked e op li ri =
  let z = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{z} = icmp eq i64 \{ri}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let zeroL = "divzero" ++ n
  let okL = "divok" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{z}, label %\{zeroL}, label %\{okL}"
  let _ = emit e (zeroL ++ ":")
  let abortFn = if op == "%" then "mdk_mod_zero" else "mdk_div_zero"
  let _ = emit e "  call void @\{abortFn}()"
  let _ = emit e "  unreachable"
  let _ = emit e (okL ++ ":")
  let res = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{res} = \{intOp op} i64 \{li}, \{ri}"
  (tagInt e res, LTInt)
-- G3: a POLYMORPHIC Num operand (declared type-var param) — could be Int OR
-- Float at runtime.  Route through the runtime tag-dispatched @mdk_num_* helper
-- (low-bit discriminator), keeping the result LTNum so a chained `Num` op and
-- the polymorphic print site stay runtime-dispatched.  An LTNum on EITHER side
-- propagates (the other operand shares the type by well-typing).

-- comparison always yields a Bool word; dispatch the compare on operand type.
emitCmp : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitCmp e env op l r =
  if staticIsFloat env l then
    emitFloatCmp e env op l r
  else
    emitCmpW e env op l r

-- fused statically-Float comparison: operands as unboxed doubles, fcmp, NO box.
emitFloatCmp : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitFloatCmp e env op l r =
  let ld = emitFloatD e env l
  let rd = emitFloatD e env r
  let c = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{c} = fcmp \{floatPred op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
  boolFromI1 e c

emitCmpW : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitCmpW e env op l r =
  let (lv, lty) = emitExpr e env l
  let (rv, rty) = emitExpr e env r
  if (isLTUnknown lty || isLTUnknown rty) && isEqOp op then emitValueEq e op lv rv
  else
    if isStrLTy lty || isStrLTy rty then emitStrCmp e op lv rv
    else match lty
      LTFloat =>
        let ld = unboxFloat e lv
        let rd = unboxFloat e rv
        let c = freshReg e
        let _ = emit e "  \{c} = fcmp \{floatPred op} double \{ld}, \{rd}"
        boolFromI1 e c
      LTInt =>
        if isEqOp op then
          emitValueEq e op lv rv
        else
          emitValueCmp e op lv rv
      -- Same split as LTInt: `==`/`!=` are @mdk_value_eq's job, ordering is
      -- emitValueCmp's.  (This arm USED to send BOTH to the 3-way, where `==`
      -- worked only incidentally — `icmp eq raw, 0` ⟺ "compare said EQ" — and
      -- inherited the 3-way's NaN collapse, so a Float reaching here compared
      -- `nan == nan` as True.  @mdk_value_eq's `da == db` is IEEE. #305)
      _ =>
        if isEqOp op then
          emitValueEq e op lv rv
        else
          emitValueCmp e op lv rv
-- A boxed-string operand (EITHER side — a `""` literal forces it even when the
-- other side's static LTy wasn't inferred to LTStr) routes `==`/`!=` through
-- @mdk_string_eq; pointer identity (the integer arm) is WRONG for string cells.
-- An LTUnknown operand (chained-`++` result) could be a String OR a list, so
-- for `==`/`!=` route through @mdk_value_eq (runtime String/word discriminator)
-- rather than @mdk_string_eq, which would crash reading a list cell as a String.

-- Unknown static type (LTInt default — could be Int/Bool/Char OR a boxed
-- String/Float the emitter couldn't recover, e.g. `k <= k2` where `k`/`k2`
-- were bound through a TUPLE destructure inside a recursive list walk, losing
-- their `(String,String)` component type → both default to LTInt): an integer
-- `icmp` then compares boxed-String/Float POINTERS as ints — pointer-identity
-- (heap-ADDRESS) ordering, and so WRONG.  Route BOTH equality AND ordering
-- through the runtime discriminators: `==`/`!=` via @mdk_value_eq, `<`/`>`/…
-- via the per-op IEEE predicates @mdk_value_lt/le/gt/ge (Float cell → direct f64
-- predicate, #305; String cell → byte compare; immediate → word compare, the
-- latter two over the shared 3-way @mdk_value_cmp_raw), mirroring mdk_append's runtime
-- String/List discrimination.  This is the ordering analog of the @mdk_value_eq
-- precedent — concrete-Int ordering also routes through the runtime helper
-- (as concrete-Int `==` already does), staying correct and uniform.
--
-- An `if`-EXPRESSION (not a guarded `LTInt if isEqOp op` arm) keeps this
-- function self-compilable: the gap-tolerant bootstrap emitter cannot lower a
-- guarded match arm.  IR is byte-identical to the guarded form.

-- ordering (`<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=`) of two operands of statically-unknown kind via the
-- runtime per-op IEEE predicates @mdk_value_lt/le/gt/ge (note at emitCmp), which
-- return a TAGGED Bool directly — no `icmp … , 0` step.
--
-- #305 (S0): this USED to call the three-way @mdk_value_cmp_raw and then `icmp
-- <intPred op> raw, 0`.  That is wrong at Float+NaN: a -1/0/1 Ordering
-- STRUCTURALLY CANNOT express NaN's unorderedness, so the runtime's Float arm
-- collapsed an unordered pair to 0 (EQ) and `nan <= nan` read as `0 <= 0` = True
-- — while `run` (eval) and the static-Float path (`fcmp ole`, #288) both said
-- False.  EMITTER-SEMANTICS §4 N5/N6: the four relational ops at Float are
-- PRIMITIVE IEEE predicates on EVERY path and are never derived from `compare`.
-- The runtime helpers take the boxed-Float arm themselves (plain f64 compare —
-- False at NaN, matching inline `fcmp`) and fall back to the 3-way only for the
-- String/immediate shapes, where -1/0/1 is exact.
emitValueCmp : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitValueCmp e op lv rv =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_value_\{valueRelName op}(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
  (r, LTBool)

-- The @mdk_value_* suffix for an ordering op.  Only `<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` reach
-- emitValueCmp — emitCmpW routes `==`/`!=` to emitValueEq before it.
valueRelName : String -> String
valueRelName "<" = "lt"
valueRelName ">" = "gt"
valueRelName "<=" = "le"
valueRelName ">=" = "ge"
valueRelName op = panic ("llvm: unsupported value ordering op " ++ op)

-- equality of two operands of statically-unknown kind via the runtime
-- discriminator (note at emitCmp).  `==` returns @mdk_value_eq's tagged Bool
-- directly; `!=` flips 3<->1 with `xor … 2` (same shape as emitStrCmp).
emitValueEq : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitValueEq e op lv rv
  | op == "==" =
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_value_eq(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
    (r, LTBool)
  | otherwise =
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_value_eq(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
    let n = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{n} = xor i64 \{r}, 2"
    (n, LTBool)

isEqOp : String -> Bool
isEqOp op = op == "==" || op == "!="

isStrLTy : LTy -> Bool
isStrLTy LTStr = True
-- LTUnknown (chained-`++` result) follows LTStr for `==`/`!=`: route through the
-- runtime string/value discriminator rather than a pointer-identity icmp.
isStrLTy LTUnknown = True
isStrLTy _ = False

isLTUnknown : LTy -> Bool
isLTUnknown LTUnknown = True
isLTUnknown _ = False

-- String `==`/`!=`: pointer identity is WRONG for boxed string cells, so route
-- content equality through @mdk_string_eq (the same helper the pattern-match
-- literal path uses, returning a TAGGED Bool word 3/1).  `==` returns it directly;
-- `!=` flips 3<->1 via `xor … 2`.  Ordering ops (`<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=`) on strings
-- route through @mdk_string_compare_raw, which returns a PLAIN -1/0/1 i64; the
-- operator then becomes `icmp <intPred op> raw, 0` (e.g. `a < b` ⟺ `cmp < 0`),
-- reusing intPred's slt/sgt/sle/sge and boolFromI1.  (Self-compile C2 exercises
-- this: the native emitter's own sanitizeId/safeChar do String `<`/`<=` at emit
-- time; a pointer `icmp` there mis-mangled module names.)
emitStrCmp : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitStrCmp e op lv rv
  | op == "==" =
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_eq(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
    (r, LTBool)
  | op == "!=" =
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_eq(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
    let n = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{n} = xor i64 \{r}, 2"
    (n, LTBool)
  | otherwise =
    let cmp = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = call i64 @mdk_string_compare_raw(i64 \{lv}, i64 \{rv})"
    let c = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{c} = icmp \{intPred op} i64 \{cmp}, 0"
    boolFromI1 e c

-- an i1 (from icmp/fcmp) -> a Bool word (zext to i64, then tag as immediate int).
boolFromI1 : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
boolFromI1 e c =
  let z = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{z} = zext i1 \{c} to i64"
  (tagInt e z, LTBool)

emitUn : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitUn e env "-" x =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env x
  match ty
    LTFloat =>
      let d = unboxFloat e v
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = fneg double \{d}"
      (boxFloat e r, LTFloat)
    _ =>
      let i = untagInt e v
      let r = freshReg e
      let _ = emit e "  \{r} = sub i64 0, \{i}"
      (tagInt e r, LTInt)
emitUn e env "!" x =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env x
  let i = untagInt e v
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = xor i64 \{i}, 1"
  (tagInt e r, LTBool)
emitUn _ _ op _ = gapE ("unsupported unary op " ++ op)

-- `if`: lower via an alloca slot (memory, not phi) so a branch's own internal
-- control flow never has to be threaded into a phi's incoming-block list — the
-- simplest correct lowering for a spike (clang -O0 keeps the alloca; mem2reg
-- would promote it under -O).  Both arms share a type; report the then-arm's.
emitIf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitIf e env c t f =
  let (cv, _) = emitExpr e env c
  let ci = untagInt e cv
  let cb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cb} = icmp ne i64 \{ci}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let thenL = "then" ++ n
  let elseL = "else" ++ n
  let endL = "end" ++ n
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cb}, label %\{thenL}, label %\{elseL}"
  let _ = emit e (thenL ++ ":")
  let (tv, tty) = emitExpr e env t
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{tv}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (elseL ++ ":")
  let (fv, _) = emitExpr e env f
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{fv}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  (r, tty)

emitLet : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Bool -> Pat -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitLet e env False (PVar x) e1 e2 =
  if staticIsFloat env e1 then
    let d = emitFloatD e env e1
    emitExpr e ((x, (d, LTFloatU))::env) e2
  else
    let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env e1
    emitExpr e ((x, (v, ty))::env) e2
emitLet e env False PWild e1 e2 =
  let _ = emitExpr e env e1
  emitExpr e env e2
emitLet e env False (PTuple ps) e1 e2 =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env e1
  let env2 = bindPattern e env (PTuple ps) v e2
  emitExpr e env2 e2
-- a recursive (function-`let`) closure: `let f p… = … in e2` lowers to
-- CLet True (PVar f) (CLam …) (slice 5b).  The lambda's own name `f` is the
-- closure itself, so it is EXCLUDED from the capture set at the alloc site and
-- bound to `%clos` (the leading closure pointer) inside the lifted define — a
-- recursive self-call re-enters the same code with the same cell.  Non-recursive
-- function-lets take the same path harmlessly (`f` simply never appears free).
emitLet e env True (PVar f) (CLam pats body) e2 =
  let (w, ty) = emitRecLam e env f pats body
  emitExpr e ((f, (w, ty))::env) e2
-- P0-2c: general expression-level `let pat = e1 in e2` for a non-recursive,
-- non-(var/wild/tuple) pattern (PCon/PCons/PList/PAs) — the expression-position
-- analog of emitBlock's general CSLet arms.  Mirrors them: an irrefutable head
-- (single-ctor PCon) destructures unchecked (bindPattern); a refutable head
-- (sibling ctors, list) gets the trap-on-miss check (emitRefutableCsLet), matching
-- the interpreter's blockLet "E-LET-REFUTE" panic.  Previously this shape hit the
-- gapE catch-all (build-fail); it now lowers.  Placed after the PVar/PWild/PTuple
-- and recursive-function arms so those stay byte-identical.
emitLet e env False pat e1 e2 =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env e1
  let env2 = if refutableCsLet e pat then
    emitRefutableCsLet e env pat v
  else
    bindPattern e env pat v e2
  emitExpr e env2 e2
emitLet _ _ _ _ _ _ =
  gapE
    "unsupported let (slice 5b: only non-recursive let var/_/tuple = e, or function-let)"

-- a `let`/`where` group.  Bindings emit one at a time, left-to-right, threading
-- the growing env, so a later binding sees the earlier ones (and the body sees
-- all).  Two binding shapes:
--   • `CBind name [CClause [] rhs]` — a nullary VALUE binding (slice 1): emit the
--     rhs, bind the word, recurse.
--   • `CBind name clauses` with non-empty params (a LOCAL FUNCTION, possibly
--     multi-clause / self-recursive — the `go`/`step`/`g` workers core.mdk writes
--     with `where`): lambda-lift it into a (self-recursive) closure via
--     `emitGroupBind` and bind its closure word, then recurse.
-- A binding that forward/mutually references a LATER sibling can't be lifted
-- one-at-a-time — the referent's closure word isn't allocated when this binding's
-- captures are taken.  When that happens the maximal contiguous run of LOCAL
-- FUNCTION bindings starting here is KNOT-TIED together (`emitLetGroupKnot`): all
-- their closure cells are allocated up front (so every sibling word exists), every
-- sibling name is bound in the env, and each cell's capture fields are then
-- back-patched with the now-available sibling words (and any enclosing-scope
-- captures).  References to EARLIER siblings, self-references (bound to `%clos`),
-- arbitrary forward refs and true mutual recursion all emit correctly.  A value
-- binding interleaved INSIDE such a run (a mutual ref crossing a non-function
-- sibling) is the residue this scheme doesn't cover — it is rejected precisely
-- (`gapE`) rather than miscompiled.
emitLetGroup : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitLetGroup e env [] body = emitExpr e env body
emitLetGroup e env ((CBind name [CClause [] rhs])::rest) body =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env rhs
  emitLetGroup e ((name, (v, ty))::env) rest body
emitLetGroup e env ((CBind name clauses)::rest) body
  | refsLater name clauses (bindNames rest) =
    emitKnotRun e env (CBind name clauses :: rest) body
  | otherwise =
    let (w, ty) = emitGroupBind e env (CBind name clauses)
    emitLetGroup e ((name, (w, ty))::env) rest body

-- knot-tie the maximal leading run of function bindings of `binds`, then continue
-- the group with the remainder.  A forward/mutual ref crossing into a LATER value
-- binding (outside the run) is the uncovered residue — rejected precisely.
emitKnotRun : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitKnotRun e env binds body =
  let run = takeFnRun binds
  let after = dropS (lengthS run) binds
  let runNames = bindNames run
  if anyRefsOutsideRun run runNames (bindNames after) then gapE "unsupported let-group: a binding forward/mutually references a later value binding (knot-tying covers only mutual function bindings)"
  else
    let env2 = emitLetGroupKnot e env run runNames
    emitLetGroup e env2 after body

-- the maximal leading run of LOCAL FUNCTION bindings (non-empty params) — a nullary
-- VALUE binding (`CBind _ [CClause [] _]`) ends the run.
takeFnRun : List CBind -> List CBind
takeFnRun [] = []
takeFnRun ((CBind _ [CClause [] _])::_) = []
takeFnRun (b::rest) = b :: takeFnRun rest

dropS : Int -> List a -> List a
dropS _ [] = []
dropS n (x::xs) = if n <= 0 then x::xs else dropS (n - 1) xs

-- True if any binding in the run references a name bound LATER but OUTSIDE the run
-- (i.e. a later value binding) — the case knot-tying cannot tie.
anyRefsOutsideRun : List CBind -> List String -> List String -> Bool
anyRefsOutsideRun [] _ _ = False
anyRefsOutsideRun ((CBind name clauses)::rest) runNames laterOutside
  | anyInList (dedupS (clausesFreeVars name clauses)) laterOutside = True
  | otherwise = anyRefsOutsideRun rest runNames laterOutside
anyRefsOutsideRun (_::rest) runNames laterOutside =
  anyRefsOutsideRun rest runNames laterOutside

-- True when any clause of a local-function binding references a sibling bound
-- LATER in the same group (a forward or mutually-recursive reference whose
-- closure word is not yet available at this binding's capture site).  Self- and
-- param-bound names are excluded by `clausesFreeVars`.
refsLater : String -> List CClause -> List String -> Bool
refsLater name clauses laterNames =
  anyInList (dedupS (clausesFreeVars name clauses)) laterNames

-- the free variables across ALL clauses of a binding, with the self name and each
-- clause's own parameter variables treated as bound.
clausesFreeVars : String -> List CClause -> List String
clausesFreeVars _ [] = []
clausesFreeVars self ((CClause pats body)::rest) = freeVars (self :: patVarNames pats) body
  ++ clausesFreeVars self rest

anyInList : List String -> List String -> Bool
anyInList [] _ = False
anyInList (x::xs) ys = if contains x ys then True else anyInList xs ys

-- lambda-lift a LOCAL FUNCTION binding from a `let`/`where` group into a (possibly
-- self-recursive) closure.  Captures are the free variables across all clauses
-- present in the emit env, MINUS the binding's own name (it is the closure cell
-- itself, bound to `%clos` inside the lift — a self-call re-enters the same cell).
-- The lifted body reuses the E1a decision-tree path (`emitClauseTree`), so a
-- multi-clause worker or a patterned param lowers exactly as a top-level fn does.
emitGroupBind : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CBind -> (String, LTy)
emitGroupBind e env (CBind name clauses) =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let arity = clauseArity (clauseList clauses)
  let captured = keepInEnv env (removeStr name (dedupS (clausesFreeVars name clauses)))
  let captureWords = capWords e env captured
  let _ = emitGroupBindDefine e env lamName name arity clauses captured
  emitClosureAllocA e lamName arity captureWords

-- emit the lifted multi-clause `define` for a let-group local function into the
-- SIDE buffer (same swap discipline as emitPatLamDefine).  Captures load from
-- `%clos` fields 1.., the self name binds to `%clos` (recursive self-call
-- re-enters the same cell), and `emitClauseTree` lowers the clauses over the
-- `%argK` words (arity ≥1 here — nullary values take the fast path above).
emitGroupBindDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> Int -> List CClause -> List String -> Unit
emitGroupBindDefine e env lamName name arity clauses captured =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let savedFt = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(i64 %clos, \{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env captured 0
  let _ = emitClauseTree e (((name, ("%clos", LTClosure))::cenv) ++ implParamEnv arity 0) arity (clauseList clauses)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFt
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved
-- A lifted define is a FRESH function: its clause chain's last-clause
-- fallthrough must be @mdk_oob (non-exhaustive abort), NOT the enclosing
-- function's leftover next-clause label.  Without this the lifted `go`'s final
-- clause would `br label %clauseN` to a block defined in the PARENT function —
-- invalid LLVM once functions are concatenated in the multi-module output
-- (issue #53).  Same save+null+restore discipline emitDecision uses.

clauseList : List CClause -> List (List Pat, CExpr)
clauseList [] = []
clauseList ((CClause pats body)::rest) = (pats, body) :: clauseList rest

-- ── knot-tying: forward/mutual local function bindings ──────────────────────
-- Tie the knot for a RUN of mutually/forward-referencing local function bindings
-- (E5 residual / EMITTER-GAPS #10).  Two passes over the run:
--   1. ALLOC: for each fn binding, lambda-lift it (the lifted `@mdk_lamN` define is
--      independent of the cells — captures load from `%clos` fields by index), then
--      allocate its closure cell with the code pointer in field 0 and PLACEHOLDER
--      `0` words in the capture fields (`emitClosureAllocPatch` returns the raw cell
--      pointer for later stores).  Every sibling name is bound to its cell word.
--   2. PATCH: now that every sibling word exists, store each cell's REAL capture
--      words — looked up in the fully-knotted env (siblings + enclosing scope) — into
--      its capture fields.  This BY-VALUE back-patch ties arbitrary mutual recursion
--      and forward references; self-recursion still flows through `%clos`.
-- The capture-NAME list per binding is computed against a sibling env (so a sibling
-- ref is kept by `keepInEnv` and indexed correctly), and must MATCH the field order
-- the lifted define's `loadCaptures` reads — both walk the same `captured` list.
emitLetGroupKnot : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> List String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitLetGroupKnot e env run runNames =
  let sibEnv = sibPlaceholderEnv runNames
  let cellEnv = sibEnv ++ env
  let (cells, env2) = knotAlloc e env cellEnv run
  let _ = knotPatch e env2 cells
  env2
-- pass 2: back-patch each cell's capture fields with real words from env2.

-- a placeholder env entry per run name (word "0", LTClosure) for capture-name
-- computation only.
sibPlaceholderEnv : List String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
sibPlaceholderEnv [] = []
sibPlaceholderEnv (n::rest) = (n, ("0", LTClosure)) :: sibPlaceholderEnv rest

-- a knot cell records the binding name, its allocated cell POINTER (for stores),
-- and its ordered capture-name list (matching the lifted define's field layout).
data KnotCell = KnotCell String String (List String)

-- pass 1: for each fn binding, lift it and alloc its closure cell with placeholder
-- captures; thread the env binding each cell word.  `cellEnv` (siblings + outer)
-- is used to compute the capture NAME set so a sibling ref is kept and indexed.
knotAlloc : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> (List KnotCell, List (String, (String, LTy)))
knotAlloc _ env _ [] = ([], env)
knotAlloc e env cellEnv ((CBind name clauses)::rest) =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
