  let arity = clauseArity (clauseList clauses)
  let captured = keepInEnv cellEnv (removeStr name (dedupS (clausesFreeVars name clauses)))
  let _ = emitGroupBindDefine e cellEnv lamName name arity clauses captured
  let (cellPtr, cellWord) = emitClosureAllocPatch e lamName arity (lengthS captured)
  let (cells, env2) = knotAlloc e ((name, (cellWord, LTClosure))::env) cellEnv rest
  (KnotCell name cellPtr captured :: cells, env2)
knotAlloc e env cellEnv (_::rest) = knotAlloc e env cellEnv rest

-- pass 2: store each cell's real capture words (from the fully-bound env) into its
-- capture fields (offset 8*(i+1), field 0 holds the code pointer).
knotPatch : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List KnotCell -> Unit
knotPatch _ _ [] = ()
knotPatch e env ((KnotCell _ cellPtr captureNames)::rest) =
  let words = capWords e env captureNames
  let _ = storeFields e cellPtr words 1
  knotPatch e env rest

-- allocate a closure cell with the code pointer in field 0 and `nCap` PLACEHOLDER
-- zero capture words, returning BOTH the raw cell pointer (for later stores) and
-- the cell word (the closure value).  Mirrors emitClosureAlloc/emitCtorAlloc's
-- layout exactly — field 0 = code_ptr — so the cell is callable immediately
-- (`emitIndirect` loads field 0); only the capture fields await back-patch.
emitClosureAllocPatch : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> (String, String)
emitClosureAllocPatch e lamName callArity nCap =
  let codePtr = "ptrtoint (ptr @" ++ lamName ++ " to i64)"
  let fields = codePtr :: zeroWords nCap
  let nFields = lengthS fields
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (nFields + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString callArity}, ptr \{p}"
  let _ = storeFields e p fields 0
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (p, w)

zeroWords : Int -> List String
zeroWords n = if n <= 0 then [] else "0" :: zeroWords (n - 1)

emitBlock : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CStmt -> (String, LTy)
emitBlock e env [CSExpr ex] = emitExpr e env ex
emitBlock e env ((CSExpr ex)::rest) =
  let _ = emitExpr e env ex
  emitBlock e env rest
-- #314: a block-statement function-`let` (`let go i = … go …`) lowers to a
-- `CSLet` whose rhs is a `CLam` and whose body may call `go` recursively (the
-- self-ref is annotated AGlobal since the non-recursive let does not scope its own
-- name).  The generic PVar arm below would `emitExpr` the CLam with `go` NOT in the
-- env, so the lifted body's self-call hits the `gapE "unbound variable"` build
-- failure (`run` worked — eval binds the name before evaluating the body).  Route
-- it through `emitRecLam` (which binds `go -> %clos` inside the lifted define, so a
-- self-call re-enters the same closure) and bind `go` for the rest of the block —
-- the exact mirror of the expression-level `emitLet _ True (PVar f) (CLam …)` arm.
-- A non-recursive function-`let` takes this path harmlessly (`go` never appears
-- free, so it is simply not captured).
emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ (PVar x) (CLam pats body))::rest) =
  let (w, ty) = emitRecLam e env x pats body
  emitBlock e ((x, (w, ty))::env) rest
emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ (PVar x) ex)::rest) =
  if staticIsFloat env ex then
    let d = emitFloatD e env ex
    emitBlock e ((x, (d, LTFloatU))::env) rest
  else
    let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env ex
    emitBlock e ((x, (v, ty))::env) rest
emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ PWild ex)::rest) =
  let _ = emitExpr e env ex
  emitBlock e env rest
emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ (PTuple ps) ex)::rest) =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  let env2 = bindPattern e env (PTuple ps) v (CBlock rest)
  emitBlock e env2 rest
-- General block-let `let pat = e` for an ARBITRARY pattern (PCon, PCons, PList,
-- PAs, nested).  Two cases (P0-2c):
--  • IRREFUTABLE pattern (PVar/PWild/PAs-of-irrefutable, or a PCon of a
--    single-constructor type) — destructure with NO failure branch, exactly as
--    cevalBlock's cBlockLet does (core_ir_eval :269) and as the PTuple arm above.
--    Reuses bindPattern; keeps existing goldens byte-identical.
--  • REFUTABLE pattern (a PCon of a type with sibling ctors, or a list pattern) —
--    a plain `let (Some y) = e` with NO `else` can FAIL at runtime.  The
--    interpreter's blockLet runtimePanics "E-LET-REFUTE"; before this fix the
--    emitter blindly loaded field 0 of a mismatching cell → SIGSEGV.  Now emit a
--    head-ctor tag check that traps @mdk_let_refute on a miss (emitRefutableCsLet).
-- Placed after the specialized PVar/PWild/PTuple arms so those stay byte-identical.
emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ pat ex)::rest) =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  if refutableCsLet e pat then
    let env2 = emitRefutableCsLet e env pat v
    emitBlock e env2 rest
  else
    let env2 = bindPattern e env pat v (CBlock rest)
    emitBlock e env2 rest
-- last-position block-let `[let pat = e]`: mirror cevalBlock's cBlockLetLast
-- (core_ir_eval :265/:277) — evaluate `e` (for effect + to honour the binding),
-- destructure, and the block yields Unit.  Without this a block ending in a let
-- would recurse into `emitBlock e env2 []` and hit the "empty block" gap.  A
-- refutable pattern still gets the trap-on-miss check (P0-2c).
emitBlock e env [CSLet _ pat ex] =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  let _ = if refutableCsLet e pat then
    let _ = emitRefutableCsLet e env pat v
    env
  else bindPattern e env pat v (CLit (LInt 0))
  ("1", LTUnit)
-- `let mut x = e` reuses the irrefutable `CSLet _ (PVar x)` arm above (the mut
-- flag carries no extra emit work — a mutable local is just an SSA value the
-- block's tail rebinds).  A reassignment `x = e` (CSAssign, gap B1) mirrors the
-- tree-walker's `cevalBlock ((CSAssign x e)::rest)`: re-evaluate `e` and SHADOW
-- the `x` binding for the rest of the block.  Blocks are linear (no back-edge re-
-- enters the binding), so functional rebinding is observationally identical to a
-- real mutable slot — no Ref cell needed.
emitBlock e env ((CSAssign x ex)::rest) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env ex
  emitBlock e ((x, (v, ty))::env) rest
-- the tail statement of a block may be a bare assignment (`x = e`): evaluate for
-- effect, the block yields Unit.
emitBlock e env [CSAssign _ ex] =
  let _ = emitExpr e env ex
  ("1", LTUnit)
emitBlock _ _ [] = gapE "empty block"
emitBlock _ _ _ = gapE "unsupported block statement (slice 1)"

-- bind a list of field patterns to a parallel list of loaded field words.
bindFieldList : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> List String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindFieldList _ env [] _ = env
bindFieldList _ env _ [] = env
bindFieldList e env (p::ps) (v::vs) =
  let env2 = bindPattern e env p v (CLit (LInt 0))
  bindFieldList e env2 ps vs

-- the head ctor (name, arity, field patterns) of a refutable let-else pattern, if
-- it is a constructor-like pattern (named ctor, cons, or non-empty list literal).
letElseHead : Pat -> Option (String, Int, List Pat)
letElseHead (PCon c args) = Some (c, listLen args, args)
letElseHead (PCons h t) = Some ("Cons", 2, [h, t])
letElseHead (PList (p::ps)) = Some ("Cons", 2, [p, PList ps])
letElseHead _ = None

-- P0-2c: does a general block-`let` pattern (already NOT PVar/PWild/PTuple — those
-- have specialized irrefutable arms) need a runtime match-failure trap?  A PCon is
-- refutable iff its type has SIBLING constructors (a single-ctor `data` — e.g.
-- `Box` — always matches → keep the no-check fast path so goldens stay
-- byte-identical); a cons / non-empty list pattern can always miss (Nil).  Only the
-- TOP-LEVEL head is classified here — nested refutability inside fields shares
-- emitRefutableCsLet's single-level-check limitation.
refutableCsLet : Emit -> Pat -> Bool
refutableCsLet e (PAs _ p) = refutableCsLet e p
refutableCsLet e (PCon c _) = ctorHasSiblings e c
refutableCsLet _ (PCons _ _) = True
refutableCsLet _ (PList (_::_)) = True
refutableCsLet _ _ = False

-- True when constructor `c` shares its type with at least one other constructor
-- (so a pattern on `c` can fail to match).  Unknown ctors (absent from the
-- ctor→type table — e.g. the built-in list Cons/Nil) are conservatively refutable.
ctorHasSiblings : Emit -> String -> Bool
ctorHasSiblings e c = match lookupAssoc c (ctorTypeTable e)
  Some ty => listLen (ctorsOfType e ty) > 1
  None => True

-- P0-2c: emit a REFUTABLE block-`let` bind with a trap-on-miss.  Emits a
-- head-ctor tag check that branches to a noreturn @mdk_let_refute panic
-- (matching the interpreter's blockLet runtimePanic "E-LET-REFUTE").  On a tag
-- match, bind the fields (bindFieldList → bindPattern, a single-level extract)
-- and return the extended env; the
-- caller continues emission in the `ok` block.  A non-constructor-head refutable
-- pattern (letElseHead None — e.g. a bare Nil `let [] = …`) falls back to the old
-- unchecked bindPattern behaviour (no regression).
emitRefutableCsLet : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitRefutableCsLet e env pat sv = match letElseHead pat
  Some (c, a, fieldPats) =>
    let tag = loadDiscriminant e sv
    let cmp = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{tag}, \{intToString (cellTag e c)}"
    let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
    let ok = "letrefok" ++ n
    let no = "letrefno" ++ n
    let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{ok}, label %\{no}"
    let _ = emit e (no ++ ":")
    let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_let_refute()"
    let _ = emit e "  unreachable"
    let _ = emit e (ok ++ ":")
    let fields = loadFields e sv a 0
    bindFieldList e env fieldPats fields
  None => bindPattern e env pat sv (CLit (LInt 0))

-- ── function application (slices 2 + 4) ─────────────────────────────────────
-- Three call shapes, by the application spine's head:
--   • a known top-level FUNCTION (slice 2) — saturated direct `call @mdk_<name>`.
--   • a CONSTRUCTOR (slice 3) — heap-cell allocation.
--   • ANYTHING ELSE (slice 4) — a closure-valued head (a parameter, a let-bound
--     lambda, an immediately-applied `CLam`, or a returned closure): emit the head
--     to a closure word, load its code_ptr, and INDIRECT-call it passing the
--     closure word as the leading argument.  SATURATED only — the Core IR is
--     type-erased, so the call site cannot see the closure's arity; the spike
--     relies on controlled fixtures applying a closure with exactly its arity in
--     one spine.  Partial application (under-application → a residual closure) and
--     over-application are OUT (the real backend will carry arity in the cell).
emitApp : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitApp e env app =
  let (hd, args) = flattenApp app []
  match hd
    CVar fname _ => if fname == "Ref" then emitRefAlloc e env args
    else
      if fname == "setRef" then emitSetRef e env args
      else
        -- A LOCALLY-BOUND name (param / let-binding) SHADOWS any same-named ctor /
        -- known top-level fn / interface method / extern: it is a runtime closure
        -- value, so applying it is an INDIRECT call, never a ctor alloc / direct
        -- call / method dispatch.  Without this, a HOF param named like a method
        -- (`pickEq eq x y = eq x y`, or `cmp`/`compare`) fell to the isImplMethod
        -- arm → emitMethodArgDispatch arg-tag-dispatched over the method's impl
        -- groups (incl. PRIMITIVE Int/String/… that own no ctor cell tag) →
        -- emitTagMatch [] slice-7 build reject — while `medaka run` was always
        -- correct (eval treats the param as the local it is).  Mirrors emitVar's
        -- isLocal-first priority for the same name in VALUE position.
        if isLocal env fname then emitIndirect e env hd args
        else if isAnyExtern fname && hasArgs args then emitExternApplied e env fname args
        else
          if isCtor e fname then
            let argOps = emitArgs e env args
            emitCtorApp e fname argOps (ctorArity e fname)
          else
            let fname2 = canonFnName e fname
            if isKnownFn e fname2 && hasArgs args then
              let argOps = emitArgs e env args
              let res = emitKnownFnSat e ("mdk_" ++ fname2) argOps (fnArity e fname2) (fnRetTy e fname2)
              let _ = recordFloatClosureResult e fname2 (lengthS argOps) (fnArity e fname2) res
              res
            else
              if isImplMethod e fname && hasArgs args then
                let argOps = emitArgs e env args
                emitMethodArgDispatch e fname argOps
              else if isFallthroughVar fname then emitFallthrough e fname else emitIndirect e env hd args
    CMethod name route implRoutes methRoutes =>
      let argOps = emitArgs e env args
      emitMethod e env name route implRoutes methRoutes argOps
    CDict name routes =>
      let argOps = emitArgs e env args
      emitDictApp e env name routes argOps
    _ => emitIndirect e env hd args
-- slice 6: an applied method / constrained-fn occurrence.  The real arguments
-- emit first (left-to-right), then the dispatch resolves to a direct impl call
-- (RKey), a runtime impl switch (RDict/RDictFwd), or a dict-passing call (CDict).

-- a saturated INDIRECT call (slice 4): emit the head to a closure word, load its
-- code_ptr (cell field 0), and `call` the loaded pointer with the closure word as
-- the leading argument.  The result type is unknown to the type-erased call site;
-- default Int — EXCEPT (native backend) when the head is a self-returning function
-- PARAM of the impl currently being emitted (`andThen`'s `f` in `f x`): that
-- callback yields the container, so type the result as `tagToLTy <curImplTag>`.
emitIndirect : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitIndirect e env hd args =
  let (cw, _) = emitExpr e env hd
  let argOps = emitArgs e env args
  match lookupAssoc cw closureArityRef.value
    Some n =>
      if n > 0 && lengthS argOps > n then
        -- OVER-APPLICATION of a let-bound closure (`(g 7) 9`): saturate with the
        -- first `n` args, then apply the surplus to the returned (opaque) value.
        let r0 = emitIndirectCallWords e cw (takeS n argOps)
        (emitApplyAny e r0 (dropS n argOps), LTInt)
      else if n > 0 && lengthS argOps < n then
        -- UNDER-APPLICATION (`let h = g 7` for arity-2 g): build a residual
        -- partial-application closure over the inner closure value + supplied args.
        emitPapClosureIndirect e cw argOps n
      else
        (emitIndirectCallWords e cw argOps, indirectRetTy cw hd)
    -- OPAQUE head (arity unknown at compile time — e.g. a closure PARAM `f` in
    -- `map f`, or a closure pulled from a container).  The supplied count may not
    -- match the closure's runtime arity, so dispatch arity-aware (emitApplyAny):
    -- exact ⇒ inline call; partial ⇒ runtime PAP; over ⇒ saturate + reapply.
    None => (emitApplyAny e cw argOps, indirectRetTy cw hd)

-- the raw indirect call: load the closure's code_ptr (cell field 0) and `call` it
-- with the closure word as the leading argument followed by `argOps`.
emitIndirectCallWords : Emit -> String -> List String -> String
emitIndirectCallWords e cw argOps =
  let cp = loadField e cw 0
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = inttoptr i64 \{cp} to ptr"
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 \{fp}(\{argDecls (cw::argOps)})"
  r

-- the static result type of an indirect call: container when the head is a
-- self-returning function param of the current impl, else LTInt (unchanged).
indirectResultTy : CExpr -> LTy
indirectResultTy (CVar f _) =
  if isCurSelfFnParam f then
    tagToLTy curImplTagRef.value
  else
    LTInt
indirectResultTy _ = LTInt

-- the result type of an indirect call: a recorded float-returning closure (keyed by
-- the closure word `cw`) types its result LTFloat; otherwise the historical
-- indirectResultTy (self-fn-param container, else LTInt).
indirectRetTy : String -> CExpr -> LTy
indirectRetTy cw hd = match lookupAssoc cw closureRetTyRef.value
  Some t => t
  None => indirectResultTy hd

hasArgs : List CExpr -> Bool
hasArgs [] = False
hasArgs _ = True

-- emit each argument expression (left-to-right); collect the i64 operand words.
emitArgs : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> List String
emitArgs _ _ [] = []
emitArgs e env (a::rest) =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env a
  v :: emitArgs e env rest

-- render an operand list as a comma-separated i64 argument list.
argDecls : List String -> String
argDecls [] = ""
argDecls [x] = "i64 " ++ x
argDecls (x::rest) = "i64 \{x}, \{argDecls rest}"

-- ── typeclass dispatch (slice 6) ────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Core IR carries the typechecker's resolved dispatch in two structural nodes
-- (core_ir.mdk): `CMethod name route implRoutes methRoutes` (a method occurrence)
-- and `CDict name routes` (a `=>`-constrained-function occurrence).  Each Route is
-- RNone | RKey String (List Route) | RDict String | RDictFwd String (ast.mdk).
--
-- The KEY TRACTABILITY PROPERTY (the reason the bootstrap is in reach): an RKey
-- route is STATICALLY resolved — the typechecker already pinned the concrete type
-- head, so the impl is known at COMPILE time and the site lowers to a DIRECT call
-- to that impl's lifted `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>`, with NO runtime tag inspection.
-- The self-host compiler dispatches return-position via RKey (compiler/README.md),
-- so RKey alone covers the bootstrap.  RDict/RDictFwd read a named dict PARAMETER
-- at run time, so they lower to an if-chain switching on the dict's head tag over
-- the method's impls (corpus completeness — the dict-passing fixtures).  This
-- mirrors core_ir_eval's `methodAtNarrow`/`applyDicts`/`narrowMethod` one-to-one,
-- resolving the same routes in emitted control flow instead of walking VMulti
-- values.  RNone (D3a: a type-variable arg-position site the front end left
-- unstamped) falls back to runtime arg-tag dispatch; nested-requires dicts are out
-- of scope.
--
-- SPIKE-REP NOTES (slice 6; the (j)/(k)/(l) entries in STAGE2-DESIGN §2.4a):
--   (j) DICTS (GAP 1, Option A — BOXED witness) — a dict witness is a POINTER to a
--       heap cell `[ i64 head_tag | i64 reqdict_0 | … ]` (via @mdk_alloc, the same
--       boxed-cell shape ADT ctors use): field 0 = `hashName(impl-head-tag)` (the
--       djb2 used for the `icmp eq` dispatch test), fields 1..n = the RECURSIVELY-
--       built witness words for the impl's per-instance `requires` dicts.  RNone ⇒ 0
--       (no cell).  The rep is UNIFORM: a one-level dict (`RKey k []`) is a 1-word
--       cell `[ hashName k ]`; a two-level dict (`RKey k [RKey k2 …]`) nests another
--       cell pointer in field 1.  Consumption: the RKey direct-call path forwards the
--       boxed pointer as the impl fn's leading dict param; the RDict dispatch chain
--       (emitDispatchChain) loads the head tag from field 0 for the `icmp` and
--       prepends fields 1..n as the matched impl's leading element-dict args.
--       (Previously the witness was a flat i64 that dropped nested reqs and panicked
--       on a non-empty set — the GAP-1 SIGSEGV `medaka build` hit on `[[Int]]`.)
--   (k) IMPL FUNCTIONS — each tagged impl method lowers to a top-level
--       `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>(params…)` (lambda-lift convention of emitFn).  A
--       nullary return-position impl (`def`/`zero`/`empty`) is a zero-arg function
--       the use site CALLs for its value; a parameterized impl takes its params.
--   (l) DISPATCH — RKey ⇒ a direct call (impl chosen at compile time).  RDict/
--       RDictFwd ⇒ a CALL to the method's SHARED dispatcher
--       `@mdk_disp_<method>_<nMeth>_<nArgs>(%dict, %mw…, %arg…)`.  ⚠️ THE IF-CHAIN IS
--       NOT AT THE SITE — it lives in that dispatcher (issue #118: it used to be
--       inlined at every site, which made emitted IR grow as `Σ over sites of
--       (#impls of that site's method)`; 107 sites shared just 9 distinct chains on a
--       9-line fixture).  INSIDE the dispatcher the chain is unchanged: compare the
--       dict witness word to each impl's hashName tag; an exhausted chain is
--       `unreachable` (the typechecker proves the dict names a real impl).
--       Debugging a dispatch miscompile? The arm you want is in `@mdk_disp_*`.
--
--       Why one dispatcher can serve every site of a method: the chain body is a pure
--       function of the method NAME, the method-level dict count, and the arg count —
--       all three are baked into the symbol — plus program-global tables
--       (`implsOf`/`defaultFor`/`ifaceTags`, all reading `e.implEntries`, which is
--       written ONCE at Emit construction and never mutated).  So the impl set cannot
--       grow between the site that mints a dispatcher and any later site.

-- a method occurrence.  `argOps` are the already-emitted real-argument words (empty
-- for a bare nullary method like `def`).  methRoutes/implRoutes apply method-/
-- instance-level constraint dicts as leading args (D3b-1): on an RKey call into a
-- parametric `requires` impl, each materializes a one-word dict witness prepended
-- ahead of the real args, matching dict_pass's leading `$dict_<method>_<slot>`
-- params.  Both empty for an unconstrained impl ⇒ the plain direct call.
-- GAP 1: the route's OWN nested `reqs` are now representable (boxed dict cells,
-- Option A) — the former `ensureNoReqs reqs` guard is gone.  This site's leading
-- dicts come from the call-site routes (methRoutes ++ implRoutes); each such
-- route's nested reqs are materialized recursively inside dictWordOfRoute.
emitMethod : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> Route -> List Route -> List Route -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitMethod e env name route implRoutes methRoutes argOps
  -- UNDER-APPLICATION of a runtime-dict-routed method (E22).  A partially-applied
  -- method whose dispatch reads a forwarded `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param (RDict/
  -- RDictFwd) — e.g. `eq y` (arity 2, one arg) passed to a HOF inside a
  -- `Eq a =>` fn — must become a residual PAP closure, NOT a truncated dispatch.
  -- emitMethodDispatch assumes a SATURATED arg list, so calling it with too few
  -- args runs the impl on garbage and the (scalar) result is then INDIRECTLY
  -- called as a closure → SIGSEGV.  The RKey/RNone paths already PAP correctly
  -- (emitImplCallSat/emitMethodArgDispatch → emitPapClosure); this is the missing
  -- RDict/RDictFwd analogue.  The PAP captures the route's dict word(s) + the
  -- supplied args; its forwarder rebinds them and re-emits the saturated dispatch.
  | dictRoutedUnderApplied route name argOps =
      emitMethodPap e env name route implRoutes methRoutes argOps (methodArityOf name)
emitMethod e env name (RKey tag _) implRoutes methRoutes argOps = match implFor e name tag
  Some entry =>
    let dictWords = dictWordsOf e env (methRoutes ++ implRoutes)
    -- [tag] is the stamped route tag — the bare head when the head is unique, the
    -- canonical key on a C7 collision.  For tagToLTy/returns-self use the matched
    -- entry's REAL head tag; the lifted-fn symbol uses implFnSymOf (head when
    -- unique, sanitized key on collision) so it matches emitGroup's name.
    let headTag = implEntryTag entry
    let resTy = if methodReturnsSelf (implEntryIface entry) name then tagToLTy headTag else LTInt
    emitImplCallSat
      e
      (implFnName (implFnSymOf (implEntriesOf e) name entry) name)
      (dictWords ++ argOps)
      (methodArityOf name + lengthS dictWords)
      resTy
  None => emitDefaultRKey e env name tag methRoutes implRoutes argOps
emitMethod e env name (RDict d) implRoutes methRoutes argOps =
  emitMethodDispatch e env name d methRoutes argOps
emitMethod e env name (RDictFwd d) implRoutes methRoutes argOps =
  emitMethodDispatch e env name d methRoutes argOps
emitMethod e env name RNone implRoutes methRoutes argOps =
  emitMethodArgDispatch e name argOps
-- S-1: a CONSTRAINED shadowing standalone was given leading dict PARAMS by
-- dictPassDecl, so its call must supply the matching dict WORDS.  Delegate to
-- emitDictApp — the SAME tested helper the ordinary CDict path uses (it prepends the
-- dict words and, via defArityOf, builds a residual PAP when genuinely
-- under-applied).  Without this the direct emitKnownFnSat call below supplies one
-- word too few, emitKnownFnSat correctly reads that as under-application, and the
-- program silently prints a PAP heap pointer instead of the answer (the S-1 bug).
-- `dicts == []` (every unconstrained standalone, incl. all 5 of the compiler's own
-- definer shadows) keeps the byte-identical pre-S1 direct call ⇒ the self-compile
-- fixpoint cannot move.
emitMethod e env name (RLocal sym dicts) implRoutes methRoutes argOps =
  let target = if sym == "" then name else sym
  if isEmpty dicts then
    emitKnownFnSat e ("mdk_" ++ target) argOps (fnArity e target) (fnRetTy e target)
  else
    emitDictApp e env target dicts argOps
-- native backend: a method whose RESULT mentions the interface/self
-- type variable (map/ap/andThen) returns the CONTAINER; type its
-- RKey-dispatched result as the impl's own type (tagToLTy tag) so a
-- downstream `++`/`::` on it dispatches list/string append.  Otherwise
-- (scalar/element result) keep the LTInt default.

-- C5 (mirror lib/eval.ml RLocal): NOT a method dispatch — the receiver has no
-- impl of this interface but an explicitly-imported/local standalone shadows the
-- method name.  Emit a DIRECT call to that standalone top-level fn (`mdk_<target>`),
-- exactly as emitApp's isKnownFn branch would for a plain call — no dispatch, no
-- dicts.  P0-18: `target` is the carried MANGLED symbol `<mid>__name` when non-empty
-- (the EMИТ path, where the def was renamed by mangleUnits and the EMethodAt carries
-- the BARE dispatch name), else the bare `name` (un-mangled run path).  The compiler's
-- OWN 5 definer shadows (map/hash_map `toList`/`isEmpty`, parser `orElse`) now reach
-- THIS arm with their mangled symbol; the emitted `emitKnownFnSat` call is byte-
-- identical to the pre-P0-18 direct `@mdk_<mid>__name` bare-EVar call (fixpoint gate).

-- Gap #50 (value position) — a bare method occurrence `CMethod name route ..` used
-- as a FIRST-CLASS VALUE (passed to a HOF, e.g. `applyOp max 3 7`): there are no
-- applied args, so the saturated dispatch chain that `emitMethod .. []` would emit
-- runs the impl/default with ZERO operands (garbage), and the result is then handed
-- to the HOF as a closure word and INDIRECTLY called → crash.  Instead eta-wrap the
-- method into a real closure value: a lifted `@mdk_methval_<name>_N(%clos, %arg…)`
-- whose body re-emits the SATURATED dispatch (emitMethod with the full %argK
-- operands) under the method's own route.  For an RDict/RDictFwd route the closure
-- CAPTURES the dict word and rebinds it from `%clos` field 1 in the body, so the
-- runtime impl switch still reads the caller's dict.  A nullary method (arity 0,
-- e.g. `def`) is a plain value — keep the immediate `emitMethod .. []`.
-- S1-RESIDUAL-A (FIXED 2026-07-16, issue #410; SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md §6): a VALUE-position
-- standalone SHADOW (`map size [1,2,3]`) used to miscompile here — the built binary
-- SEGFAULTed (wasm: `illegal cast`) while `check`/`run` were both green.  The lift built
-- the closure at `methodArityOf name` — the interface METHOD's arity — for a body that
-- calls the STANDALONE, whose value arity need not match.  With the two arities EQUAL the
-- lie is invisible, which is why every pre-#410 probe (and the whole d4/d10 corpus) missed
-- it.  When they differ the closure advertises N args to the HOF but its body forwards
-- them to an M-arg function: N>M makes `emitKnownFnSat` take the `emitOverApp` path
-- (:3941), which calls the target, gets a SCALAR back, and hands that Int to
-- `emitApplyAny`, which reads an arity out of the "cell header" of an integer ⇒ SEGFAULT.
-- Fix: the arity is now ROUTE-derived (`methValArity`), not name-derived — the RLocal arm
-- below already knew the truth via `fnArity e target`.
emitMethodValue : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> Route -> List Route -> List Route -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodValue e env name route implRoutes methRoutes =
  let arity = methValArity e name route
  if arity <= 0 then emitMethod e env name route implRoutes methRoutes []
  else
    let capNames = methValDictNames route
    let capWords = capDictWords env capNames
    let lamName = "mdk_methval_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
    let _ = emitMethodValDefine e env lamName name route implRoutes methRoutes arity capNames
    emitClosureAllocA e lamName arity capWords

-- the VALUE arity the lifted method-value closure must advertise to a HOF — i.e. the
-- number of value args the body it wraps actually consumes, which is a property of the
-- ROUTE, not of the method name (#410).
--
-- RLocal is the definer-shadow route: the body (`emitMethod`'s RLocal arm) forwards to
-- the STANDALONE `mdk_<target>`, so the closure must expose the STANDALONE's value arity.
-- `fnArity` is the right source precisely because it is what that arm's own
-- `emitKnownFnSat`/`emitDictApp` measure against — deriving the closure arity from
-- anything else re-opens the same mismatch from the other side.
--
-- ⚠️ the `- listLen dicts` is load-bearing and NOT cosmetic: `inferSigs` runs over the
-- LOWERED (post-dict-pass) clauses, so `fnArity`'s `ptys` is DICT-INCLUSIVE (see the
-- `trmcTryFn` note ~:7081).  A CONSTRAINED shadow's `fnArity` therefore counts its leading
-- dict params, but the closure exposes VALUE args only — the dict words are CAPTURED (see
-- `methValDictNames`) and re-prepended inside the body by `emitDictApp`.  Without the
-- subtraction a constrained shadow would advertise nDicts too many and segfault exactly
-- like the unconstrained case it fixes.  `dicts == []` ⇒ subtract 0 ⇒ plain `fnArity`.
--
-- A non-RLocal route (RDict/RDictFwd/RKey/RNone) really is dispatching the METHOD, so it
-- keeps `methodArityOf` — byte-identical to pre-#410 codegen, so the fixpoint cannot move.
-- `<= 0` means the target has no known signature (uninstalled sig table / unknown name);
-- fall back to the old name-derived arity rather than emit a 0-arity closure.
methValArity : Emit -> String -> Route -> Int
methValArity e name (RLocal sym dicts) =
  let target = if sym == "" then name else sym
  let a = fnArity e target - listLen dicts
  if a <= 0 then methodArityOf name else a
methValArity _ name _ = methodArityOf name

-- the dict-witness param name(s) a route reads at runtime (the names whose words the
-- value-closure must capture so the lifted body can rebind them).  Only RDict/
-- RDictFwd name a dict; RKey/RNone/RLocal capture nothing.  (Distinct from the
-- `freeVars` helper `routeDictNames`, which scans a route LIST for free dict vars.)
methValDictNames : Route -> List String
methValDictNames (RDict d) = [d]
methValDictNames (RDictFwd d) = [d]
-- S-1: a VALUE-position constrained shadow (`map size xs`) lifts to a closure, and its
-- RLocal route's dict slots may include an RDict — the enclosing constrained fn's own
-- dict param.  That word must be CAPTURED or the lifted body reads an out-of-scope
-- name.  Empty dicts ⇒ [] ⇒ byte-identical to the pre-S1 catch-all.
methValDictNames (RLocal _ dicts) = routeDictNames [] dicts
methValDictNames _ = []

-- the operand words for each captured dict name, looked up in the current emit env.
capDictWords : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List String
capDictWords _ [] = []
capDictWords env (d::rest) = dictOperand env d :: capDictWords env rest

-- emit the lifted method-value forwarder.  Captures (the dict words) load from
-- `%clos` fields 1.. and rebind the route's dict name(s); the body re-emits the
-- SATURATED method dispatch over the `%argK` value operands.
emitMethodValDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> Route -> List Route -> List Route -> Int -> List String -> Unit
emitMethodValDefine e env lamName name route implRoutes methRoutes arity capNames =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls arity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env capNames 0
  let synArgs = etaArgOps arity 0
  let (r, _) = emitMethod e cenv name route implRoutes methRoutes synArgs
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- CDict in VALUE position (EMITTER-GAPS #18, value facet): a `=>`-constrained
-- NAMED function used as a FIRST-CLASS value — passed unapplied to a higher-order
-- combinator (`filter myParity xs`, `map inc xs`, a user HOF `apply isEv 4`).  The
-- dict_pass-elaborated define `@mdk_<name>` takes `listLen routes` leading dict
-- PARAMS + the value args; emitDictApp handles the CALL (head) position, but a bare
-- occurrence used as a value reached emitExpr's catch-all and panicked
-- "unsupported Core IR node CDict".  Mirror emitMethodValue (the CMethod-as-value
-- path): build the route dict words at THIS site, capture them in a closure cell,
-- and emit a forwarder that reloads them and prepends them ahead of the `%argK`
-- value words when the HOF invokes the closure — yielding a plain closure VALUE with
-- the ABI filter/map/apply expect (field 0 = code_ptr, fields 1.. = captured dicts).
-- The forwarder body is a DIRECT `@mdk_<name>` call (vs emitMethodValue's dispatch).
-- A nullary value arity (the constrained fn takes no value args) degenerates to the
-- plain saturated dict call.
emitDictValue : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Route -> (String, LTy)
emitDictValue e env name routes =
  if isKnownFn e name then
    let valArity = fnArity e name - listLen routes
    if valArity <= 0 then emitDictApp e env name routes []
    else
      let dictWords = dictWordsOf e env routes
      let lamName = "mdk_dictval_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
      let _ = emitDictValDefine e lamName name (lengthS dictWords) valArity
      emitClosureAllocA e lamName valArity dictWords
  else gapE ("CDict head '" ++ name ++ "' is not a known top-level function (slice 6)")

-- emit the lifted constrained-fn value forwarder.  Closure cell layout: field 0 =
-- code_ptr, fields 1..nDicts = captured dict words.  The forwarder takes `valArity`
-- fresh `%argK` params, reloads the dict words from %clos fields 1..nDicts, and
-- calls `@mdk_<name>(dicts.., %arg0.., %arg(valArity-1))` — the same leading-dict-then-
-- value-arg order emitDictApp / dict_pass use at every direct call site.
emitDictValDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
emitDictValDefine e lamName name nDicts valArity =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls valArity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let dictOps = loadPapCaptures e nDicts 0
  let synArgs = dictOps ++ etaArgOps valArity 0
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_\{name}(\{argDecls synArgs})"
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- E22: a runtime-dict-routed (RDict/RDictFwd) method occurrence is UNDER-APPLIED —
-- arity known, fewer real args than that, and the route reads a forwarded dict
-- param.  (RKey/RNone routes PAP through their own emitImplCallSat/arg-dispatch
-- paths, so they are excluded here and keep their existing lowering.)
dictRoutedUnderApplied : Route -> String -> List String -> Bool
dictRoutedUnderApplied (RDict _) name argOps =
  lengthS argOps < methodArityOf name
dictRoutedUnderApplied (RDictFwd _) name argOps =
  lengthS argOps < methodArityOf name
dictRoutedUnderApplied _ _ _ = False

-- E22: build the residual PAP closure for an under-applied dict-routed method.
-- Captures the route's dict word(s) (so the runtime impl switch still reads the
-- caller's forwarded dict after the closure escapes) PLUS the already-supplied
-- args; the forwarder rebinds both and re-emits the SATURATED `emitMethod` once
-- the remaining args arrive.  Mirrors emitMethodValue (Gap #50, the zero-supplied
-- case) generalised to N supplied args.  Recorded arity is the RESIDUAL value
-- arity so a later over-application of the PAP also splits correctly.
emitMethodPap : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> Route -> List Route -> List Route -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodPap e env name route implRoutes methRoutes argOps arity =
  let capNames = dedupS (routeDictNames [] (route :: implRoutes ++ methRoutes))
  let dictWords = capDictWords env capNames
  let remaining = arity - lengthS argOps
  let lamName = "mdk_methpap_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitMethodPapDefine e env lamName name route implRoutes methRoutes arity capNames (lengthS argOps)
  emitClosureAllocA e lamName remaining (dictWords ++ argOps)

-- emit the under-applied-method PAP forwarder.  Closure cell layout:
-- field 0 = code_ptr, fields 1..K = captured dict words (K = listLen capNames),
-- fields K+1..K+S = the S supplied arg words.  The forwarder takes `remaining`
-- fresh `%argK` params; its body rebinds the dict names from fields 1..K, reloads
-- the supplied args from fields K+1.., then re-emits the saturated dispatch over
-- (supplied ++ fresh) under the method's own route.
emitMethodPapDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> Route -> List Route -> List Route -> Int -> List String -> Int -> Unit
emitMethodPapDefine e env lamName name route implRoutes methRoutes arity capNames supplied =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let remaining = arity - supplied
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls remaining}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env capNames 0
  let capCount = listLen capNames
  let suppliedOps = loadPapCaptures e (capCount + supplied) capCount
  let synArgs = suppliedOps ++ etaArgOps remaining 0
  let (r, _) = emitMethod e cenv name route implRoutes methRoutes synArgs
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- E19: an RKey route into a method with NO tagged impl for `tag` — the type's
-- `impl` did not override an interface DEFAULT.  Emit the default body as a
-- lifted `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` define (eta-expanded to full arity, inner
-- same-interface calls re-stamped to `RKey tag` so a partially-applied inner
-- method like `filterMap` lowers to a direct call), cache it, and call it
-- directly with the call-site args.  No default ⇒ the original missing-impl gap.
-- G7: a default-method body may call methods of a DIFFERENT interface whose dict
-- is threaded per-call by the method's own `=>` constraint (foldMap's `empty`, a
-- Monoid method routed by the RESULT type, not the Foldable container).  Those
-- method-level dicts (methRoutes, resolved from the CALL-SITE constraint monos) are
-- passed as LEADING dict-word args to the shared `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` define,
-- whose cross-interface methods the emitter restamped to `RDict $dict_<method>_<slot>`
-- (the dict-passing param) and dispatch the right impl at run time.  The define is
-- still shared across monoids: only the runtime dict word differs per call, so one
-- `@mdk_default_foldMap_List` serves both a List- and a String-monoid fold.
emitDefaultRKey : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> List Route -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitDefaultRKey e env name tag methRoutes implRoutes argOps = match defaultFor e name
  Some entry =>
    let fname = defaultFnName tag name
    let _ = ensureDefaultEmitted e fname tag name entry
    let dictWords = dictWordsOf e env (methRoutes ++ implRoutes)
    let resTy = if methodReturnsSelf (methodIfaceOf name) name then
      tagToLTy tag
    else
      LTInt
    emitImplCall e fname (dictWords ++ argOps) resTy
  None => gapE "no impl of method '\{name}' for type '\{tag}' (slice 6)"
-- Native Gap C (parametric default-method dict-synthesis, 2026-06-12): a
-- relational OPERATOR (`<`→`lt`/…) on a parametric Ord head (tuple/`List a`/
-- `Box a`) carries the impl's element `requires` dicts on implRoutes (stamped by
-- typecheck's stampBinopRoute, keyed on the inner `compare`).  Thread them — with
-- the method-level dicts — as LEADING words to `@mdk_default_<op>_<tag>`, whose
-- emitDefaultDefine eta-prepends the matching dict params and forwards them into
-- the inner `@mdk_impl_<tag>_compare(dict.., a, b)` call.  Concrete Ord has empty
-- implRoutes → the plain `(a, b)` call, byte-identical to before.

-- a direct call to a statically-resolved impl fn (RKey).  Nullary (argOps == [])
-- ⇒ `call @mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>()`, yielding the impl's value.  `resTy` is the
-- statically-inferred result type: LTInt for a scalar/element-returning method,
-- or the container (tagToLTy tag) for a returns-self method (map/ap/andThen) so
-- a downstream `++`/`::` types its operand correctly (native backend).
emitImplCall : Emit -> String -> List String -> LTy -> (String, LTy)
emitImplCall e fname argOps resTy =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @\{fname}(\{argDecls argOps})"
  (r, resTy)

-- a call to a statically-resolved impl fn (RKey / single arg-tag group) that
-- may be UNDER-APPLIED.  A point-free prelude binding (`length = fold f 0`)
-- lowers to the impl method applied to FEWER args than the impl fn's full arity:
-- a saturated `emitImplCall` would emit a call missing its trailing parameter(s),
-- which on the native backend reads a garbage register for the missing arg and
-- crashes.  When `lengthS argOps < arity`, build a PARTIAL-APPLICATION closure
-- capturing the supplied words; the residual closure completes the call when the
-- remaining args arrive at an indirect call site.  Saturated (`==`) ⇒ the plain
-- direct call, unchanged.  (Over-application can't arise at an impl call head —
-- the method's declared arity is its full arity.)
emitImplCallSat : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> LTy -> (String, LTy)
emitImplCallSat e fname argOps arity resTy
  | lengthS argOps < arity = emitPapClosure e fname argOps arity
  | otherwise = emitImplCall e fname argOps resTy

-- a call to a known top-level function (`@<sym>`, sym already mdk-prefixed) that
-- may be UNDER-APPLIED.  A partial application of a named function passed to a
-- higher-order combinator (`map (desugarDottedField e) fields` — 1 of 2 args)
-- lowers to a residual closure capturing the supplied args, not a truncated
-- direct call (which would read garbage for the missing trailing param).
-- Saturated (`==`) ⇒ the plain direct call, unchanged.  fnArity returns 0 for a
-- fn with no recorded signature; then `lengthS argOps >= 0` is always saturated,
-- preserving the old direct-call behaviour for such (signature-less) callees.
emitKnownFnSat : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> LTy -> (String, LTy)
emitKnownFnSat e sym argOps arity resTy
  | arity > 0 && lengthS argOps < arity = emitPapClosure e sym argOps arity
  | arity > 0 && lengthS argOps > arity = emitOverApp e sym argOps arity resTy
  | otherwise = emitImplCall e sym argOps resTy

-- OVER-APPLICATION: a known fn of arity N applied to >N args (`makeAdder 10 5` —
-- `makeAdder` returns a closure that consumes the extra arg).  Saturate the direct
-- call with the FIRST N args, then apply the residual VALUE (a closure) to each
-- remaining arg through an indirect call (the same load-code_ptr / call-(closure,
-- arg) shape as emitIndirect).  Without this the trailing args are silently dropped
-- into @sym (which ignores them) and the returned closure is never applied —
-- garbage result / crash.
emitOverApp : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> LTy -> (String, LTy)
emitOverApp e sym argOps arity resTy =
  let satArgs = takeS arity argOps
  let extra = dropS arity argOps
  let (r0, _) = emitImplCall e sym satArgs resTy
  (emitApplyAny e r0 extra, LTInt)

-- ARITY-AWARE application of an OPAQUE closure word `cw` to `argOps` (count N): the
-- compile-time arity is unknown (a closure param, a container element, an over-app
-- residual), so the supplied count may not equal the closure's runtime arity.  Read
-- the arity from the cell header (field 0); if it equals N, inline the direct call
-- (the historical fast path); otherwise route through the runtime @mdk_apply, which
-- builds a PAP for a partial application, flattens a PAP, and splits an
-- over-application.  A PAP carries a -1 header so it never matches N (>=1) and always
-- takes the runtime path.  Uses an alloca result slot (like emitIf) so no phi
-- predecessor-label bookkeeping is needed.
emitApplyAny : Emit -> String -> List String -> String
emitApplyAny e cw [] = cw
emitApplyAny e cw argOps =
  let n = lengthS argOps
  let hdr = loadHeader e cw
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{hdr}, \{intToString n}"
  let m = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let fastL = "apfast" ++ m
  let slowL = "apslow" ++ m
  let endL = "apend" ++ m
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{fastL}, label %\{slowL}"
  let _ = emit e (fastL ++ ":")
  let rf = emitIndirectCallWords e cw argOps
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{rf}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (slowL ++ ":")
  let rs = emitApplyRuntime e cw argOps
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{rs}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  r

-- the runtime-generic path: materialize `argOps` into an [N x i64] alloca and call
-- @mdk_apply(cw, N, ptr).  Handles partial / over application + PAP flatten.
emitApplyRuntime : Emit -> String -> List String -> String
emitApplyRuntime e cw argOps =
  let n = lengthS argOps
  let arr = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{arr} = alloca i64, i64 \{intToString n}"
  let _ = storeArgv e arr argOps 0
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @__mdk_apply(i64 \{cw}, i64 \{intToString n}, ptr \{arr})"
  r

-- store each arg word into the argv alloca at index `i` (getelementptr i64 stride).
storeArgv : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> Unit
storeArgv _ _ [] _ = ()
storeArgv e arr (a::rest) i =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = getelementptr i64, ptr \{arr}, i64 \{intToString i}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{a}, ptr \{p}"
  storeArgv e arr rest (i + 1)

-- load the header word (field 0, offset 0 — the closure arity) of a boxed cell.
loadHeader : Emit -> String -> String
loadHeader e ptrWord =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{ptrWord} to ptr"
  let f = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{f} = load i64, ptr \{p}"
  f

-- build a partial-application closure over `@fname` with `argOps` already-supplied
-- arg words captured.  Emits a forwarder `@mdk_pap_..(%clos, %arg0..%arg(rem-1))`
-- that loads the captured words from the closure cell (fields 1..k, code_ptr is
-- field 0) and tail-calls `@fname(captured.., remaining..)`; allocates the closure
-- capturing `argOps`.  The residual is a closure VALUE (LTClosure) consumed at an
-- indirect call site — the same cell shape emitClosureAlloc / emitIndirect use.
emitPapClosure : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitPapClosure e fname argOps arity =
  let supplied = lengthS argOps
  let remaining = arity - supplied
  let papName = "mdk_pap_\{fname}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitPapDefine e papName fname supplied remaining
  emitClosureAlloc e papName remaining argOps

-- emit the PAP forwarder define into the side buffer (same swap discipline as
-- emitEtaDefine).  `supplied` captured words are loaded from %clos fields 1..k;
-- `remaining` fresh %argK params follow; the body calls @fname with the captured
-- words first, then the fresh params, in original order, and returns the result.
emitPapDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
emitPapDefine e papName fname supplied remaining =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{papName}(\{etaParamDecls remaining}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let capOps = loadPapCaptures e supplied 0
  let allArgs = capOps ++ argNames remaining 0
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @\{fname}(\{argDecls allArgs})"
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- load the `k` captured argument words from %clos fields 1..k (code_ptr is 0).
loadPapCaptures : Emit -> Int -> Int -> List String
loadPapCaptures e k i
  | i >= k = []
  | otherwise = loadField e "%clos" (i + 1) :: loadPapCaptures e k (i + 1)

-- UNDER-APPLICATION of an INDIRECT closure VALUE `cw` of arity `arity`, given
-- `argOps` supplied (1..arity-1).  Unlike emitPapClosure (which closes over a known
-- @fname symbol), here the callee is a runtime closure word, so the cell captures
-- BOTH the inner closure word and the supplied args: field 1 = inner closure, fields
-- 2.. = supplied args.  The forwarder loads them and does an INDIRECT call of the
-- inner closure with (supplied.. ++ remaining..) — now saturated.  Recorded with its
-- residual arity so a further over-application of the PAP also splits correctly.
emitPapClosureIndirect : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitPapClosureIndirect e cw argOps arity =
  let supplied = lengthS argOps
  let remaining = arity - supplied
  let papName = "mdk_pap_ind_" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let _ = emitPapDefineIndirect e papName supplied remaining
  emitClosureAllocA e papName remaining (cw::argOps)

-- emit the indirect-PAP forwarder define into the side buffer.  field 1 = the
-- captured inner closure word; fields 2.. = `supplied` captured arg words; then
-- `remaining` fresh %argK params.  Body: load the inner closure's code_ptr (its
-- field 0) and `call` it with (innerClosure, supplied.., remaining..).
emitPapDefineIndirect : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
emitPapDefineIndirect e papName supplied remaining =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{papName}(\{etaParamDecls remaining}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let innerCw = loadField e "%clos" 1
  let capOps = loadPapCaptures e (supplied + 1) 1
  let allArgs = capOps ++ argNames remaining 0
  let cp = loadField e innerCw 0
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = inttoptr i64 \{cp} to ptr"
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 \{fp}(\{argDecls (innerCw::allArgs)})"
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- the interface name a resolved impl entry belongs to (for the returns-self
-- lookup); "" for a default entry (it carries no tag/iface — never RKey-reached).
implEntryIface : CImplEntry -> String
implEntryIface (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged _ _ iface _ _ _)) = iface
implEntryIface (CImplEntry _ _ (CImplDefault _ _)) = ""

-- a runtime method dispatch (RDict/RDictFwd): the enclosing function's dict
-- PARAMETER `d` (a BOXED dict-witness pointer, GAP 1 Option A) selects the impl at
-- run time.  Load the head tag from the cell's field 0, then emit an if-chain over
-- the method's tagged impls — compare the head tag to each impl's hashName tag, on
-- a match load the matched impl's nested per-instance dicts from the cell's fields
-- 1..n, PREPEND them to argOps (the impl fn's leading element-dict params), call
-- that impl's lifted fn into a result slot and branch to the end; an exhausted
-- chain is `unreachable`.
emitMethodDispatch : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodDispatch e env name d methRoutes argOps = match soleImplDirectSite e name
  Some ent =>
    -- WS-1b: the method has exactly ONE impl with no `requires` element dicts, so a
    -- well-typed call always reaches that impl regardless of the runtime dict (mirrors
    -- eval's `narrowMethod (VMulti vs) ""` → sole-impl pick, and the arg-position
    -- single-impl-group DIRECT-call optimization).  Emitting a direct call here avoids
    -- LOADING the dict head tag — which, under an AMBIGUOUS return-position superclass
    -- constraint (`build : Sub a => Int -> a; build n = mk n`, where `mk`'s `Sup` dict
    -- stays ungrounded → the caller passes a NULL dict), would dereference null → the
    -- run≠build SIGTRAP.  Concrete-grounded callers (a real dict) are unaffected: the
    -- direct call is the same impl the tag-dispatch chain would have selected.
    let symTag = implFnSymOf (implEntriesOf e) name ent
    emitImplCall e (implFnName symTag name) argOps LTInt
  None => emitMethodDispatchChain e env name d methRoutes argOps

-- WS-1b: Some impl iff [name] has exactly one tagged impl AND that impl takes no
-- leading `requires` element-dict params (reqCount 0) — the case where a direct call
-- needs nothing from the runtime dict.  A multi-impl method, or a sole impl WITH
-- requires (whose element dicts live in the dict cell), still goes through the
-- tag-switching chain (which reads the dict).
soleImplDirect : Emit -> String -> Option CImplEntry
soleImplDirect e name = match implsOf e name
  [ent] => if implReqCount e name ent <= 0 then Some ent else None
  _ => None

-- The SITE-level form of the same test.  In prelude-object mode a dict-routed
-- site must NOT bake an impl decision into its enclosing define, because that
-- define may be a PRELUDE body destined for the shared `prelude.o` — and the sole
-- impl of a method in the prelude ALONE is not the sole impl in a program that
-- adds one (`impl Bimappable MyPair`).  Baking it there would be a silent
-- miscompile: `prelude.o` would direct-call the wrong impl forever.
--
-- So under `MEDAKA_PRELUDE_OBJ` every site routes through the shared dispatcher,
-- and the sole-impl shortcut moves INSIDE the dispatcher body (emitDispatcherDefine),
-- which is re-emitted per program from the WHOLE-program impl table.  The
-- null-dict property WS-1b exists for is preserved exactly: that body still makes
-- a direct call and still never loads `%dict` (see emitDispatcherDefine).
--
-- Mode 0 (every ordinary `medaka build`) is unchanged, so the default path's IR
-- stays byte-for-byte what it was.
soleImplDirectSite : Emit -> String -> Option CImplEntry
soleImplDirectSite e name =
  if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfWhole then
    soleImplDirect e name
  else
    None

-- ── issue #118 step 3: the shared prelude object (MEDAKA_PRELUDE_OBJ) ────────
-- 88% of a small program's emitted IR is the PRELUDE (270 of 281 defines, 9,935
-- of 11,841 lines on `test/wasm/fixtures/adt_enum_nullary.mdk` — a NINE-line
-- program), and `clang -O2` re-optimises all of it on every single build.  The fix
-- mirrors MEDAKA_RT_OBJ one level up: compile the prelude ONCE to `prelude.o`, and
-- per program emit only the program's own code plus `declare`s for what it uses.
--
-- This was IMPOSSIBLE until the dispatch chains were outlined (step 2, above): a
-- program's own `impl` decls used to be INLINED into prelude function bodies, so no
-- one object could serve two programs.  With the chains outlined into shared
-- `@mdk_disp_*` dispatchers, prelude bodies are program-independent — verified by
-- emitting two programs differing only by an `impl Eq Color` and comparing all 249
-- shared prelude defines: ZERO structural differences (only the module-global
-- gensym counter shifts the `@mdk_lamN`/`@mdk_etac_*_N` names).
--
-- THREE MODES, selected by MEDAKA_EMIT_HALF (read by the emit driver):
--
--   0 WHOLE   (unset — every ordinary `medaka build`).  Untouched.  Every branch
--             this section adds is keyed on `emitHalfRef.value != 0`, so the
--             default path's IR is byte-for-byte what it was.
--
--   1 PRELUDE (`medaka build --emit-prelude-obj <path>`).  Emit core.mdk ALONE, as
--             a library: no `@mdk_program_main`, `@mdk_g_*` globals declared
--             `external` (the program half defines and initialises them), and the
--             two per-program symbol families — `@mdk_disp_*` and
--             `@mdk_default_*` — DECLARED, not defined.
--
--   2 PROGRAM (a build with MEDAKA_PRELUDE_OBJ set).  Emit the whole program, but
--             replace every PRELUDE define's BODY with a `declare` (emitAsDeclares).
--             The prelude's bodies are still WALKED — that is what registers the
--             dispatchers and defaults `prelude.o` declares — their text is just
--             dropped.
--
-- ⚠️ DCE MUST BE OFF in modes 1 and 2 (the emit driver does this).  Source DCE is
-- per-program, so an on-DCE prelude.o would ship only what the STUB reached; and an
-- on-DCE program half would walk fewer prelude bodies than prelude.o was built from
-- and so fail to define a dispatcher it declares.  The LINKER's `--gc-sections`
-- (already in the link line, issue #120) recovers the size, from the real
-- relocation graph rather than source reachability.
emitHalfWhole : Int
emitHalfWhole = 0

emitHalfPrelude : Int
emitHalfPrelude = 1

emitHalfProgram : Int
emitHalfProgram = 2

emitHalfRef : Ref Int
emitHalfRef = Ref emitHalfWhole

export installEmitHalf : Int -> Unit
installEmitHalf n = setRef emitHalfRef n

-- The gensym-id base for the PROGRAM half.  `freshId` (the module-global counter)
-- names every LIFTED define — `@mdk_lamN`, `@mdk_eta*_N`, `@mdk_pap_*_N` — and
-- those have external linkage, so if the two halves drew from the same range they
-- would collide on a symbol name (loudly, at link).  The halves are emitted by two
-- INDEPENDENT emitter runs, so give them disjoint ranges: the prelude half counts
-- up from 0, the program half from this base.  `preludeIdSpaceGuard` turns the
-- "0 .. base is enough for the prelude" assumption into a build-time check rather
-- than a hope — emitting core.mdk whole draws ~500 ids, four orders of magnitude
-- below the base.
programHalfIdBase : Int
programHalfIdBase = 1000000

preludeIdSpaceGuard : Emit -> Unit
preludeIdSpaceGuard e =
  if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude && e.counter.value >= programHalfIdBase then
    panic "prelude-object emit exhausted its gensym id space (\{intToString e.counter.value} >= \{intToString programHalfIdBase}) — raise programHalfIdBase in llvm_emit.mdk"
  else
    ()

-- The PRELUDE-owned symbols, installed by the emit driver in PROGRAM-half mode
-- (mode 2 only — in mode 1 every decl in the program IS the prelude).  Derived by
-- lowering core.mdk's decls on their own, so the classification comes from where a
-- decl was DEFINED, not from guessing at its emitted name.
preludeBindsRef : Ref (List String)
preludeBindsRef = Ref []

preludeImplsRef : Ref (List (String, String))
preludeImplsRef = Ref []

export installPreludeSyms : List String -> List (String, String) -> Unit
installPreludeSyms binds impls =
  let _ = setRef preludeBindsRef binds
  setRef preludeImplsRef impls

-- Is this top-level bind / impl group one whose define lives in `prelude.o`?
-- Only ever True in mode 2.
hiddenBind : String -> Bool
hiddenBind name = emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfProgram
  && contains name preludeBindsRef.value

hiddenImplGroup : ImplGroup -> Bool
hiddenImplGroup (ImplGroup method tag _ _ _ _) = emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfProgram
  && hasImplKey method tag preludeImplsRef.value

hasImplKey : String -> String -> List (String, String) -> Bool
hasImplKey _ _ [] = False
hasImplKey m t ((m2, t2)::rest) = m == m2 && t == t2 || hasImplKey m t rest

-- Run `act` (an ordinary emitFn/emitGroup), then throw its BODY TEXT away and keep
-- only a `declare` per define it produced.
--
-- Running it and discarding the output is the whole point, not waste: emitting a
-- prelude body is what REGISTERS the `@mdk_disp_*` dispatchers and
-- `@mdk_default_*` defines that `prelude.o` only declares, so the program half
-- defines exactly the set the prelude half demanded.  The lifted lambdas/strings/
-- dict-cells it also produces land (dead) in `e.lams` and are dropped by
-- `--gc-sections` — they cost IR lines, not correctness, and their names cannot
-- collide with prelude.o's (disjoint gensym range, see programHalfIdBase).
--
-- Deriving the `declare` from the `define` line the emitter ACTUALLY produced —
-- rather than re-deriving the signature — is what makes the two halves' ABI
-- impossible to drift apart.  It also handles, for free, the cases where a bind
-- emits no define (a TMC dispatch-group MEMBER) or more than one (a float `__fw`
-- worker): whatever was emitted is exactly what gets declared.
emitAsDeclares : Emit -> (Unit -> Unit) -> Unit
emitAsDeclares e act =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let _ = act ()
  let produced = reverseL (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) saved
  emitDeclaresFor e produced

emitDeclaresFor : Emit -> List String -> Unit
emitDeclaresFor _ [] = ()
emitDeclaresFor e (l::rest) =
  let _ = match declareOfDefine l
    Some d => emit e d
    None => ()
  emitDeclaresFor e rest

-- `define i64 @f(i64 %a, i64 %b) {`  →  `declare i64 @f(i64 %a, i64 %b)`.
-- Every define the emitter writes has exactly this shape (verified: all 281 define
-- lines of the reference module end in `) {`); a line that does not is not a define
-- header and yields None.
declareOfDefine : String -> Option String
declareOfDefine l =
  let n = stringLength l
  if n > 9 && stringSlice 0 7 l == "define " && stringSlice (n - 2) n l == " {" then
    Some ("declare " ++ stringSlice 7 (n - 2) l)
  else
    None

-- ── issue #118: the dispatch chain is OUTLINED, one per method ──────────────
-- A runtime-dict dispatch site used to INLINE the whole impl chain — one `icmp` +
-- branch + call arm per impl of the method, at EVERY site.  That made the emitted IR
-- grow as `Σ over sites of (#impls of that site's method)`, and it is the dominant
-- term in a small program's IR: on `test/wasm/fixtures/adt_enum_nullary.mdk` (9 lines
-- of source) there were 107 dispatch sites but only NINE distinct chains between
-- them — 2,123 emitted arms, ~80% of the module's 32,895 lines, to express 9 chains.
--
-- So the chain now lives ONCE, in a shared `@mdk_disp_<method>_<nMeth>_<nArgs>`
-- define, and the site is a single `call`.  The chain BODY is unchanged — the same
-- head-tag `icmp` arms in the same order, the same default-synthesis arms, the same
-- `unreachable` tail — so every dispatch decision is byte-for-byte the one the inline
-- chain made; only its PLACEMENT moved.
--
-- The dispatcher's parameters are exactly what the inline chain read out of the
-- enclosing define, in that order:
--   %dict   — the dict-witness word (the enclosing fn's dict PARAM; `loadTag`/
--             `loadField` read the cell's head tag and per-instance `requires`).
--   %mwK    — the METHOD-LEVEL constraint dicts.  These are AMBIENT at the CALL site
--             (a `traverse : Thenable m =>` whose `m` is not the dispatch receiver),
--             so they are still resolved from `env` HERE and passed IN — they are not,
--             and never were, in the dict cell.
--   %argK   — the value args.  Always exactly `methodArityOf name`: under-application
--             is diverted to a PAP by `dictRoutedUnderApplied` before we get here, and
--             over-application cannot arise at an impl head (see emitImplCallSat).
--
-- Both counts are baked into the dispatcher's NAME, so a site that somehow disagreed
-- on either would get its own dispatcher rather than a silently mis-arity'd call.
emitMethodDispatchChain : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodDispatchChain e env name d methRoutes argOps =
  let dictPtr = dictOperand env d
  let methWords = dictWordsOf e env methRoutes
  let nmw = lengthS methWords
  let nargs = lengthS argOps
  let fname = dispFnName name nmw nargs
  let _ = ensureDispatcherEmitted e fname name nmw nargs
  emitImplCall e fname (dictPtr :: methWords ++ argOps) LTInt

-- the shared dispatcher's symbol.  Method names are plain identifiers here (an
-- operator is a `CBinPrim`, never a method name — same assumption `implFnName`
-- already makes), so the concatenation is a legal LLVM symbol.
dispFnName : String -> Int -> Int -> String
dispFnName name nmw nargs =
  "mdk_disp_\{name}_\{intToString nmw}_\{intToString nargs}"

-- the dispatchers emitted so far, by symbol.  Module-level Ref (mirroring
-- `dictConstCacheRef`), reset per program by emitProgram/emitProgramGaps.
dispCacheRef : Ref (List String)
dispCacheRef = Ref []

ensureDispatcherEmitted : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
ensureDispatcherEmitted e fname name nmw nargs =
  if contains fname dispCacheRef.value then
    ()
  else
    -- REGISTER BEFORE EMITTING.  A default-synthesis arm lifts `@mdk_default_<m>_<tag>`
    -- on demand (ensureDefaultEmitted), and that body can itself reach a dict-routed
    -- site for the SAME method — which would re-enter here and recurse forever if the
    -- symbol were only recorded after the body was emitted.
    let _ = setRef dispCacheRef (fname :: dispCacheRef.value)
    -- PRELUDE HALF: the dispatcher body enumerates every impl of the method in THIS
    -- program, so it is inherently per-program and must NOT be baked into the shared
    -- `prelude.o` — declare it and let the program half define it.  The program half
    -- re-walks the prelude's own bodies (emitAsDeclares), so it reaches this exact
    -- (name, nmw, nargs) site and DEFINES every dispatcher the prelude declares: the
    -- demand set is derived, never encoded, and a mismatch is a LOUD undefined symbol.
    if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude then
      emitGlobal e "declare i64 @\{fname}(\{dispParamDecls nmw nargs})"
    else
      emitDispatcherDefine e fname name nmw nargs

-- emit the shared dispatcher define.  Same side-buffer swap every other lifted define
-- uses (emitMethodValDefine/emitDictValDefine): redirect `buf`, emit, splice into
-- `lams`, restore.  `beginDefine` resets the per-define `%tN`/label counter, so the
-- dispatcher's text depends only on the method — not on where the first site of it
-- happened to appear (issue #118 step 1).
emitDispatcherDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
emitDispatcherDefine e fname name nmw nargs =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{fname}(\{dispParamDecls nmw nargs}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let (r, _) = emitDispatcherBody e name nmw nargs
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- The dispatcher's body.  Ordinarily (mode 0) this is only ever reached when the
-- method has ≥2 impls — soleImplDirect already diverted the sole-impl case to a
-- direct call at the SITE — so the `Some` arm below is dead and the emitted IR is
-- byte-identical to what it was.
--
-- Under MEDAKA_PRELUDE_OBJ the site no longer takes that shortcut
-- (soleImplDirectSite), so the shortcut lives HERE instead, where it is computed
-- from the whole-program impl table each build.  It reproduces WS-1b exactly: a
-- direct call to the sole impl that never touches `%dict` — which is what keeps an
-- ambiguous return-position superclass constraint's NULL dict from being
-- dereferenced.  `%mw`/`%dict` are simply left unread, as at the direct call site.
emitDispatcherBody : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitDispatcherBody e name nmw nargs = match soleImplDirect e name
  Some ent =>
    let symTag = implFnSymOf (implEntriesOf e) name ent
    emitImplCall e (implFnName symTag name) (dispWordNames "%arg" nargs 0) LTInt
  None => emitDispatchBody e name "%dict" (dispWordNames "%mw" nmw 0) (dispWordNames "%arg" nargs 0)

dispParamDecls : Int -> Int -> String
dispParamDecls nmw nargs =
  argDecls
    ("%dict" :: dispWordNames "%mw" nmw 0 ++ dispWordNames "%arg" nargs 0)

dispWordNames : String -> Int -> Int -> List String
dispWordNames pre n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = pre ++ intToString i :: dispWordNames pre n (i + 1)

-- the dispatch chain itself, verbatim from the old inline site — now the BODY of the
-- shared dispatcher.  [dictPtr] is the dict-witness word, [methWords] the ambient
-- method-level constraint dicts (`emitDispatchChain` prepends them ahead of the
-- cell-sourced per-instance `requires`, matching the lifted fn's leading dict-param
-- order: dict_pass binds methRoutes ++ implRoutes), [argOps] the saturated value args.
emitDispatchBody : Emit -> String -> String -> List String -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitDispatchBody e name dictPtr methWords argOps =
  let headTag = loadTag e dictPtr
  let impls = implsOf e name
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "dispend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = match impls
    [] => match defaultFor e name
      Some entry => emitDefaultDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag (ifaceTags e (methodIfaceOf name)) name entry argOps slot endL
      None => emitDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag impls name methWords argOps slot endL
    _ => match defaultFor e name
      -- MIXED default+impls: append default-synthesis arms for interface tags with no
      -- explicit impl (derived-Ord ADTs inheriting `max`/`min` from the default).
      Some entry =>
        let covered = implEntryTags impls
        let uncovered = tagsMinus (ifaceTags e (methodIfaceOf name)) covered
        match uncovered
          [] => emitDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag impls name methWords argOps slot endL
          _ => emitDispatchChainDefaulted e dictPtr headTag impls uncovered name entry methWords argOps slot endL
      None => emitDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag impls name methWords argOps slot endL
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  (r, LTInt)
-- GAP 2 phase-b: a method with NO tagged impl but a CImplDefault (a pure
-- interface default like `max`/`min` over `Ord`) reaches an RDict site
-- generically (`maximum [..]` keeps `Ord a`).  An empty impl chain emits only
-- `unreachable`, so the dispatched body blanks.  Synthesize a per-tag default
-- (the E19 machinery) for every tag the interface's dict can carry: the chain
-- switches the dict's head tag and calls `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>`, whose
-- inner same-interface `compare` is restamped to that concrete `RKey tag` (so
-- it stays a direct call, never the unsound arg-tag site of emitDefaultArgTag).

-- GAP 2 phase-b: the distinct type-head tags an `iface`'s dict can carry — every
-- tagged impl entry whose interface equals `iface`, deduped.  These are exactly
-- the runtime element tags a generic `iface a` dict-word may hold, so the default
-- dispatch chain enumerates one default-synthesis arm per tag.
ifaceTags : Emit -> String -> List String
ifaceTags e iface = dedupS (ifaceTagsGo iface (implEntriesOf e))

ifaceTagsGo : String -> List CImplEntry -> List String
ifaceTagsGo _ [] = []
ifaceTagsGo iface ((CImplEntry _ _ (CImplTagged tag _ ifc _ _ _))::rest)
  | ifc == iface = tag :: ifaceTagsGo iface rest
  | otherwise = ifaceTagsGo iface rest
ifaceTagsGo iface (_::rest) = ifaceTagsGo iface rest

-- GAP 2 phase-b: emit the default-synthesis dispatch chain.  For each `tag` the
-- interface's dict may carry, test the dict head tag and on a match synthesize +
-- call `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` (ensureDefaultEmitted restamps its inner
-- same-interface calls to `RKey tag`, keeping `compare` a concrete direct call —
-- the unsoundness trap is avoided precisely because the tag is concrete here).
-- An exhausted chain is `unreachable` (the dict names a real interface impl).
emitDefaultDispatchChain : Emit -> String -> String -> List String -> String -> CImplEntry -> List String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitDefaultDispatchChain e _ _ [] _ _ _ _ _ = emit e "  unreachable"
emitDefaultDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag (tag::rest) name entry argOps slot endL =
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{headTag}, \{intToString (hashName tag)}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "ddispyes" ++ n
  let next = "ddispnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let fname = defaultFnName tag name
  let _ = ensureDefaultEmitted e fname tag name entry
  let reqCount = innerDefaultReqCount e name tag
  let reqDicts = loadReqDicts e dictPtr reqCount 0
  let (rv, _) = emitImplCall e fname (reqDicts ++ argOps) LTInt
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{rv}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
  emitDefaultDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag rest name entry argOps slot endL
-- Native Gap C (parametric default-method dict-synthesis, 2026-06-12): when this
-- default reduces to a PARAMETRIC inner `compare` (tag = tuple/`List`/`Box`), the
-- lifted `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` now takes leading element-dict params (see
-- emitDefaultDefine).  They live in THIS runtime dict cell (the `Ord <tag>` witness),
-- fields 0..nReq-1, exactly like emitDispatchChain loads an impl's own requires —
-- load + PREPEND them.  Concrete tag ⇒ nReq=0 ⇒ unchanged (`maximum [Int]`).

-- the count of leading element-dict params a matched impl's lifted fn expects: its
-- full clause arity (number of patterns) minus the method's interface arity.  This
-- equals the number of per-instance `requires` dicts stored in the boxed dict cell
-- (fields 1..count), so the chain loads exactly that many and prepends them.
implReqCount : Emit -> String -> CImplEntry -> Int
implReqCount e name ent = listLen (implPats ent) - methodArityOf name

-- load `n` nested dict words from the boxed dict cell `dictPtr`.  The head tag is
-- the cell header (offset 0); the nested req dicts are fields 0..n-1 (loadField i ⇒
-- offset 8*(i+1)), stored by emitDictCell via storeFields at the same field indices.
loadReqDicts : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> List String
loadReqDicts e dictPtr n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = loadField e dictPtr i :: loadReqDicts e dictPtr n (i + 1)

-- emit ONE tagged-impl dispatch arm: test the dict head tag against this impl's route
-- key and, on a match, call the impl fn (prepending method-level + per-instance
-- `requires` dicts) and jump to `endL`; otherwise fall through to the `next` label the
-- caller continues after.
--
-- The matched impl's lifted fn expects `implReqCount` leading dict params, ordered
-- methRoutes ++ implRoutes.  The first `lengthS methWords` are METHOD-LEVEL
-- constraint dicts — already materialized from the caller's ambient args (env);
-- the remaining are this impl's per-instance `requires` dicts, loaded from the
-- runtime dispatch-dict cell's fields 0..k-1.  Sourcing the method-level ones from
-- the cell (the old behavior) was an OUT-OF-BOUNDS load whenever the dispatch dict
-- was a bare tag (no requires) → a garbage dict → SIGSEGV (e.g. `traverse` whose
-- `Thenable m =>` is over a tyvar that is not the `Traversable` receiver).
emitDispatchArm : Emit -> String -> String -> CImplEntry -> String -> List String -> List String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitDispatchArm e dictPtr headTag ent name methWords argOps slot endL =
  let routeKey = implEntryRouteKey (implEntriesOf e) ent
  let symTag = implFnSymOf (implEntriesOf e) name ent
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{headTag}, \{intToString (hashName routeKey)}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "dispyes" ++ n
  let next = "dispnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let cellCount = implReqCount e name ent - lengthS methWords
  let cellDicts = loadReqDicts e dictPtr cellCount 0
  let (rv, _) = emitImplCall e (implFnName symTag name) (methWords ++ cellDicts ++ argOps) LTInt
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{rv}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  emit e (next ++ ":")

emitDispatchChain : Emit -> String -> String -> List CImplEntry -> String -> List String -> List String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitDispatchChain e _ _ [] _ _ _ _ _ = emit e "  unreachable"
emitDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag (ent::rest) name methWords argOps slot endL =
  let _ = emitDispatchArm e dictPtr headTag ent name methWords argOps slot endL
  emitDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag rest name methWords argOps slot endL

-- GAP 2 phase-b (MIXED case): a method that HAS tagged impls but whose interface ALSO
-- owns a default AND can carry element tags with no explicit impl — the derived-Ord
-- ADT case.  `max`/`min` have real impls at primitive types (Int/Float/…), yet a
-- `deriving (Ord)` ADT (e.g. `Color`) materializes only `compare` and inherits
-- `max`/`min` from the interface DEFAULT.  A generic `Ord a` dict flowing through
-- `maximum`/`minimum`'s fold reaches this chain; the plain chain enumerates ONLY the
-- tagged impls, so the ADT tag matches no arm and falls to `unreachable`/nonexhaustive.
-- Emit the tagged-impl arms, then — instead of an immediate `unreachable` — append one
-- default-synthesis arm per `uncovered` iface tag (the same `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>`
-- machinery as the pure-default path, its inner `compare` restamped to `RKey tag`).
emitDispatchChainDefaulted : Emit -> String -> String -> List CImplEntry -> List String -> String -> CImplEntry -> List String -> List String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitDispatchChainDefaulted e dictPtr headTag [] uncovered name entry _ argOps slot endL = emitDefaultDispatchChain e dictPtr headTag uncovered name entry argOps slot endL
emitDispatchChainDefaulted e dictPtr headTag (ent::rest) uncovered name entry methWords argOps slot endL =
  let _ = emitDispatchArm e dictPtr headTag ent name methWords argOps slot endL
  emitDispatchChainDefaulted
    e
    dictPtr
    headTag
    rest
    uncovered
    name
    entry
    methWords
    argOps
    slot
    endL

-- the type tags a method's tagged impls already cover (the `t` field of each entry),
-- for diffing against the interface's full tag set to find the derived-default tags.
implEntryTags : List CImplEntry -> List String
implEntryTags [] = []
implEntryTags (ent::rest) = implEntryTag ent :: implEntryTags rest

-- interface tags NOT covered by any tagged impl — the derived-default tags that need a
-- `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` synthesis arm appended to the tagged-impl dispatch chain.
tagsMinus : List String -> List String -> List String
tagsMinus [] _ = []
tagsMinus (t::rest) covered
  | contains t covered = tagsMinus rest covered
  | otherwise = t :: tagsMinus rest covered
-- GAP 1: prepend the matched impl's nested element-dicts (cell fields 1..reqCount)
-- ahead of the real args, matching its leading dict-param order (dict_pass's
