-- methRoutes ++ implRoutes; here the impl's own requires, stored in cell order).

-- ── arg-position (arg-tag) dispatch at a call site (slice 7) ─────────────────
-- A bare CVar naming an impl method, applied to args.  The impl is chosen by the
-- discriminating argument's runtime constructor TYPE (mirrors eval's
-- filterByTag → runtimeTypeTag).  Two shapes:
--   • ONE impl group (the method has impls at a single type) — no runtime test is
--     needed: a well-typed call always reaches that impl, so emit a DIRECT call
--     (covers single-impl + the multi-clause-body fixture; the multi-clause
--     fall-through lives INSIDE the impl fn's decision tree, note (n)/(o)).
--   • SEVERAL impl groups (impls at distinct types) — load the discriminating
--     argument's cell tag and emit an if-chain: for each group, test whether the
--     loaded ctor tag is one the group's TYPE owns (OR over its constructors'
--     hashName, via ctorsOfType), and on a match call that group's impl fn.  An
--     exhausted chain is `unreachable` (the typechecker proved a real impl).
--     The discriminating arg may be an IMMEDIATE nullary ctor, so the tag is read
--     via `loadDiscriminant` (low-bit branch), not a bare `loadTag` (note (p)).
emitMethodArgDispatch : Emit -> String -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodArgDispatch e method argOps =
  let groups = implGroupsForMethod e method
  match groups
    [] => emitDefaultArgTag e method argOps
    [g] => emitImplCallSat e (implFnName (groupSymTag g) method) argOps (methodArityOf method) LTInt
    _ => emitArgTagDispatch e method groups argOps

-- E19: an arg-tag site for a method with NO tagged impl groups — an interface
-- DEFAULT reached on the RNone fallback (`max`/`min`, whose `maximum`/`minimum`
-- callers stay generic `Ord a`).  EMITTING the default body here is UNSOUND for
-- the prelude's Ord defaults: `max`/`min` reduce to `compare m x`, and `compare`'s
-- only impls are at PRIMITIVE types (Int/Float/String/Char/Bool/Ordering) which
-- carry NO runtime constructor cell tag — so the inner `compare` arg-tag dispatch
-- hits `emitTagMatch`'s "owns no constructors" gap (one event per primitive Ord
-- impl × caller).  Emitting the default would therefore REPLACE 1 honest gap with
-- ~6 deeper ones.  A correct max/min needs RKey-concrete `compare` (a monomorphic
-- caller) or primitive-typed arg-tag dispatch — both out of this slice's scope.
-- So a no-impl-groups default is left as the original honest gap; only a default
-- whose inner same-interface calls reach a CONCRETE tag is emittable (the RKey
-- path, emitDefaultRKey — `filter`@List).
emitDefaultArgTag : Emit -> String -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitDefaultArgTag e method argOps =
  gapE
    ("arg-tag dispatch for method '" ++ method ++ "' has no impl groups (slice 7: unresolved RNone fallback)")

emitArgTagDispatch : Emit -> String -> List ImplGroup -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitArgTagDispatch e method groups argOps
  | headPos (groupPositionsOf (headGroup groups)) >= lengthS argOps = gapE ("arg-tag dispatch for method '" ++ method ++ "': discriminating arg position not supplied (slice 7: under-applied / unapplied method)")
  | otherwise = emitArgTagDispatchGo e method groups argOps (headPos (groupPositionsOf (headGroup groups)))

emitArgTagDispatchGo : Emit -> String -> List ImplGroup -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitArgTagDispatchGo e method groups argOps discrimPos =
  let discrimWord = nthStr argOps discrimPos
  let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e discrimWord
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "argdispend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = emitArgDispatchChain e method tagReg groups argOps slot endL
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  (r, LTInt)

emitArgDispatchChain : Emit -> String -> String -> List ImplGroup -> List String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitArgDispatchChain e _ _ [] _ _ _ = emit e "  unreachable"
emitArgDispatchChain e method tagReg (g::rest) argOps slot endL =
  let tag = groupTag g
  let cond = emitTagMatch e tagReg (ctorsOfType e tag)
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "argyes" ++ n
  let next = "argnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cond}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let (rv, _) = emitImplCall e (implFnName (groupSymTag g) method) argOps LTInt
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{rv}, ptr \{slot}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
  emitArgDispatchChain e method tagReg rest argOps slot endL

-- an i1 that is true when the loaded ctor tag equals ANY of a type's constructor
-- tags (OR-chain of icmp eq against each hashName).
emitTagMatch : Emit -> String -> List String -> String
emitTagMatch _ _ [] =
  gapStr
    "arg-tag dispatch on impl type that owns no constructors (slice 7: primitive receiver carries no cell tag)"
emitTagMatch e tagReg [c] =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = icmp eq i64 \{tagReg}, \{intToString (cellTag e c)}"
  r
emitTagMatch e tagReg (c::rest) =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = icmp eq i64 \{tagReg}, \{intToString (cellTag e c)}"
  let rrest = emitTagMatch e tagReg rest
  let o = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{o} = or i1 \{r}, \{rrest}"
  o

-- the impl groups of one method, in program order.
implGroupsForMethod : Emit -> String -> List ImplGroup
implGroupsForMethod e method =
  filterGroupsByMethod method (groupImpls (implEntriesOf e))

filterGroupsByMethod : String -> List ImplGroup -> List ImplGroup
filterGroupsByMethod _ [] = []
filterGroupsByMethod method (g::rest)
  | groupMethodOf g == method = g :: filterGroupsByMethod method rest
  | otherwise = filterGroupsByMethod method rest

groupTag : ImplGroup -> String
groupTag (ImplGroup _ tag _ _ _ _) = tag

groupSymTag : ImplGroup -> String
groupSymTag (ImplGroup _ _ symTag _ _ _) = symTag

groupMethodOf : ImplGroup -> String
groupMethodOf (ImplGroup m _ _ _ _ _) = m

groupPositionsOf : ImplGroup -> List Int
groupPositionsOf (ImplGroup _ _ _ p _ _) = p

headGroup : List ImplGroup -> ImplGroup
headGroup (g::_) = g
headGroup [] = panic "llvm spike: no impl group for arg-tag dispatch (slice 7)"

-- the discriminating arg position (first dispatch position; default 0 — for a
-- single-position method like `eq` the head suffices, both args share the type).
headPos : List Int -> Int
headPos (p::_) = p
headPos [] = 0

nthStr : List String -> Int -> String
nthStr (x::_) 0 = x
nthStr (_::rest) i = nthStr rest (i - 1)
nthStr [] _ =
  panic "llvm spike: discriminating arg index out of range (slice 7)"

-- a constrained-function occurrence (CDict): `name` is a `=>`-constrained function
-- that dict_pass gave leading dict PARAMETERS; the routes give the dict values for
-- this call site.  Lower to a direct call passing the dict words FOLLOWED by the
-- real argument words — the same order applyDicts prepends them at run time and
-- the order emitFn bound `name`'s leading dict params.
emitDictApp : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Route -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitDictApp e env name routes argOps =
  if isKnownFn e name then
    let dictWords = dictWordsOf e env routes
    let allWords = dictWords ++ argOps
    -- E22: a constrained fn that RETURNS A CLOSURE is under-applied at the call site
    -- (`mkEq : Eq a => a -> (a -> Bool); mkEq y = (z => eq y z)` called `mkEq 1`).
    -- Its #12-eta-saturated define has `nDicts + sigValueArity` params, but the site
    -- supplies fewer value args (the rest go to the returned closure).  A direct call
    -- would under-supply the trailing param (garbage → a bad closure word indirectly
    -- called → SIGSEGV).  When the RECORDED define arity says we are short, build a
    -- residual PAP capturing the dict + supplied words.  Saturated sites (the only
    -- shape the compiler's own source produces) keep the byte-identical direct call
    -- below, so the self-compile fixpoint is unaffected.
    match defArityOf name
      Some n => if lengthS allWords < n then
          emitPapClosure e ("mdk_" ++ name) allWords n
        else emitDictAppDirect e name allWords
      None => emitDictAppDirect e name allWords
  else gapE ("CDict head '" ++ name ++ "' is not a known top-level function (slice 6)")

-- the plain direct constrained-fn call: dict words followed by the real arg words,
-- in the order dict_pass bound `name`'s leading dict params (the historical lowering).
emitDictAppDirect : Emit -> String -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitDictAppDirect e name allWords =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_\{name}(\{argDecls allWords})"
  (r, fnRetTy e name)

-- the dict witness words for a constrained-function call's routes (note (j)).
dictWordsOf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Route -> List String
dictWordsOf _ _ [] = []
dictWordsOf e env (r::rest) = dictWordOfRoute e env r :: dictWordsOf e env rest

-- one route -> its dict witness word (GAP 1, Option A — BOXED dict witness).
-- A dict witness is a POINTER to a heap cell `[ i64 head_tag | i64 reqdict_0 | … ]`
-- allocated via @mdk_alloc (the same boxed-cell shape emitCtorAlloc uses): field 0
-- is `hashName(impl-head-tag)`, fields 1..n are the RECURSIVELY-built witness words
-- for the impl's per-instance `requires` dicts, in `reqs` order (matching the
-- interpreter's structured `VDict(key, map dict_of_route reqs)` and the leading
-- dict-param order dict_pass binds — methRoutes then implRoutes, here the impl's
-- own requires).  This makes the rep UNIFORM: a one-level dict (`RKey k []`) is a
-- pointer to a 1-word cell `[ hashName k ]`; a two-level dict nests another cell
-- pointer in field 1.  Consumption (emitDispatchChain / the RKey direct-call path)
-- loads field 0 for the icmp and prepends fields 1..n as the matched impl's leading
-- element-dict args.  RDict/RDictFwd forward the enclosing dict parameter's word
-- (already a boxed-cell pointer); RNone is the 0 dict (no cell).
dictWordOfRoute : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Route -> String
dictWordOfRoute e env (RKey key reqs) =
  let reqWords = dictWordsOf e env reqs
  if allConstWords reqWords then
    emitConstDictCell e (hashName key) reqWords
  else
    emitDictCell e (hashName key) reqWords
dictWordOfRoute _ env (RDict d) = dictOperand env d
dictWordOfRoute _ env (RDictFwd d) = dictOperand env d
dictWordOfRoute _ _ RNone = "0"
-- S-1: RLocal's own dicts are the call's leading dict ARGS (emitted by emitMethod's
-- RLocal arm via emitDictApp), not a dict WITNESS for this route — so as a witness it
-- stays the no-op 0 dict, mirroring eval's `dictOfRoute (RLocal _ _) = VDict "" []`.
dictWordOfRoute _ _ (RLocal _ _) = "0"

-- allocate a boxed dict-witness cell `[ i64 headTag | i64 req_0 | … | i64 req_(n-1) ]`
-- via @mdk_alloc and return its pointer word (ptrtoint).  Mirrors emitCtorAlloc's
-- header+fields layout (header at offset 0, field i at 8*(i+1)) so loadField /
-- loadTag read it back without type info.  A one-word cell (no reqs) is the
-- uniform one-level dict.
emitDictCell : Emit -> Int -> List String -> String
emitDictCell e headTag reqWords =
  let n = lengthS reqWords
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (n + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString headTag}, ptr \{p}"
  let _ = storeFields e p reqWords 0
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  w

-- ── constant dict-witness hoisting (perf/dict-const) ────────────────────────
-- A dict witness whose head tag and every requirement word are compile-time
-- constants (a one-level `RKey k []` → `[hashName k]`, or a nested RKey whose reqs
-- are themselves constant) does NOT need a per-call `@mdk_alloc`: the cell is
-- immutable and identical at every invocation. Emit it ONCE as an `internal
-- constant` module global and pass `ptrtoint (ptr @g to i64)` — eliminating the
-- per-invocation allocation that dominated dispatch-heavy polymorphic loops (~4×
-- on a tight `Ord` loop; broad — every constant dict across stdlib/compiler/user
-- code). Deduped by initializer content so identical dicts share one global.
dictConstCacheRef : Ref (List (String, String))
dictConstCacheRef = Ref []

-- every dict word is a compile-time constant (not a runtime %register): a literal
-- tag, "0" (RNone), or a `ptrtoint`-of-global constant-expr (a nested const dict).
allConstWords : List String -> Bool
allConstWords [] = True
allConstWords (w::rest) = not (startsWith "%" w) && allConstWords rest

constDictReqInits : List String -> String
constDictReqInits [] = ""
constDictReqInits (w::rest) = ", i64 \{w}\{constDictReqInits rest}"

-- emit (or reuse) a module-global constant dict cell; return its pointer-as-i64
-- constant-expr. Layout byte-identical to emitDictCell's heap cell, so every
-- consumer (RKey dispatch loadField, nested-dict store, call arg) reads it the same.
emitConstDictCell : Emit -> Int -> List String -> String
emitConstDictCell e headTag reqWords =
  let n = lengthS reqWords
  let initBody = "[\{intToString (n + 1)} x i64] [i64 \{intToString headTag}\{constDictReqInits reqWords}]"
  match lookupAssoc initBody dictConstCacheRef.value
    Some gname => "ptrtoint (ptr @" ++ gname ++ " to i64)"
    None =>
      let gname = "mdk_dc_" ++ intToString (freshId e)
      let _ = emitGlobal e "@\{gname} = internal constant \{initBody}"
      let _ = setRef dictConstCacheRef ((initBody, gname)::dictConstCacheRef.value)
      "ptrtoint (ptr @" ++ gname ++ " to i64)"

-- the operand word a dict parameter name is bound to in the emit env.
dictOperand : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String
dictOperand env d = match lookupAssoc d env
  Some (op, _) => op
  None => gapStr ("unbound dict witness '" ++ d ++ "' in emit env (slice 6: dict not threaded to this site)")

-- ── impl method definitions (slice 6 + slice 7) ─────────────────────────────
-- Each typeclass impl method lowers to ONE top-level `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>`,
-- emitted alongside the ordinary function defines (before @main).  Untagged
-- interface defaults (CImplDefault) are the arg-tag fallback path — never a
-- static-dispatch target — so they are skipped.
--
-- SLICE 7 — multi-clause / arg-position impl methods.  The Core IR carries one
-- CImplEntry PER CLAUSE (eval coalesces same-named candidates into a VMulti that
-- falls through arg-by-arg, leftmost clause winning — exactly Maranget column
-- specialization, see ceval/applyOpt/collectPartials).  So same-`(method,tag)`
-- entries are COALESCED here into a single lifted fn whose body is a decision
-- tree built by the SAME `compileTree` the match lowering uses (Axis-1: shared
-- backend-neutral pass).  By arity:
--   • 0 (slice-6 nullary return-position `zero`/`def`): the body is emitted in
--     TAIL position (emitFnBody), byte-for-byte the old single-clause path.
--   • 1: the lone param `%arg0` is the scrutinee; the clauses' single patterns
--     are the match arms.
--   • ≥2: the params are wrapped in a synthetic tuple `(a0, a1, …)` so the
--     multi-param match reduces to a single scrutinee — letting emitDecision /
--     emitTree / bindPattern be reused VERBATIM (note (o); one extra `$tuple`
--     cell alloc per call, acceptable for the spike).

-- a coalesced impl group: method, type-head tag, the SYMBOL tag (= head tag when
-- this is the sole impl at that head, else the sanitized canonical key — C7), the
-- dispatch positions, arity, and the clauses (patterns + body) in source order
-- (leftmost-first — the priority the decision tree preserves).
data ImplGroup =
  | ImplGroup String String String (List Int) Int (List (List Pat, CExpr))

emitImpls : Emit -> List CImplEntry -> Unit
emitImpls e entries = emitGroups e (groupImpls entries)

emitGroups : Emit -> List ImplGroup -> Unit
emitGroups _ [] = ()
emitGroups e (g::rest) =
  -- PROGRAM half (issue #118): same treatment as a prelude fn bind — see emitFns.
  let _ = if hiddenImplGroup g then
    emitAsDeclares e (_ => emitGroup e g)
  else
    emitGroup e g
  emitGroups e rest

emitGroup : Emit -> ImplGroup -> Unit
emitGroup e (ImplGroup method tag symTag positions arity clauses) =
  let fname = implFnName symTag method
  let iface = implGroupIface e method tag
  let selfFnPos = selfFnParamPositions iface method
  let _ = setCurImplSelfFns tag (selfFnParamNames clauses selfFnPos)
  if trmcImplTry e fname method tag positions arity clauses then
    let _ = clearCurImplSelfFns ()
    ()
  else
    let _ = beginDefine e
    let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{fname}(\{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
    let _ = emit e "entry:"
    let _ = emitGroupBody e fname tag positions arity clauses
    let _ = clearCurImplSelfFns ()
    let _ = emit e "}"
    emit e ""
-- native backend: set the self-returning-fn-param context for this impl body so
-- `emitIndirect` types `f x` (a callback returning the container) as the impl's
-- own type.  The self-fn positions come from the method's signature (keyed by
-- iface, looked up from the resolved impl entry); their names are the PVar names
-- the clauses bind at those positions.  (Set BEFORE choosing the TRMC vs ordinary
-- body so a cons-head callback `f x` — e.g. `map`'s `f x :: …` — types correctly.)

-- Phase 2 B-dispatch (TRMC-DESIGN.md): a DISPATCHED self-recursive cons/ctor-tail
-- impl method (`map`/`filterMap`/`ap` over List, recursing via a `CMethod method
-- (RKey tag)` node) is emitted as an O(1)-stack destination-passing loop, just like
-- a top-level builder.  Scope: uniform-leading-dict impls (`dictUniformPairs`); a
-- point-free eta-reshaped impl (`filter p = filterMap …`) is a single dispatched
-- call, NOT cons-tail-recursive, so `trmcEligible` rejects it (stays ordinary).

-- Phase 2 B-dispatch: try to emit an impl group as a TRMC destination-passing loop.
-- Returns True (and emits the whole define) when the impl is F2(a)/F2(b)-shape-(a)
-- eligible: uniform leading-dict count (`dictUniformPairs` — dicts are admitted as
-- ordinary loop slots) and cons/ctor-tail self-recursive via its own `CMethod method
-- (RKey tag)` dispatch (`trmcEligible (SelfByMethod …)`).  Otherwise returns False so
-- emitGroup falls back to the ordinary `emitGroupBody`.  The loop body reuses
-- emitTrmcLoopBody verbatim (the shared Axis-A machinery); the impl path supplies the
-- `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>` define name, the receiver-typed `aK` scrutinee env
-- (`patPosTys`), and the SelfByMethod self-ref for the cons/ctor leaf's self-call
-- recognition + the safety-critical `mentionsSelfMethod` disqualification.
trmcImplTry : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> List Int -> Int -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool
trmcImplTry e fname method tag positions arity clauses =
  let self = SelfByMethod method tag
  if arity > 0 && dictUniformPairs clauses && trmcEligible (isCtor e) (ctorArity e) self arity clauses then
    let slotTys = patPosTys tag positions arity 0
    let (spats, sbody) = match clauses
      (ps, b)::_ => (ps, b)
      [] => ([], CLit LUnit)
    let single = listLen clauses == 1 && allClausesPVar clauses
    let _ = tmcMarker e fname "trmc"
    let _ = emitTrmcHeader e fname arity
    let _ = emitTrmcLoopBody e self arity slotTys clauses single spats sbody
    let _ = emit e "}"
    let _ = emit e ""
    True
  else False

-- ── interface DEFAULT method bodies (E19) ───────────────────────────────────
-- An interface default (`CImplDefault`) the type's `impl` did not override is
-- lifted to a `@mdk_default_<method>[_<tag>]` define ON DEMAND (the first dispatch
-- that needs it), cached so each (method, tag) is emitted once.  Like a lifted
-- lambda, the define is emitted into the SIDE buffer (LLVM forbids nesting it
-- inside the body being emitted) via the bufRef save/restore swap.
--
-- Two transforms make the point-free / return-position default callable as a
-- DIRECT impl-shaped fn:
--   • ETA-EXPAND to the method's full declared arity (methodArityOf): `filter p =
--     filterMap …` declares arity 2 (`(a->Bool) -> f a -> f a`) but binds one
--     param; the missing trailing params are added and the body applied to them,
--     so the direct call passes every call-site argument.
--   • RE-STAMP inner SAME-interface method calls to `RKey tag` (concrete-tag
--     defaults only — the `filter`@List path): the inner `filterMap` is partially
--     applied (container not yet supplied) so it cannot arg-tag-dispatch; stamping
--     it to the same dispatch tag lowers it to a direct `@mdk_impl_<tag>_filterMap`
--     call.  The arg-tag default path (max/min, tag="") skips this — its inner
--     `compare m x` HAS the discriminating arg and dispatches at run time.
ensureDefaultEmitted : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> CImplEntry -> Unit
ensureDefaultEmitted e fname tag method entry =
  if defaultAlreadyEmitted fname then ()
  else
    let _ = markDefaultEmitted fname
    emitDefaultDefine e fname tag method entry

emitDefaultDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> CImplEntry -> Unit
emitDefaultDefine e fname tag method entry =
  let pats0 = implPats entry
  let body0 = implBody entry
  let dictIfaces = methodConstraintIfacesOf method
  let nDicts = listLen dictIfaces
  let arity0 = maxInt (nDicts + methodArityOf method) (listLen pats0)
  let (pats1, body1) = etaExpand pats0 body0 arity0 (listLen pats0)
  let nReq = innerDefaultReqCount e method tag
  let reqPats = reqDictPats nReq 0
  let pats = reqPats ++ pats1
  let arity = arity0 + nReq
  -- PRELUDE half (issue #118): `@mdk_default_<method>_<tag>` is NAME-derived, and
  -- BOTH halves can demand the same one (a prelude body via emitDefaultRKey, the
  -- program's own code via its dispatch chain) — defining it in each would be a
  -- duplicate symbol.  So it joins `@mdk_disp_*` in the program half, which walks
  -- the prelude's bodies and therefore reaches every default the prelude declares.
  if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude then
    emitGlobal e "declare i64 @\{fname}(\{implParamDecls arity 0})"
  else
    let bodyS = restampIfaceDicts methodIfaceTableRef.value (methodIfaceOf method) tag (reqDictRoutes nReq 0) body1
    let body = restampCrossIface methodIfaceTableRef.value method dictIfaces bodyS
    let saved = (bufRef e).value
    let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
    let scope = beginDefine e
    let savedFt = fallthroughLabelRef.value
    let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
    let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{fname}(\{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
    let _ = emit e "entry:"
    let _ = emitGroupBody e fname tag [] arity [(pats, body)]
    let _ = emit e "}"
    let _ = emit e ""
    let defLines = (bufRef e).value
    let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (defLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
    let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFt
    let _ = endDefine e scope
    setRef (bufRef e) saved
-- G7: the dict-passing pass already PREPENDED one `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param per
-- method-level constraint (foldMap's `Monoid m`) to this default's pats, so the full
-- arity is nDicts + the method's interface arity.  `methodArityOf` counts only the
-- interface args (f + container); add nDicts back so eta-expansion supplies the
-- still-missing trailing interface params (the container) — without it the body stays
-- the under-applied `fold lam empty` (a partial closure, never run on the container).

-- Native Gap C (parametric default-method dict-synthesis, 2026-06-12): a parametric
-- Ord head (tuple/`List a`/`Box a`) reaches this default (`lt`/`gt`/…) whose inner
-- same-interface `compare` is impl'd with element `requires` dicts.  PREPEND that many
-- `$reqdict_<k>` PVar params (so emitGroupBody's all-PVar path binds them by name to
-- the leading `%argK`), and restamp the inner `compare` to carry them as its impl-dict
-- routes (RDict `$reqdict_<k>`).  Then `@mdk_default_<op>_<tag>(reqdict.., a, b)` calls
-- `@mdk_impl_<tag>_compare(reqdict.., a, b)`.  Concrete Ord (no requires) ⇒ nReq=0 ⇒
-- no prepended params, the inner compare keeps empty impl-dicts — byte-identical.

-- re-stamp inner same-interface method occurrences to the concrete RKey tag so a
-- partially-applied inner method (`filterMap` in `filter`'s body) lowers to a
-- direct `@mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>` call instead of an arg-tag site.  The parametric
-- variant also fills the matched call's impl-dict routes from the `$reqdict_<k>` params.

-- G7: a CROSS-interface method in the body (the Monoid `empty`, routed per-call by
-- the RESULT type) is left RNone by restampIface (different interface) → arg-tag
-- dispatch with no discriminating arg → panic.  Route it through the dict-passing's
-- own `$dict_<method>_<slot>` PARAM (RDict) so it dispatches the right Monoid impl at
-- run time from the threaded word; emitDefaultRKey supplies that word at the call site.

-- Native Gap C: the element-`requires` dict count of the inner same-interface method
-- a default reduces to at head [tag].  Ord's `lt`/`gt`/`lte`/`gte`/`min`/`max` defaults
-- reduce to `compare`; that impl carries the parametric element dicts.  A method WITH
-- its own impl at [tag], or whose inner impl has no requires, yields 0 (concrete path).
innerDefaultReqCount : Emit -> String -> String -> Int
innerDefaultReqCount e method tag = match implFor e (innerDefaultMethodE method) tag
  Some entry => maxInt 0 (implReqCount e (innerDefaultMethodE method) entry)
  None => 0

-- the inner same-interface method a relational default reduces to (mirror of
-- typecheck.mdk's innerDefaultMethod): Ord defaults reduce to `compare`.
innerDefaultMethodE : String -> String
innerDefaultMethodE m
  | contains m ["lt", "gt", "lte", "gte", "min", "max"] = "compare"
  | otherwise = m

-- `n` element-dict PVar params named `$reqdict_<i>..` (emitGroupBody's all-PVar path
-- binds each by name to the leading `%argK`).
reqDictPats : Int -> Int -> List Pat
reqDictPats n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = PVar ("$reqdict_" ++ intToString i) :: reqDictPats n (i + 1)

-- `n` RDict routes forwarding the `$reqdict_<i>` params into the inner same-interface
-- call's impl-dict slots (matching the inner impl fn's leading element-dict params).
reqDictRoutes : Int -> Int -> List Route
reqDictRoutes n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = RDict ("$reqdict_" ++ intToString i) :: reqDictRoutes n (i + 1)

maxInt : Int -> Int -> Int
-- Intentional: this IS the compiler's hot monomorphic Int max (AGENTS.md
-- anti-pattern: don't delegate hot inner-loop helpers to the dict-passed
-- prelude Ord method).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-if-max-min
maxInt a b = if a >= b then a else b

-- eta-expand `pats`/`body` from `have` params to `arity` by appending fresh
-- `$etaK` params and applying the body to each (left-to-right).
etaExpand : List Pat -> CExpr -> Int -> Int -> (List Pat, CExpr)
etaExpand pats body arity have
  | have >= arity = (pats, body)
  | otherwise =
    let name = "$eta" ++ intToString have
    etaExpand
      (pats ++ [PVar name])
      (CApp body (CVar name AGlobal))
      arity
      (have + 1)

-- rewrite every inner SAME-interface method occurrence (a bare `CVar m` whose
-- interface equals `iface`) to a `CMethod m (RKey tag) reqRoutes`, so a partially-
-- applied inner method lowers to a direct impl call.  Walks the whole CExpr tree.  The
-- method→(iface,arity) table is passed in (a pure snapshot) so the walk stays pure.
-- [reqRoutes] are the element `requires` dict routes (RDict `$reqdict_<k>`) threaded
-- into the matched inner call's impl-dict slots for a PARAMETRIC head (tuple/`List a`/
-- `Box a`); [] for a concrete head (the `filter`@List / non-parametric default path),
-- byte-identical to the old empty-impl-dicts restamp.
restampIfaceDicts : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> CExpr -> CExpr
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CVar m ad)
  | iface != "" && ifaceOfIn tbl m == iface = CMethod m (RKey tag []) rr []
  | otherwise = CVar m ad
-- An inner same-interface method that method_marker already rewrote to a
-- `CMethod m RNone` (e.g. `compare` inside the `lt`/`gt`/… Ord defaults) must
-- ALSO be re-stamped to `RKey tag`, or it falls to arg-tag dispatch and a
-- primitive-Ord receiver (which owns no cell tag) panics in emitTagMatch.  Only
-- an unrouted (RNone) same-iface method is restamped — an already-concrete route
-- (a different element type) is left intact.  The parametric element dicts [rr]
-- REPLACE the call's empty impl-dicts so the inner `@mdk_impl_<tag>_compare` sees
-- its leading element-dict params (`reqDictRoutes`); [] preserves the old behaviour.
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CMethod m RNone irs mrs)
  | iface != "" && ifaceOfIn tbl m == iface =
    CMethod m (RKey tag []) (chooseReqRoutes rr irs) mrs
  | otherwise = CMethod m RNone irs mrs
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CApp f a) =
  CApp
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr f)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CLam ps b) =
  CLam ps (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CLet r p rhs b) =
  CLet
    r
    p
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr rhs)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CLetGroup bs b) =
  CLetGroup
    (map (restampBind tbl iface tag rr) bs)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CMatch s arms) =
  CMatch
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr s)
    (map (restampArm tbl iface tag rr) arms)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CDecision s arms tr) =
  CDecision
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr s)
    (map (restampArm tbl iface tag rr) arms)
    tr
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CIf c t el) =
  CIf
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr c)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr t)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr el)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CBinPrim op a b stag) =
  CBinPrim
    op
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
    stag
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CUnOp op a) =
  CUnOp op (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CTuple xs) =
  CTuple (map (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr) xs)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CList xs) =
  CList (map (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr) xs)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CFieldAccess a f n) =
  CFieldAccess (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a) f n
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CIndex a b) =
  CIndex
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CStringIndex a b) =
  CStringIndex
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr (CListIndex a b) =
  CListIndex
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr a)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)
restampIfaceDicts _ _ _ _ other = other

-- prefer the threaded element dict routes [rr] when present; else keep whatever the
-- call already carried (empty for the non-parametric path).
chooseReqRoutes : List Route -> List Route -> List Route
chooseReqRoutes [] irs = irs
chooseReqRoutes rr _ = rr

-- G7: route every CROSS-interface method occurrence in a default body (a bare
-- `CVar m` / `CMethod m RNone` whose interface is one of the method's constraint
-- interfaces, e.g. the Monoid `empty` in foldMap's default) to read the dict PARAM
-- the dict-passing pass already prepended (`$dict_<method>_<slot>`, slot = the
-- iface's index in dictIfaces).  emitGroupBody binds that PVar (by name) to its
-- %argK, so `dictOperand env "$dict_<method>_<slot>"` resolves it at the dispatch
-- site, and emitMethodDispatch switches the right Monoid impl off the threaded word.
-- Same-interface methods were already restamped to RKey by restampIface — untouched.
restampCrossIface : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> List String -> CExpr -> CExpr
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CVar m ad) = match crossDictSlot tbl difs m
  Some k => CMethod m (RDict (dictParamNameE method k)) [] []
  None => CVar m ad
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CMethod m RNone irs mrs) = match crossDictSlot tbl difs m
  Some k => CMethod m (RDict (dictParamNameE method k)) irs mrs
  None => CMethod m RNone irs mrs
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CApp f a) =
  CApp
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs f)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CLam ps b) =
  CLam ps (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CLet r p rhs b) =
  CLet
    r
    p
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs rhs)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CLetGroup bs b) =
  CLetGroup
    (map (restampCrossBind tbl method difs) bs)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CMatch s arms) =
  CMatch
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs s)
    (map (restampCrossArm tbl method difs) arms)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CDecision s arms tr) =
  CDecision
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs s)
    (map (restampCrossArm tbl method difs) arms)
    tr
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CIf c t el) =
  CIf
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs c)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs t)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs el)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CBinPrim op a b stag) =
  CBinPrim
    op
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
    stag
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CUnOp op a) =
  CUnOp op (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CTuple xs) =
  CTuple (map (restampCrossIface tbl method difs) xs)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CList xs) =
  CList (map (restampCrossIface tbl method difs) xs)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CFieldAccess a f n) =
  CFieldAccess (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a) f n
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CIndex a b) =
  CIndex
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CStringIndex a b) =
  CStringIndex
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface tbl method difs (CListIndex a b) =
  CListIndex
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs a)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)
restampCrossIface _ _ _ other = other

-- the dict-passing param name for [method]'s [slot]-th method-level constraint dict;
-- matches typecheck.mdk's dictParamName ("$dict_" ++ fn ++ "_" ++ slot).
dictParamNameE : String -> Int -> String
dictParamNameE method slot = "$dict_\{method}_\{intToString slot}"

-- the dict slot (index in dictIfaces) for method [m], if its interface is one of
-- the method's method-level constraint interfaces; None otherwise.
crossDictSlot : List (String, (String, Int)) -> List String -> String -> Option Int
crossDictSlot tbl difs m =
  let mi = ifaceOfIn tbl m
  if mi == "" then None else indexOfStr mi difs

restampCrossBind : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> List String -> CBind -> CBind
restampCrossBind tbl method difs (CBind n cs) =
  CBind n (map (restampCrossClause tbl method difs) cs)

restampCrossClause : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> List String -> CClause -> CClause
restampCrossClause tbl method difs (CClause ps b) =
  CClause ps (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)

restampCrossArm : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> List String -> CArm -> CArm
restampCrossArm tbl method difs (CArm p gs b) =
  CArm
    p
    (map (restampCrossGuard tbl method difs) gs)
    (restampCrossIface tbl method difs b)

restampCrossGuard : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> List String -> CGuard -> CGuard
restampCrossGuard tbl method difs (CGBool c) =
  CGBool (restampCrossIface tbl method difs c)
restampCrossGuard tbl method difs (CGBind p c) =
  CGBind p (restampCrossIface tbl method difs c)

-- the interface a method name belongs to, looked up in a pure table snapshot.
ifaceOfIn : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
ifaceOfIn tbl m = match lookupAssoc m tbl
  Some (iface, _) => iface
  None => ""

restampBind : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> CBind -> CBind
restampBind tbl iface tag rr (CBind n cs) =
  CBind n (map (restampClause tbl iface tag rr) cs)

restampClause : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> CClause -> CClause
restampClause tbl iface tag rr (CClause ps b) =
  CClause ps (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)

restampArm : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> CArm -> CArm
restampArm tbl iface tag rr (CArm p gs b) =
  CArm
    p
    (map (restampGuard tbl iface tag rr) gs)
    (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr b)

restampGuard : List (String, (String, Int)) -> String -> String -> List Route -> CGuard -> CGuard
restampGuard tbl iface tag rr (CGBool c) =
  CGBool (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr c)
restampGuard tbl iface tag rr (CGBind p c) =
  CGBind p (restampIfaceDicts tbl iface tag rr c)

-- the interface a (method, tag) impl belongs to, via the resolved impl entry.
implGroupIface : Emit -> String -> String -> String
implGroupIface e method tag = match implFor e method tag
  Some entry => implEntryIface entry
  None => ""

-- the PVar names bound at the self-fn positions, taken from the FIRST clause that
-- binds a plain variable there (impl callback params are PVars across clauses; a
-- non-PVar position contributes no name and is simply not typed specially).
selfFnParamNames : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> List Int -> List String
selfFnParamNames clauses positions =
  dedupS (concatMapClausePVars clauses positions)

concatMapClausePVars : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> List Int -> List String
concatMapClausePVars [] _ = []
concatMapClausePVars ((pats, _)::rest) positions = pvarsAtPositions pats positions 0
  ++ concatMapClausePVars rest positions

pvarsAtPositions : List Pat -> List Int -> Int -> List String
pvarsAtPositions [] _ _ = []
pvarsAtPositions ((PVar x)::rest) positions i
  | listContains positions i = x :: pvarsAtPositions rest positions (i + 1)
  | otherwise = pvarsAtPositions rest positions (i + 1)
pvarsAtPositions (_::rest) positions i = pvarsAtPositions rest positions (i + 1)

-- the lifted impl fn's positional parameter list: `i64 %arg0, …` (arity words),
-- or "" for a nullary return-position impl.
implParamDecls : Int -> Int -> String
implParamDecls n i
  | i >= n = ""
  | i == n - 1 = "i64 %arg" ++ intToString i
  | otherwise = "i64 %arg\{intToString i}, \{implParamDecls n (i + 1)}"

-- bind each positional param to its `%argK` operand under a synthetic name aK
-- (the names the synthetic scrutinee CVars reference).
implParamEnv : Int -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
implParamEnv n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = ("a" ++ intToString i, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) :: implParamEnv n (i + 1)

-- map an impl head tag name to its static LTy for use in param typing.
-- Known primitives → their type; everything else (ADT, List, Array, tuples, …) → LTCon.
tagToLTy : String -> LTy
tagToLTy "String" = LTStr
tagToLTy "Int" = LTInt
tagToLTy "Float" = LTFloat
tagToLTy "Bool" = LTBool
tagToLTy "Char" = LTChar
tagToLTy _ = LTCon

-- like implParamEnv, but types param `i` as `tagToLTy tag` when `i ∈ positions`
-- (the receiver-type positions from CImplTagged), otherwise LTInt.
-- This lets `append xs ys = xs ++ ys` in impl Semigroup (List a) emit xs/ys as
-- LTCon → mdk_list_append, and in impl Semigroup String emit them as LTStr →
-- mdk_string_append — without touching emitBin.
implParamEnvByPos : String -> List Int -> Int -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
implParamEnvByPos _ _ n i
  | i >= n = []
implParamEnvByPos tag positions n i =
  let ty = if listContains positions i then tagToLTy tag else LTInt
  ("a" ++ intToString i, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, ty)) :: implParamEnvByPos tag positions n (i + 1)

listContains : List Int -> Int -> Bool
listContains [] _ = False
listContains (x::rest) v
  | x == v = True
  | otherwise = listContains rest v

emitGroupBody : Emit -> String -> String -> List Int -> Int -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Unit
emitGroupBody e fname _ _ 0 [([], body)] = emitFnBody e [] fname body
emitGroupBody e fname _ _ 0 _ =
  gapU "nullary impl group with more than one clause (slice 6)"
-- Fast path: single-clause all-PVar impl (e.g. `append xs ys = xs ++ ys`).
-- Build a typed env directly from param names × receiver type so that `++`/`::`
-- operands inherit the correct LTy (LTStr/LTCon) from the impl head tag.
-- The decision-tree path (emitClauseTree) loses this type info through the
-- tuple-scrutinee indirection → paramUseTy falls back to LTInt → ++ gaps.
--
-- NOTE: this dispatch is written with `if`-EXPRESSIONS (not a guarded match arm)
-- so the emitter itself is self-compilable — the gap-tolerant bootstrap driver
-- cannot lower a guarded match arm (it inserts a `store 0` placeholder, see the
-- CTGuard arm of emitTree), which would silently blank this body in the native
-- emitter.  The IR produced is byte-identical to the guarded form; only the
-- source shape changes.  (The other compiler stages contain NO guarded matches
-- for the same reason.)
emitGroupBody e fname tag positions arity (clauses@[(pats, body)]) =
  if allPVar pats then
    let tyList = patPosTys tag positions (listLen pats) 0
    let env = paramEnvByPos pats tyList 0
    emitFnBody e env fname body
  else emitClauseTree e (implParamEnvByPos tag positions arity 0) arity clauses
emitGroupBody e fname tag positions arity clauses =
  emitClauseTree e (implParamEnvByPos tag positions arity 0) arity clauses

-- build a LTy list of length n, typed as tagToLTy tag at positions ∈ positions,
-- LTInt elsewhere.
patPosTys : String -> List Int -> Int -> Int -> List LTy
patPosTys _ _ n i
  | i >= n = []
patPosTys tag positions n i =
  let ty = if listContains positions i then tagToLTy tag else LTInt
  ty :: patPosTys tag positions n (i + 1)

-- bind PVar params to their %argK operands at the given LTy list.
paramEnvByPos : List Pat -> List LTy -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
paramEnvByPos [] _ _ = []
paramEnvByPos ((PVar x)::rest) (ty::tys) i =
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, ty)) :: paramEnvByPos rest tys (i + 1)
paramEnvByPos ((PVar x)::rest) [] i =
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) :: paramEnvByPos rest [] (i + 1)
paramEnvByPos _ _ _ = []

-- the shared decision-tree body for a coalesced multi-clause group (impl methods
-- AND top-level fns, slice 2/E1a).  `env` binds the synthetic `aK` scrutinee
-- params to their `%argK` operands at the caller's chosen LTy (impl: all LTInt;
-- top-level fn: the inferred signature param types).
--
-- The params are scrutinised AS A MATRIX — one decision-tree COLUMN per param,
-- each column's occurrence word being the param's own operand.  Historically an
-- arity-≥2 group boxed its params into a synthetic `CTuple` cell so the
-- multi-param match reduced to a SINGLE scrutinee; that cost an `@mdk_alloc` on
-- EVERY call (24 B at arity 2) — including every tail-recursive self-call and
-- every TRMC loop iteration — purely to destructure the cell back into the very
-- words it was built from.  Maranget's algorithm is already multi-column
-- (`compileTree` specializes the leftmost column and `emitTree` carries `occs` as
-- a column LIST), so the tuple was never needed: `implRows` now emits one column
-- per param and the occurrences start as the param words themselves.  A leaf
-- binds the clause's patterns element-wise against those words (`bindArm`) rather
-- than loading them back out of a cell.  Zero allocation, one fewer (always-true)
-- tuple-discriminant test, and the param LTys survive into the leaf.
emitClauseTree : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Int -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Unit
emitClauseTree e env arity clauses =
  let roots = emitArgs e env (implScrutVars arity 0)
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "decend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let rty = Ref LTInt
  let saved = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = emitClauseChain e env arity clauses roots slot endL rty saved
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef saved
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
-- Emit the clauses as a CHAIN of single-clause decision trees sharing one result
-- slot + end label.  Each clause sets fallthroughLabelRef to the NEXT clause's
-- entry block before emitting, so a pattern miss (CTFail) or a guard fallthrough
-- (`__fallthrough__`) resumes the next clause — the interpreter's "try next
-- clause" semantics.  The scrutinee is emitted ONCE, before the chain.  (A lone
-- clause degenerates to the historical single-tree path: no next label → CTFail
-- stays `unreachable`, fallthrough stays oob.)

-- emit clause chain: each clause's fallthrough target is the NEXT clause's entry
-- block; the LAST clause falls through to `outer` (the fallthrough that was in
-- effect when the chain began — "" at top level → oob; a nested chain inside a
-- guarded clause body inherits the enclosing clause's next-clause label).  A
-- single-clause function degenerates to `outer` directly, preserving the historical
-- single-tree behaviour.
emitClauseChain : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Int -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> List String -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> String -> Unit
emitClauseChain _ _ _ [] _ _ _ _ _ = ()
emitClauseChain e env arity (c::rest) roots slot endL rty outer =
  let nextL = chainNextLabel e rest outer
  -- Stamp this clause's next-clause label INTO its `__fallthrough__` sentinels
  -- (emit_support's labelFallthrough) before building the arms, so the sentinel
  -- lowers to `br %nextL` from the var name alone.  The old code left the sentinel
  -- bare and had emitFallthrough read `fallthroughLabelRef` — which emitDecision
  -- NULLS, so a refutable pattern-guard clause (whose guard chain desugars to a
  -- body-level CDecision) fell through to `@mdk_oob` instead of the next clause.
  let c2 = ftClause nextL c
  let arms = [clauseArm arity c2]
  let tree = compileTree [False] (implRows [c2] 0)
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef nextL
  let _ = emitTree e env roots arms roots slot endL rty tree
  if isEmptyL rest then ()
  else
    let _ = emit e (nextL ++ ":")
    emitClauseChain e env arity rest roots slot endL rty outer

-- label a clause's fall-through sentinels with the block to resume at.  An EMPTY
-- label means "no next clause" (the last clause of a top-level chain): leave the
-- sentinels BARE so emitFallthrough lowers them to the non-exhaustive abort.
ftClause : String -> (List Pat, CExpr) -> (List Pat, CExpr)
ftClause "" c = c
ftClause lbl (pats, body) = (pats, labelFallthrough body lbl)

-- the next clause's entry label: the outer fallthrough when this is the last
-- clause, else a fresh `clauseN` block label.
chainNextLabel : Emit -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> String -> String
chainNextLabel _ [] outer = outer
chainNextLabel e _ _ = "clause" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)

isEmptyL : List a -> Bool
isEmptyL [] = True
isEmptyL _ = False

-- the synthetic scrutinee components of a group: `[a0, a1, …]`.  Emitted through
-- `emitArgs`, so each resolves to the param's operand word — `%argK` for an
-- ordinary define, the loop-head reload register under TRMC.
implScrutVars : Int -> Int -> List CExpr
implScrutVars n i
  | i >= n = []
  | otherwise = CVar ("a" ++ intToString i) AGlobal :: implScrutVars n (i + 1)

-- one match arm per clause: arity 1 uses the lone pattern directly; arity ≥2
-- keeps the clause's params as a `PTuple` — NOT because a tuple cell exists (it
-- does not; see emitClauseTree), but because `CArm` carries a single `Pat`.  The
-- leaf binder (`bindArm`) recognises the multi-root case and binds the tuple's
-- element patterns element-wise against the param words.
clauseArm : Int -> (List Pat, CExpr) -> CArm
clauseArm 1 (pats, body) = CArm (headPat pats) [] body
clauseArm _ (pats, body) = CArm (PTuple pats) [] body

-- one matrix row per clause, ONE COLUMN PER PARAM (each CANONICALISED into matrix
-- form via canonPat — the same normalisation initialRows applies to match arms, so
-- compileTree recognises list/bool/ctor heads); index = the clause position so the
-- decision-tree leaf selects the right arm.  The ARM keeps the RAW patterns
-- (clauseArm) — bindArm re-matches those to bind variables.
implRows : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Int -> List (List Pat, Int)
implRows [] _ = []
implRows ((pats, _)::rest) i = (map canonPat pats, i) :: implRows rest (i + 1)

headPat : List Pat -> Pat
headPat (p::_) = p
headPat [] = PWild

-- ── impl coalescing (slice 7) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
-- group the per-clause CImplTagged entries by `(method, key)` — the canonical
-- full-type impl key, so two impls sharing a head tycon but differing in type args
-- (C7) land in DISTINCT groups (own body each) while a multi-clause impl (one key)
-- stays coalesced.  Preserves first-seen group order and source clause order within
-- each (the Maranget priority).  CImplDefault entries (interface defaults) are
-- skipped — they are the arg-tag fallback, never a lifted static-dispatch target.
groupImpls : List CImplEntry -> List ImplGroup
groupImpls entries = mapMut (gatherGroup entries) (distinctImplKeys entries [])

-- map a <Mut> function over a list (map is pure; gatherGroup now reads the method
-- arity table to eta-expand point-free impls, so the walk must be <Mut>).
mapMut : (a -> b) -> List a -> List b
mapMut _ [] = []
mapMut f (x::rest) = f x :: mapMut f rest

-- the (method, key) keys of tagged entries, in first-seen order.
distinctImplKeys : List CImplEntry -> List (String, String) -> List (String, String)
distinctImplKeys [] seen = reverseL seen
distinctImplKeys ((CImplEntry method _ (CImplTagged _ key _ _ _ _))::rest) seen
  | keySeen method key seen = distinctImplKeys rest seen
  | otherwise = distinctImplKeys rest ((method, key)::seen)
distinctImplKeys (_::rest) seen = distinctImplKeys rest seen

keySeen : String -> String -> List (String, String) -> Bool
keySeen _ _ [] = False
keySeen m t ((m2, t2)::rest) =
  if m == m2 && t == t2 then
    True
  else
    keySeen m t rest

-- build the group for one (method, key) key: head tag + symTag + positions + arity
-- from the first matching entry, clauses (pats, body) from all matching entries in
-- order.  The SYMBOL tag (C7) is the head tag when this is the sole impl of the head
-- (byte-identical to the pre-C7 scheme), else the sanitized canonical key.  A
-- POINT-FREE impl (`length = fold f 0` — clauseArity < the method's declared
-- arity) is ETA-EXPANDED to the method's full arity, mirroring emitDefaultDefine:
-- the lifted `@mdk_impl_<symTag>_<method>` then takes every call-site arg, so the
-- RKey/arg-tag call sites (which use methodArityOf) and the define agree.  A
-- multi-clause or already-full-arity impl is unchanged (etaExpand is a no-op when
-- have >= arity).
gatherGroup : List CImplEntry -> (String, String) -> ImplGroup
gatherGroup entries (method, key) =
  let tag = headTagForKey method key entries
  let symTag = implFnSymTag entries method tag key
  let cls0 = gatherClauses method key entries
  let positions = groupPositions method key entries
  -- Eta-expand a point-free / ETA-SHORT impl clause up to the method's FULL arity
  -- INCLUDING its leading dict params: `methodArityOf` counts only the source value
  -- params, so a default like `foldMap f = fold (acc x => acc ++ f x) empty`
  -- (synthesized into an impl, clause `[$dict_foldMap_0, f]`) must expand to
  -- `dict + f + container`, not stop at `dict + f`.  Adding `nDicts` (the leading
  -- `$dict_*` params dict_pass prepended) makes the lifted fn take every call-site
  -- arg (RKey/arg-tag callers use `methodArityOf + dictWords`), so the unapplied PAP
  -- (container dropped) → SIGSEGV no longer happens.  nDicts = 0 for a concrete impl
  -- ⇒ identical to the old `maxInt (clauseArity) (methodArityOf)`.
  let nDicts = leadingDictPats (firstClausePats cls0)
  let arity = maxInt (clauseArity cls0) (methodArityOf method + nDicts)
  let cls = map (etaExpandClause arity) cls0
  ImplGroup method tag symTag positions arity cls

-- the head tycon tag of the impl whose (method, key) matches (the first such entry).
headTagForKey : String -> String -> List CImplEntry -> String
headTagForKey _ _ [] = ""
headTagForKey method key ((CImplEntry m _ (CImplTagged t k _ _ _ _))::rest)
  | m == method && k == key = t
  | otherwise = headTagForKey method key rest
headTagForKey method key (_::rest) = headTagForKey method key rest

-- eta-expand one impl clause's (pats, body) to `arity` (no-op when already there).
etaExpandClause : Int -> (List Pat, CExpr) -> (List Pat, CExpr)
etaExpandClause arity (pats, body) = etaExpand pats body arity (lengthS pats)

gatherClauses : String -> String -> List CImplEntry -> List (List Pat, CExpr)
gatherClauses _ _ [] = []
gatherClauses method key ((CImplEntry m _ (CImplTagged _ k _ _ pats body))::rest)
  | m == method && k == key = (pats, body) :: gatherClauses method key rest
  | otherwise = gatherClauses method key rest
gatherClauses method key (_::rest) = gatherClauses method key rest

groupPositions : String -> String -> List CImplEntry -> List Int
groupPositions _ _ [] = []
groupPositions method key ((CImplEntry m _ (CImplTagged _ k _ positions _ _))::rest)
  | m == method && k == key = positions
  | otherwise = groupPositions method key rest
groupPositions method key (_::rest) = groupPositions method key rest

clauseArity : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Int
clauseArity ((pats, _)::_) = lengthS pats
clauseArity [] = 0

-- the first clause's patterns (for counting leading dict params); [] when no clause.
firstClausePats : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> List Pat
firstClausePats ((pats, _)::_) = pats
firstClausePats [] = []

-- ── closures + higher-order functions (slice 4) ─────────────────────────────
-- VALUE REPRESENTATION (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §8.5 "Closures"): a closure value is a
-- BOXED heap cell, the same boxed-pointer discipline as ADTs (slice 3) and Floats
-- (slice 1).  Cell layout (8-byte words):
--   [ 0: i64 header ] [ 8: i64 code_ptr ] [ 16: capt0 ] [ 24: capt1 ] …
-- DECISION (filed in PLAN.md as a spike-rep note): the cell carries a one-word
-- HEADER (a fixed closure tag) before `code_ptr`, mirroring §8.5's `{ header,
-- code_ptr, captured… }` and the slice-3 ADT header — NOT code_ptr-at-offset-0.
-- This lets the closure cell reuse `emitCtorAlloc`/`storeFields`/`loadField`
-- VERBATIM: it is allocated exactly like a constructor whose "fields" are
-- `[code_ptr, capt0, capt1, …]`, so `code_ptr` is field 0 and capture i is field
-- i+1.  The CALLING CONVENTION (§8.5): load `code_ptr`, then call passing the
-- closure pointer as a LEADING argument (`@mdk_lamN(i64 %clos, i64 %arg0, …)`).
--
-- Each CLam is LAMBDA-LIFTED to a top-level `define @mdk_lamN` (LLVM has no nested
-- functions).  Its CAPTURED set is freeVars(body) − params, intersected with the
-- emit-time env (free names NOT in the env are top-level globals — known fns /
-- ctors — which need no capture).  At the CLam site we allocate the cell storing
-- `[&@mdk_lamN, capturedWords…]`; in the lifted body we bind params from `%argK`
-- and re-load captures from `%clos`.  Lifted-lambda params/captures default to Int
-- (the type-erased subset; every slice-4 fixture's lambda is Int-typed) — a
-- documented scope limit, like the saturated-only call rule above.

-- allocate a closure cell {arity, code_ptr, captured…}.  Field 0 is the closure's
-- callable ARITY (the value-arg count of its lifted `@mdk_lamN` define, i.e. the
-- args AFTER the leading `%clos` word) — the runtime reads it (mdk_apply) to decide
-- exact / partial / over application.  `code_ptr` is a constant-expression operand
-- (`ptrtoint (ptr @mdk_lamN to i64)`).  The arity MUST equal the emitted define's
-- param count (the E22 hazard): an inline exact call trusts field 0, so a mismatch
-- would divert a saturated call to the PAP path.  Result is the cell pointer word,
-- typed LTClosure.
emitClosureAlloc : Emit -> String -> Int -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitClosureAlloc e lamName arity captureWords =
  let codePtr = "ptrtoint (ptr @" ++ lamName ++ " to i64)"
  let (w, _) = emitClosureCell e arity (codePtr::captureWords)
  (w, LTClosure)

-- allocate a boxed cell whose header word is the raw integer `hdr` (the closure
-- arity) rather than a hashed ctor tag; fields stored at offset 8*(i+1) as usual.
emitClosureCell : Emit -> Int -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitClosureCell e hdr fields =
  let n = lengthS fields
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (n + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString hdr}, ptr \{p}"
  let _ = storeFields e p fields 0
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTClosure)

-- emitClosureAlloc + record the closure's compile-time arity in closureArityRef
-- (keyed by its SSA word) so an indirect over-application of this closure value can
-- split saturate+apply-extra.  Used for user lambdas and let-group/method-value
-- closures whose arity is statically known.
emitClosureAllocA : Emit -> String -> Int -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitClosureAllocA e lamName arity captureWords =
  let (w, lty) = emitClosureAlloc e lamName arity captureWords
  let _ = setRef closureArityRef ((w, arity)::closureArityRef.value)
  (w, lty)

-- emit a CLam: compute its captured set, lift its body to a top-level define, and
-- allocate the closure cell capturing the live operand words.
-- E2b — a CLam whose params are NOT all plain `PVar` (a tuple/constructor
-- destructuring lambda) routes through `emitLamPatDefine`: the SAME lifted-frame
-- machinery as `emitLamGo`, but the patterned params are bound by a single-clause
-- decision tree (`emitClauseTree`) over the `%argK` words instead of a verbatim
-- `paramEnv`.  Capture computation is identical — `patVarNames` already collects
-- nested pattern vars, so the patterned param vars are correctly excluded from the
-- captured set.  Closes the lambda half of EMITTER-GAPS #5.
emitLam : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitLam e env pats body =
  if allPVar pats then
    emitLamGo e env pats body
  else
    emitLamPat e env pats body

emitLamGo : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitLamGo e env pats body =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let pnames = patVarNames pats
  let captured = keepInEnv env (dedupS (freeVars pnames body))
  let captureWords = capWords e env captured
  let _ = emitLamDefine e env lamName pats captured body
  let (cw, clty) = emitClosureAllocA e lamName (listLen pats) captureWords
  let _ = recordFloatRet e env cw body
  (cw, clty)

emitLamPat : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitLamPat e env pats body =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let pnames = patVarNames pats
  let captured = keepInEnv env (dedupS (freeVars pnames body))
  let captureWords = capWords e env captured
  let _ = emitPatLamDefine e env lamName [] pats captured body
  let (cw, clty) = emitClosureAllocA e lamName (listLen pats) captureWords
  let _ = recordFloatRet e env cw body
  (cw, clty)

-- emit a RECURSIVE function-`let` closure (slice 5b): lambda-lift exactly like
-- emitLam, but EXCLUDE the binding's own name `f` from the captured set (it is the
-- closure cell itself, not an external free variable) and, inside the lifted body,
-- bind `f` to `%clos` so a self-call re-enters the same code with the same cell.
emitRecLam : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitRecLam e env f pats body =
  if allPVar pats then
    emitRecLamGo e env f pats body
  else
    emitRecLamPat e env f pats body

emitRecLamGo : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitRecLamGo e env f pats body =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let pnames = patVarNames pats
  let captured = keepInEnv env (removeStr f (dedupS (freeVars pnames body)))
  let captureWords = capWords e env captured
  let _ = emitRecLamDefine e env lamName f pats captured body
  let (cw, clty) = emitClosureAllocA e lamName (listLen pats) captureWords
  let _ = recordFloatRet e env cw body
  (cw, clty)

-- E2b — recursive-`let` lambda with non-`PVar` params: same patterned-frame body
-- as `emitLamPat`, but binds `f -> %clos` ahead of captures so a self-call
-- re-enters the same code with the same cell (mirrors `emitRecLamGo` vs
-- `emitLamGo`).  `f` is removed from the free set before computing captures.
emitRecLamPat : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitRecLamPat e env f pats body =
  let lamName = "mdk_lam" ++ intToString (freshId e)
  let pnames = patVarNames pats
  let captured = keepInEnv env (removeStr f (dedupS (freeVars pnames body)))
  let captureWords = capWords e env captured
  let _ = emitPatLamDefine e env lamName [(f, ("%clos", LTClosure))] pats captured body
  let (cw, clty) = emitClosureAllocA e lamName (listLen pats) captureWords
  let _ = recordFloatRet e env cw body
  (cw, clty)

-- like emitLamDefine, but prepends `f -> %clos` to the body's env so the lifted
-- body's recursive references resolve to the closure pointer (the leading param).
-- (Gap E3: lambda params are per-param inferred from the body via `inferParamTys`/
-- `paramUseTy`, so a Float-param lambda gets float instruction selection; params
-- `paramUseTy` can't resolve default to LTInt as before.)
emitRecLamDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> Unit
emitRecLamDefine e env lamName f pats captured body =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{closureParamDecls pats}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env captured 0
  let penv = paramEnv pats (inferParamTys (sigTable e) pats body) 0
  let _ = emitFnBody e (((f, ("%clos", LTClosure))::cenv) ++ penv) lamName body
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- E2b — emit a lifted lambda whose params are NON-variable patterns, into the side
-- buffer (same swap discipline as emitLamDefine).  Captures load from `%clos`
-- fields 1.., the patterned params are the synthetic `aK` scrutinee words
-- (`%argK`, via `implParamEnv`), and the SINGLE clause's body is lowered by
-- `emitClauseTree` — the SAME decision-tree path the patterned top-level fns use
-- (E1a) — so a tuple/constructor param binds its vars via `bindPattern` and the
-- body `ret`s.  `extra` is the leading env binding the recursive case prepends
-- (`f -> %clos`); the non-recursive case passes `[]`.  Lambda params/captures
-- default to Int (`implParamEnv`) — the documented slice-4 scope limit, identical
-- to emitLamDefine's `paramEnv … (allInt pats)`.
emitPatLamDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> Unit
emitPatLamDefine e env lamName extra pats captured body =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let savedFt = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
  let arity = listLen pats
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(i64 %clos, \{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env captured 0
  let _ = emitClauseTree e (extra ++ cenv ++ implParamEnv arity 0) arity [(pats, body)]
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFt
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- drop every occurrence of a name from a list (the recursive-closure self name is
-- removed from its own free-variable set before computing the capture set).
removeStr : String -> List String -> List String
removeStr _ [] = []
removeStr x (y::ys) = if x == y then removeStr x ys else y :: removeStr x ys

-- eta-wrap a NAMED top-level function used as a value into a captureless static
-- closure whose lifted body forwards every argument to `@mdk_<fname>`.  The arity
-- is the function's inferred parameter count (signature table).
emitEtaClosure : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitEtaClosure e fname =
  let arity = fnArity e fname
  let lamName = "mdk_eta_\{fname}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitEtaDefine e lamName fname arity
  emitClosureAlloc e lamName arity []

-- the inferred arity of a known top-level function (its signature's param count).
fnArity : Emit -> String -> Int
fnArity e fname = match sigMapRef.value
  Some m => match omLookup fname m
    Some (FnSig ptys _) => lengthS ptys
    None => 0
  None => match lookupAssoc fname (sigTable e)
    Some (FnSig ptys _) => lengthS ptys
    None => 0

-- the declared arity of constructor `name` (its field count, from the ctor table).
ctorArity : Emit -> String -> Int
ctorArity e name = match ctorMapRef.value
  Some m => match omLookup name m
    Some n => n
    None => 0
  None => match lookupAssoc name (ctorTable e)
    Some n => n
    None => 0

-- #35: an APPLIED constructor, dispatched on saturation.  This is the arity check
-- that the emitVar-side comment below has always ASSUMED but which emitApp did not
-- actually perform: the ctor arm used to call `emitCtorAlloc` unconditionally, so an
-- UNDER-applied ctor (`Bin OAdd` for arity-3 `Bin`) allocated a `Bin` cell holding 1
-- of its 3 fields.  Saturating that later applied args to a DATA cell as if it were a
-- closure (@mdk_apply read the ctor TAG out of the header as if it were an arity), so
-- the value carried the right tag but junk fields — a SILENT wrong-value miscompile:
-- `check` accepted, `run` was correct, `build` produced garbage (E-NONEXHAUSTIVE-MATCH).
--   • SATURATED (the overwhelmingly common case) ⇒ `emitCtorAlloc`, byte-identical to
--     before, so no gate/fixpoint churn.
--   • UNDER-applied ⇒ a ctor PAP (emitCtorPap): a real closure cell over the supplied
--     args whose forwarder allocates the cell once the rest arrive.
--   • OVER-applied ⇒ unreachable for a well-typed program (typecheck rejects applying
--     a data value), but route the surplus through the runtime @mdk_apply rather than
--     silently storing past the cell's end.
emitCtorApp : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitCtorApp e name argOps arity
  | lengthS argOps < arity = emitCtorPap e name argOps arity
  | lengthS argOps > arity =
    let (cw, _) = emitCtorAlloc e name (takeS arity argOps)
    (emitApplyAny e cw (dropS arity argOps), LTInt)
  | otherwise = emitCtorAlloc e name argOps

-- the residual PAP closure for an UNDER-applied constructor.  Exactly the E22
-- method-PAP shape (emitMethodPap) with the dict captures dropped: the closure cell
-- captures the k supplied arg words, its recorded arity is the RESIDUAL (arity − k)
-- so a later over-application of the PAP still splits correctly, and the lifted
-- forwarder reloads the captures and allocates the cell from (supplied ++ fresh).
-- Equivalently: emitCtorEtaClosure (the captureless k==0 eta-wrapper below)
-- generalised to k > 0 supplied args.
emitCtorPap : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> (String, LTy)
emitCtorPap e name argOps arity =
  let remaining = arity - lengthS argOps
  let lamName = "mdk_ctorpap_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitCtorPapDefine e lamName name arity (lengthS argOps)
  emitClosureAllocA e lamName remaining argOps

-- emit the under-applied-ctor PAP forwarder.  Closure cell layout: field 0 =
-- code_ptr, fields 1..S = the S supplied arg words.  The forwarder takes `remaining`
-- fresh `%argK` params, reloads the supplied words from fields 1..S, and allocates
-- the ctor cell from (supplied ++ fresh).  Side-buffer swap discipline is
-- emitCtorEtaDefine's, verbatim.
emitCtorPapDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Int -> Unit
emitCtorPapDefine e lamName name arity supplied =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let remaining = arity - supplied
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls remaining}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let suppliedOps = loadPapCaptures e supplied 0
  let (w, _) = emitCtorAlloc e name (suppliedOps ++ etaArgOps remaining 0)
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ w)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- a CONSTRUCTOR with arity>0 referenced as a FIRST-CLASS value (`map PVar vars`):
-- emitApp only builds the cell when the ctor is SATURATED (via emitCtorAlloc — see
-- emitCtorApp above, which now ENFORCES that), so a bare ctor name reaches emitVar.
-- Eta-wrap it into a captureless static closure whose lifted forwarder allocates the
-- ctor cell from its `%argK` words — mirrors emitEtaClosure (known-fn eta) but the
-- body is emitCtorAlloc, not a call.
-- (Arity-0 ctors stay the immediate `emitCtorAlloc e name []` in emitVar — already a
-- complete value, not a function.)
emitCtorEtaClosure : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitCtorEtaClosure e name =
  let arity = ctorArity e name
  let lamName = "mdk_etac_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitCtorEtaDefine e lamName name arity
  emitClosureAlloc e lamName arity []

-- emit the captureless ctor-eta forwarder `@mdk_etacN(i64 %clos, %arg…) { <alloc
-- cell from %arg0..%argN-1>; ret }` into the side buffer (same swap discipline as
-- emitEtaDefine).
emitCtorEtaDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Unit
emitCtorEtaDefine e lamName name arity =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls arity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let (w, _) = emitCtorAlloc e name (argNames arity 0)
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ w)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- emit a lifted lambda's top-level `define` into the SIDE buffer (LLVM forbids a
-- nested define).  Swap the main buffer out, emit the define with `emit` (so it
-- lands in a private buffer), then move those lines (forward order) into the lams
-- buffer and restore the main buffer.  Captures bind by loading `%clos` fields
-- 1.., params bind to `%argK`; the body is emitted in TAIL position (it `ret`s).
-- (Gap E3: param types are per-param inferred from the body via `inferParamTys`/
-- `paramUseTy` — a Float-param lambda gets float ops; unresolved params → LTInt.)
emitLamDefine : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> Unit
emitLamDefine e env lamName pats captured body =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{closureParamDecls pats}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let cenv = loadCaptures e env captured 0
  let penv = paramEnv pats (inferParamTys (sigTable e) pats body) 0
  let _ = emitFnBody e (cenv ++ penv) lamName body
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- emit the captureless eta-forwarder `@mdk_etaN(i64 %clos, %arg…) { ret = call
-- @mdk_fname(%arg…); ret }` into the side buffer (same swap discipline).
emitEtaDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Unit
emitEtaDefine e lamName fname arity =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls arity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_\{fname}(\{argDecls (argNames arity 0)})"
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- gap-2 (GENERAL): a known extern of ANY family (str/num/io/char/file/hash/…)
-- referenced as a FIRST-CLASS value (point-free alias `f = stringLength`, or
-- passed to a HOF like a fold that applies the extern).  emitApp lowers an extern
-- only when SATURATED (via emitExternApplied); a bare name reaches emitVar.  Eta-
-- wrap it into a captureless static closure whose lifted body REPLAYS
-- emitExternApplied's applied-form codegen on the incoming `%argK` operands, so
-- the value form is byte-consistent with the applied form for every family —
-- including INTRINSICS with no C symbol (e.g. `stringLength`, which inlines a load
-- of the cached codepoint count) since we reuse the exact applied emitter.  Arity
-- comes from the runtime sig (externArity → declSigOf, the runtime extern decls'
-- arrow count), so no per-family arity is hardcoded.
emitExternEtaClosure : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitExternEtaClosure e name =
  let lamName = "mdk_eta_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitExternEtaDefine e lamName name (externArity e name)
  emitClosureAlloc e lamName (externArity e name) []

-- Gap #50 (value position) — an interface METHOD name reaching emitVar as a bare
-- first-class value (passed to a HOF, e.g. `apply2 max 3 8`).  At a value site the
-- receiver type is monomorphic but NO route accompanies the bare CVar (routes live
-- on CMethod, not CVar), so dispatch must fall to the runtime arg-tag path.  Eta-
-- wrap into a captureless closure whose lifted body REPLAYS emitMethodArgDispatch's
-- applied-form codegen on the incoming `%argK` operands — byte-consistent with the
-- saturated `isImplMethod` call site (emitApp).  Arity = methodArityOf.  (A method
-- reached point-free under a `=>` CONSTRAINT instead becomes a fn-bind with a dict
-- param and is eta-saturated at the define by etaSaturateMethodBody.)
emitMethodEtaClosure : Emit -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitMethodEtaClosure e name =
  let lamName = "mdk_eta_\{name}_\{intToString (freshId e)}"
  let _ = emitMethodEtaDefine e lamName name (methodArityOf name)
  emitClosureAlloc e lamName (methodArityOf name) []

-- the captureless method-eta forwarder `@mdk_etaN(i64 %clos, %arg…) { <inline
-- arg-tag dispatch over %arg0..%arg(N-1)>; ret }` (mirror of emitExternEtaDefine).
emitMethodEtaDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Unit
emitMethodEtaDefine e lamName name arity =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls arity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let synArgs = etaArgOps arity 0
  let (r, _) = emitMethodArgDispatch e name synArgs
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- the declared arity of extern `name` = the arrow count of its runtime signature
-- (`declSigOf` is seeded from the runtime extern decls via declSigTypeNames).  An
-- extern with no recorded signature defaults to arity 1 (every current extern is
-- annotated, so this is a defensive fallback, not a live path).
externArity : Emit -> String -> Int
externArity _ name = match declSigOf name
  Some (ptys, _) => lengthS ptys
  None => 1

-- emit the captureless extern-eta forwarder `@mdk_etaN(i64 %clos, %arg…) {
-- <inline applied-extern body over %arg0..%arg(N-1)>; ret }` into the side buffer
-- (same swap discipline as emitEtaDefine).  The body is produced by calling
-- emitExternApplied with synthetic `__eta_argK__` CVars bound to the `%argK`
-- regs — reuses the EXACT applied-form codegen, guaranteeing value/applied
-- consistency across all families.
emitExternEtaDefine : Emit -> String -> String -> Int -> Unit
emitExternEtaDefine e lamName name arity =
  let saved = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (bufRef e) []
  let scope = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{lamName}(\{etaParamDecls arity}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let argEnv = etaArgEnv arity 0
  let synArgs = etaArgVars arity 0
  let (r, _) = emitExternApplied e argEnv name synArgs
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  let _ = emit e ""
  let lamLines = (bufRef e).value
  let _ = setRef (lamsRef e) (lamLines ++ (lamsRef e).value)
  let _ = endDefine e scope
  setRef (bufRef e) saved

-- env binding `__eta_argK__ -> (%argK, LTInt)` for K in [i, arity) — so the
-- replayed applied-form emitter resolves each synthetic arg to its `%argK` reg.
etaArgEnv : Int -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
etaArgEnv n i =
  if i >= n then
    []
  else
    ("__eta_arg" ++ intToString i ++ "__", ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) :: etaArgEnv n (i + 1)

-- the synthetic argument exprs `CVar "__eta_argK__" AGlobal` for K in [i, arity).
etaArgVars : Int -> Int -> List CExpr
etaArgVars n i =
  if i >= n then
    []
  else
    CVar ("__eta_arg" ++ intToString i ++ "__") AGlobal :: etaArgVars n (i + 1)

-- the eta-define's `%arg0..%arg(n-1)` parameter OPERAND words (as raw register
-- strings), for an eta body that consumes operands directly (emitMethodArgDispatch)
-- rather than re-emitting CVars through an env.
etaArgOps : Int -> Int -> List String
etaArgOps n i =
  if i >= n then
    []
  else
    "%arg" ++ intToString i :: etaArgOps n (i + 1)

closureParamDecls : List Pat -> String
closureParamDecls [] = "i64 %clos"
closureParamDecls pats = "i64 %clos, " ++ paramDecls pats 0

-- the eta-forwarder's parameter list: `%clos` then `arity` `%argK`s.
etaParamDecls : Int -> String
etaParamDecls n = "i64 %clos" ++ etaArgDecls n 0

etaArgDecls : Int -> Int -> String
etaArgDecls n i =
  if i >= n then
    ""
  else
    ", i64 %arg\{intToString i}\{etaArgDecls n (i + 1)}"

-- the `%argK` operand names 0..n-1 (the eta-forwarder's call arguments).
argNames : Int -> Int -> List String
argNames n i =
  if i >= n then
    []
  else
    "%arg" ++ intToString i :: argNames n (i + 1)

-- bind each captured variable by loading `%clos` field i+1 (field 0 is code_ptr).
loadCaptures : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
loadCaptures _ _ [] _ = []
loadCaptures e env (c::rest) i =
  let r = loadField e "%clos" (i + 1)
  (c, (r, capTypeOf env c)) :: loadCaptures e env rest (i + 1)

-- a captured FLOAT is stored BOXED in the closure cell (capWords/lookupVarG boxes
-- an unboxed LTFloatU on escape), so bind it LTFloat inside the lifted body —
-- matching emitLit's boxed-float convention, so arith (the Win-12 one-float-operand
-- path), print, and list ops all unbox it correctly.  Every OTHER capture stays
-- LTInt (the uniform-i64 default), so non-float captures are unaffected → zero
-- regression surface.  Fixes the closure-float-capture arith soundness bug
-- (`map (x => x * scale)` with a captured float `scale`, `(z => z + k)` etc., which
-- previously did INTEGER arith on boxed-float pointers → garbage).
