capTypeOf : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> LTy
capTypeOf env c = match lookupAssoc c env
  Some (_, LTFloat) => LTFloat
  Some (_, LTFloatU) => LTFloat
  _ => LTInt

-- the live operand words for a captured-name list (looked up in the emit env).
-- Routed through lookupVarG (Mut) so an LTFloatU let-bound float is BOXED to an
-- i64 word before being stored into a closure capture slot (escape coercion) —
-- the closure ABI is uniform i64, and loadCaptures reads captures back as words.
capWords : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List String
capWords _ _ [] = []
capWords e env (c::rest) =
  let (op, _) = lookupVarG e env c
  op :: capWords e env rest

-- keep only the names present as keys in the emit env (the capturable locals);
-- drop free names that resolve to top-level globals (known fns / ctors).
keepInEnv : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List String
keepInEnv _ [] = []
keepInEnv env (k::rest) =
  if envHasKey env k then
    k :: keepInEnv env rest
  else
    keepInEnv env rest

envHasKey : List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> Bool
envHasKey [] _ = False
envHasKey ((n, _)::rest) k = if n == k then True else envHasKey rest k

dedupS : List String -> List String
dedupS [] = []
dedupS (x::xs) = if contains x xs then dedupS xs else x :: dedupS xs

-- ── ADT constructors + pattern matching (slice 3) ───────────────────────────
-- VALUE REPRESENTATION (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §8.4 "Option A", extended from the
-- slice-1 Float box): an ADT value is a BOXED heap cell with a one-word header.
--   cell layout (8-byte words):  [ 0: i64 tag ] [ 8: field0 ] [ 16: field1 ] …
-- the tag is the constructor's COMPOSITE (typeId, ordinal) — see `cellTag` (computed
-- identically at the alloc site and at every switch test, so the two always agree); the word
-- carried in registers is the cell pointer (`ptrtoint`, low bit 0 — disjoint from
-- the immediate-int low-bit-1 encoding, exactly as the Float box is).  Allocation
-- reuses the slice-1 `@mdk_alloc` (malloc-and-leak; no GC this slice).

-- `hashName` (djb2, seed 5381) lives in `backend.private_mangle`, shared with the
-- WasmGC emitter so the dict-witness tag agrees across backends.  STILL used here
-- for the NON-ctor sentinel headers ("$tuple"/"$ref"/"$closure") and dict witness
-- words; constructor cell tags use `cellTag` (ordinals) instead.

-- ── constructor tag = dense per-type ordinal (RUNTIME-DESIGN §8.4, ratified ──────
--    2026-06-07; supersedes the spike's i64 string-hash for CONSTRUCTOR tags).
-- A constructor C of type T is tagged with its 0-based index in T's
-- declaration-ordered constructor list (the ratified value — `br_table`-ready and
-- collision-free).  But the spike's ARG-TAG dispatch (emitTagMatch) discriminates a
-- value's TYPE by loading its cell tag and OR-ing it against a candidate type's
-- ctor tags — and per-type ordinals COLLIDE across types (every type has an
-- ordinal-0 ctor), so a bare ordinal cannot identify the type.  The one-word header
-- therefore carries a COMPOSITE: a dense per-type id in the high 32 bits and the
-- ratified per-type ctor ordinal in the low 32 bits.
--   • match-head test (within ONE statically-known type) and `br_table` use the low
--     half — `tag & 0xffffffff` is the dense 0-based ordinal.
--   • the spike's arg-tag dispatch uses the FULL composite, which is globally unique
--     (distinct type ⇒ distinct high half), so cross-type discrimination is exact.
-- The high half is spike scaffolding for that runtime dispatch; the real backend
-- resolves such call sites statically / by dictionary and keeps only the low-half
-- ordinal.  hashName is gone from every constructor tag — collisions are impossible.
-- 2^32, the type-id shift.  (Int is 63-bit; type-id × 2^32 + ordinal stays well
-- under 2^62 for any real program.)
ctorTagShift : Int
ctorTagShift = 4294967296

-- the cell-header tag a NAME stamps / is tested against.  Sentinel headers
-- ("$tuple"/"$ref"/"$closure") keep their hash; every constructor (incl. the
-- built-in list Cons/Nil and a user ctor that owns those names) gets the composite
-- (typeId, ordinal).  Alloc and every tag test route through THIS one function, so
-- the stored tag and the compared tag are identical by construction.
-- RESERVED ADT-TAG BLOCK (slice 10 precursor).  The runtime ADTs — List, Option,
-- Result, Ordering — get FIXED composite tags, far above any real program's dense
-- type-id range, so a C extern can construct these cells (mdk_some/mdk_cons/… in
-- runtime/medaka_rt.c) with a tag a later `match` agrees with.  The driver
-- (llvm_emit_main.mdk) does NOT load the prelude, so a prelude-free fixture using
-- `Some`/`None`/`Ok`/… has them ABSENT from the ctor→type table — without this
-- reservation `Some` and `Cons` both fall to the "one past" type-id with ordinal 0
-- and COLLIDE.  Both alloc and every tag test route through `cellTag`, so reserving
-- here keeps construction and the match-head test consistent by construction.
-- The C side hardcodes the SAME constants (MDK_TAG_* in runtime/medaka_rt.c); keep
-- the two in sync.  Ordinals follow core.mdk: Option = Some|None, Result = Ok|Err,
-- Ordering = Lt|Eq|Gt; List (synthetic) = Cons|Nil.  #361: the comment that used to
-- stand here called a user type reusing one of these names a "KNOWN SPIKE LIMITATION"
-- that "the real backend resolves statically" — as if this emitter were the spike and
-- some other, more careful backend existed downstream. THIS is the real (and only)
-- backend; there is no other one to defer to. The actual guard is upstream, in the
-- RESOLVER: `data Foo = Ok Int | Bad` is rejected before it ever reaches this file
-- (`dupErr "constructor"`, resolve.mdk:1422, message "Duplicate constructor: Ok" —
-- probed). So a user ctor can never actually alias `Cons`/`Some`/`None`/`Ok`/`Err`/
-- `Lt`/`Eq`/`Gt` by the time `reservedTag` runs; this function's fixed-tag table is
-- safe because that invariant is enforced elsewhere, not because collisions here are
-- merely "consistent." If that resolver check is ever removed, THIS is the place a
-- silent tag collision would resurface — worth an emit-time assertion, not just a
-- comment (EMITTER-SEMANTICS M2).
reservedTypeBase : Int
reservedTypeBase = 65536

reservedTag : String -> Option Int
reservedTag name =
  if name == "Cons" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 0) * ctorTagShift + 0)
  else if name == "Nil" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 0) * ctorTagShift + 1)
  else if name == "Some" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 1) * ctorTagShift + 0)
  else if name == "None" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 1) * ctorTagShift + 1)
  else if name == "Ok" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 2) * ctorTagShift + 0)
  else if name == "Err" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 2) * ctorTagShift + 1)
  else if name == "Lt" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 3) * ctorTagShift + 0)
  else if name == "Eq" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 3) * ctorTagShift + 1)
  else if name == "Gt" then
    Some ((reservedTypeBase + 3) * ctorTagShift + 2)
  else
    None

-- the cell-header tag a NAME stamps / is tested against.  Sentinel headers
-- ("$tuple"/"$ref"/"$closure") keep their hash; the runtime ADTs route through
-- `reservedTag` (fixed, program-stable); every other constructor gets the composite
-- (typeId, ordinal).  Alloc and every tag test route through THIS one function, so
-- the stored tag and the compared tag are identical by construction.
-- Bool immediates are hardcoded by emitVar/emitLit: True→word 3 (ashr1→tag 1),
-- False→word 1 (ashr1→tag 0).  Special-case before the ctor-table lookup so
-- construction and discrimination agree regardless of whether Bool is in the table.
cellTag : Emit -> String -> Int
cellTag e name =
  if name == "True" then 1
  else
    if name == "False" then 0
    else
      if contains name ["$tuple", "$ref", "$closure"] then hashName name
      else match reservedTag name
        Some t => t
        None => ctorTypeId e name * ctorTagShift + ctorOrdinal e name

-- the 0-based index of constructor `name` in its type's declaration-ordered ctor
-- list.  The built-in list Cons/Nil are not user-declared (absent from the
-- ctor→type table) → reserved ordinals 0/1 of the synthetic list type.
ctorOrdinal : Emit -> String -> Int
ctorOrdinal e name = match lookupAssoc name (ctorTypeTable e)
  Some ty => orZero (indexOfStr name (ctorsOfType e ty))
  None => if name == "Nil" then 1 else 0

-- the dense per-type id of the type that owns constructor `name`: its index among
-- the program's distinct type names (declaration order).  The built-in list (and
-- any name not in the ctor→type table) gets a reserved id one past the user types,
-- so its composite tags can never collide with a user type's.
ctorTypeId : Emit -> String -> Int
ctorTypeId e name = match lookupAssoc name (ctorTypeTable e)
  Some ty => orZero (indexOfStr ty (distinctTypeNames e))
  None => lengthS (distinctTypeNames e)

-- the distinct type names of the program, in declaration order (the ctor→type
-- table lists each type's ctors consecutively, so dedup-preserving-order gives the
-- type declaration order).
-- `distinctTypeNames` is constant during a program's emission (the ctor→type
-- table is set once), but `ctorTypeId` calls it per ADT-constructor emission and
-- it recomputes the O(n²) `nubStr` each time. Memoize the result (a plain
-- List String — no hash container, so it self-compiles cleanly) in a module-level
-- ref, reset to None at each emitProgram/emitProgramGaps so batched emits recompute.
distinctTypeNamesRef : Ref (Option (List String))
distinctTypeNamesRef = Ref None

resetDistinctTypeNames : Unit -> Unit
resetDistinctTypeNames _ = setRef distinctTypeNamesRef None

distinctTypeNames : Emit -> List String
distinctTypeNames e = match distinctTypeNamesRef.value
  Some xs => xs
  None =>
    let xs = nubStr (typeNamesOf (ctorTypeTable e))
    let _ = setRef distinctTypeNamesRef (Some xs)
    xs

typeNamesOf : List (String, String) -> List String
typeNamesOf [] = []
typeNamesOf ((_, t)::rest) = t :: typeNamesOf rest

orZero : Option Int -> Int
orZero (Some i) = i
orZero None = 0

-- dedup a list of strings, preserving first-occurrence order.
nubStr : List String -> List String
nubStr xs = nubStrAcc xs []

nubStrAcc : List String -> List String -> List String
nubStrAcc [] _ = []
nubStrAcc (x::xs) seen =
  if contains x seen then
    nubStrAcc xs seen
  else
    x :: nubStrAcc xs (x::seen)

-- allocate a constructor cell {tag, field…}; the field words are already emitted.
-- Result is the cell pointer as an i64 word, typed LTCon.  A NULLARY constructor
-- (RUNTIME-DESIGN §8.1 / §8.4 decision 1: nullary ctors are immediate/free) does
-- NOT allocate — it is the IMMEDIATE word `(cellTag<<1)|1`, carrying the SAME
-- composite (typeId, ordinal) tag a boxed cell would store in its header, untaggable
-- by `ashr 1`.  This generalises the existing Bool immediate (emitVar True→"3"/
-- False→"1") to every nullary ctor.  The constant operand emits no instructions; a
-- `match` recovers the tag via `loadDiscriminant` (low-bit branch), so construction
-- and the match-head test agree on `cellTag` whether the value is immediate or boxed.
emitCtorAlloc : Emit -> String -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitCtorAlloc e name [] = (intToString (cellTag e name * 2 + 1), LTCon)
emitCtorAlloc e name fields =
  let arity = lengthS fields
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (arity + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString (cellTag e name)}, ptr \{p}"
  let _ = storeFields e p fields 0
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTCon)

-- store each field at offset 8*(i+1) (field 0 follows the one-word header).
storeFields : Emit -> String -> List String -> Int -> Unit
storeFields _ _ [] _ = ()
storeFields e p (f::rest) i =
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{intToString (8 * (i + 1))}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{f}, ptr \{fp}"
  storeFields e p rest (i + 1)

-- load a cell's tag (header word at offset 0) given the cell's pointer word.
loadTag : Emit -> String -> String
loadTag e ptrWord =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{ptrWord} to ptr"
  let t = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{t} = load i64, ptr \{p}"
  t

-- the DISCRIMINANT word a constructor `match` head (and arg-tag dispatch) tests.
-- A value of an ADT type is EITHER an immediate nullary ctor (low bit 1 — its
-- composite (typeId, ordinal) tag rides the payload, recovered by `ashr 1`) OR a
-- boxed pointer to a cell whose header word IS that same composite tag (low bit 0 —
-- `loadTag` it).  A type that mixes the two (e.g. Option = None immediate + Some
-- boxed) means a single scrutinee can be either, so branch on the low bit BEFORE
-- choosing — an immediate must NEVER be dereferenced (`loadTag` on an odd word
-- inttoptr's to a misaligned address and segfaults).  Both arms phi to ONE tag word
-- equal to the `cellTag` the alloc site stamps, so emitConChain/emitTagMatch compare
-- against `cellTag` UNCHANGED: immediate and boxed reps are tag-comparable by
-- construction (RUNTIME-DESIGN §8.1).  loadTag stays the raw boxed-cell load — used
-- directly only where the value is known boxed (array length header).
loadDiscriminant : Emit -> String -> String
loadDiscriminant e word =
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let imm = "discimm" ++ n
  let box = "discbox" ++ n
  let cont = "disccont" ++ n
  let lb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{lb} = and i64 \{word}, 1"
  let isImm = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{isImm} = icmp eq i64 \{lb}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{isImm}, label %\{imm}, label %\{box}"
  let _ = emit e (imm ++ ":")
  let iv = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv} = ashr i64 \{word}, 1"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ cont)
  let _ = emit e (box ++ ":")
  let bv = loadTag e word
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ cont)
  let _ = emit e (cont ++ ":")
  let d = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{d} = phi i64 [ \{iv}, %\{imm} ], [ \{bv}, %\{box} ]"
  d

-- load field `idx` (offset 8*(idx+1)) of a cell given its pointer word.
loadField : Emit -> String -> Int -> String
loadField e ptrWord idx =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{ptrWord} to ptr"
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{intToString (8 * (idx + 1))}"
  let f = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{f} = load i64, ptr \{fp}"
  f

-- load the first `a` fields of a cell as a list of operand words.
loadFields : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> List String
loadFields e foc a i
  | i >= a = []
  | otherwise =
    let f = loadField e foc i
    f :: loadFields e foc a (i + 1)

-- a `match` lowered to a decision tree (CDecision).  Mirrors core_ir_eval's
-- cevalDecision exactly, but emits an LLVM CFG instead of walking values: the
-- scrutinee word, an `alloca` result slot, the tree (which routes control via
-- br/icmp to the selected arm, where the arm stores its body value into the slot
-- and branches to the end label), then a load of the slot in the end block.  The
-- arm's static result type is captured into `rty` as the arm body is emitted (all
-- arms agree — well-typed match).
emitDecision : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> List CArm -> CTree -> (String, LTy)
emitDecision e env scrut arms tree =
  let (sv, _) = emitExpr e env scrut
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "decend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let rty = Ref LTInt
  let saved = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
  let savedTrmc = trmcCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef trmcCtxRef TrmcOff
  let savedGDisp = gDispCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef gDispCtxRef GDispOff
  let _ = emitTree e env [sv] arms [sv] slot endL rty tree
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef saved
  let _ = setRef trmcCtxRef savedTrmc
  let _ = setRef gDispCtxRef savedGDisp
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  (r, rty.value)
-- A `match` in expression position is self-contained: its own non-exhaustive
-- CTFail is a genuine abort, NOT the enclosing clause-chain's fallthrough.  Save
-- + null the fallthrough label across the tree so a body-level match emitted
-- inside a chained clause leaf doesn't inherit (and wrongly branch to) the
-- next-clause block.  Restored after.

-- Likewise self-contained for TRMC: an expression-position `match`'s arms
-- compute VALUES into `slot`, never cons-into-dest — so clear the TRMC ctx
-- across the tree (a tail cons in a match arm is Phase 2, deliberately not here).

-- walk the compiled CTree, emitting basic blocks.  `root` is the whole scrutinee
-- word (kept for the leaf re-match that recovers bindings — mirrors ceval's
-- `root`); `occs` is the live column words (the matrix columns), starting as just
-- [root] and expanding into a constructor's fields as the tree descends.  Every
-- path terminates its current block (a `br` to `endL` after storing, a `br` into
-- a sibling test block, or `unreachable` for CTFail).
emitTree : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> List String -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> CTree -> Unit
-- CTFail: the matrix path matched no row.  In a CHAINED single-clause tree
-- (emitClauseChain) this is "this clause's pattern didn't match" → resume the next
-- clause via the fallthrough label; with no next clause (`""`) it is a genuine
-- non-exhaustive-match abort.  A bare `unreachable` here was UB (LLVM `ud2` → a
-- silent SIGTRAP, no output); call the noreturn @mdk_nonexhaustive_match (prints
-- `runtime error [E-NONEXHAUSTIVE-MATCH]` to stderr, exits nonzero — the
-- interpreter's message MINUS `file:L:C:`, Core IR carrying no loc) before the
-- `unreachable`, mirroring the div-by-zero trap.  (The GUARD-chain half of "try the
-- next clause" is NOT this arm — that is the `__fallthrough__` sentinel, lowered by
-- emitFallthrough from the label stamped into the node.)
emitTree e _ _ _ _ _ _ _ CTFail = match fallthroughLabelRef.value
  "" =>
    let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_nonexhaustive_match()"
    emit e "  unreachable"
  lbl => emit e ("  br label %" ++ lbl)
emitTree e env roots arms _ slot endL rty (CTLeaf i) =
  emitLeaf e env roots arms i slot endL rty
-- CTGuard i fail: arm i matched but is GUARDED.  Re-bind its pattern (like a
-- leaf), evaluate its guards; all pass → eval the body (emitLeaf-style); any
-- guard fails → branch to the `fail` subtree, which resumes the ordered match at
-- the same column context `occs` (mirrors core_ir_eval's `cevalGuardedBody`).
-- This CLOSES the historical CTGuard emitter gap — `compiler/` code on the native
-- self-compile path no longer needs the guard-free convention for `CGBool`
-- guards.  A refutable `p <- e` pattern guard stays a contained gap (not produced
-- by current source); a bad arm index likewise.
emitTree e env roots arms occs slot endL rty (CTGuard i fail) = match nthArm arms i
  Some (CArm pat guards body) => if allGuardsEmittable guards then emitGuardedArm e env roots arms occs pat guards body fail slot endL rty
  else
    let _ = gapU "refutable pattern guard (`p <- e`) in a match arm (CTGuard) — not yet lowered"
    let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ slot)
    emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  None =>
    let _ = gapU "guarded arm index out of range (CTGuard)"
    let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ slot)
    emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
emitTree e env roots arms occs slot endL rty (CTDrop sub) =
  emitTree e env roots arms (tailS occs) slot endL rty sub
emitTree e env roots arms occs slot endL rty (CTSwitch branches dft) =
  emitSwitch e env roots arms occs branches dft slot endL rty

-- a terminal leaf: re-match the selected arm's ORIGINAL pattern against the leaf's
-- root word(s) — `roots` (the tree path guarantees it matches) to bind its
-- variables — emitting getelementptr loads for each field a `PVar` deconstructs —
-- then emit the body, store the result, and branch to the end block.
emitLeaf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> Int -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> Unit
emitLeaf e env roots arms i slot endL rty = match nthArm arms i
  Some (CArm pat _ body) =>
    let env2 = bindArm e env pat roots body
    match gDispCtxRef.value
      GDispOn _ _ _ _ _ => emitGDispBody e env2 body
      GDispOff => match trmcCtxRef.value
        TrmcOn _ _ _ _ _ _ => emitTrmcBody e env2 body
        TrmcOff =>
          let (bv, bty) = emitExpr e env2 body
          let _ = setRef rty bty
          let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{bv}, ptr \{slot}"
          emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  None => gapU "decision arm index out of range"
-- TRMC (TRMC-DESIGN.md): when a destination-passing loop is active (multi-clause
-- eligible fn — emitClauseTree's clause-selection tree), the clause body is a
-- TAIL leaf → descend it via emitTrmcBody (cons-into-dest+loop / store+exit)
-- instead of computing a value into `slot`.  trmcCtxRef is cleared inside any
-- expression-position `match` (emitDecision), so a nested match's arms still
-- compute values normally.

-- a guarded clause: re-match the arm pattern against `root` (a RANGE/`PRng` arm
-- canonicalizes to PWild in the matrix, so the tree routed here unconditionally
-- and the actual range comparison happens HERE — `emitRefutMatch` branches to
-- `failL` on no-match; an irrefutable pattern emits no test, preserving the
-- plain-`if`-guard case), run the guards (branching to `failL` on any failure),
-- and on all-pass eval the body into `slot`; then emit `failL` and the `fail`
-- subtree (resumes the match at the same column context `occs`).
emitGuardedArm : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> List String -> Pat -> List CGuard -> CExpr -> CTree -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> Unit
emitGuardedArm e env roots arms occs pat guards body fail slot endL rty =
  let failL = "guardfail" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let env2 = emitRefutArm e env pat roots body failL
  let env3 = emitGuardChain e env2 guards body failL
  -- Under an ACTIVE TRMC loop the guarded arm's body is a TAIL leaf: descend it
  -- via emitTrmcBody (cons-into-dest+loop / plain-tail+loop / base+exit), exactly
  -- like emitLeaf's TrmcOn branch.  Value-emitting it here instead stored into
  -- `slot` and branched to the TAIL match's DEAD end block (`trmcdecend:` … loads
  -- then `unreachable`) — runtime UB, a SILENT MISCOMPILE (native returned `[]`/0
  -- where the interpreter and WasmGC returned the right list; wasm's tail engine
  -- always handled guards).  Fixture: test/stack_fixtures/guard_arm_trmc.mdk.
  let _ = match gDispCtxRef.value
    GDispOn _ _ _ _ _ => emitGDispBody e env3 body
    GDispOff => match trmcCtxRef.value
      TrmcOn _ _ _ _ _ _ => emitTrmcBody e env3 body
      TrmcOff =>
        let (bv, bty) = emitExpr e env3 body
        let _ = setRef rty bty
        let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{bv}, ptr \{slot}"
        emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)
  let _ = emit e (failL ++ ":")
  emitTree e env roots arms occs slot endL rty fail

-- emit the guard tests left-to-right: a `CGBool` tests a Bool (`True` = the word
-- 3) and branches to `failL` on failure, falling through on success; a
-- `CGBind p <- e` evaluates `e` and refutably matches `p` against it via
-- `emitRefutMatch` (an irrefutable `p` emits no test + just binds; a refutable
-- `Just x <- e` tests the discriminant and branches to `failL` on no-match — G5).
-- Returns the env extended by any `CGBind` bindings (visible to later guards +
-- the body).
emitGuardChain : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CGuard -> CExpr -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitGuardChain _ env [] _ _ = env
emitGuardChain e env ((CGBool g)::rest) body failL =
  let (bw, _) = emitExpr e env g
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{bw}, 3"
  let pass = "guardok" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{pass}, label %\{failL}"
  let _ = emit e (pass ++ ":")
  emitGuardChain e env rest body failL
emitGuardChain e env ((CGBind p ex)::rest) body failL =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  let env2 = emitRefutMatch e env p v body failL
  emitGuardChain e env2 rest body failL

-- a clause's guards are now ALL emittable: `CGBool` guards always were; a
-- `CGBind p <- e` is lowered via `emitRefutMatch` whether `p` is irrefutable
-- (just binds) or refutable (`Just x <- e` — runtime discriminant test, G5).
allGuardsEmittable : List CGuard -> Bool
allGuardsEmittable [] = True
allGuardsEmittable ((CGBool _)::rest) = allGuardsEmittable rest
allGuardsEmittable ((CGBind _ _)::rest) = allGuardsEmittable rest

-- ── arm binding against the leaf's ROOT WORD(S) ─────────────────────────────
-- ONE root is the ordinary single scrutinee (a `match` expression, or an arity-1
-- clause chain): re-match the arm pattern against it, exactly as before.
--
-- SEVERAL roots are the un-boxed PARAMETER WORDS of an arity-≥2 clause chain
-- (emitClauseTree).  There, `clauseArm` wrapped the clause's param patterns in a
-- synthetic `PTuple` only because `CArm` carries a single `Pat` — there is NO
-- tuple cell at runtime.  So bind element-wise: pattern i against param word i.
-- This is what retires the per-call argument-tuple `@mdk_alloc`.
bindArm : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindArm e env pat [v] body = bindPattern e env pat v body
bindArm e env (PTuple ps) roots body = bindEach e env ps roots body
bindArm e env pat (v::_) body = bindPattern e env pat v body
bindArm _ env _ [] _ = env

-- bind each pattern against its OWN word — no cell, hence no `loadField`.  The
-- multi-root counterpart of `bindFields` (which loads field i out of one cell).
bindEach : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindEach e env (p::ps) (v::vs) body =
  let env2 = bindPattern e env p v body
  bindEach e env2 ps vs body
bindEach _ env _ _ _ = env

-- the refutable counterpart of `bindArm` (guarded / range-pattern clause): test +
-- bind each param pattern against its own word, branching to `failL` on a miss.
emitRefutArm : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitRefutArm e env pat [v] body failL = emitRefutMatch e env pat v body failL
emitRefutArm e env (PTuple ps) roots body failL =
  emitRefutEach e env ps roots body failL
emitRefutArm e env pat (v::_) body failL = emitRefutMatch e env pat v body failL
emitRefutArm _ env _ [] _ _ = env

emitRefutEach : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> List String -> CExpr -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitRefutEach e env (p::ps) (v::vs) body failL =
  let env2 = emitRefutMatch e env p v body failL
  emitRefutEach e env2 ps vs body failL
emitRefutEach _ env _ _ _ _ = env

-- bind a pattern's variables against a value word, emitting field loads for the
-- constructor sub-patterns.  Mirrors the binding half of eval.mdk's matchPat, but
-- since the decision tree already proved the shape, it only EXTRACTS (no tests).
-- A `PVar`'s static type is recovered from its first typed use in the arm body
-- (reusing the slice-2b `paramUseTy`); pointer-typed fields default to Int, which
-- is harmless — they only ever flow on as LTy-agnostic call args.
bindPattern : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> String -> CExpr -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindPattern _ env PWild _ _ = env
bindPattern _ env (PLit _) _ _ = env
bindPattern e env (PVar x) v body = bindVarTy e env x v body ""
-- A constructor pattern binds each field with the field's DECLARED type (Gap E2):
-- a scalar declared field type (esp. Float/Bool) types the bound var directly,
-- so `match (Rect w h) { … w * h }` does FLOAT arith on the unboxed fields rather
-- than guessing LTInt from body-use.  Non-scalar / unknown fields fall back to the
-- paramUseTy guess (declared name "" passed through bindFieldsDecl).
bindPattern e env (PCon c args) v body =
  bindFieldsDecl e env args (ctorFieldTypeNamesOf c) v body 0
bindPattern e env (PTuple ps) v body = bindFields e env ps v body 0
-- built-in list patterns (slice 5b): a cons cell is a 2-field "Cons" constructor
-- (head = field 0, tail = field 1); the empty-list pattern (a "Nil" cell) binds
-- nothing.  The decision tree already proved the shape (HCons/HNil switch), so
-- this only EXTRACTS — same as the PCon binding path, with [head, tail] fields.
bindPattern e env (PCons h t) v body = bindFields e env [h, t] v body 0
bindPattern _ env (PList []) _ _ = env
-- as-pattern `x@p`: bind `x` to the WHOLE value v (same LTy lookup as PVar),
-- then descend into `p` so its inner vars also bind.
bindPattern e env (PAs x p) v body =
  let env2 = bindVarTy e env x v body ""
  bindPattern e env2 p v body
-- non-empty list-literal pattern `[p::ps]`: v is a Cons cell (the switch proved
-- it); bind the head element (field 0) to `p` and the tail (field 1) to `PList ps`.
bindPattern e env (PList (p::ps)) v body =
  bindFields e env [p, PList ps] v body 0
bindPattern _ env p _ _ =
  gapEnv
    ("unsupported pattern in match arm (slice 3/5b: variable / wildcard / constructor / tuple / cons / nil / int-literal only): " ++ ptag p)
    env

-- bind variable `x` to word `v`.  Its LTy is the DECLARED field type-head name
-- `declName` when that is a known scalar (Gap E2), else the paramUseTy guess from
-- the arm body, else LTInt — so the declared-type override is additive.
bindVarTy : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> CExpr -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindVarTy e env x v body declName =
  let ty = match fieldNameToLTy declName
    Some t => t
    None => match paramUseTy (sigTable e) [] x body
      Some t => t
      None => LTInt
  (x, (v, ty))::env

bindFields : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> String -> CExpr -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindFields _ env [] _ _ _ = env
bindFields e env (p::rest) v body i =
  let f = loadField e v i
  let env2 = bindPattern e env p f body
  bindFields e env2 rest v body (i + 1)

-- like bindFields, but pairs each field pattern with its constructor's DECLARED
-- field type-head name (Gap E2).  A bare PVar/PAs field uses the declared scalar
-- type when known; everything else (nested patterns, unknown/non-scalar fields,
-- missing declared names) falls through to the paramUseTy guess.
bindFieldsDecl : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> List String -> String -> CExpr -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindFieldsDecl _ env [] _ _ _ _ = env
bindFieldsDecl e env (p::rest) declNames v body i =
  let f = loadField e v i
  let declName = nthOr declNames i ""
  let env2 = bindFieldDecl e env p declName f body
  bindFieldsDecl e env2 rest declNames v body (i + 1)

-- bind one constructor field: a bare PVar/PAs uses its declared scalar type, any
-- other pattern recurses through bindPattern (no scalar to apply at the top).
bindFieldDecl : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> String -> String -> CExpr -> List (String, (String, LTy))
bindFieldDecl e env (PVar x) declName f body = bindVarTy e env x f body declName
bindFieldDecl e env (PAs x p) declName f body =
  let env2 = bindVarTy e env x f body declName
  bindPattern e env2 p f body
bindFieldDecl e env p _ f body = bindPattern e env p f body

-- ── refutable pattern match (G5 refutable pattern-guards / G6 range patterns) ──
-- Test pattern `p` against value word `v`, branching to `failL` on a MISMATCH;
-- on a match, bind `p`'s variables and FALL THROUGH, returning the extended env
-- (visible to later guards + the body).  Unlike `bindPattern` (which only
-- EXTRACTS at a tree leaf the decision tree already proved), this emits the
-- runtime TEST too — for the two sites the decision tree can't pre-test: a
-- refutable `p <- e` pattern guard (CGBind) and a range/`PRng` arm pattern
-- (which canonicalizes to PWild in the matrix, so the tree routes to it
-- unconditionally and the guarded-arm re-match must do the comparison — exactly
-- mirroring core_ir_eval's `cevalGuardedBody` re-running `matchPat`).
--   Irrefutable patterns (PWild/PVar/PAs/PTuple) emit NO test (reuse
-- bindPattern's extraction); refutable ones (PLit/PRng/PCon/PCons/PList) emit a
-- discriminant/range/equality test + a `br … failL`.  An unsupported shape is a
-- gap (records, nested-refutable beyond this set).
emitRefutMatch : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Pat -> String -> CExpr -> String -> List (String, (String, LTy))
-- irrefutable: no test, just bind (delegates to bindPattern's extraction).
emitRefutMatch e env PWild v body _ = bindPattern e env PWild v body
emitRefutMatch e env (PVar x) v body _ = bindPattern e env (PVar x) v body
emitRefutMatch e env (PAs x p) v body failL =
  let env2 = bindVarTy e env x v body ""
  emitRefutMatch e env2 p v body failL
-- a tuple is irrefutable in SHAPE (the type fixes the arity), but its element
-- patterns can be refutable — recurse element-wise on the loaded fields.
emitRefutMatch e env (PTuple ps) v body failL =
  emitRefutFields e env ps v body failL 0
-- integer / char literal: compare the tagged immediate word directly (both are
-- `n*2+1`); String literal via @mdk_string_eq (tagged Bool result); Bool literal
-- compares the immediate (3/1).
emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LInt k)) v _ failL =
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e v (intToString (k * 2 + 1)) failL
  env
emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LChar c)) v _ failL =
  let cp = charCode (arrayGetUnsafe 0 (stringToChars c))
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e v (intToString (cp * 2 + 1)) failL
  env
emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LBool b)) v _ failL =
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e v (if b then "3" else "1") failL
  env
emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LString s)) v _ failL =
  let (lit, _) = emitLit e (LString s)
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_eq(i64 \{v}, i64 \{lit})"
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e r "3" failL
  env
-- range pattern (G6): `lo <= v && v <= hi` (`..=`) or `lo <= v && v < hi` (`..`),
-- on the tagged immediate words (the `n*2+1` transform is strictly monotonic for
-- both Int and Char, so the tagged comparison matches the untagged one).  Mirrors
-- eval.mdk's inIntRange/inCharRange: incl ⇒ `<= hi`, else `<= hi-1` ≡ `< hi`.
emitRefutMatch e env (PRng lo hi incl) v _ failL =
  let _ = emitRangeTest e v (rngBound lo) (rngBound hi) incl failL
  env
-- constructor: test the discriminant against the ctor's composite tag; on
-- mismatch branch to failL, on match load fields and recurse element-wise.
emitRefutMatch e env (PCon "True" []) v body failL =
  emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LBool True)) v body failL
emitRefutMatch e env (PCon "False" []) v body failL =
  emitRefutMatch e env (PLit (LBool False)) v body failL
emitRefutMatch e env (PCon c args) v body failL =
  let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e v
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e tagReg (intToString (cellTag e c)) failL
  emitRefutFields e env args v body failL 0
-- list cons cell: test the discriminant is "Cons", then recurse on head/tail.
emitRefutMatch e env (PCons h t) v body failL =
  let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e v
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e tagReg (intToString (cellTag e "Cons")) failL
  emitRefutFields e env [h, t] v body failL 0
emitRefutMatch e env (PList []) v _ failL =
  let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e v
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e tagReg (intToString (cellTag e "Nil")) failL
  env
emitRefutMatch e env (PList (p::ps)) v body failL =
  let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e v
  let _ = emitWordEqTest e tagReg (intToString (cellTag e "Cons")) failL
  emitRefutFields e env [p, PList ps] v body failL 0
emitRefutMatch e env p _ _ _ =
  gapEnv
    ("refutable pattern in guard/range arm not lowered (variable / wildcard / as / tuple / literal / range / constructor / cons / list only): " ++ ptag p)
    env

-- recurse a refutable test over a cell's leading fields (loadField per index).
emitRefutFields : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Pat -> String -> CExpr -> String -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
emitRefutFields _ env [] _ _ _ _ = env
emitRefutFields e env (p::rest) v body failL i =
  let f = loadField e v i
  let env2 = emitRefutMatch e env p f body failL
  emitRefutFields e env2 rest v body failL (i + 1)

-- emit `icmp eq word, k`; on inequality branch to failL, else fall through.
emitWordEqTest : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> Unit
emitWordEqTest e word k failL =
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{word}, \{k}"
  let ok = "refok" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{ok}, label %\{failL}"
  emit e (ok ++ ":")

-- emit a range membership test on the tagged word `v`: `v >= loWord` AND
-- (incl ? `v <= hiWord` : `v < hiWord`); any failing comparison branches to failL.
emitRangeTest : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> Bool -> String -> Unit
emitRangeTest e v lo hi incl failL =
  let loW = intToString (lo * 2 + 1)
  let hiW = intToString (hi * 2 + 1)
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let cLo = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cLo} = icmp sge i64 \{v}, \{loW}"
  let mid = "rngmid" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cLo}, label %\{mid}, label %\{failL}"
  let _ = emit e (mid ++ ":")
  let cHi = freshReg e
  let op = if incl then "sle" else "slt"
  let _ = emit e "  \{cHi} = icmp \{op} i64 \{v}, \{hiW}"
  let ok = "rngok" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cHi}, label %\{ok}, label %\{failL}"
  emit e (ok ++ ":")

-- (`rngBound` — the Int codepoint of a range bound literal — is backend-neutral and
-- now lives in backend.emit_support, shared with the WasmGC emitter.)

-- the i-th element of a list, or `dflt` if out of range.
nthOr : List String -> Int -> String -> String
nthOr [] _ dflt = dflt
nthOr (x::rest) i dflt
  | i == 0 = x
  | otherwise = nthOr rest (i - 1) dflt

-- a switch on the focus column (occs head): constructor-LIKE heads (named ADT
-- constructors HCon, built-in list HCons/HNil, and tuples HTuple) test a
-- DISCRIMINANT word (`loadDiscriminant`: an immediate nullary ctor's `ashr 1`
-- payload, or a boxed cell's loaded header — both the composite `cellTag`); int
-- literals test the immediate directly.  A homogeneous switch (the
-- matrix is either all-constructor-like or all-literal in a column), so the first
-- branch's head selects the test kind.  HCons/HNil/HTuple map to the same tag the
-- alloc site stamps (`cellTag` of "Cons"/"Nil"/"$tuple"), so a constructor
-- test reaches them with no special case beyond the name/arity (see conHeadInfo).
emitSwitch : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> List String -> List CTBranch -> CTree -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> Unit
emitSwitch e env roots arms [] branches dft slot endL rty =
  emitTree e env roots arms [] slot endL rty dft
emitSwitch e env roots arms (foc::rest) [] dft slot endL rty =
  emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty dft
emitSwitch e env roots arms (foc::rest) (branches@((CTBranch (HLit _) _)::_)) dft slot endL rty = emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest branches dft slot endL rty
emitSwitch e env roots arms (foc::rest) ((CTBranch HUnit sub)::_) dft slot endL rty = emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty sub
emitSwitch e env roots arms (foc::rest) (branches@((CTBranch h _)::_)) dft slot endL rty = match conHeadInfo h
  Some _ =>
    let tagReg = loadDiscriminant e foc
    emitConChain e env roots arms foc rest tagReg branches dft slot endL rty
  None => gapSwitchHead e slot endL
-- HUnit is irrefutable (Unit has exactly one inhabitant): no discriminant test
-- needed.  Drop the focus occurrence and descend into the single branch body.
-- This closes gap #12 (the 6 Arbitrary impls in core.mdk and any user f()=…).

-- gap-TOLERANT terminator (bootstrap): a switch head the spike cannot lower
-- records the gap, then — when recording is ON — still TERMINATES the current block
-- (store "0" placeholder, branch to the decision end) so the module stays
-- structurally valid LLVM even though the function is dead code.  With recording
-- OFF the `gapU` panics first, exactly as before.  The harness byte-diff catches
-- any switch the program actually REACHES (its output would diverge).
-- Note: HUnit is now handled before reaching here (irrefutable — no test needed).
gapSwitchHead : Emit -> String -> String -> Unit
gapSwitchHead e slot endL =
  let _ = gapU "unsupported switch head (slice 5b: constructor / list / tuple / int-literal / unit heads only)"
  let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ slot)
  emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)

-- the name + arity a constructor-LIKE head tests against: a named ADT constructor
-- carries them directly; built-in list HCons/HNil and tuples HTuple reuse the same
-- boxed-cell discipline with the fixed names the alloc sites stamp (emitTuple uses
-- "$tuple"; a user `data … = Cons … | Nil` stamps "Cons"/"Nil").  HLit/HUnit are
-- not constructor-like (HLit goes through emitLitChain; HUnit handled irrefutably
-- before this call — see emitSwitch).
conHeadInfo : CHead -> Option (String, Int)
conHeadInfo (HCon c a) = Some (c, a)
conHeadInfo HCons = Some ("Cons", 2)
conHeadInfo HNil = Some ("Nil", 0)
conHeadInfo (HTuple n) = Some ("$tuple", n)
conHeadInfo _ = None

-- the constructor-switch test chain: compare the discriminant word (immediate or
-- boxed, via loadDiscriminant) against each branch's composite (type, ctor) tag
-- (`cellTag`); on a match, descend with the matched
-- cell's fields as the new leading columns; on exhausting the branches, take the
-- default (focus dropped).
emitConChain : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> String -> List String -> String -> List CTBranch -> CTree -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> Unit
emitConChain e env roots arms _ rest _ [] dft slot endL rty =
  emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty dft
emitConChain e env roots arms foc rest tagReg ((CTBranch h sub)::more) dft slot endL rty = match conHeadInfo h
  Some (c, a) =>
    let cmp = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{tagReg}, \{intToString (cellTag e c)}"
    let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
    let yes = "conyes" ++ n
    let next = "connext" ++ n
    let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
    let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
    let fields = loadFields e foc a 0
    let _ = emitTree e env roots arms (fields ++ rest) slot endL rty sub
    let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
    emitConChain e env roots arms foc rest tagReg more dft slot endL rty
  None =>
    let _ = gapU "heterogeneous constructor switch (slice 3: a column is all-constructor or all-literal)"
    let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ slot)
    emit e ("  br label %" ++ endL)

-- the literal-switch test chain: compare the focus immediate against each branch's
-- tagged-int constant; a literal head has arity 0, so a match descends with the
-- focus simply dropped (no field columns).
emitLitChain : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List String -> List CArm -> String -> List String -> List CTBranch -> CTree -> String -> String -> Ref LTy -> Unit
emitLitChain e env roots arms _ rest [] dft slot endL rty =
  emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty dft
emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest ((CTBranch (HLit (LInt k)) sub)::more) dft slot endL rty =
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{foc}, \{intToString (k * 2 + 1)}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "lityes" ++ n
  let next = "litnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let _ = emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty sub
  let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
  emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest more dft slot endL rty
-- Char = immediate codepoint word (cp << 1) | 1 — same comparison shape as Int.
emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest ((CTBranch (HLit (LChar c)) sub)::more) dft slot endL rty =
  let cp = charCode (arrayGetUnsafe 0 (stringToChars c))
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{foc}, \{intToString (cp * 2 + 1)}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "lityes" ++ n
  let next = "litnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let _ = emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty sub
  let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
  emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest more dft slot endL rty
-- String = boxed cell; equality via the @mdk_string_eq runtime helper, which
-- returns a TAGGED Bool word (3 = True), so branch on `icmp eq i64 r, 3`.
-- Materialize the literal operand the same way emitLit (LString _) does.
emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest ((CTBranch (HLit (LString s)) sub)::more) dft slot endL rty =
  let (lit, _) = emitLit e (LString s)
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_eq(i64 \{foc}, i64 \{lit})"
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp eq i64 \{r}, 3"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let yes = "lityes" ++ n
  let next = "litnext" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{yes}, label %\{next}"
  let _ = emit e (yes ++ ":")
  let _ = emitTree e env roots arms rest slot endL rty sub
  let _ = emit e (next ++ ":")
  emitLitChain e env roots arms foc rest more dft slot endL rty
emitLitChain _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ =
  gapU "non-Int literal switch (slice 3: no Float / Bool literal heads)"

-- ── small list helpers (slice 3) ────────────────────────────────────────────
takeS : Int -> List a -> List a
takeS n xs
  | n <= 0 = []
  | otherwise = match xs
    [] => []
    y::ys => y :: takeS (n - 1) ys

tailS : List a -> List a
tailS [] = []
tailS (_::xs) = xs

nthArm : List CArm -> Int -> Option CArm
nthArm (a::_) 0 = Some a
nthArm (_::rest) n = nthArm rest (n - 1)
nthArm [] _ = None

-- a readable tag for the unsupported-pattern panic.
ptag : Pat -> String
ptag (PTuple _) = "PTuple"
ptag (PCons _ _) = "PCons"
ptag (PList _) = "PList"
ptag (PAs _ _) = "PAs"
ptag (PRng _ _ _) = "PRng"
ptag (PRec _ _ _) = "PRec"
ptag _ = "?"

-- ── top-level function definitions (slice 2) ────────────────────────────────
-- Each non-`main` top-level binding with parameters becomes an LLVM function
-- `@mdk_<name>(i64 %arg0, …) -> i64`.  Parameters bind to %argN at their
-- INFERRED static type (slice 2b — every param is still an i64 word at the ABI;
-- the type only selects int-vs-float instructions in the body).  The
-- body is emitted in TAIL position so a self-recursive tail call lowers to
-- `musttail call` immediately followed by `ret` (the calling-convention proof:
-- self-recursion guarantees the caller/callee prototypes match, which musttail
-- requires).  Cross-function tail calls lower to an ordinary `call` (still
-- correct; guaranteed-TCO across distinct prototypes is a later increment).
emitFns : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
emitFns _ [] = ()
emitFns e (b::rest) =
  -- PROGRAM half (issue #118): a prelude bind's define already lives in prelude.o,
  -- so emit it, keep the side effects, and ship only its `declare`.
  let _ = if hiddenBind (bindName b) then
    emitAsDeclares e (_ => emitFn e b)
  else
    emitFn e b
  emitFns e rest

-- (b′) routing first (mirrors wasm's emitRefFnBind precedence: a multi-member
-- group claims its members ahead of Stage-1 TRMC; the shared detection's v5 keeps
-- Stage-1-claimable SINGLETONS out of groups).  A group MEMBER emits nothing
-- standalone — its body lives as basic blocks inside the root's group define
-- (v3/v4 prove nothing outside the group references it); the ROOT emits the whole
-- group define at its own bind position (deterministic emission order).
emitFn : Emit -> CBind -> Unit
emitFn e (CBind name [CClause pats body]) = match dispGroupOf gDispGroupsRef.value name
  Some grp => if dispRootOf grp == name then emitGDispGroup e grp else ()
  None =>
    let (pats2, body2) = etaSaturateMethodBody e name pats body
    if trmcTryFn e name [(pats2, body2)] True then
      ()
    else if allPVar pats2 then
      emitFnClause e name pats2 body2
    else
      emitMultiClauseFn e name [(pats2, body2)]
emitFn e (CBind name (c::rest)) = match dispGroupOf gDispGroupsRef.value name
  Some grp => if dispRootOf grp == name then emitGDispGroup e grp else ()
  None =>
    let clauses = clausePairs (c::rest)
    if trmcTryFn e name clauses False then
      ()
    else
      emitMultiClauseFn e name clauses
emitFn _ (CBind _ []) = gapU "top-level function with no clauses"

-- TRMC branch point (TRMC-DESIGN.md §"Emitter touchpoints" #2): if `name`'s
-- clauses are TRMC-eligible AND its leading-dict count is uniform across clauses
-- (`dictUniformPairs` — a dict-carrying define, i.e. every unannotated
-- polymorphic builder, is admitted; the dict is just another loop slot, and
-- `inferSigs` runs over the LOWERED clauses so `ptys` is already dict-inclusive),
-- emit it as a destination-passing loop and return True; else
-- return False so the caller falls back to the current (stack-growing) codegen.
-- `forceSingle` marks the all-PVar single-clause path so emitTrmcFn descends the
-- CIf-guarded body directly instead of the decision-tree chain.
trmcTryFn : Emit -> String -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool -> Bool
trmcTryFn e name clauses forceSingle =
  let arity = clauseArity clauses
  let single = forceSingle && allClausesPVar clauses
  if arity > 0 && dictUniformPairs clauses && trmcEligible (isCtor e) (ctorArity e) (SelfByVar name) arity clauses then
    let ptys = match sigLookup e name
      Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
      None => []
    let (spats, sbody) = match clauses
      (ps, b)::_ => (ps, b)
      [] => ([], CLit LUnit)
    let _ = emitTrmcFn e name arity ptys clauses single spats sbody
    True
  else False

-- every clause's params are all plain PVars (the single-clause TRMC ABI shape).
allClausesPVar : List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool
allClausesPVar [] = True
allClausesPVar ((pats, _)::rest) = allPVar pats && allClausesPVar rest

-- Gap #50 — eta-SATURATE a point-free constrained binding whose body is an
-- UNDER-APPLIED interface-method (CMethod) or constrained-fn (CDict) occurrence.
-- `callMax : Ord a => a -> a -> a; callMax = max` dict-passes to a clause with ONE
-- param (the `Ord a` dict) and body `CMethod "max" (RDict $d) ..` applied to ZERO
-- value args — but `max` needs TWO.  The lifted define then has arity 1 (dict only)
-- while the call site (emitDictApp `callMax $d x y`) passes dict + the two real
-- args, OVER-applying the define and silently DROPPING the value args → the method
-- dispatch runs with garbage operands (observed: 0).  Append `deficit` fresh value
-- params to the clause and apply them to the body, so (1) the define's arity matches
-- what the call site passes, and (2) the body becomes a SATURATED method application
-- (emitMethod threads the full argOps through the RDict dispatch chain).  General:
-- any method/constrained-fn used point-free under a `=>` constraint (the dict makes
-- it a fn-bind, not the arity-0 value global that an UNCONSTRAINED alias becomes and
-- which already eta-closures correctly via emitVar→emitEtaClosure).
--
-- Gap #12 (3rd wrapper shape — CLam/compose) — a point-free `=>`-constrained binding
-- whose body is a CLOSURE VALUE, not a method spine.  `clamp lo hi = min hi >> max lo`
-- dict-passes to a clause `[$dict, lo, hi]` (arity 3) whose body is the `CLam` compose
-- (`>>`/`<<` desugar to CLam) producing `\x -> max lo (min hi x)`.  The closure is
-- well-formed and dispatches correctly, but the define RETURNS it unapplied (arity 3)
-- while the call site `clamp 0 10 7` passes `$dict + 3 value args` (arity 4) — the
-- extra arg is DROPPED, garbage out (SIGSEGV at -O2).  methodBodyDeficit is 0 (the
-- body is a CLam value, not an under-applied CMethod/CDict head), so the method-spine
-- path can't fire.  The SIGNATURE-arity deficit catches it: a constrained binding's
-- define MUST have arity `dictCount + sigValueArity` (what emitDictApp passes at every
-- call site = leading dict words ++ all declared value args); when the clause binds
-- fewer params (the closure body supplies the rest), append the shortfall as fresh
-- eta params and APPLY them to the body.  applyEtaArgs' `CApp body etaVar` over a CLam
-- body lowers via emitIndirect (closure-alloc → load code_ptr → call), i.e. it applies
-- the returned closure — exactly the missing application.  General for any point-free
-- constrained binding under-applied relative to its declared value arity, whatever the
-- wrapper (bare CLam, or a method/closure spine the method-spine path already covers —
-- both deficits agree there, so the method-spine path is kept as the primary signal).
etaSaturateMethodBody : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> (List Pat, CExpr)
etaSaturateMethodBody e name pats body =
  let mDeficit = methodBodyDeficit e body
  let deficit = etaDeficit e name pats body mDeficit
  if deficit <= 0 then (pats, body)
  else
    let fresh = etaFreshPats deficit (listLen pats)
    (pats ++ fresh, applyEtaArgsTail body fresh)
-- method-spine deficit is the primary signal (#50/#11).  When it is 0 the body may
-- still be a CLOSURE VALUE (CLam from compose `>>`/`<<`) that the define returns
-- unapplied while the call site passes more value args (clamp/#12) — recover that
-- shortfall from the SIGNATURE arity, but ONLY when the tail is a CLam (a bare value
-- return is legitimate point-free currying; eta-applying it would corrupt the body).

-- the eta deficit: the method-spine count when positive, else the signature-arity
-- shortfall when the body tail is a CLam closure value (#12), else 0.
etaDeficit : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Int -> Int
etaDeficit e name pats body mDeficit
  | mDeficit > 0 = mDeficit
  | tailIsLam body = sigArityDeficit e name pats
  | otherwise = 0

-- does the body's TAIL (through where/let wrappers) reduce to a CLam closure value?
-- Mirrors applyEtaArgsTail's CLet/CLetGroup transparency so a compose under a
-- `where` is still recognised.
tailIsLam : CExpr -> Bool
tailIsLam (CLet _ _ _ b) = tailIsLam b
tailIsLam (CLetGroup _ b) = tailIsLam b
tailIsLam (CLam _ _) = True
tailIsLam _ = False

-- the shortfall between a constrained binding's REQUIRED define arity
-- (`dictCount + sigValueArity` = what every call site passes through emitDictApp)
-- and the clause's actual param count.  0 (or negative) when the clause already
-- binds every dict + value param (the ordinary case) or when no signature is known
-- (fnArity 0 ⇒ no eta-expansion — leave it to the method-spine path).  dictCount is
-- the number of leading `$dict_…` params dict_pass prepended; the remaining pats are
-- the source value params, and a point-free closure body (e.g. compose) makes that
-- count fall short of the declared sigValueArity.
sigArityDeficit : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> Int
sigArityDeficit e name pats =
  let sigArity = fnArity e name
  if sigArity <= 0 then
    0
  else
    maxInt 0 (leadingDictPats pats + sigArity - listLen pats)

-- count of leading `$dict_…` parameter patterns (the dict witnesses dict_pass
-- prepends ahead of the source value params).
leadingDictPats : List Pat -> Int
leadingDictPats ((PVar x)::rest) =
  if isDictParamName x then
    1 + leadingDictPats rest
  else
    0
leadingDictPats _ = 0

-- (`isDictParamName` — a dict-witness parameter name, `$dict_<method>_<slot>` — now
-- lives in backend/emit_support.mdk: BOTH backends need it, for the same reason.)

-- the count of still-unapplied VALUE params of an under-applied method/dict body
-- (0 = saturated / not a method-or-dict head ⇒ no eta-expansion).  A bare CMethod
-- needs its full `methodArityOf`; an applied spine `(CApp.. (CMethod..) a1..ak)`
-- needs `methodArityOf - k`.  A CDict (a `=>`-constrained-function occurrence) head is
-- handled the SAME way (#21 point-free CAF), but its arity needs care: `fnArity name`
-- counts the LEADING DICT params dict_pass prepended as well as the value params, so a
-- CDict's remaining VALUE arity is `fnArity name - <dict count> - <value args applied>`.
-- The dict count is exactly `listLen routes` (the witnesses this occurrence supplies —
-- emitDictApp passes one word per route ahead of the value args).  So a bare
-- `seqp = trav` needs `fnArity trav - routes` eta params and an applied spine
-- `seqp = trav identity` needs `fnArity trav - routes - k` (here 3 - 1 - 1 = 1).  This
-- is the load-bearing case the old "leave CDict alone" rule MISSED: when seqp's define
-- is under-applied (clause `[$dict]`, body `trav identity`) the emitter lowered the body
-- as a DIRECT under-saturated call to trav (emitDictApp forwards only the supplied args)
-- AND gave seqp's define arity 1 (dict only) while the saturated call site `seqp xs`
-- passes dict + xs → the value arg was dropped and trav ran on garbage → SIGSEGV.
-- Appending the deficit as fresh eta params makes BOTH the define arity and the inner
-- trav application saturate, byte-identically to the eta-expanded source form.  (The old
-- comment's "partially-applied CDict passes its supplied args through emitDictApp" is true
-- but does not produce the missing-args closure a point-free define returns — that is the
-- bug.)  A SATURATED clause (`seqp xs = trav identity xs`, deficit 3 - 1 - 2 = 0) is left
-- untouched.
-- A `where`/`let` wrapper (CLet/CLetGroup) is TRANSPARENT to the deficit — the
-- under-applied method may be the let-body TAIL (`maximum = fold step None`
-- desugars its `step` `where`-helper to a CLetGroup around the `fold` spine).  The
-- companion applyEtaArgsTail threads the fresh args into that same tail.
methodBodyDeficit : Emit -> CExpr -> Int
methodBodyDeficit e (CLet _ _ _ b) = methodBodyDeficit e b
methodBodyDeficit e (CLetGroup _ b) = methodBodyDeficit e b
methodBodyDeficit _ (CMethod name _ _ _) = methodArityOf name
methodBodyDeficit e (CDict name routes) =
  maxInt 0 (fnArity e name - listLen routes)
methodBodyDeficit e app =
  let (hd, args) = flattenApp app []
  match hd
    CMethod name _ _ _ => maxInt 0 (methodArityOf name - listLen args)
    CDict name routes =>
      maxInt 0 (fnArity e name - listLen routes - listLen args)
    _ => 0

-- `n` fresh value-param PVars (`__eta_<i>`) numbered after the existing `start`
-- params so paramEnv/paramDecls index them positionally without clashing.
etaFreshPats : Int -> Int -> List Pat
etaFreshPats 0 _ = []
etaFreshPats n start =
  PVar ("__eta_" ++ intToString start) :: etaFreshPats (n - 1) (start + 1)

-- apply the fresh eta params at the body's TAIL position, threading THROUGH any
-- enclosing `where`/`let` wrappers so the let bindings (e.g. `maximum`'s `step`
-- helper) stay in scope for the now-saturated tail application.  Mirrors
-- methodBodyDeficit's CLet/CLetGroup transparency.
applyEtaArgsTail : CExpr -> List Pat -> CExpr
applyEtaArgsTail (CLet r p rhs b) fresh =
  CLet r p rhs (applyEtaArgsTail b fresh)
applyEtaArgsTail (CLetGroup bs b) fresh =
  CLetGroup bs (applyEtaArgsTail b fresh)
applyEtaArgsTail body fresh = applyEtaArgs body fresh

-- apply `body` to each fresh eta param as a CVar (AGlobal — emit resolves a CVar by
-- NAME against the param env, ignoring the Addr), left to right.
applyEtaArgs : CExpr -> List Pat -> CExpr
applyEtaArgs body [] = body
applyEtaArgs body ((PVar x)::rest) =
  applyEtaArgs (CApp body (CVar x AGlobal)) rest
applyEtaArgs body (_::rest) = applyEtaArgs body rest

-- E1a — multi-clause / patterned top-level fns.  Route ordinary fn-binds whose
-- clauses don't all reduce to plain-variable params through the SAME
-- clause-coalescing + decision-tree lowering the impl methods use
-- (`emitClauseTree`).  This closes EMITTER-GAPS #1 (multi-clause top-level fn)
-- and the fn half of #4 (non-variable parameter pattern), since the decision
-- tree binds patterned params via `bindPattern` in each leaf.  The synthetic
-- `aK` scrutinee params are typed from the fn's inferred signature (sigTable /
-- FnSig) so body arithmetic/printing selects the right instruction; absent a
-- sig, params default to Int (the impl-path behaviour).
emitMultiClauseFn : Emit -> String -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Unit
emitMultiClauseFn e name clauses =
  let arity = clauseArity clauses
  let ptys = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
    None => []
  let env = implParamEnvTyped arity ptys 0
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @mdk_\{name}(\{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let _ = emitClauseTree e env arity clauses
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

-- the (List Pat, CExpr) clause pairs of a fn-bind, in source order.
clausePairs : List CClause -> List (List Pat, CExpr)
clausePairs [] = []
clausePairs ((CClause pats body)::rest) = (pats, body) :: clausePairs rest

-- ── TRMC emit (destination-passing loop) ────────────────────────────────────
-- emit a TRMC-eligible function as an O(1)-stack destination-passing loop.
-- entry sets up the result hole + the mutable destination slot + the mutable
-- param slots, then jumps to the loop head; the loop reloads the params, runs the
-- ORDINARY param dispatch (single-clause: CIf-tail descent; multi-clause: the
-- decision-tree clause chain) whose LEAVES are redirected via trmcCtxRef:
--   • cons leaf:  alloc Cons cell, store head, link cell into `*dest`, advance
--                 `dest` to the cell's tail slot (+16), recompute the recursion
--                 args into the param slots, br loop.
--   • base leaf:  store the value into `*dest`, br exit.
-- exit loads the hole (the final list head) and rets it.  destSlot holds the
-- ADDRESS of the next i64 to fill; the hole's address is the first destination, a
-- linked cell's tail slot (cell+16) each subsequent one.
-- census marker (TMC-parity gate): one deterministic `; tmc: <fn> <mode>`
-- comment per TMC decision — the LLVM peer of wasm's wTmcMarker.
-- test/tmc_census.sh + diff_compiler_tmc_parity.sh extract these to diff both
-- backends' TMC'd source-function sets.  Census-normalized name (leading `mdk_`
-- stripped).
tmcMarker : Emit -> String -> String -> Unit
tmcMarker e sym mode =
  let name = if startsWith "mdk_" sym then
    stringSlice 4 (stringLength sym) sym
  else
    sym
  emit e "; tmc: \{name} \{mode}"

tmcGroupMarkers : Emit -> String -> List String -> Unit
tmcGroupMarkers _ _ [] = ()
tmcGroupMarkers e root (m::rest) =
  let _ = if m == root then
    tmcMarker e m "group-root"
  else
    tmcMarker e m ("group:" ++ root)
  tmcGroupMarkers e root rest

emitTrmcFn : Emit -> String -> Int -> List LTy -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Unit
emitTrmcFn e name arity ptys clauses single singlePats singleBody =
  let _ = tmcMarker e name "trmc"
  let _ = emitTrmcHeader e ("mdk_" ++ name) arity
  let _ = emitTrmcLoopBody e (SelfByVar name) arity ptys clauses single singlePats singleBody
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

-- emit the `define` header + entry label for a TRMC define.  `defineName` is the
-- bare symbol (`mdk_<name>` for a top-level fn, `mdk_impl_<tag>_<method>` for a
-- dispatched impl method).  The caller emits the closing `}` (the impl path's
-- emitGroup already brackets the define).
emitTrmcHeader : Emit -> String -> Int -> Unit
emitTrmcHeader e defineName arity =
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @\{defineName}(\{implParamDecls arity 0}) {"
  emit e "entry:"

-- the REUSABLE TRMC loop body (assumes `define`+`entry:` already emitted; does NOT
-- emit the closing `}`).  Sets up the result hole + mutable destination slot +
-- mutable param slots, jumps to the loop head, reloads the params, runs the
-- ordinary param dispatch (single-clause: CIf-tail descent; multi-clause: the
-- decision-tree clause chain) with the LEAVES redirected via trmcCtxRef (TrmcOn
-- carries the SelfRef so the cons/ctor leaf recognises the self-call — a CVar for
-- a top-level fn, a CMethod for a dispatched impl method), then emits the exit
-- block (load hole, ret).  `self` is the SelfRef the loop recurses on; `slotTys`
-- types each reloaded `aK` scrutinee word (top-level: inferred sig types, padded
-- with LTInt; impl: receiver type at the impl positions, LTInt elsewhere).
emitTrmcLoopBody : Emit -> SelfRef -> Int -> List LTy -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Unit
emitTrmcLoopBody e self arity slotTys clauses single singlePats singleBody =
  let hole = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ hole ++ " = alloca i64")
  let destSlot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ destSlot ++ " = alloca ptr")
  let _ = emit e "  store ptr \{hole}, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let pslots = trmcEmitParamSlots e arity 0
  let loopL = "trmcloop" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let exitL = "trmcexit" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ loopL)
  let _ = emit e (loopL ++ ":")
  let reloaded = trmcReloadParams e pslots slotTys 0
  let saved = trmcCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef trmcCtxRef (TrmcOn self arity destSlot loopL exitL pslots)
  let _ = trmcEmitDispatch e reloaded arity clauses single singlePats reloaded singleBody
  let _ = setRef trmcCtxRef saved
  let _ = emit e (exitL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{hole}"
  emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
-- mutable param slots, seeded from the incoming %argK words.

-- reload the params into fresh regs for THIS iteration (each loop re-executes
-- the loads, so a back-edge to %loop re-reads the mutated slots — valid SSA).

-- one mutable `alloca i64` param slot per arg, seeded from %argK; returns the slot
-- registers in order.
trmcEmitParamSlots : Emit -> Int -> Int -> List String
trmcEmitParamSlots e arity i
  | i >= arity = []
  | otherwise =
    let slot = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
    let _ = emit e "  store i64 %arg\{intToString i}, ptr \{slot}"
    slot :: trmcEmitParamSlots e arity (i + 1)

-- reload each param slot into a fresh reg at the loop head, binding the synthetic
-- `aK` scrutinee name (the name emitClauseTree's scrutinee tuple uses) to the
-- loaded word at the fn's inferred param type.
trmcReloadParams : Emit -> List String -> List LTy -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
trmcReloadParams _ [] _ _ = []
trmcReloadParams e (slot::rest) tys i =
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  let ty = match tys
    t::_ => t
    [] => LTInt
  ("a" ++ intToString i, (r, ty)) :: trmcReloadParams e rest (trmcTail tys) (i + 1)

trmcTail : List a -> List a
trmcTail [] = []
trmcTail (_::xs) = xs

-- run the in-loop param dispatch.  Single-clause (all-PVar params): rename the
-- reloaded `aK` env to the clause's PVar names and descend the body in tail
-- position (emitTrmcBody).  Multi-clause: reuse emitClauseTree's decision-tree
-- chain verbatim — its emitLeaf is TRMC-aware via trmcCtxRef.
trmcEmitDispatch : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> Int -> List (List Pat, CExpr) -> Bool -> List Pat -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
trmcEmitDispatch e reloaded arity clauses single singlePats aenv singleBody =
  if single then
    let env = trmcRenameEnv singlePats aenv
    emitTrmcBody e env singleBody
  else emitClauseTree e reloaded arity clauses

-- bind the clause's PVar param names to the reloaded `aK` operand words (single
-- clause, all-PVar params — the slice-2 ABI requirement also holds for TRMC).
trmcRenameEnv : List Pat -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List (String, (String, LTy))
trmcRenameEnv [] _ = []
trmcRenameEnv ((PVar x)::ps) ((_, op)::rest) = (x, op) :: trmcRenameEnv ps rest
trmcRenameEnv (_::ps) (_::rest) = trmcRenameEnv ps rest
trmcRenameEnv _ [] = []

-- single-clause TRMC body in TAIL position: descend CIf/CLet/CLetGroup wrappers,
-- emit each leaf via the TRMC context (cons leaf or base leaf).  Mirrors
-- emitFnBody's CIf descent but redirects the value leaves.  CLet/CLetGroup descend
-- only their (self-free, verified by trmcBodyOk) simple value-binding shapes; any
-- richer let is emitted whole as a base leaf (no self-call inside ⇒ no stack risk).
emitTrmcBody : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
emitTrmcBody e env (CIf c t f) =
  let (cv, _) = emitExpr e env c
  let ci = untagInt e cv
  let cb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cb} = icmp ne i64 \{ci}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let thenL = "trmcthen" ++ n
  let elseL = "trmcelse" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cb}, label %\{thenL}, label %\{elseL}"
  let _ = emit e (thenL ++ ":")
  let _ = emitTrmcBody e env t
  let _ = emit e (elseL ++ ":")
  emitTrmcBody e env f
emitTrmcBody e env (CLet False (PVar x) rhs b) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env rhs
  emitTrmcBody e ((x, (v, ty))::env) b
emitTrmcBody e env (CLetGroup binds b) =
  if allNullaryBinds binds then
    let env2 = trmcBindNullaryGroup e env binds
    emitTrmcBody e env2 b
  else emitTrmcLeaf e env (CLetGroup binds b)
-- B-match-descent (TRMC-DESIGN §"Phase 2 sub-part 3"): a TAIL-position decision
-- tree.  Emit the scrutinee, then walk the SAME decision-tree machinery as
-- emitDecision (emitTree) but keep trmcCtxRef LIVE — so each arm body is descended
-- as a TRMC tail leaf (cons-into-dest+loop / plain-tail-call+loop / base+exit) via
-- emitLeaf's TrmcOn branch, NOT computed into a value slot.  This is the inverse of
-- emitDecision's save+clear, which stays for an EXPRESSION-position match inside an
-- arm (a value-producing match whose arms must store into its own slot).  The
-- result slot + end label passed to emitTree are a DEAD block (no leaf stores into
-- them under TrmcOn — every leaf branches to loopL/exitL); LLVM tolerates the
-- unreachable load/branch (Phase-1 precedent: the nested decision result slot is a
-- dead block).  fallthroughLabelRef is saved+cleared (a non-exhaustive tail match's
-- CTFail is a genuine abort, not the enclosing clause chain's fallthrough).
emitTrmcBody e env (CDecision scrut arms tree) =
  let (sv, _) = emitExpr e env scrut
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "trmcdecend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let rty = Ref LTInt
  let savedFT = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
  let _ = emitTree e env [sv] arms [sv] slot endL rty tree
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFT
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  emit e "  unreachable"
-- trmcCtxRef is INTENTIONALLY left LIVE across the tree (the tail arms cons/loop
-- into the active destination); a nested EXPRESSION-position match within an arm
-- body re-enters emitDecision, which saves+clears it for its own value arms.

-- the dead end block: no TrmcOn leaf branches here, but emit it well-formed so a
-- (non-treeable / fallthrough) path that did would still verify.

emitTrmcBody e env ex = emitTrmcLeaf e env ex

-- every binding is a nullary value binding (the descendable let-group shape).
allNullaryBinds : List CBind -> Bool
allNullaryBinds [] = True
allNullaryBinds ((CBind _ [CClause [] _])::rest) = allNullaryBinds rest
allNullaryBinds _ = False

-- emit each nullary value binding left-to-right, threading the env (mirrors
-- emitLetGroup's value-binding arm).
trmcBindNullaryGroup : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> List (String, (String, LTy))
trmcBindNullaryGroup _ env [] = env
trmcBindNullaryGroup e env ((CBind name [CClause [] rhs])::rest) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env rhs
  trmcBindNullaryGroup e ((name, (v, ty))::env) rest
trmcBindNullaryGroup _ env (_::rest) = env

-- a TRMC tail leaf: an eligible ctor-tail conses into the destination + loops; a
-- bare plain-tail self-call (F3 — `None => self …`, no enclosing cons/ctor)
-- recomputes its args + loops with NO cell alloc + NO dest store (iterate on a
-- shorter list); any other leaf stores its value into the destination + exits.
emitTrmcLeaf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
emitTrmcLeaf e env ex = match trmcCtxRef.value
  TrmcOff => emitValueRet e env ex
  TrmcOn self arity destSlot loopL exitL pslots =>
    if isCtorTail (isCtor e) (ctorArity e) self arity ex then
      emitTrmcCtor e env (ctorTailName ex) (ctorTailLeadFields ex) (consTailArgs ex) (ctorTailSelfIdx ex) destSlot loopL pslots
    else if isSelfSatApp self arity ex then
      emitTrmcTailCall e env ex loopL pslots
    else
      emitTrmcBase e env ex destSlot exitL

-- F3 plain-tail self-call leaf (TRMC-DESIGN §"Phase 2 sub-part 3"): a bare
-- saturated self-call in tail position (`None => filterMap f xs`).  It is the
-- cons-leaf MINUS the cell alloc + head store + dest link/advance — an
-- iterate-on-a-shorter-list step: recompute the recursion args into TEMPS (an arg
-- may read an earlier param), store them into the loop's param slots, and branch
-- back to the loop head.  The destination is left UNCHANGED (this arm produces no
-- list cell), so the next iteration fills the same destination slot — exactly the
-- "drop this element" semantics of `filter`/`filterMap`.  The self-call is NOT
-- emitted as a `call` (that is what removes the stack-growing recursion).
emitTrmcTailCall : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String -> List String -> Unit
emitTrmcTailCall e env ex loopL pslots =
  let args = match flattenApp ex []
    (_, as_) => as_
  let argOps = emitArgs e env args
  -- a dict-carrying (CDict-headed) self-call's args cover only the VALUE params;
  -- its leading dict slots are loop-invariant (identity-forwarded routes,
  -- trmc_analysis dictRoutesForwarded) — store into the LAST |args| slots only.
  let _ = trmcStoreParamSlots e (dropFirstN (lengthS pslots - lengthS argOps) pslots) argOps
  emit e ("  br label %" ++ loopL)

-- ctor leaf (Phase 2 Axis A): alloc a single-constructor cell (header + the
-- non-last fields), link it into the current destination at the SELF-CALL field
-- slot (offset `8*(selfIdx+1)`), advance the destination to that slot, recompute
-- the recursion args into the param slots, and branch back to the loop head.  The
-- self-call is NOT emitted — only the leading fields + arg recomputations are,
-- which is what removes the stack-growing `call`.  Store order (GC-safe,
-- TRMC-DESIGN §"Risk"): alloc → header → leading fields → link-into-dest → advance;
-- the cell's own self-call slot stays ZEROED (GC_malloc returns zeroed memory; an
-- even/null word is never dereferenced by Boehm) until the next iteration fills it.
-- For `::`/Cons this is arity 2, selfIdx 1 (offset 16) — the Phase-1 cons shape.
-- F1(b) SEAM: the dest-offset is COMPUTED from `selfIdx`, not hardcoded "last", so a
-- future self-call-in-any-field is a detection-only patch (this emit is unchanged).
emitTrmcCtor : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> List CExpr -> Int -> String -> String -> List String -> Unit
emitTrmcCtor e env ctor leadFields args selfIdx destSlot loopL pslots =
  let arity = lengthS leadFields + 1
  let leadVals = emitArgs e env leadFields
  let cell = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cell} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (arity + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString (cellTag e ctor)}, ptr \{cell}"
  let _ = storeFields e cell leadVals 0
  let cellw = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cellw} = ptrtoint ptr \{cell} to i64"
  let d = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{d} = load ptr, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{cellw}, ptr \{d}"
  let newdest = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{newdest} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{cell}, i64 \{intToString (8 * (selfIdx + 1))}"
  let _ = emit e "  store ptr \{newdest}, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let argOps = emitArgs e env args
  -- dict-carrying self-call: skip the loop-invariant leading dict slots (see
  -- emitTrmcTailCall); non-dict is |argOps| == |pslots| ⇒ drop 0.
  let _ = trmcStoreParamSlots e (dropFirstN (lengthS pslots - lengthS argOps) pslots) argOps
  emit e ("  br label %" ++ loopL)
-- emit the leading (non-last) field values FIRST (left-to-right), then alloc.

-- store the leading fields at their positional offsets (field 0 at +8, …).  The
-- self-call slot (offset 8*(selfIdx+1)) is left zeroed for the link below.

-- advance the destination to THIS cell's self-call field slot (offset
-- 8*(selfIdx+1)) — COMPUTED from the field index (F1(b) seam).

-- recompute every recursion arg into a TEMP first (an arg may read an earlier
-- param), THEN store all temps into the slots, so an in-place store can't clobber
-- a not-yet-read param within the same iteration.

-- store the recomputed arg operands back into the loop's mutable param slots.
trmcStoreParamSlots : Emit -> List String -> List String -> Unit
trmcStoreParamSlots _ [] _ = ()
trmcStoreParamSlots _ _ [] = ()
trmcStoreParamSlots e (slot::slots) (op::ops) =
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{op}, ptr \{slot}"
  trmcStoreParamSlots e slots ops

-- base/other leaf: store the value into the current destination, branch to exit.
emitTrmcBase : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String -> String -> Unit
emitTrmcBase e env ex destSlot exitL =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  let d = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{d} = load ptr, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{v}, ptr \{d}"
  emit e ("  br label %" ++ exitL)

-- ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- Dispatch-graph (b′) TMC — the LLVM group emit (TMC-parity arc, TRMC-DESIGN
-- §Phase 3).  The shared detection (backend/trmc_analysis.mdk) finds a family of
-- mutually tail-calling fns rooted at ONE external entry (the lexer's `scan`
-- token spine, the offside-rule `layout` family), whose spine conses bottom in
-- saturated member calls.  The WasmGC emit keeps members as separate funcs and
-- loops via `return_call` + per-group dest GLOBALS; LLVM has no `return_call`
-- and `musttail` demands caller/callee prototype equality — but the detection's
-- v4 check PROVES no bind or impl outside the group references a non-root
-- member, so the whole group INLINES into the root's single define:
--   • one labeled entry block per member (`gdisp_<member>:`), its body emitted
--     by the ORDINARY clause machinery (direct tail descent / emitClauseTree)
--     under a live gDispCtxRef, leaves redirected;
--   • every sanctioned tail edge = recompute the callee's args into fresh regs,
--     store them into the shared param slots, `br %gdisp_<callee>` — a plain
--     branch, no call, no musttail (the "heterogeneous member arities" obstacle
--     that deferred the 2026-06-22 port is moot: there is no cross-define edge);
--   • the destination is a LOCAL `alloca` seeded to &hole — the Stage-1 protocol
--     verbatim (no module globals, no first-cell flag; a nested outer call to
--     the root gets a fresh frame, so re-entrancy is safe by construction);
--   • a spine-cons leaf links its 1..N Cons cells into *dest in source order
--     and advances dest through each tail slot before branching to the bottom
--     callee; a base leaf stores its value into *dest and exits (emitTrmcBase).
-- Members emit NO standalone define; the root's public `@mdk_<root>` IS the
-- group define, so external callers and fn-as-value eta closures over the root
-- are untouched.

-- the member's basic-block label inside the group define (member names are
-- unique within a group; LLVM labels are function-scoped, so two groups may
-- reuse the scheme).
gDispLabel : String -> String
gDispLabel m = "gdisp_" ++ m

-- emit the whole group define, rooted at `@mdk_<root>` with the root's real
-- params.  U = max member arity shared slots; slots 0..rootArity seed from
-- %argK, the rest from 0 (never read before a store — zeroed only so no undef
-- load exists).
emitGDispGroup : Emit -> DispGroup -> Unit
