emitGDispGroup e grp =
  let root = dispRootOf grp
  let members = dispMembersOf grp
  let binds = gDispBindsRef.value
  let rootArity = gDispFnArity root
  let u = gDispMaxArity members 0
  let _ = tmcGroupMarkers e root members
  let _ = emitTrmcHeader e ("mdk_" ++ root) rootArity
  let uslots = gDispEmitSlots e u rootArity 0
  let hole = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ hole ++ " = alloca i64")
  let destSlot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ destSlot ++ " = alloca ptr")
  let _ = emit e "  store ptr \{hole}, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let exitL = "gdispexit" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ gDispLabel root)
  let saved = gDispCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef gDispCtxRef (GDispOn root members uslots destSlot exitL)
  let _ = emitGDispMembers e binds members
  let _ = setRef gDispCtxRef saved
  let _ = emit e (exitL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{hole}"
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

-- one shared `alloca i64` slot per uniform param position, seeded from the
-- root's %argK (< rootArity) or 0.
gDispEmitSlots : Emit -> Int -> Int -> Int -> List String
gDispEmitSlots e u rootArity i
  | i >= u = []
  | otherwise =
    let slot = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
    let seed = if i < rootArity then "%arg" ++ intToString i else "0"
    let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{seed}, ptr \{slot}"
    slot :: gDispEmitSlots e u rootArity (i + 1)

gDispMaxArity : List String -> Int -> Int
gDispMaxArity [] acc = acc
gDispMaxArity (m::rest) acc =
  let a = gDispFnArity m
  gDispMaxArity rest (maxInt a acc)

emitGDispMembers : Emit -> List CBind -> List String -> Unit
emitGDispMembers _ _ [] = ()
emitGDispMembers e binds (m::rest) =
  let _ = match dispFindBind binds m
    Some b => emitGDispMember e b
    None => gapU ("(b') group member without a bind: " ++ m)
  emitGDispMembers e binds rest

-- a member's entry block: reload its arity-many slots (typed from its inferred
-- sig, LTInt-padded — the same trmcReloadParams the Stage-1 loop head uses,
-- binding the synthetic `aK` scrutinee names), then run the ordinary dispatch:
-- a single all-PVar clause descends its body directly (emitGDispBody); anything
-- else reuses emitClauseTree verbatim (its emitLeaf/emitGuardedArm are
-- gDispCtx-aware).  fallthroughLabelRef is saved+nulled per member (each
-- member's clause chain manages its own fallthroughs).
emitGDispMember : Emit -> CBind -> Unit
emitGDispMember e (CBind name clauses) = match gDispCtxRef.value
  GDispOff => gapU "(b') member emit with no active group context"
  GDispOn _ _ uslots _ _ =>
    let arity = clauseArityOf clauses
    let ptys = match sigLookup e name
      Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
      None => []
    let _ = emit e (gDispLabel name ++ ":")
    let reloaded = trmcReloadParams e (gDispTake arity uslots) ptys 0
    let savedFT = fallthroughLabelRef.value
    let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
    let pairs = clausePairs clauses
    let _ = match pairs
      [(pats, body)] =>
        if allPVar pats then
          emitGDispBody e (trmcRenameEnv pats reloaded) body
        else
          emitClauseTree e reloaded arity pairs
      _ => emitClauseTree e reloaded arity pairs
    setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFT

gDispTake : Int -> List a -> List a
gDispTake n _
  | n <= 0 = []
gDispTake _ [] = []
gDispTake n (x::rest) = x :: gDispTake (n - 1) rest

-- member-body TAIL descent.  Mirrors the shared detection's wrapper set EXACTLY
-- (dispLeavesOk: CIf / non-rec CLet PVar+PWild / CBlock / CDecision), so the
-- emit redirects precisely the leaves the detection sanctioned.  A CLetGroup is
-- deliberately NOT descended (the detection treats it as a leaf: base if
-- member-free, else the group was rejected).
emitGDispBody : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
emitGDispBody e env (CIf c t f) =
  let (cv, _) = emitExpr e env c
  let ci = untagInt e cv
  let cb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cb} = icmp ne i64 \{ci}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let thenL = "gdispthen" ++ n
  let elseL = "gdispelse" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cb}, label %\{thenL}, label %\{elseL}"
  let _ = emit e (thenL ++ ":")
  let _ = emitGDispBody e env t
  let _ = emit e (elseL ++ ":")
  emitGDispBody e env f
emitGDispBody e env (CLet False (PVar x) rhs b) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env rhs
  emitGDispBody e ((x, (v, ty))::env) b
emitGDispBody e env (CLet False PWild rhs b) =
  let _ = emitExpr e env rhs
  emitGDispBody e env b
emitGDispBody e env (CBlock stmts) = emitGDispBlock e env stmts
-- tail-position decision tree: keep gDispCtxRef LIVE across the tree (each arm
-- body is a redirected tail leaf via emitLeaf/emitGuardedArm), save+null the
-- fallthrough label (a non-exhaustive tail match's CTFail is a genuine abort).
-- The result slot + end label are a DEAD block (no leaf stores there under an
-- active group ctx) — the Stage-1 B-match precedent.
emitGDispBody e env (CDecision scrut arms tree) =
  let (sv, _) = emitExpr e env scrut
  let slot = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ slot ++ " = alloca i64")
  let endL = "gdispdecend" ++ intToString (freshLocal e)
  let rty = Ref LTInt
  let savedFT = fallthroughLabelRef.value
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef ""
  let _ = emitTree e env [sv] arms [sv] slot endL rty tree
  let _ = setRef fallthroughLabelRef savedFT
  let _ = emit e (endL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{slot}"
  emit e "  unreachable"
emitGDispBody e env ex = emitGDispLeaf e env ex

-- block TAIL descent: emit the prefix statements exactly as emitBlock does
-- (minus its float-let unboxing specialization — a boxed binding is the
-- universal rep, semantics unchanged), then descend the final CSExpr.  The
-- detection only sanctions blocks whose LAST statement is a CSExpr
-- (lastStmtExpr); any other tail form falls to the base leaf (member-free by
-- detection, so emitting it whole as a value is sound).
emitGDispBlock : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CStmt -> Unit
emitGDispBlock e env [CSExpr ex] = emitGDispBody e env ex
emitGDispBlock e env ((CSExpr ex)::rest) =
  let _ = emitExpr e env ex
  emitGDispBlock e env rest
emitGDispBlock e env ((CSLet _ (PVar x) ex)::rest) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env ex
  emitGDispBlock e ((x, (v, ty))::env) rest
emitGDispBlock e env ((CSLet _ PWild ex)::rest) =
  let _ = emitExpr e env ex
  emitGDispBlock e env rest
emitGDispBlock e env ((CSLet _ pat ex)::rest) =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  if refutableCsLet e pat then
    let env2 = emitRefutableCsLet e env pat v
    emitGDispBlock e env2 rest
  else
    let env2 = bindPattern e env pat v (CBlock rest)
    emitGDispBlock e env2 rest
emitGDispBlock e env ((CSAssign x ex)::rest) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env ex
  emitGDispBlock e ((x, (v, ty))::env) rest
emitGDispBlock e env stmts = emitGDispLeaf e env (CBlock stmts)

-- a group TAIL leaf.  Three classes, matching the detection's dispLeafOk:
--   • spine cons `<h1> :: … :: f <sat>` (f a member) — link the 1..N cells into
--     the dest in source order, then edge to `f`;
--   • bare saturated member call — edge to `f` (dest threads through untouched);
--   • anything else — a base value: close the dest + exit (emitTrmcBase; the
--     detection guarantees it references no member, so no member symbol — which
--     does not exist — can be emitted).
emitGDispLeaf : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
emitGDispLeaf e env ex = match gDispCtxRef.value
  GDispOff => emitValueRet e env ex
  GDispOn _ members uslots destSlot exitL => match gDispSpine e members ex
    Some (heads, f, args) =>
      let _ = emitGDispCells e env heads destSlot
      emitGDispEdge e env f args uslots
    None => match flattenApp ex []
      (CVar f0 _, args) =>
        let f = canonFnName e f0
        let fa = gDispFnArity f
        if contains f members && fa >= 0 && listLen args == fa then
          emitGDispEdge e env f args uslots
        else
          emitTrmcBase e env ex destSlot exitL
      _ => emitTrmcBase e env ex destSlot exitL

-- the shared spine classifier, closed over this emitter's hooks (identical to
-- what the detection ran, so emit and detection agree leaf-by-leaf).
gDispSpine : Emit -> List String -> CExpr -> Option (List CExpr, String, List CExpr)
gDispSpine e members ex = dispSpineParts
  (n => canonFnName e n)
  (n => gDispIsFn n)
  (n => gDispFnArity n)
  (f => contains f members)
  ex

-- link one Cons cell per peeled head into the destination, in source order:
-- head value → alloc 24 → tag Cons → store head at +8 → link the cell word into
-- *dest → advance dest to THIS cell's tail slot (+16).  The tail word stays
-- zeroed (GC_malloc) until the next cell/base fills it — the Stage-1 GC-safety
-- argument verbatim.
emitGDispCells : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> String -> Unit
emitGDispCells _ _ [] _ = ()
emitGDispCells e env (h::rest) destSlot =
  let hv = emitArgs e env [h]
  let cell = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cell} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 24)"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString (cellTag e "Cons")}, ptr \{cell}"
  let _ = storeFields e cell hv 0
  let cellw = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cellw} = ptrtoint ptr \{cell} to i64"
  let d = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{d} = load ptr, ptr \{destSlot}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{cellw}, ptr \{d}"
  let newdest = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{newdest} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{cell}, i64 16"
  let _ = emit e "  store ptr \{newdest}, ptr \{destSlot}"
  emitGDispCells e env rest destSlot

-- a sanctioned tail edge: recompute the callee's args into fresh regs FIRST (an
-- arg may read the current member's params), store them into the shared slots
-- (positional, first arity_f slots), branch to the callee's entry block.  NO
-- call — the branch IS the loop edge.
emitGDispEdge : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CExpr -> List String -> Unit
emitGDispEdge e env f args uslots =
  let argOps = emitArgs e env args
  let _ = trmcStoreParamSlots e uslots argOps
  emit e ("  br label %" ++ gDispLabel f)

-- the clause-derived fn-arity table (wasm's collectFnArities contract: name →
-- first clause's param count) — the detection's isFn/fa hooks read it.
gDispFnArsOf : List CBind -> List (String, Int)
gDispFnArsOf [] = []
gDispFnArsOf ((CBind n cs)::rest) = (n, clauseArityOf cs) :: gDispFnArsOf rest

-- would the LLVM Stage-1 self-TMC (trmcTryFn's gate) already claim this fn?
-- (the shared dispValidate v5 keeps such singletons on Stage-1.)
gDispStage1Claims : Emit -> String -> Int -> List CClause -> Bool
gDispStage1Claims e name arity clauses =
  let pairs = clausePairs clauses
  arity > 0
    && dictUniformPairs pairs
    && trmcEligible (isCtor e) (ctorArity e) (SelfByVar name) arity pairs

-- keep only groups whose every SINGLE-CLAUSE member has eta deficit 0 (the
-- multi-clause emitFn arm never eta-saturates, so multi-clause members are
-- always stable).  A deficit member's ordinary define would gain params
-- (etaSaturateMethodBody), diverging from the group's clause-arity slot
-- protocol — no real group contains one (members are non-dict with cons/call/
-- base tails), but reject deterministically rather than assume.
gDispKeepEtaStable : Emit -> List CBind -> List DispGroup -> List DispGroup
gDispKeepEtaStable _ _ [] = []
gDispKeepEtaStable e binds (g::rest) =
  if gDispMembersEtaStable e binds (dispMembersOf g) then
    g :: gDispKeepEtaStable e binds rest
  else
    gDispKeepEtaStable e binds rest

gDispMembersEtaStable : Emit -> List CBind -> List String -> Bool
gDispMembersEtaStable _ _ [] = True
gDispMembersEtaStable e binds (m::rest) = match dispFindBind binds m
  Some (CBind name [CClause pats body]) => etaDeficit e name pats body (methodBodyDeficit e body) == 0
    && gDispMembersEtaStable e binds rest
  Some _ => gDispMembersEtaStable e binds rest
  None => False

-- drop group members from the float worker-wrapper advertisement set.
gDispDropMembers : List (String, (Int, List Int)) -> List (String, (Int, List Int))
gDispDropMembers [] = []
gDispDropMembers ((n, v)::rest) =
  let isMember = gDispIsMember n
  if isMember then gDispDropMembers rest else (n, v) :: gDispDropMembers rest

-- like implParamEnv, but types each synthetic `aK` scrutinee param from the fn's
-- inferred signature param types (FnSig ts) instead of defaulting to LTInt.
-- Params past the signature length (or with no sig) fall back to LTInt.
implParamEnvTyped : Int -> List LTy -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
implParamEnvTyped n _ i
  | i >= n = []
implParamEnvTyped n (ty::tys) i =
  ("a" ++ intToString i, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, ty)) ::
    implParamEnvTyped n tys (i + 1)
implParamEnvTyped n [] i =
  ("a" ++ intToString i, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) ::
    implParamEnvTyped n [] (i + 1)

-- emit one variable-pattern function clause (the slice-2 path).
-- ── worker-wrapper float-param unboxing (perf/float-param-unbox) ─────────────
-- A self-recursive float-accumulator fn (`f acc n | n==0 = acc | otherwise =
-- f (acc + e) (n-1)`) boxes `acc` at every self-call (i64 ABI) then unboxes it
-- next iteration; clang TCO-loops the self-call, so the box is the only cost.
-- Emit a WORKER `@mdk_<name>__fw(double acc, …)` that carries float params as
-- native `double` (self-calls musttail back into it with unboxed args → NO box per
-- iteration) + a WRAPPER `@mdk_<name>(i64 …)` (the original ABI) that unboxes float
-- params, calls the worker, boxes the result. External callers + eta/dispatch see
-- the unchanged wrapper. Scope: single-clause, all-PVar, non-dict, ≥1 Float param,
-- Float return, body is CIf/value only (every leaf is a full-arity tail self-call
-- OR a self-free value). A `match`-bodied fn (CDecision) falls back to normal
-- codegen (safe; the CDecision-double extension is future work).
floatPositions : List LTy -> Int -> List Int
floatPositions [] _ = []
floatPositions (LTFloat::rest) i = i :: floatPositions rest (i + 1)
floatPositions (_::rest) i = floatPositions rest (i + 1)

elemInt : Int -> List Int -> Bool
elemInt _ [] = False
elemInt n (x::rest) = if n == x then True else elemInt n rest

hasFloatPty : List LTy -> Bool
hasFloatPty [] = False
hasFloatPty (LTFloat::_) = True
hasFloatPty (_::rest) = hasFloatPty rest

-- a 2-arm `match scrut { lit => A ; _ => B }` (literal pattern then wildcard, no
-- guards) as the equivalent `if scrut == lit then A else B`, so a match-bodied float
-- accumulator (the idiomatic floatsum form) reuses the CIf worker machinery. None for
-- any other shape → the function stays ineligible / falls back. == on a scalar literal
-- is value-equality (emitCmp routes Int/Char/Bool/String), matching match semantics.
decisionToIf : CExpr -> List CArm -> Option CExpr
decisionToIf scrut ((CArm (PLit l) [] a)::(CArm PWild [] b)::[]) =
  Some (CIf (CBinPrim "==" scrut (CLit l) "") a b)
decisionToIf _ _ = None

-- `self` does not occur free in `ex` (so the leaf is an ordinary value, safe to
-- compute via the wrapper-ABI path).
selfFreeW : String -> CExpr -> Bool
selfFreeW self ex = not (contains self (freeVars [] ex))

-- every leaf of the body is either a full-arity tail self-call or a self-free value;
-- descends CIf and a desugarable 2-arm match. Any self outside a tail self-call ⇒
-- ineligible.
floatWorkerOk : String -> Int -> CExpr -> Bool
floatWorkerOk self arity (CIf c t f) = selfFreeW self c
  && floatWorkerOk self arity t
  && floatWorkerOk self arity f
floatWorkerOk self arity (CDecision scrut arms tree) = match decisionToIf scrut arms
  Some cif => floatWorkerOk self arity cif
  None => selfFreeW self (CDecision scrut arms tree)
floatWorkerOk self arity (CBlock stmts) = floatWorkerBlockOk self arity stmts
floatWorkerOk self arity ex = match flattenApp ex []
  (CVar f _, args) =>
    if f == self then
      lengthS args == arity
    else
      selfFreeW self ex
  _ => selfFreeW self ex

-- a let-block body is worker-eligible if every leading CSLet binds a plain var with a
-- self-free RHS (the lets are evaluated before the tail) and the final stmt is a
-- CSExpr that is itself worker-eligible (the tail). Any other stmt shape (assign,
-- let-else, non-PVar pattern, non-CSExpr final) disqualifies → fall back.
floatWorkerBlockOk : String -> Int -> List CStmt -> Bool
floatWorkerBlockOk self arity ((CSExpr ex)::[]) = floatWorkerOk self arity ex
floatWorkerBlockOk self arity ((CSLet _ (PVar _) rhs)::rest) = selfFreeW self rhs
  && floatWorkerBlockOk self arity rest
floatWorkerBlockOk _ _ _ = False

floatWorkerEligible : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Bool
floatWorkerEligible e name pats ptys body =
  let rty = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig _ r) => r
    None => LTInt
  let isFloatRet = match rty
    LTFloat => True
    _ => False
  allPVar pats
    && leadingDictPats pats == 0
    && hasFloatPty ptys
    && isFloatRet
    && contains name (freeVars [] body)
    && floatWorkerOk name (lengthS pats) body

-- a float-RETURNING fn that gets a `$fw` double-ABI variant for float-context callers
-- (no self-recursive / float-param requirement — return-unboxing alone is a win).
-- Single-clause, all-PVar, non-dict, float return, body shape `emitFnBodyD` handles.
floatFwEligible : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Bool
floatFwEligible e name pats ptys body =
  let rty = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig _ r) => r
    None => LTInt
  let isFloatRet = match rty
    LTFloat => True
    _ => False
  allPVar pats
    && leadingDictPats pats == 0
    && isFloatRet
    && floatWorkerOk name (lengthS pats) body

-- module set of fns that have an emitted `@mdk_<name>__fw` (name → (arity, float
-- param positions)), for emitFloatLeaf to reroute float-context calls. Built once
-- per program (call sites may precede the callee's emission).
floatFwSetRef : Ref (List (String, (Int, List Int)))
floatFwSetRef = Ref []

-- collect the float-fw set from the program's single-clause function binds.
collectFloatFw : Emit -> List CBind -> List (String, (Int, List Int))
collectFloatFw _ [] = []
collectFloatFw e ((CBind name ((CClause pats body)::[]))::rest) =
  let ptys = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
    None => allInt pats
  if floatFwEligible e name pats ptys body then
    (name, (lengthS pats, floatPositions ptys 0)) :: collectFloatFw e rest
  else
    collectFloatFw e rest
collectFloatFw e (_::rest) = collectFloatFw e rest

-- module set of top-level fns that RETURN a float-returning closure (their single
-- clause body is a CLam whose body evidently returns Float, e.g.
-- `mkAdd base = (x => x + base)`).  A SATURATED call to such a fn yields a closure
-- whose own saturated call returns Float — so `recordFloatClosureResult` records the
-- call-result word in closureRetTyRef, letting indirectRetTy type `f 1.0` LTFloat
-- for `let f = mkAdd 100.0`.  Closes the returned-from-fn case of the closure-call-
-- result float-arith bug (the direct `let f = (x=>…)` case is handled at emitLam).
floatClosureFnsRef : Ref (List String)
floatClosureFnsRef = Ref []

collectFloatClosureFns : Emit -> List CBind -> List String
collectFloatClosureFns _ [] = []
collectFloatClosureFns e ((CBind name ((CClause pats body)::[]))::rest) =
  if isFloatClosureBody e name pats body then
    name :: collectFloatClosureFns e rest
  else
    collectFloatClosureFns e rest
collectFloatClosureFns e (_::rest) = collectFloatClosureFns e rest

isFloatClosureBody : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Bool
isFloatClosureBody e name pats (CLam _ lamBody) =
  let ptys = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
    None => allInt pats
  bodyFloatRet (paramEnv pats ptys 0) lamBody
isFloatClosureBody e name pats _ = False

-- after a SATURATED known-fn call whose callee returns a float-returning closure,
-- record the result word as a float-returning closure (closureRetTyRef) so a later
-- indirect call of it types its result LTFloat.
recordFloatClosureResult : Emit -> String -> Int -> Int -> (String, LTy) -> Unit
recordFloatClosureResult e fname argc arity (reg, _) =
  if argc == arity && contains fname floatClosureFnsRef.value then
    setRef closureRetTyRef ((reg, LTFloat)::closureRetTyRef.value)
  else
    ()

-- param decls with `double` at the float positions, `i64` elsewhere.
paramDeclsD : List Pat -> List Int -> Int -> String
paramDeclsD [] _ _ = ""
paramDeclsD ((PVar _)::rest) fp i =
  let ty = if elemInt i fp then "double" else "i64"
  let sep = match rest
    [] => ""
    _ => ", "
  "\{ty} %arg\{intToString i}\{sep}\{paramDeclsD rest fp (i + 1)}"
paramDeclsD _ _ _ = panic "llvm: float-worker params must be plain variables"

-- env for the worker: float params are unboxed doubles (LTFloatU → %argN holds a
-- double); other params keep their LTy.
paramEnvD : List Pat -> List LTy -> List Int -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
paramEnvD [] _ _ _ = []
paramEnvD ((PVar x)::rest) (ty::tys) fp i =
  let lty = if elemInt i fp then LTFloatU else ty
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, lty)) :: paramEnvD rest tys fp (i + 1)
paramEnvD ((PVar x)::rest) [] fp i =
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) :: paramEnvD rest [] fp (i + 1)
paramEnvD _ _ _ _ = panic "llvm: float-worker params must be plain variables"

-- a self-call's argument operands: `double <d>` at float positions, `i64 <w>` else,
-- emitted left-to-right.
emitWorkerArgs : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List Int -> Int -> List CExpr -> String
emitWorkerArgs _ _ _ _ [] = ""
emitWorkerArgs e env fp i (a::rest) =
  let op = match elemInt i fp
    True => "double " ++ emitFloatD e env a
    False =>
      let (w, _) = emitExpr e env a
      "i64 " ++ w
  let sep = match rest
    [] => ""
    _ => ", "
  op ++ sep ++ emitWorkerArgs e env fp (i + 1) rest

-- the worker body in tail position: CIf recurses; a full-arity tail self-call
-- musttails into the worker (NO box); any other (self-free) value rets a double.
emitFnBodyD : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Int -> Int -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity (CIf c t f) =
  let (cv, _) = emitExpr e env c
  let ci = untagInt e cv
  let cb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cb} = icmp ne i64 \{ci}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let thenL = "then" ++ n
  let elseL = "else" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cb}, label %\{thenL}, label %\{elseL}"
  let _ = emit e (thenL ++ ":")
  let _ = emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity t
  let _ = emit e (elseL ++ ":")
  emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity f
emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity (CDecision scrut arms tree) = match decisionToIf scrut arms
  Some cif => emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity cif
  None =>
    let d = emitFloatD e env (CDecision scrut arms tree)
    emit e ("  ret double " ++ d)
emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity (CBlock stmts) =
  emitFnBodyDBlock e env self worker fp arity stmts
emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity other =
  emitTailLeafD e env self worker fp arity other (flattenApp other [])

-- emit a worker-mode let-block: bind each leading CSLet (float RHS → unboxed double
-- LTFloatU, same as emitBlock; else normal), then emit the final CSExpr in tail
-- (double-return) mode. Gated by floatWorkerBlockOk, so the shape is guaranteed.
emitFnBodyDBlock : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Int -> Int -> List CStmt -> Unit
emitFnBodyDBlock e env self worker fp arity ((CSExpr ex)::[]) =
  emitFnBodyD e env self worker fp arity ex
emitFnBodyDBlock e env self worker fp arity ((CSLet _ (PVar x) rhs)::rest) =
  if staticIsFloat env rhs then
    let d = emitFloatD e env rhs
    emitFnBodyDBlock e ((x, (d, LTFloatU))::env) self worker fp arity rest
  else
    let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env rhs
    emitFnBodyDBlock e ((x, (v, ty))::env) self worker fp arity rest
emitFnBodyDBlock e env _ _ _ _ _ =
  gapU
    "float-worker block: unexpected statement shape (should be gated by floatWorkerBlockOk)"

emitTailLeafD : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> String -> List Int -> Int -> CExpr -> (CExpr, List CExpr) -> Unit
emitTailLeafD e env self worker fp arity _ (CVar f0 _, args)
  | f0 == self && lengthS args == arity =
    let ops = emitWorkerArgs e env fp 0 args
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = musttail call double @mdk_\{worker}(\{ops})"
    emit e ("  ret double " ++ r)
emitTailLeafD e env _ _ _ _ other _ =
  let d = emitFloatD e env other
  emit e ("  ret double " ++ d)

-- the wrapper's call args: `double <unbox %argN>` at float positions, `i64 %argN` else.
wrapperCallArgs : Emit -> List Int -> Int -> List Pat -> String
wrapperCallArgs _ _ _ [] = ""
wrapperCallArgs e fp i (_::rest) =
  let op = match elemInt i fp
    True => "double " ++ unboxFloat e ("%arg" ++ intToString i)
    False => "i64 %arg" ++ intToString i
  let sep = match rest
    [] => ""
    _ => ", "
  op ++ sep ++ wrapperCallArgs e fp (i + 1) rest

-- emit ONLY the double-ABI `$fw` variant of a float fn (no wrapper) — used both by
-- emitFloatWorkerFn (self-recursive: wrapper added separately) and for non-recursive
-- float fns emitted alongside their original i64-ABI definition.
emitFloatFwOnly : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFloatFwOnly e name pats ptys body =
  let fp = floatPositions ptys 0
  let arity = lengthS pats
  let worker = name ++ "__fw"
  let wenv = paramEnvD pats ptys fp 0
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define double @mdk_\{worker}(\{paramDeclsD pats fp 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let _ = emitFnBodyD e wenv name worker fp arity body
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

emitFloatWorkerFn : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFloatWorkerFn e name pats ptys body =
  let fp = floatPositions ptys 0
  let worker = name ++ "__fw"
  -- worker ($fw): float params as doubles, body emitted double-returning
  let _ = emitFloatFwOnly e name pats ptys body
  -- wrapper: original i64 ABI; unbox float params, call worker, box the result
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @mdk_\{name}(\{paramDecls pats 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let callArgs = wrapperCallArgs e fp 0 pats
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call double @mdk_\{worker}(\{callArgs})"
  let b = boxFloat e r
  let _ = emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ b)
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

emitFnClause : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFnClause e name pats body =
  let ptys = match sigLookup e name
    Some (FnSig ts _) => ts
    None => allInt pats
  if floatWorkerEligible e name pats ptys body then
    emitFloatWorkerFn e name pats ptys body
  else
    emitFnClauseStd e name pats ptys body

-- non-worker single-clause fn: emit the original i64-ABI define; if it is a
-- float-returning fn `emitFnBodyD` can handle, ALSO emit a standalone `$fw` variant
-- (the original stays for i64-ABI callers; float-context callers use `$fw`).
emitFnClauseStd : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFnClauseStd e name pats ptys body =
  let _ = emitFnClauseW e name pats ptys body
  if floatFwEligible e name pats ptys body then
    emitFloatFwOnly e name pats ptys body
  else
    ()

emitFnClauseW : Emit -> String -> List Pat -> List LTy -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFnClauseW e name pats ptys body =
  let env = paramEnv pats ptys 0
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i64 @mdk_\{name}(\{paramDecls pats 0}) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let _ = emitFnBody e env name body
  let _ = emit e "}"
  emit e ""

-- every parameter is a plain variable pattern (the slice-2 ABI requirement).
allPVar : List Pat -> Bool
allPVar [] = True
allPVar ((PVar _)::rest) = allPVar rest
allPVar _ = False

sigTable : Emit -> List (String, FnSig)
sigTable e = e.sigs.value

-- default param types when no signature was inferred (every param Int).
allInt : List Pat -> List LTy
allInt [] = []
allInt (_::rest) = LTInt :: allInt rest

paramDecls : List Pat -> Int -> String
paramDecls [] _ = ""
paramDecls [PVar _] i = "i64 %arg" ++ intToString i
paramDecls ((PVar _)::rest) i =
  "i64 %arg\{intToString i}, \{paramDecls rest (i + 1)}"
paramDecls _ _ =
  panic "llvm spike: function parameters must be plain variables in slice 2"

-- bind each parameter to %argN at its inferred static type (every param is an i64
-- word at the ABI; the type only drives int-vs-float instruction selection).
paramEnv : List Pat -> List LTy -> Int -> List (String, (String, LTy))
paramEnv [] _ _ = []
paramEnv ((PVar x)::rest) (ty::tys) i =
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, ty)) :: paramEnv rest tys (i + 1)
paramEnv ((PVar x)::rest) [] i =
  (x, ("%arg" ++ intToString i, LTInt)) :: paramEnv rest [] (i + 1)
paramEnv _ _ _ =
  panic "llvm spike: function parameters must be plain variables in slice 2"

-- emit a function body in TAIL position: every control path ends in a `ret`.
-- An `if` recurses into both arms (each arm is itself in tail position and rets);
-- a tail call to a known function emits `[musttail] call` + `ret`; anything else
-- is computed as a value and returned.
emitFnBody : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> Unit
emitFnBody e env self (CIf c t f) =
  let (cv, _) = emitExpr e env c
  let ci = untagInt e cv
  let cb = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cb} = icmp ne i64 \{ci}, 0"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let thenL = "then" ++ n
  let elseL = "else" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cb}, label %\{thenL}, label %\{elseL}"
  let _ = emit e (thenL ++ ":")
  let _ = emitFnBody e env self t
  let _ = emit e (elseL ++ ":")
  emitFnBody e env self f
emitFnBody e env self other =
  let (hd, args) = flattenApp other []
  match hd
    CVar fname0 _ =>
      let fname = canonFnName e fname0
      if isKnownFn e fname && hasArgs args then
        let argOps = emitArgs e env args
        let r = freshReg e
        let kw = if fname == self then "musttail call" else "call"
        let _ = emit e "  \{r} = \{kw} i64 @mdk_\{fname}(\{argDecls argOps})"
        emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ r)
      else emitValueRet e env other
    _ => emitValueRet e env other

emitValueRet : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> Unit
emitValueRet e env ex =
  let (v, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  emit e ("  ret i64 " ++ v)

-- ── classifying top-level bindings ──────────────────────────────────────────
-- a function binding = non-`main`, first clause has at least one parameter.
isFnBind : CBind -> Bool
isFnBind (CBind "main" _) = False
isFnBind (CBind _ ((CClause (_::_) _)::_)) = True
isFnBind _ = False

collectFns : List CBind -> List String
collectFns [] = []
collectFns (b::rest) =
  if isFnBind b then
    bindName b :: collectFns rest
  else
    collectFns rest

fnBinds : List CBind -> List CBind
fnBinds [] = []
fnBinds (b::rest) = if isFnBind b then b :: fnBinds rest else fnBinds rest

-- the nullary value bindings (non-`main`, no parameters), in source order.
valBinds : List CBind -> List CBind
valBinds [] = []
valBinds ((CBind "main" cs)::rest) = valBinds rest
valBinds (b::rest) = if isFnBind b then valBinds rest else b :: valBinds rest

-- value bindings ORDERED so a binding precedes the ones that reference it (E1b).
-- The init prologue computes each rhs once, eagerly, so a binding whose rhs reads
-- ANOTHER value global (`prog = andThen skip …`, `skip` defined later) must run
-- after that dependency or it captures the still-zero global cell — a null that
-- crashes at use.  Source order is NOT dependency order in general (the self-host
-- parser binds combinator values forward of their producers), so topologically
-- sort by intra-value-global references.  A genuine VALUE cycle (mutual recursion
-- through nullary globals — none arise in practice) keeps its members in source
-- order, the pre-existing behaviour (still caught by the forward-ref gap).
orderedValBinds : List CBind -> List CBind
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
orderedValBinds binds =
  let names = map bindName binds
  let (_, ordered) = topoValGo binds names binds [] []
  ordered

-- DFS post-order: visit each binding's value-global dependencies first, appending
-- the binding after them.  `visiting` guards against cycles (a back-edge is
-- skipped — its target stays where source order placed it).  Returns the grown
-- (done-names, ordered-binds) accumulators.
topoValGo : List CBind -> List String -> List CBind -> List String -> List CBind -> (List String, List CBind)
topoValGo _ _ [] done acc = (done, acc)
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
topoValGo all names (b::rest) done acc =
  let (done2, acc2) = topoValVisit all names b done acc []
  topoValGo all names rest done2 acc2

topoValVisit : List CBind -> List String -> CBind -> List String -> List CBind -> List String -> (List String, List CBind)
topoValVisit all names b done acc visiting
  | contains (bindName b) done = (done, acc)
  | contains (bindName b) visiting = (done, acc)
  | otherwise =
    let deps = intersectStr (dedupS (bindFreeVars b)) names
    let (done2, acc2) = topoValVisitDeps all names deps done acc (bindName b :: visiting)
    (bindName b :: done2, acc2 ++ [b])

topoValVisitDeps : List CBind -> List String -> List String -> List String -> List CBind -> List String -> (List String, List CBind)
topoValVisitDeps _ _ [] done acc _ = (done, acc)
topoValVisitDeps all names (d::rest) done acc visiting = match findBind d all
  Some db =>
    let (done2, acc2) = topoValVisit all names db done acc visiting
    topoValVisitDeps all names rest done2 acc2 visiting
  None => topoValVisitDeps all names rest done acc visiting

-- the value-global names a binding's rhs references EAGERLY (at init time) — i.e.
-- references NOT inside a lambda body.  A `CLam` captures a global by reference
-- and resolves it only when the closure is later CALLED (at parse time, long after
-- all globals are initialised), so a name appearing only inside a lambda is NOT an
-- init-order dependency.  Counting it would forge a false cycle between two
-- combinator globals that reference each other solely through their closures
-- (`stmtsLoop = orElse stmtsCons (pure [])` ← eager; `stmtsCons = do … stmtsLoop`
-- ← the back-reference is inside stmtsCons's `do` closure, lazy), and the
-- cycle-fallback would then emit them in source order, leaving the eager ref null.
bindFreeVars : CBind -> List String
bindFreeVars (CBind _ [CClause [] body]) = eagerVars [] body
bindFreeVars _ = []

findBind : String -> List CBind -> Option CBind
findBind _ [] = None
findBind name (b::rest) =
  if bindName b == name then
    Some b
  else
    findBind name rest

-- the elements of `xs` that also appear in `ys` (membership, order from xs).
intersectStr : List String -> List String -> List String
intersectStr [] _ = []
intersectStr (x::rest) ys
  | contains x ys = x :: intersectStr rest ys
  | otherwise = intersectStr rest ys

-- the initial globals registry (slice E1b): every value binding's name registered
-- UNinitialised (`False`) with a placeholder LTy.  The `@main` init prologue flips
-- each to `(True, <its rhs LTy>)` in source order as it computes the rhs, so a
-- reference to a not-yet-computed global is caught as a forward/mutual dependency.
initGlobalsReg : List CBind -> List (String, (Bool, LTy))
initGlobalsReg [] = []
initGlobalsReg (b::rest) = (bindName b, (False, LTInt)) :: initGlobalsReg rest

-- a fully-initialised globals registry: every value binding registered `True` with
-- a placeholder LTy.  The D4 gaps probe uses this so a function body referencing a
-- top-level value binding resolves to a global load (no spurious gap) — the probe
-- discards the emitted text, so the placeholder LTy is immaterial.
initGlobalsRegReady : List CBind -> List (String, (Bool, LTy))
initGlobalsRegReady [] = []
initGlobalsRegReady (b::rest) =
  (bindName b, (True, LTInt)) :: initGlobalsRegReady rest

-- emit a zero-initialised module global `@mdk_g_<name> = global i64 0` for each
-- value binding (into the side buffer, like string-literal globals — LLVM module
-- item order is irrelevant).  The `@main` prologue stores each binding's computed
-- rhs word here; references anywhere load it back.
emitGlobalDecls : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
emitGlobalDecls _ [] = ()
emitGlobalDecls e (b::rest) =
  -- PRELUDE half (issue #118): a top-level value's storage is DEFINED (and, in
  -- source order, INITIALISED) by `@mdk_program_main`, which lives in the program
  -- half — so prelude.o only declares it.  Defining it in both halves would be a
  -- duplicate symbol.
  let line = if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude then
    "@mdk_g_" ++ bindName b ++ " = external global i64"
  else
    "@mdk_g_" ++ bindName b ++ " = global i64 0"
  let _ = emitGlobal e line
  emitGlobalDecls e rest

-- ── function signature inference (slice 2b) ──────────────────────────────────
-- Core IR is type-erased, so to lower a Bool/Float-typed function boundary we
-- must RECOVER the static type of each parameter and result.  `typeOf` is the
-- pure (non-emitting) twin of the type-recovery that `emitExpr` already does as
-- it emits — it computes a CExpr's LTy from the signature table + a var→LTy env,
-- with no side effects.  `inferSigs` runs it to a two-pass fixpoint so a callee's
-- signature is available before its caller's body is typed (one extra pass lets a
-- caller see a callee defined later / mutual recursion settle).
--
-- Float/Bool ride the EXISTING uniform i64 word (Float boxed, Bool immediate), so
-- inference changes no LLVM type — only which instructions the body emits and the
-- print routine `main` picks.  Param/return remain i64 at the ABI, so musttail's
-- prototype-match invariant is untouched.

-- structural type of an expression given known fn signatures + a var→type env.
-- Unknown vars (top-level value globals) default to Int — the slice's existing
-- assumption (function bodies referencing globals are out of scope anyway).
typeOf : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> CExpr -> LTy
typeOf _ _ (CLit (LInt _)) = LTInt
typeOf _ _ (CLit (LFloat _)) = LTFloat
typeOf _ _ (CLit (LBool _)) = LTBool
typeOf _ _ (CLit (LString _)) = LTStr
typeOf _ _ (CLit (LChar _)) = LTChar
-- A list literal / range-list is a chain of Cons/Nil cells — a constructor
-- value (LTCon), NOT an Int.  Without this, the empty-list base clause of a
-- list-returning multi-clause fn (e.g. `externNames [] = []`) typed as LTInt, so
-- the fn's inferred return LTy was LTInt; a `++` on its result then fell to the
-- runtime-dispatch arm (default LTStr), and a CHAINED outer `++` saw LTStr and
-- statically emitted mdk_string_append on a List → memmove crash.  LTCon routes
-- the whole `++` chain through mdk_list_append correctly.
typeOf _ _ (CList _) = LTCon
typeOf _ _ (CRangeList _ _ _) = LTCon
typeOf _ env (CVar x _) = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some LTFloatU => LTFloat
  Some t => t
  None => LTInt
typeOf sigs env (CBinPrim op l r _)
  | isCmpOp op = LTBool
  | op == "::" = LTCon
  | otherwise = typeOf sigs env l
typeOf _ _ (CUnOp "!" _) = LTBool
typeOf sigs env (CUnOp "-" x) = typeOf sigs env x
typeOf sigs env (CIf _ t _) = typeOf sigs env t
typeOf sigs env (CLet False (PVar x) e1 e2) =
  typeOf sigs ((x, typeOf sigs env e1)::env) e2
typeOf sigs env (CLetGroup binds body) =
  typeOf sigs (letGroupEnv sigs env binds) body
typeOf sigs env (CBlock stmts) = blockTy sigs env stmts
typeOf sigs env (CDecision _ arms _) = armsRetTy sigs env arms
-- A constrained-function or return-position-method occurrence (CDict/CMethod)
-- carries the SAME callee name as the bare CVar would; its emit path
-- (`emitDictApp`/`emitMethod`) types the result via `fnRetTy e name`, so the pure
-- twin must mirror that and route through `callRetTy sigs name` — not default to
-- LTInt.  Without this, a Unit-returning constrained stdlib fn called inside a
-- user fn (e.g. `f s = println s`, where `println : Display a => a -> <IO> Unit`
-- lowers `f`'s body to `CDict "println" …`) inferred `f`'s return as LTInt,
-- mis-printing `main`'s `()` result as `0` (construct-gap F1; layers on #2b's
-- LTUnit IO-extern fix).
typeOf sigs env app =
  let (hd, args) = flattenApp app []
  match hd
    CVar fname _ => if hasArgs args then callRetTy sigs fname else LTInt
    CDict fname _ => callRetTy sigs fname
    CMethod fname _ _ _ => callRetTy sigs fname
    _ => LTInt

-- the return type of a known function from the sig table (Int if unknown).
-- the return type of a known function from the sig table (Int if unknown).
-- IO output externs (putStr/putStrLn/…) return Unit but are NOT user-fn binds,
-- so they are absent from `sigs` and would default to LTInt — typing a
-- Unit-returning wrapper (e.g. `println x = putStrLn (display x)`) as LTInt and
-- mis-rendering `main`'s result as `0` instead of `()` (Stage 3 #2b).  Resolve
-- them to LTUnit up front, matching the real emit path (`emitIoExtern`).
callRetTy : List (String, FnSig) -> String -> LTy
callRetTy sigs fname =
  if isIoExtern fname then LTUnit
  else match lookupAssoc fname sigs
    Some (FnSig _ rty) => rty
    None => LTInt

-- a decision's result type is its first arm's body type (all arms agree —
-- well-typed match).  Pattern-bound vars are unknown to the pure pass, so a body
-- referencing them defaults via typeOf's CVar/None branch; the fixtures' arms are
-- Int-typed, matching the runtime-captured `rty`.
armsRetTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> List CArm -> LTy
armsRetTy _ _ [] = LTInt
armsRetTy sigs env ((CArm _ _ body)::_) = typeOf sigs env body

letGroupEnv : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> List CBind -> List (String, LTy)
letGroupEnv _ env [] = env
letGroupEnv sigs env ((CBind name [CClause [] rhs])::rest) =
  letGroupEnv sigs ((name, typeOf sigs env rhs)::env) rest
letGroupEnv _ env _ = env

blockTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> List CStmt -> LTy
blockTy sigs env [CSExpr ex] = typeOf sigs env ex
blockTy sigs env ((CSExpr _)::rest) = blockTy sigs env rest
blockTy sigs env ((CSLet _ (PVar x) ex)::rest) =
  blockTy sigs ((x, typeOf sigs env ex)::env) rest
-- a constructor / nested block-let (emitBlock now binds these): type its pattern
-- vars in blockTy's env so a tail expr that uses a bound field still recurses
-- (the old catch-all stopped at LTInt for the whole tail).  blockTy is pure, so
-- it cannot consult the (Mut) ctor field-type table — each var gets LTInt, the
-- same default the catch-all produced.  emitBlock's bindPattern applies the
-- precise declared scalar types at emit time; this only types the result lookup.
blockTy sigs env ((CSLet _ pat ex)::rest) =
  blockTy sigs (patBindTys pat ++ env) rest
blockTy sigs env ((CSAssign x ex)::rest) =
  blockTy sigs ((x, typeOf sigs env ex)::env) rest
blockTy _ _ _ = LTInt

-- bind a non-PVar block-let pattern's vars for blockTy's env.  blockTy is pure,
-- so we cannot consult the (Mut) ctor field-type table; each var gets LTInt (the
-- same value the catch-all `blockTy _ _ _ = LTInt` produced for these lets).
patBindTys : Pat -> List (String, LTy)
patBindTys pat = mapVarLTInt (patVars pat)

mapVarLTInt : List String -> List (String, LTy)
mapVarLTInt [] = []
mapVarLTInt (x::rest) = (x, LTInt) :: mapVarLTInt rest

-- infer one parameter's type from its first typed USE in the body: the condition
-- of an `if` or the operand of `!` forces Bool; an operand of an arithmetic/
-- comparison binop shares its SIBLING's type; an argument to a known function
-- takes that function's parameter type.  None ⇒ no determining use found (the
-- caller defaults it to Int).

-- infer the type of `p` given the OTHER operand of a binop.  For ARITHMETIC ops the
-- operand is provably numeric (`+`/`-`/`*`/`/`/`%`, not the String `++`/`::`), so
-- when the other operand does NOT pin a concrete Int/Float (e.g. `a + b` where both
-- are tuple-destructured vars with no type source — typeOf DEFAULTS them to LTInt and
-- emits INTEGER arith on boxed-float pointers → garbage) fall back to LTNum, whose
-- arith/print route through the runtime tag-dispatched @mdk_num_* helpers (correct
-- for Int AND Float).  When the other operand DOES pin a concrete numeric type (a
-- literal, an env-typed var) use it — so int-literal-anchored arith (`a + 1`) keeps
-- its inline fast path.  Non-arith ops keep the historical `typeOf other` (a
-- comparison var has the same type as what it is compared to).
binOperandTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> String -> CExpr -> LTy
binOperandTy sigs env op other =
  if isArithOp op then match concreteNumTy sigs env other
    Some t => t
    None => LTNum
  else typeOf sigs env other

-- Some (concrete numeric LTy) when `e` is DEFINITELY typed; None when its type would
-- only be a guess (a bare var absent from env, a call result) — mirrors typeOf but
-- treats typeOf's LTInt-default as "unknown" so binOperandTy can pick LTNum instead.
concreteNumTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> CExpr -> Option LTy
concreteNumTy _ _ (CLit (LInt _)) = Some LTInt
concreteNumTy _ _ (CLit (LFloat _)) = Some LTFloat
concreteNumTy sigs env (CUnOp "-" x) = concreteNumTy sigs env x
concreteNumTy _ env (CVar x _) = match lookupAssoc x env
  Some LTFloatU => Some LTFloat
  Some t => Some t
  None => None
concreteNumTy sigs env (CBinPrim op l r _) =
  if isArithOp op then
    firstSome (concreteNumTy sigs env l) (concreteNumTy sigs env r)
  else
    None
concreteNumTy _ _ _ = None

paramUseTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> String -> CExpr -> Option LTy
paramUseTy sigs env p (CIf c t f) =
  if isVarNamed p c then
    Some LTBool
  else
    firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p t) (paramUseTy sigs env p f)
paramUseTy _ _ p (CUnOp "!" x) = if isVarNamed p x then Some LTBool else None
paramUseTy sigs env p (CUnOp _ x) = paramUseTy sigs env p x
-- `::` is NOT a symmetric binop: in `l :: r`, l is the ELEMENT and r is the
-- `List element` — their LTys differ.  So a var on the LEFT of `::` cannot take
-- the right operand's (list) type; doing so mistypes a String accumulator as
-- LTCon (e.g. `acc :: …` in splitLines), routing its OTHER `++` use to
-- mdk_list_append on a String → crash.  Skip the element side; a var on the
-- right of `::` is a list (LTCon).
paramUseTy sigs env p (CBinPrim "::" l r _) =
  if isVarNamed p r then
    Some LTCon
  else
    firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p l) (paramUseTy sigs env p r)
paramUseTy sigs env p (CBinPrim op l r _) =
  if isVarNamed p l then
    Some (binOperandTy sigs env op r)
  else if isVarNamed p r then
    Some (binOperandTy sigs env op l)
  else
    firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p l) (paramUseTy sigs env p r)
paramUseTy sigs env p (CLet _ _ e1 e2) =
  firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p e1) (paramUseTy sigs env p e2)
-- scan a block's statements for a determining use of `p` (a tuple/pattern-destructure
-- in a block passes its continuation here as `CBlock rest` so the bound var's type
-- can be inferred from later uses, not a dummy `0`).
paramUseTy sigs env p (CBlock stmts) = paramUseStmts sigs env p stmts
paramUseTy sigs env p app =
  let (hd, args) = flattenApp app []
  match hd
    CVar fname _ =>
      if hasArgs args then
        firstSome (argPosTy sigs env p fname args 0) (paramUseArgs sigs env p args)
      else
        None
    _ => paramUseArgs sigs env p args

-- if `p` is passed as the Nth argument to a known function, its type is that
-- function's Nth parameter type.
argPosTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> String -> String -> List CExpr -> Int -> Option LTy
argPosTy _ _ _ _ [] _ = None
argPosTy sigs env p fname (a::rest) i =
  if isVarNamed p a then
    nthParamTy sigs fname i
  else
    argPosTy sigs env p fname rest (i + 1)

-- the type of the Nth parameter of a known function (None if unknown).
nthParamTy : List (String, FnSig) -> String -> Int -> Option LTy
nthParamTy sigs fname i = match lookupAssoc fname sigs
  Some (FnSig ptys _) => nthTy ptys i
  None => None

nthTy : List LTy -> Int -> Option LTy
nthTy [] _ = None
nthTy (t::_) 0 = Some t
nthTy (_::rest) i = nthTy rest (i - 1)

paramUseArgs : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> String -> List CExpr -> Option LTy
paramUseArgs _ _ _ [] = None
paramUseArgs sigs env p (a::rest) =
  firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p a) (paramUseArgs sigs env p rest)

-- scan block statements for a determining use of `p` in any statement's expression
-- (CSExpr / CSLet rhs); unknown statement shapes are skipped.
paramUseStmts : List (String, FnSig) -> List (String, LTy) -> String -> List CStmt -> Option LTy
paramUseStmts _ _ _ [] = None
paramUseStmts sigs env p ((CSExpr ex)::rest) =
  firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p ex) (paramUseStmts sigs env p rest)
paramUseStmts sigs env p ((CSLet _ _ ex)::rest) =
  firstSome (paramUseTy sigs env p ex) (paramUseStmts sigs env p rest)
paramUseStmts sigs env p (_::rest) = paramUseStmts sigs env p rest

isVarNamed : String -> CExpr -> Bool
isVarNamed p (CVar x _) = p == x
isVarNamed _ _ = False

firstSome : Option a -> Option a -> Option a
firstSome (Some x) _ = Some x
firstSome None y = y

-- infer one function's full signature against the current (partial) sig table.
inferOneSig : List (String, FnSig) -> Option (List String, String) -> CBind -> FnSig
inferOneSig sigs dsig (CBind _ [CClause pats body]) =
  let ptys = inferParamTysSeed sigs dsig pats body 0
  let env = zipParamEnv pats ptys
  FnSig ptys (seedRetTy dsig (typeOf sigs env body))
inferOneSig sigs dsig (CBind _ (c::rest)) = inferMultiSig sigs dsig (c::rest)
inferOneSig _ _ _ = FnSig [] LTInt

-- E1: a DECLARED scalar param/return type (from `declSigTypesRef`, sourced from
-- the fn's `DTypeSig`) WINS over the body-use guess.  `seedParamTy` overrides the
-- i-th inferred param type with its declared scalar LTy when one is known (else
-- keeps the guess); `seedRetTy` likewise for the return.  ADDITIVE: an
-- unannotated fn (`dsig = None`) or a non-scalar declared type leaves the guess
-- untouched, so literal-anchored Float fns and Int fns are unchanged.
seedParamTy : Option (List String, String) -> Int -> LTy -> LTy
seedParamTy (Some (ptyNames, _)) i guessed = match declScalarAt ptyNames i
  Some t => t
  None => guessed
seedParamTy None i guessed = guessed

seedRetTy : Option (List String, String) -> LTy -> LTy
seedRetTy (Some (_, retName)) guessed = match fieldNameToLTy retName
  Some t => t
  None => guessed
seedRetTy None guessed = guessed

-- the declared scalar LTy at param index `i` of a declared param-type-head list,
-- or None when out of range / not a known scalar.
declScalarAt : List String -> Int -> Option LTy
declScalarAt names i = match nthName names i
  Some n => fieldNameToLTy n
  None => None

-- E1a: signature inference for a MULTI-CLAUSE binding.  A param's type is the
-- first type-revealing finding scanning clauses left-to-right (a literal pattern
-- names it directly; a plain-var pattern is inferred from that clause's body use
-- via `paramUseTy`; a constructor/tuple/wild pattern is an i64 word, left to
-- default Int).  The return type is the first clause's body typed under its
-- param env.  Without this, a multi-clause fn fell to `FnSig [] LTInt`, so its
-- non-Int params (`implParamEnvTyped`) and non-Int result (`fnRetTy` → print
-- selection) were both lost.  (Gap E1: a declared scalar param/return seeds over
-- the guess via `seedParamTy`/`seedRetTy`.)
inferMultiSig : List (String, FnSig) -> Option (List String, String) -> List CClause -> FnSig
inferMultiSig sigs dsig clauses =
  let arity = clauseArityC clauses
  let ptys = inferMultiParamTys sigs dsig clauses arity 0
  FnSig ptys (seedRetTy dsig (inferMultiRetTy sigs clauses ptys))

clauseArityC : List CClause -> Int
clauseArityC ((CClause pats _)::_) = listLen pats
clauseArityC [] = 0

inferMultiParamTys : List (String, FnSig) -> Option (List String, String) -> List CClause -> Int -> Int -> List LTy
inferMultiParamTys sigs dsig clauses arity i
  | i >= arity = []
  | otherwise = seedParamTy dsig i (paramTyAcross sigs clauses i) :: inferMultiParamTys sigs dsig clauses arity (i + 1)

-- the i-th param's type: the first informative finding across clauses, else Int.
paramTyAcross : List (String, FnSig) -> List CClause -> Int -> LTy
paramTyAcross sigs clauses i = match firstInformative sigs clauses i
  Some t => t
  None => LTInt

firstInformative : List (String, FnSig) -> List CClause -> Int -> Option LTy
firstInformative _ [] _ = None
firstInformative sigs ((CClause pats body)::rest) i =
  firstSome (clausePatTy sigs pats body i) (firstInformative sigs rest i)

-- one clause's contribution to the i-th param type: a plain var is inferred from
-- its body use; a literal pattern carries its literal type; a ctor/tuple/wild
-- pattern is uninformative (an i64 word, defaulted to Int by the caller).
clausePatTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Int -> Option LTy
clausePatTy sigs pats body i = match nthPat pats i
  Some (PVar x) => paramUseTy sigs [] x body
  Some (PLit l) => Some (litTy l)
  _ => None

litTy : Lit -> LTy
litTy (LInt _) = LTInt
litTy (LFloat _) = LTFloat
litTy (LBool _) = LTBool
litTy (LString _) = LTStr
litTy (LChar _) = LTChar
litTy LUnit = LTInt

nthPat : List Pat -> Int -> Option Pat
nthPat (p::_) 0 = Some p
nthPat (_::rest) i = nthPat rest (i - 1)
nthPat [] _ = None

-- the multi-clause return type: the first clause's body typed under that clause's
-- (var-pattern → inferred-param-type) env.
inferMultiRetTy : List (String, FnSig) -> List CClause -> List LTy -> LTy
inferMultiRetTy sigs ((CClause pats body)::_) ptys =
  typeOf sigs (zipParamEnv pats ptys) body
inferMultiRetTy _ [] _ = LTInt

inferParamTys : List (String, FnSig) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> List LTy
inferParamTys _ [] _ = []
inferParamTys sigs pats body
  | isArithSectionLam pats body = mapConst LTNum pats
inferParamTys sigs ((PVar x)::rest) body =
  let ty = match paramUseTy sigs [] x body
    Some t => t
    None => LTInt
  ty :: inferParamTys sigs rest body
inferParamTys sigs (_::rest) body = LTInt :: inferParamTys sigs rest body

-- G9: an operator-SECTION lambda — `\_a _b => _a OP _b` for an arithmetic OP,
-- the desugaring of a bare arithmetic section `(+)`/`(*)`/… (desugar.mdk
-- `sectionToCore (SecBare op)`).  Such a section flows as a first-class function
-- value (`fold (+) seed xs`), so it is POLYMORPHIC over `Num` — neither
-- statically Int nor Float.  Its two params carry no declared sig and no Float
-- literal anchor, so the default LTInt path would emit integer `add`/`mul`,
-- which is WRONG when the section is applied at Float (untags a boxed Float
-- pointer → garbage/SIGSEGV).  Seeding LTNum routes the body through the runtime
-- tag-dispatched @mdk_num_* helper (low-bit Int/Float discriminator), correct for
-- BOTH (Int: low-bit immediate → int op, byte-identical value to the old int
-- path; Float: boxed → float op).  Shape is EXACT: body is a single CBinPrim of
-- an arith op over the two params in order, so only the genuine section lambda is
-- affected — a lambda doing other work keeps its per-param inference.
isArithSectionLam : List Pat -> CExpr -> Bool
isArithSectionLam [PVar a, PVar b] (CBinPrim op (CVar la _) (CVar rb _) _) = isArithOp op
  && la == a
  && rb == b
isArithSectionLam _ _ = False

mapConst : a -> List b -> List a
mapConst _ [] = []
mapConst v (_::rest) = v :: mapConst v rest

-- like inferParamTys, but a DECLARED scalar type at param index `i` seeds (wins)
-- over the body-use guess (Gap E1: `double x = x + x : Float -> Float` has no
-- Float literal anchor, so the guess is LTInt → int arith on a boxed Float;
-- the declared `Float` overrides to LTFloat).  Index-tracked so each param's
-- declared type lines up by position.
inferParamTysSeed : List (String, FnSig) -> Option (List String, String) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Int -> List LTy
inferParamTysSeed sigs dsig pats body i =
  inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig pats body i i

-- like inferParamTysSeed, but tracks the DECLARED-param index `di` separately from
-- the emit/IR position `i`.  A dict-passing param (`$dict_<fn>_<slot>`, prepended
-- by elaborateModules to a `=>`-constrained fn) advances `i` but NOT `di`, so the
-- declared signature's param types — which DON'T include the synthesized dict
-- params — stay aligned to the source params.  Without this, a `Num a =>` fn's
-- real param sits one slot right of where `declScalarAt`/`isNumPolyParam` look
-- (the G3 root cause: x's LTNum seed was read at the dict's index → missed).
inferParamTysSeedD : List (String, FnSig) -> Option (List String, String) -> List Pat -> CExpr -> Int -> Int -> List LTy
inferParamTysSeedD _ _ [] _ _ _ = []
inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig ((PVar x)::rest) body i di
  | isDictParamName x =
    LTInt :: inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig rest body (i + 1) di
  | otherwise =
    let guessed = match paramUseTy sigs [] x body
      Some t => t
      None => LTInt
    let seeded = seedParamTy dsig di guessed
    let final = numPolySeed dsig di seeded (paramUsedInArith x body)
    final :: inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig rest body (i + 1) (di + 1)
-- G3: a param whose DECLARED type head is a type variable (`a` in
-- `double : Num a => a -> a`) and which is USED arithmetically is polymorphic
-- over Num — neither statically Int nor Float.  Seed LTNum so emitArith/emitPrint
-- route through the runtime tag-dispatched @mdk_num_* helpers.  A concrete
-- declared scalar (Int/Float/…) still wins via seedParamTy; a type-var param
-- NOT used in arithmetic (e.g. `id : a -> a`) keeps its guess (no LTNum).

inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig (_::rest) body i di =
  seedParamTy dsig di LTInt ::
    inferParamTysSeedD sigs dsig rest body (i + 1) (di + 1)

-- G3: override a type-var param's LTInt seed with LTNum when it's used in
-- arithmetic (a polymorphic Num operand); otherwise keep the seed unchanged.
numPolySeed : Option (List String, String) -> Int -> LTy -> Bool -> LTy
numPolySeed dsig i seeded usedArith
  | isNumPolyParam dsig i seeded && usedArith = LTNum
  | otherwise = seeded

-- G3: True when param index `i`'s declared type head is a type VARIABLE (a
-- lowercase-initial name like `a` — concrete cons are uppercase) and the
-- seedParamTy result is still LTInt (i.e. no concrete scalar declared/inferred).
-- This is the "polymorphic Num operand" signal.
isNumPolyParam : Option (List String, String) -> Int -> LTy -> Bool
isNumPolyParam (Some (ptyNames, _)) i LTInt = match nthName ptyNames i
  Some n => isTypeVarName n
  None => False
isNumPolyParam None i LTInt = True
isNumPolyParam dsig i _ = False

-- a type-variable head name is lowercase-initial (`a`, `elem`); a concrete type
-- (`Int`, `Float`, a user type) is uppercase-initial.  Empty ⇒ not a var.
isTypeVarName : String -> Bool
isTypeVarName n =
  let cs = stringToChars n
  numVarFirst cs

numVarFirst : Array Char -> Bool
numVarFirst cs
  | arrayLength cs == 0 = False
  | otherwise = isLowerCode (charCode (arrayGetUnsafe 0 cs))

isLowerCode : Int -> Bool
isLowerCode c = c >= 97 && c <= 122

-- G3: True when `p` is an operand of an arithmetic binop anywhere in the body.
paramUsedInArith : String -> CExpr -> Bool
paramUsedInArith p (CBinPrim op l r _) =
  if isArithOp op && (isVarNamed p l || isVarNamed p r) then
    True
  else
    paramUsedInArith p l || paramUsedInArith p r
paramUsedInArith p (CIf c t f) = paramUsedInArith p c
  || paramUsedInArith p t
  || paramUsedInArith p f
paramUsedInArith p (CUnOp _ x) = paramUsedInArith p x
paramUsedInArith p (CLet _ _ e1 e2) = paramUsedInArith p e1
  || paramUsedInArith p e2
paramUsedInArith p app =
  let (_, args) = flattenApp app []
  anyArithArg p args
paramUsedInArith _ _ = False

anyArithArg : String -> List CExpr -> Bool
anyArithArg _ [] = False
anyArithArg p (a::rest) = paramUsedInArith p a || anyArithArg p rest

zipParamEnv : List Pat -> List LTy -> List (String, LTy)
zipParamEnv ((PVar x)::ps) (t::ts) = (x, t) :: zipParamEnv ps ts
zipParamEnv (_::ps) (_::ts) = zipParamEnv ps ts
zipParamEnv _ _ = []

-- one inference pass: recompute every function's signature using the prior table.
-- (Gap E1: each binding's DECLARED signature, looked up by name in the installed
-- `declSigTypesRef` table, seeds its param/return types over the body-use guess.)
inferPass : List (String, FnSig) -> List CBind -> List (String, FnSig)
inferPass _ [] = []
inferPass sigs (b::rest) =
  (bindName b, inferOneSig sigs (declSigOf (bindName b)) b) ::
    inferPass sigs rest

-- fixpoint to two passes: pass 1 seeds signatures (callees defined earlier are
-- already typed); pass 2 lets a caller see a callee defined later / mutual
-- recursion settle.  Two passes suffice for the spike's call graphs (no deeper
-- type-flow chains than caller→callee→sibling exist in the subset).
inferSigs : List CBind -> List (String, FnSig)
inferSigs fns =
  let pass1 = inferPass [] fns
  inferPass pass1 fns

-- ── operator -> LLVM mnemonic tables ────────────────────────────────────────
intOp : String -> String
intOp "+" = "add"
intOp "-" = "sub"
intOp "*" = "mul"
intOp "/" = "sdiv"  -- OCaml `/` truncates toward zero; sdiv matches
intOp "%" = "srem"  -- OCaml `mod` sign-of-dividend; srem matches
intOp op = panic ("llvm spike: unsupported int operator " ++ op)

floatOp : String -> String
floatOp "+" = "fadd"
floatOp "-" = "fsub"
floatOp "*" = "fmul"
floatOp "/" = "fdiv"
floatOp "%" = "frem"  -- OCaml Float.rem == C fmod == LLVM frem
floatOp op = panic ("llvm spike: unsupported float operator " ++ op)

-- G3: the runtime tag-dispatched numeric helper for a POLYMORPHIC Num operand
-- (mdk_num_* branch on the low bit: Int → int op, boxed Float → float op).
numOp : String -> String
numOp "+" = "mdk_num_add"
numOp "-" = "mdk_num_sub"
numOp "*" = "mdk_num_mul"
numOp "/" = "mdk_num_div"
numOp "%" = "mdk_num_mod"
numOp op = panic ("llvm spike: unsupported num operator " ++ op)

intPred : String -> String
intPred "==" = "eq"
intPred "!=" = "ne"
intPred "<" = "slt"
intPred ">" = "sgt"
intPred "<=" = "sle"
intPred ">=" = "sge"
intPred op = panic ("llvm spike: unsupported comparison " ++ op)

-- IEEE-754 comparison predicates (issue #285).  `==`/`<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` are all
-- ORDERED (`o*`): every relational comparison against NaN is false, which is
-- correct IEEE semantics.  `!=` is the LONE exception — `a != b` must be TRUE
-- when either operand is NaN — so it needs the UNORDERED-or-not-equal predicate
-- `une`, not the ordered `one` (which is false for NaN and would break both IEEE
-- and the identity `(a != b) == !(a == b)`).  Matches eval and the wasm backend's
-- `f64.ne` (also une-equivalent).
floatPred : String -> String
floatPred "==" = "oeq"
floatPred "!=" = "une"
floatPred "<" = "olt"
floatPred ">" = "ogt"
floatPred "<=" = "ole"
floatPred ">=" = "oge"
floatPred op = panic ("llvm spike: unsupported comparison " ++ op)

isCmpOp : String -> Bool
isCmpOp op = op == "=="
  || op == "!="
  || op == "<"
  || op == ">"
  || op == "<="
  || op == ">="

-- ── records, tuples, and mutable refs (slice 5a) ────────────────────────────

emitTuple : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitTuple e env es =
  let words = emitArgs e env es
  if allConstWords words then
    (emitConstDictCell e (cellTag e "$tuple") words, LTCon)
  else
    emitCtorAlloc e "$tuple" words

emitRecordCreate : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> List CField -> (String, LTy)
emitRecordCreate e env name fields =
  let words = emitFieldExprs e env fields
  if allConstWords words then
    (emitConstDictCell e (cellTag e name) words, LTCon)
  else
    emitCtorAlloc e name words

emitFieldAccess : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> String -> String -> (String, LTy)
emitFieldAccess e env ex label recName =
  let (w, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  if label == "value" then
    let f = loadField e w 0
    (f, LTInt)
  else
    let idx = fieldIdxByName (recFieldTable e) recName label
    let f = loadField e w idx
    (f, LTInt)

-- Resolve a field's positional index by the typecheck-stamped record name when
-- known (recName ≠ "" and that record actually has the label); otherwise fall
-- back to today's label-only search (safety for unstamped / synthesized
-- accesses).  The by-name path is what makes two records sharing a field name at
-- DIFFERENT indices resolve to the correct offset.
fieldIdxByName : List (String, List String) -> String -> String -> Int
fieldIdxByName table recName label =
  if recName == "" then findFieldIdx table label
  else match lookupAssoc recName table
    Some labels => match indexOfStr label labels
      Some i => i
      None => findFieldIdx table label
    None => findFieldIdx table label

emitRecordUpdate : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> List CField -> (String, LTy)
emitRecordUpdate e env recName base updates =
  let (bw, _) = emitExpr e env base
  let firstLabel = cFieldFirstLabel updates
  match recordByName (recFieldTable e) recName firstLabel
    None => gapE ("CRecordUpdate: unknown record type for field '" ++ firstLabel ++ "'")
    Some (name, labels) =>
      let updatePairs = emitUpdatePairs e env updates
      let words = buildUpdateWords e bw labels updatePairs 0
      emitCtorAlloc e name words

-- Resolve the RECEIVER record of an update by the typecheck-stamped record name
-- (CRecordUpdate's 1st field) when it is usable — non-empty, present in the
-- field-order table, and actually owning the update label — else fall back to
-- today's label-only search (unstamped / synthesized updates).  The by-name path
-- is what makes two records that share a field name at DIFFERENT slot indices
-- update the correct slot; the label-only guess silently wrote the other
-- record's slot (bug #38).  Peer of fieldIdxByName on the access path.
recordByName : List (String, List String) -> String -> String -> Option (String, List String)
recordByName table recName label =
  if recName == "" then findRecordByLabel table label
  else match lookupAssoc recName table
    Some labels => match indexOfStr label labels
      Some _ => Some (recName, labels)
      None => findRecordByLabel table label
    None => findRecordByLabel table label

emitVariantUpdate : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> String -> CExpr -> List CField -> (String, LTy)
emitVariantUpdate e env con base updates =
  let (bw, _) = emitExpr e env base
  match lookupAssoc con (recFieldTable e)
    None => gapE ("CVariantUpdate: unknown variant type for constructor '" ++ con ++ "'")
    Some labels =>
      let updatePairs = emitUpdatePairs e env updates
      let words = buildUpdateWords e bw labels updatePairs 0
      emitCtorAlloc e con words

emitRefAlloc : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitRefAlloc e env [x] =
  let (xw, _) = emitExpr e env x
  emitCtorAlloc e "$ref" [xw]
emitRefAlloc e env _ = gapE "Ref expects exactly 1 argument"

emitSetRef : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitSetRef e env [r, x] =
  let (rw, _) = emitExpr e env r
  let (xw, _) = emitExpr e env x
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{rw} to ptr"
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 8"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{xw}, ptr \{fp}"
  ("1", LTInt)
emitSetRef e env _ = gapE "setRef expects exactly 2 arguments"

emitFieldExprs : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CField -> List String
emitFieldExprs _ _ [] = []
emitFieldExprs e env ((CField _ ex)::rest) =
  let (w, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  w :: emitFieldExprs e env rest

emitUpdatePairs : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CField -> List (String, String)
emitUpdatePairs _ _ [] = []
emitUpdatePairs e env ((CField k ex)::rest) =
  let (w, _) = emitExpr e env ex
  (k, w) :: emitUpdatePairs e env rest

buildUpdateWords : Emit -> String -> List String -> List (String, String) -> Int -> List String
buildUpdateWords _ _ [] _ _ = []
buildUpdateWords e bw (label::rest) updates i =
  let w = match lookupAssoc label updates
    Some newWord => newWord
    None => loadField e bw i
  w :: buildUpdateWords e bw rest updates (i + 1)

cFieldFirstLabel : List CField -> String
cFieldFirstLabel [] = panic "llvm spike: CRecordUpdate: empty update list"
cFieldFirstLabel ((CField k _)::_) = k

cFieldLabels : List CField -> List String
cFieldLabels [] = []
cFieldLabels ((CField k _)::rest) = k :: cFieldLabels rest

cFieldExprs : List CField -> List CExpr
cFieldExprs [] = []
cFieldExprs ((CField _ ex)::rest) = ex :: cFieldExprs rest

findFieldIdx : List (String, List String) -> String -> Int
findFieldIdx table label = match findRecordByLabel table label
  Some (_, labels) => match indexOfStr label labels
    Some i => i
    None => panic ("llvm spike: field '" ++ label ++ "' not found in record (internal error)")
  None => panic ("llvm spike: CFieldAccess: unknown field '" ++ label ++ "'")

findRecordByLabel : List (String, List String) -> String -> Option (String, List String)
findRecordByLabel [] _ = None
findRecordByLabel ((name, labels)::rest) label =
  if contains label labels then
    Some (name, labels)
  else
    findRecordByLabel rest label

indexOfStr : String -> List String -> Option Int
indexOfStr _ [] = None
indexOfStr x (y::rest) =
  if x == y then
    Some 0
  else
    map (+ 1) (indexOfStr x rest)

collectRecords : List CBind -> List (String, List String)
collectRecords [] = []
collectRecords ((CBind _ clauses)::rest) = collectRecordsClauses clauses
  ++ collectRecords rest

collectRecordsClauses : List CClause -> List (String, List String)
collectRecordsClauses [] = []
collectRecordsClauses ((CClause _ body)::rest) = scanExprRecords body
  ++ collectRecordsClauses rest

scanExprRecords : CExpr -> List (String, List String)
scanExprRecords (CRecord name fields) =
  (name, cFieldLabels fields) :: scanExprsRecords (cFieldExprs fields)
scanExprRecords (CFieldAccess ex _ _) = scanExprRecords ex
scanExprRecords (CRecordUpdate _ base updates) = scanExprRecords base
  ++ scanExprsRecords (cFieldExprs updates)
scanExprRecords (CVariantUpdate _ base updates) = scanExprRecords base
  ++ scanExprsRecords (cFieldExprs updates)
scanExprRecords (CTuple es) = scanExprsRecords es
scanExprRecords (CApp f a) = scanExprRecords f ++ scanExprRecords a
scanExprRecords (CLam _ body) = scanExprRecords body
scanExprRecords (CIf c t f) = scanExprRecords c
  ++ scanExprRecords t
  ++ scanExprRecords f
scanExprRecords (CBinPrim _ l r _) = scanExprRecords l ++ scanExprRecords r
scanExprRecords (CUnOp _ x) = scanExprRecords x
scanExprRecords (CLet _ _ e1 e2) = scanExprRecords e1 ++ scanExprRecords e2
scanExprRecords (CLetGroup binds body) = collectRecords binds
  ++ scanExprRecords body
scanExprRecords (CBlock stmts) = scanStmtsRecords stmts
scanExprRecords (CDecision scrut arms _) = scanExprRecords scrut
  ++ scanArmsRecords arms
scanExprRecords (CMatch scrut arms) = scanExprRecords scrut
  ++ scanArmsRecords arms
scanExprRecords (CArray es) = scanExprsRecords es
scanExprRecords (CList es) = scanExprsRecords es
scanExprRecords (CRangeArray lo hi _) = scanExprRecords lo ++ scanExprRecords hi
scanExprRecords (CRangeList lo hi _) = scanExprRecords lo ++ scanExprRecords hi
scanExprRecords (CIndex a i) = scanExprRecords a ++ scanExprRecords i
scanExprRecords (CStringIndex a i) = scanExprRecords a ++ scanExprRecords i
scanExprRecords (CStringSlice a lo hi _) = scanExprRecords a
scanExprRecords (CListIndex a i) = scanExprRecords a ++ scanExprRecords i
scanExprRecords (CListSlice a lo hi _) = scanExprRecords a
  ++ scanExprRecords lo
  ++ scanExprRecords hi
scanExprRecords (CSlice a lo hi _) = scanExprRecords a
  ++ scanExprRecords lo
  ++ scanExprRecords hi
scanExprRecords _ = []

scanExprsRecords : List CExpr -> List (String, List String)
scanExprsRecords [] = []
scanExprsRecords (e::rest) = scanExprRecords e ++ scanExprsRecords rest

scanArmsRecords : List CArm -> List (String, List String)
scanArmsRecords [] = []
scanArmsRecords ((CArm _ _ body)::rest) = scanExprRecords body
  ++ scanArmsRecords rest

scanStmtsRecords : List CStmt -> List (String, List String)
scanStmtsRecords [] = []
scanStmtsRecords ((CSExpr ex)::rest) = scanExprRecords ex
  ++ scanStmtsRecords rest
scanStmtsRecords ((CSLet _ _ ex)::rest) = scanExprRecords ex
  ++ scanStmtsRecords rest
scanStmtsRecords ((CSAssign _ ex)::rest) = scanExprRecords ex
  ++ scanStmtsRecords rest
scanStmtsRecords (_::rest) = scanStmtsRecords rest

-- ── arrays + ranges (slice 8) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- VALUE REPRESENTATION (RUNTIME-DESIGN.md §8 — see also §2.4a-5 in STAGE2-DESIGN.md):
-- an array is a BOXED heap cell with the same layout as an ADT cell but with the
-- header word = the raw element count (not a constructor hash):
--   [ 0: i64 raw_len | 8: elem0 | 16: elem1 … ]
-- `loadTag` thus reads the raw element count; `loadField e w i` reads element i
-- (at offset 8*(i+1)), exactly the ADT field convention.  Allocation reuses
-- @mdk_alloc; element words are uniform i64 (tagged Ints for range elements).
-- BOUNDS CHECKS: OOB calls @mdk_oob() (noreturn; prints + exits, matching eval's
-- panic).  Fixtures are in-bounds so the check never fires in practice.
-- LOOPS: CRangeArray and CSlice use an alloca-counter loop (the first non-recursion
-- loop in the spike — avoids phi instructions and matches the alloca/store/load
-- pattern the rest of the emitter uses for multi-path merges).
-- SCOPE: CList and CRangeList are covered by slice 9 ("lists" section below).
-- list MATCH (HCons/HNil) works from slice 5b.
-- SPIKE-REP NOTES (slice 8; extending (a)–(p)):
--   (q) ARRAY CELL — header word = raw_len (not hashName), so `loadTag` gives the
--       count directly.  Element i is at offset 8*(i+1), consistent with `loadField`.
--   (r) BOUNDS CHECK — two icmps (idx < 0 || idx >= len) OR'd, br to an @mdk_oob
--       block (call + unreachable); the ok path continues inline.
--   (s) RANGE LOOP — alloca i64 counter + load/store inside the loop; each element
--       = `tagInt(lo_raw + i)`.  CFG: br to cond block → load/icmp/br → body
--       (compute+store+increment) → back to cond; done block receives the br on
--       exit.  No phi nodes (consistent with the rest of the emitter).
--   (t) SLICE LOOP — same alloca-counter pattern; each iteration loads element
--       (lo_raw+i) from the source array via `loadFieldDyn` and stores it to dest
--       field i.

-- allocate an array cell with a STATICALLY known element list; header = raw count.
emitArrayAlloc : Emit -> Int -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitArrayAlloc e n fields =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{intToString (8 * (n + 1))})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{intToString n}, ptr \{p}"
  let _ = storeFields e p fields 0
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTCon)

-- allocate an array cell with a DYNAMIC element count (for ranges and slices);
-- returns the raw ptr register (caller fills element fields).
emitArrayDynAlloc : Emit -> String -> String
emitArrayDynAlloc e len_raw =
  let sz = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{sz} = add i64 \{len_raw}, 1"
  let bytes = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{bytes} = mul i64 \{sz}, 8"
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = call ptr @mdk_alloc(i64 \{bytes})"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{len_raw}, ptr \{p}"
  p

-- CArray [e0, e1, …]: allocate a static-size array cell, emit each element,
-- store them as fields, return the cell pointer word.
emitArrayCreate : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArrayCreate e env es =
  let words = emitArgs e env es
  emitArrayAlloc e (lengthS words) words

-- load a cell field at a DYNAMIC index (offset = (idxRaw+1)*8).  Used for CIndex
-- and the CSlice copy loop.
loadFieldDyn : Emit -> String -> String -> String
loadFieldDyn e ptrWord idxRaw =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{ptrWord} to ptr"
  let off1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off1} = add i64 \{idxRaw}, 1"
  let off = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off} = mul i64 \{off1}, 8"
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{off}"
  let f = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{f} = load i64, ptr \{fp}"
  f

-- store `val` (an i64 word) to element at dynamic index `idxRaw` of a boxed array.
-- mirrors loadFieldDyn verbatim, but ends with a store instead of a load.
storeFieldDyn : Emit -> String -> String -> String -> Unit
storeFieldDyn e ptrWord idxRaw val =
  let p = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{p} = inttoptr i64 \{ptrWord} to ptr"
  let off1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off1} = add i64 \{idxRaw}, 1"
  let off = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off} = mul i64 \{off1}, 8"
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{off}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{val}, ptr \{fp}"
  ()

-- CIndex arr i: bounds-check, then load element at dynamic offset.
emitArrayIndex : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArrayIndex e env a i =
  let (arr_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (idx_w, _) = emitExpr e env i
  let idx_raw = untagInt e idx_w
  let arr_len = loadTag e arr_w
  let c1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{c1} = icmp slt i64 \{idx_raw}, 0"
  let c2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{c2} = icmp sge i64 \{idx_raw}, \{arr_len}"
  let oob = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{oob} = or i1 \{c1}, \{c2}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let oobL = "oob" ++ n
  let okL = "idx_ok" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{oob}, label %\{oobL}, label %\{okL}"
  let _ = emit e (oobL ++ ":")
  let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_oob()"
  let _ = emit e "  unreachable"
  let _ = emit e (okL ++ ":")
  let r = loadFieldDyn e arr_w idx_raw
  (r, LTInt)

-- CRangeArray lo hi incl: compute count, alloc, fill with alloca-counter loop.
emitRangeArray : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> Bool -> (String, LTy)
emitRangeArray e env lo hi incl =
  let (lo_w, _) = emitExpr e env lo
  let (hi_w, _) = emitExpr e env hi
  let lo_raw = untagInt e lo_w
  let hi_raw = untagInt e hi_w
  let hi_adj = if incl then
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = add i64 \{hi_raw}, 1"
    r
  else hi_raw
  let count = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{count} = sub i64 \{hi_adj}, \{lo_raw}"
  let p = emitArrayDynAlloc e count
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let condL = "rng_cond" ++ n
  let bodyL = "rng_body" ++ n
  let doneL = "rng_done" ++ n
  let ic = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ ic ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ ic)
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (condL ++ ":")
  let iv = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv} = load i64, ptr \{ic}"
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp sge i64 \{iv}, \{count}"
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{doneL}, label %\{bodyL}"
  let _ = emit e (bodyL ++ ":")
  let elem_sum = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{elem_sum} = add i64 \{lo_raw}, \{iv}"
  let elem_w = tagInt e elem_sum
  let off1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off1} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let off = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off} = mul i64 \{off1}, 8"
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{off}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{elem_w}, ptr \{fp}"
  let iv2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv2} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{iv2}, ptr \{ic}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (doneL ++ ":")
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTCon)
