-- CSlice arr lo hi incl: compute new_len, alloc, copy elements with loop.
emitArraySlice : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> CExpr -> Bool -> (String, LTy)
emitArraySlice e env a lo hi incl =
  let (arr_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (lo_w, _) = emitExpr e env lo
  let (hi_w, _) = emitExpr e env hi
  let lo_raw = untagInt e lo_w
  let hi_raw = untagInt e hi_w
  let end_raw = if incl then
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = add i64 \{hi_raw}, 1"
    r
  else hi_raw
  let new_len = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{new_len} = sub i64 \{end_raw}, \{lo_raw}"
  let p = emitArrayDynAlloc e new_len
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let condL = "sl_cond" ++ n
  let bodyL = "sl_body" ++ n
  let doneL = "sl_done" ++ n
  let ic = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ ic ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ ic)
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (condL ++ ":")
  let iv = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv} = load i64, ptr \{ic}"
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp sge i64 \{iv}, \{new_len}"
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{doneL}, label %\{bodyL}"
  let _ = emit e (bodyL ++ ":")
  let src_idx = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{src_idx} = add i64 \{lo_raw}, \{iv}"
  let elem = loadFieldDyn e arr_w src_idx
  let dst_off1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{dst_off1} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let dst_off = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{dst_off} = mul i64 \{dst_off1}, 8"
  let dst_fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{dst_fp} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{dst_off}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{elem}, ptr \{dst_fp}"
  let iv2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv2} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{iv2}, ptr \{ic}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (doneL ++ ":")
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTCon)

-- CStringIndex s i: codepoint-indexed Char.  The String runtime rep is a packed
-- UTF-8 cell (NOT an i64-element array), so we mirror the interpreter's
-- stringIndexCp: convert to a Char array via @mdk_string_to_chars (each element
-- already a Char immediate (cp<<1)|1, header = codepoint count), bounds-check the
-- codepoint index against that count, then load element i.  Result LTy = LTChar.
-- Bounds violation calls @mdk_oob (matching the interpreter's index-OOB panic).
emitStringIndex : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitStringIndex e env a i =
  let (str_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (idx_w, _) = emitExpr e env i
  let chars = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{chars} = call i64 @mdk_string_to_chars(i64 \{str_w})"
  let idx_raw = untagInt e idx_w
  let arr_len = loadTag e chars
  let c1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{c1} = icmp slt i64 \{idx_raw}, 0"
  let c2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{c2} = icmp sge i64 \{idx_raw}, \{arr_len}"
  let oob = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{oob} = or i1 \{c1}, \{c2}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let oobL = "soob" ++ n
  let okL = "sidx_ok" ++ n
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{oob}, label %\{oobL}, label %\{okL}"
  let _ = emit e (oobL ++ ":")
  let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_oob()"
  let _ = emit e "  unreachable"
  let _ = emit e (okL ++ ":")
  let r = loadFieldDyn e chars idx_raw
  (r, LTChar)

-- CStringSlice s lo hi incl: codepoint substring.  Mirror the interpreter's
-- evalSliceInt VString = stringSlice lo (incl ? hi+1 : hi) s — which CLAMPS lo/hi
-- to [0, cp_count] (no OOB panic, unlike the array slice).  Reuse the
-- @mdk_string_slice extern (it >>1-untags the tagged Int bounds internally); for
-- the inclusive form hi+1 in value space is +2 on the tagged word (tag = n*2+1).
emitStringSlice : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> CExpr -> Bool -> (String, LTy)
emitStringSlice e env a lo hi incl =
  let (str_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (lo_w, _) = emitExpr e env lo
  let (hi_w, _) = emitExpr e env hi
  let hi_adj = if incl then
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = add i64 \{hi_w}, 2"
    r
  else hi_w
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_string_slice(i64 \{lo_w}, i64 \{hi_adj}, i64 \{str_w})"
  (r, LTStr)

-- CListIndex xs i: walk the Cons chain to the i-th element.  A List is a chain of
-- Cons cells ([tag|head@8|tail@16], Nil = odd immediate), NOT an i64-element
-- array, so we can't array-GEP it.  Mirror the interpreter's listNthAt: walk
-- (i decrementing) returning the head once i<=0, panic (@mdk_oob) on running off
-- the end.  @mdk_list_index does the walk in C (>>1-untags the tagged index).
-- Result word is the stored head (same shape as an array load → LTInt).
emitListIndex : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitListIndex e env a i =
  let (lst_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (idx_w, _) = emitExpr e env i
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_list_index(i64 \{lst_w}, i64 \{idx_w})"
  (r, LTInt)

-- CListSlice xs lo hi incl: collect elements at indices [lo, hi) into a NEW list.
-- Mirror the interpreter's listSliceV/listSliceGo — which CLAMPS (no OOB panic):
-- walks the chain with a running index, keeps heads in [lo, hi), stops at hi or
-- end-of-list.  @mdk_list_slice does the walk + rebuild in C (>>1-untags lo/hi);
-- for the inclusive form hi+1 in value space is +2 on the tagged word (tag n*2+1).
-- Result is a boxed Cons/Nil list → LTCon (same as the array slice).
emitListSlice : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> CExpr -> Bool -> (String, LTy)
emitListSlice e env a lo hi incl =
  let (lst_w, _) = emitExpr e env a
  let (lo_w, _) = emitExpr e env lo
  let (hi_w, _) = emitExpr e env hi
  let hi_adj = if incl then
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = add i64 \{hi_w}, 2"
    r
  else hi_w
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = call i64 @mdk_list_slice(i64 \{lst_w}, i64 \{lo_w}, i64 \{hi_adj})"
  (r, LTCon)

-- arrayMakeWith n f: INLINE INTRINSIC.  Allocates a dynamic array of `n` elements;
-- fills element i by calling the Medaka closure `f` with the tagged index `i`.
-- Shape mirrors emitRangeArray (alloca-counter loop) + emitIndirect (closure call).
-- No C extern; the closure is called directly from the emitted loop body.
emitArrayMakeWith : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitArrayMakeWith e env [nExpr, fExpr] =
  let (n_w, _) = emitExpr e env nExpr
  let (fw, _) = emitExpr e env fExpr
  let count = untagInt e n_w
  let p = emitArrayDynAlloc e count
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let condL = "mw_cond" ++ n
  let bodyL = "mw_body" ++ n
  let doneL = "mw_done" ++ n
  let ic = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ ic ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e ("  store i64 0, ptr " ++ ic)
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (condL ++ ":")
  let iv = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv} = load i64, ptr \{ic}"
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp sge i64 \{iv}, \{count}"
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{doneL}, label %\{bodyL}"
  let _ = emit e (bodyL ++ ":")
  let ti = tagInt e iv
  let cp = loadField e fw 0
  let fp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{fp} = inttoptr i64 \{cp} to ptr"
  let elem = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{elem} = call i64 \{fp}(i64 \{fw}, i64 \{ti})"
  let off1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off1} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let off = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{off} = mul i64 \{off1}, 8"
  let ep = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{ep} = getelementptr i8, ptr \{p}, i64 \{off}"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{elem}, ptr \{ep}"
  let iv2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv2} = add i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{iv2}, ptr \{ic}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (doneL ++ ":")
  let w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{w} = ptrtoint ptr \{p} to i64"
  (w, LTCon)
emitArrayMakeWith e env _ = gapE "arrayMakeWith takes two arguments"

-- ── lists (slice 9) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- VALUE REPRESENTATION: a list is a chain of boxed Cons cells (matching the
-- slice-5b match heads HCons/HNil, which test `cellTag` of "Cons"/"Nil").
-- The built-in list is not user-declared, so "Cons"/"Nil" are absent from the
-- ctor→type table and `cellTag` gives them the reserved list-type id with ordinals
-- 0 (Cons) and 1 (Nil) — UNLESS a user `data` owns those names, in which case both
-- the list literal and the user ctor resolve to that type's ordinal (they alias,
-- exactly as they did under the old hash).  Cell layouts (8-byte words):
--   Nil  → odd immediate (nullary; low bit 1)                  (no cell allocated)
--   Cons → [ i64 cellTag "Cons" | i64 head | i64 tail ]   (3-word cell)
-- Construction uses emitCtorAlloc "Nil" [] / emitCtorAlloc "Cons" [hw, tw]
-- verbatim — the SAME tag the slice-5b switch tests, so construction
-- and match agree by construction.
--
-- SPIKE-REP NOTES (slice 9; extending (a)–(t)):
--   (u) CList [e0…en] is RIGHT-FOLDED INLINE (compile-time unroll): N+1 allocs
--       emitted as a straight-line sequence from tail to head.  No loop, no
--       stack growth — the list size is statically known.  Consistent with
--       emitTuple (which also unrolls inline for a static arity).
--   (v) CRangeList lo hi incl uses an ALLOCA-COUNTER LOOP (back-to-front):
--       initialise tail=Nil, i=count-1; while i≥0 prepend Cons(lo+i, tail) and
--       decrement i.  Back-to-front builds the list in ascending order (lo…hi)
--       without reversing.  An alloca i64 `acc` holds the current list head word
--       (a cell pointer as i64), so the same alloca/store/load discipline the
--       rest of the emitter uses for multi-path merges.  An empty range (count≤0)
--       returns Nil immediately (i=count-1 < 0 on first test).  This approach
--       matches the slice-8 loop pattern and avoids stack overflow on large ranges.

-- CList [e0, e1, …, en]: right-fold to Cons cells, innermost first.
emitList : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> (String, LTy)
emitList e _ [] = emitCtorAlloc e "Nil" []
emitList e env xs =
  let words = emitListWords e env xs
  if allConstWords words then emitConstList e words else emitRtList e words

-- emit every element to its operand word, head-to-tail (same order as the original
-- recursive emitList's element emission).
emitListWords : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CExpr -> List String
emitListWords _ _ [] = []
emitListWords e env (x::xs) =
  let (w, _) = emitExpr e env x
  w :: emitListWords e env xs

-- runtime cons chain from already-emitted element words (back-to-front), IR-identical
-- to the original recursive emitList for the non-constant case.
emitRtList : Emit -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitRtList e [] = emitCtorAlloc e "Nil" []
emitRtList e (w::rest) =
  let (tw, _) = emitRtList e rest
  emitCtorAlloc e "Cons" [w, tw]

-- a list literal whose elements are ALL compile-time constants: build the cons spine
-- as a chain of `internal constant` module globals (one cell per element, tail = the
-- next cell's pointer-constant; the base is the Nil immediate) instead of allocating
-- N cons cells at every evaluation. `==` on lists is structural, so sharing/dedup is
-- value-safe. Deduped via the same constant-cell cache as dict witnesses.
emitConstList : Emit -> List String -> (String, LTy)
emitConstList e words =
  let nilImm = intToString (cellTag e "Nil" * 2 + 1)
  let head = emitConstListGo e (cellTag e "Cons") (reverseL words) nilImm
  (head, LTCon)

emitConstListGo : Emit -> Int -> List String -> String -> String
emitConstListGo _ _ [] tl = tl
emitConstListGo e consTag (w::rest) tl =
  let cell = emitConstDictCell e consTag [w, tl]
  emitConstListGo e consTag rest cell

-- CRangeList lo hi incl: build an ascending list [lo…hi] (or [lo…<hi]) via a
-- back-to-front alloca-counter loop.  An alloca i64 `acc` holds the current
-- head pointer; a second alloca i64 `ic` holds the loop counter (count-1 downto 0).
emitRangeList : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> CExpr -> CExpr -> Bool -> (String, LTy)
emitRangeList e env lo hi incl =
  let (lo_w, _) = emitExpr e env lo
  let (hi_w, _) = emitExpr e env hi
  let lo_raw = untagInt e lo_w
  let hi_raw = untagInt e hi_w
  let hi_adj = if incl then
    let r = freshReg e
    let _ = emit e "  \{r} = add i64 \{hi_raw}, 1"
    r
  else hi_raw
  let count = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{count} = sub i64 \{hi_adj}, \{lo_raw}"
  let (nil_w, _) = emitCtorAlloc e "Nil" []
  let acc = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ acc ++ " = alloca i64")
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{nil_w}, ptr \{acc}"
  let ic = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e ("  " ++ ic ++ " = alloca i64")
  let im1 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{im1} = sub i64 \{count}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{im1}, ptr \{ic}"
  let n = intToString (freshLocal e)
  let condL = "rl_cond" ++ n
  let bodyL = "rl_body" ++ n
  let doneL = "rl_done" ++ n
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (condL ++ ":")
  let iv = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv} = load i64, ptr \{ic}"
  let cmp = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{cmp} = icmp slt i64 \{iv}, 0"
  let _ = emit e "  br i1 \{cmp}, label %\{doneL}, label %\{bodyL}"
  let _ = emit e (bodyL ++ ":")
  let elem_raw = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{elem_raw} = add i64 \{lo_raw}, \{iv}"
  let elem_w = tagInt e elem_raw
  let tail_w = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{tail_w} = load i64, ptr \{acc}"
  let (cons_w, _) = emitCtorAlloc e "Cons" [elem_w, tail_w]
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{cons_w}, ptr \{acc}"
  let iv2 = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{iv2} = sub i64 \{iv}, 1"
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{iv2}, ptr \{ic}"
  let _ = emit e ("  br label %" ++ condL)
  let _ = emit e (doneL ++ ":")
  let r = freshReg e
  let _ = emit e "  \{r} = load i64, ptr \{acc}"
  (r, LTCon)
-- Seed the accumulator with Nil.

-- Counter starts at count-1 (back-to-front).

-- ── program ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Emit a whole CProgram as a textual LLVM module: the runtime declares, then one
-- LLVM function per top-level `name p… = …` binding (slice 2), then a single
-- `@main` that computes the top-level nullary value bindings (in source order),
-- then `main`'s body, then a type-directed print.
export emitProgram : CProgram -> String
emitProgram (CProgram groups ctorArs ctorTypes implEntries) =
  let e = Emit {
    -- issue #118: the two halves are emitted by INDEPENDENT emitter runs, so they
    -- get disjoint gensym ranges or their lifted defines collide — see
    -- programHalfIdBase.  Mode 0 keeps starting at 0, so its IR is unchanged.
    counter = Ref (if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfProgram then programHalfIdBase else 0),
    buf = Ref [],
    fnNames = Ref (collectFns groups),
    sigs = Ref (inferSigs (fnBinds groups)),
    ctors = Ref ctorArs,
    lams = Ref [],
    recFields = Ref (collectRecords groups),
    implEntries = Ref implEntries,
    ctorTypes = Ref ctorTypes,
    -- The PRELUDE half emits no `@mdk_program_main`, hence no init prologue to mark
    -- the globals initialised as it computes them — so they start READY, exactly as
    -- the non-emitting gaps driver does (initGlobalsRegReady).  Without this every
    -- prelude body that loads a global would hit the forward-dependency gap.
    globalsReg = Ref (if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude then
      initGlobalsRegReady (valBinds groups)
    else
      initGlobalsReg (valBinds groups)),
  }
  let _ = resetDistinctTypeNames ()
  let _ = setRef dictConstCacheRef []
  let _ = setRef dispCacheRef []
  let _ = installKnownFnMap (collectFns groups)
  let _ = installCtorMap ctorArs
  let _ = installSigMap (sigTable e)
  let _ = installDefArityMap e (fnBinds groups)
  -- (b′) dispatch-graph TMC: run the SHARED detection (backend/trmc_analysis.mdk)
  -- before any fn emit, so emitFn can route group members/roots.  Hooks: the LLVM
  -- canonFnName + a clause-derived fn-arity table with wasm's progFnArity contract
  -- (-1 when unknown); v5's stage-1-claims hook is the LLVM Stage-1 gate (no
  -- uniform-ctor condition — the backends' hooks unify when wasm's gate retires).
  -- gDispGroupEtaStable then drops any group containing a single-clause member
  -- that eta-saturation would re-arity (deficit > 0 — such a member's inlined
  -- clause-arity body would diverge from the ordinary define's ABI); dropped
  -- groups fall back to ordinary codegen, no transform, no regression.
  let _ = setRef gDispArsRef (gDispFnArsOf (fnBinds groups))
  let _ = setRef gDispBindsRef (fnBinds groups)
  let dispGroups0 = detectDispatchGroups
    (n => canonFnName e n)
    (n => gDispIsFn n)
    (n => gDispFnArity n)
    (nm ar cs => gDispStage1Claims e nm ar cs)
    (fnBinds groups)
    groups
    implEntries
  let _ = setRef gDispGroupsRef (gDispKeepEtaStable e (fnBinds groups) dispGroups0)
  -- a group member must never be ADVERTISED as having a float `__fw` worker (the
  -- group path emits no standalone defines, so a float-context caller would call
  -- a nonexistent symbol; v3/v4 make external float callers impossible anyway —
  -- belt and braces).
  let _ = setRef floatFwSetRef (gDispDropMembers (collectFloatFw e (fnBinds groups)))
  let _ = setRef floatClosureFnsRef (collectFloatClosureFns e (fnBinds groups))
  let _ = resetEmittedDefaults ()
  let _ = emitPreamble e
  let _ = emitGlobalDecls e (valBinds groups)
  -- issue #118: the PRELUDE half is a LIBRARY — it has no entry point, and the
  -- program half's `@mdk_program_main` is the one that defines and initialises
  -- every `@mdk_g_*` (the prelude's included).
  let _ = if emitHalfRef.value == emitHalfPrelude then () else emitProgramMain e groups
  let _ = emitFns e (fnBinds groups)
  let _ = emitImpls e implEntries
  let _ = preludeIdSpaceGuard e
  joinNl (reverseL e.buf.value ++ reverseL e.lams.value)

-- `@mdk_program_main`: the init prologue (every top-level value binding's rhs, in
-- source order) followed by `main`'s own body and the type-directed print.
emitProgramMain : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
emitProgramMain e groups =
  let _ = beginDefine e
  let _ = emit e "define i32 @mdk_program_main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {"
  let _ = emit e "entry:"
  let _ = emit e "  call void @mdk_set_args(i32 %argc, ptr %argv)"
  let _ = emitTopGlobals e (orderedValBinds (valBinds groups))
  let (mv, mty) = emitExpr e [] (mainBody groups)
  -- A Unit-returning `main` produces output ONLY through explicit print calls (or
  -- a panic); never auto-print its `()` result.  Detected by the DECLARED return
  -- type (`declSigOf "main"` → "Unit", robust even when the body LTy is
  -- mis-inferred — e.g. the medaka CLI's own `match`-bodied main, which otherwise
  -- printed a stray `0`) OR a body LTy that is already LTUnit (an unannotated IO
  -- `main = putStrLn …`).  Value-returning mains keep auto-printing (the pp_value
  -- differential-test contract).
  -- C4: if the typecheck Float hint is set, override the structural LTy so the
  -- print path uses the Float formatter even when emitExpr returns LTUnknown/LTInt
  -- (the type-erased result from a polymorphic HOF like `fold`).
  let mty2 = if mainIsFloatHintRef.value then LTFloat else mty
  let _ = if mainIsUnit e mty2 then () else emitPrint e mv mty2
  let _ = emit e "  ret i32 0"
  emit e "}"
-- declare a module global per top-level value/`Ref` binding (slice E1b).

-- emit `@main` FIRST: its init prologue computes each value-binding rhs in
-- SOURCE ORDER and marks the registry, so the globals' LTys are resolved before
-- the function `define`s (emitted next) reference them.  LLVM module-item order
-- is irrelevant, so emitting `@main` ahead of the fn/impl defines is sound.

-- the function + impl defines (registry now fully initialised, so any body may
-- reference any global via a `load i64, ptr @mdk_g_<name>`).

-- the module body (preamble, @main, fn/impl defines) in forward order, then the
-- side buffer (string-literal + value-binding globals, lifted-lambda defines).
-- LLVM module-item order is irrelevant, so appending the side buffer is sound.

emitPreamble : Emit -> Unit
emitPreamble e = emitLines e preambleLines

-- emit each constant preamble line in order; the strings live in
-- backend.llvm_preamble (pure data) so this module's `emit`/`Emit` machinery
-- stays here without a circular import.
emitLines : Emit -> List String -> Unit
emitLines _ [] = ()
emitLines e (line::rest) =
  let _ = emit e line
  emitLines e rest

-- the `@main` init prologue (slice E1b): compute each top-level value/`Ref`
-- binding's rhs in SOURCE ORDER and `store` the word into its module global
-- `@mdk_g_<name>`, then mark the registry entry initialised with the rhs's LTy.
-- Computing in source order means a binding referencing an EARLIER one loads the
-- already-stored global; a reference to a LATER (not-yet-marked) global hits the
-- forward/mutual-dependency gap in lookupVarG instead of an uninitialised 0.
-- A `Ref v` rhs allocs its cell as usual → the stored word is the cell POINTER, so
-- `.value`/`setRef` deref through the loaded global.
emitTopGlobals : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
emitTopGlobals _ [] = ()
emitTopGlobals e ((CBind name [CClause [] body])::rest) =
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e [] body
  let _ = emit e "  store i64 \{v}, ptr @mdk_g_\{name}"
  let _ = markGlobalInit e name ty
  emitTopGlobals e rest
emitTopGlobals _ ((CBind name _)::_) =
  panic
    ("llvm spike: top-level function '" ++ name ++ "' unsupported in slice 1 (value bindings only)")

-- Hint from the driver: True when typecheck resolved main's inferred return type to
-- Unit (e.g. via a polymorphic HOF whose result specialises to Unit with no explicit
-- annotation).  Set by the emit driver after elaborateModules via installMainIsUnitHint.
mainIsUnitHintRef : Ref Bool
mainIsUnitHintRef = Ref False

export installMainIsUnitHint : Bool -> Unit
installMainIsUnitHint b = setRef mainIsUnitHintRef b

-- Hint from the driver: True when typecheck resolved main's inferred return type to
-- Float (e.g. via a polymorphic HOF whose result specialises to Float with no explicit
-- annotation).  Set by the emit driver after elaborateModules via installMainIsFloatHint.
-- C4 (SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL-DESIGN §6): fixes bare Float value mains that print garbage
-- because the structural mainKind sees LTInt on a type-erased polymorphic-HOF return.
mainIsFloatHintRef : Ref Bool
mainIsFloatHintRef = Ref False

export installMainIsFloatHint : Bool -> Unit
installMainIsFloatHint b = setRef mainIsFloatHintRef b

-- Is `main` Unit-returning (→ suppress the auto-print of its result)?  Three checks:
-- 1. The typecheck-inferred-Unit hint (installMainIsUnitHint) — catches unannotated
--    mains whose return type resolved to Unit through a polymorphic HOF.
-- 2. The DECLARED return type (declSigOf "main" → "Unit") — catches the annotated
--    case `main : <IO> Unit`; effects already stripped by core_ir_lower.methodRetTy.
-- 3. The body's emit-time LTy (LTUnit) — catches the simple `main = putStrLn …` case.
mainIsUnit : Emit -> LTy -> Bool
mainIsUnit e mty =
  if mainIsUnitHintRef.value then True
  else match declSigOf "main"
    Some (_, "Unit") => True
    _ => match mty
      LTUnit => True
      _ => False

emitPrint : Emit -> String -> LTy -> Unit
emitPrint e v LTInt =
  let i = untagInt e v
  emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_int(i64 " ++ i ++ ")")
emitPrint e v LTBool =
  let i = untagInt e v
  emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_bool(i64 " ++ i ++ ")")
emitPrint e v LTFloat =
  let d = unboxFloat e v
  emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_float(double " ++ d ++ ")")
emitPrint e v LTStr = emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_str(i64 " ++ v ++ ")")
-- LTUnknown reaches print only for the dominant String-builder case (a chained
-- `++` whose value is a String); print it as a String, matching the old LTStr
-- default.  A list-valued LTUnknown is never printed by the bootstrap fixtures.
emitPrint e v LTUnknown = emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_str(i64 " ++ v ++ ")")
emitPrint e _ LTUnit = emit e "  call void @mdk_print_unit()"
emitPrint e v LTChar = emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_char(i64 " ++ v ++ ")")
-- G3: a polymorphic Num result (LTNum) is an Int OR boxed-Float word; dispatch
-- the print on its runtime tag via @mdk_print_num (mirrors the @mdk_num_* arith).
emitPrint e v LTNum = emit e ("  call void @mdk_print_num(i64 " ++ v ++ ")")
emitPrint _ _ LTCon =
  panic
    "llvm spike: cannot print an ADT value (slice 3: `main` must reduce to a scalar Int/Bool/Float)"
emitPrint _ _ LTClosure =
  panic
    "llvm spike: cannot print a closure value (slice 4: `main` must reduce to a scalar Int/Bool/Float)"

-- the body of the `main` binding (must be a nullary clause).
mainBody : List CBind -> CExpr
mainBody [] = panic "llvm spike: no `main` binding"
mainBody ((CBind "main" [CClause [] body])::_) = body
mainBody ((CBind "main" _)::_) =
  panic "llvm spike: `main` must be a nullary value (main = ...)"
mainBody (_::rest) = mainBody rest

-- a readable tag for the unsupported-node panic (only the nodes a slice-1
-- fixture could hit if it strayed out of scope).
ctag : CExpr -> String
ctag (CApp _ _) = "CApp (function application)"
ctag (CLam _ _) = "CLam (lambda)"
ctag (CMatch _ _) = "CMatch"
ctag (CDecision _ _ _) = "CDecision (match)"
ctag (CTuple _) = "CTuple"
ctag (CList _) = "CList"
ctag (CArray _) = "CArray"
ctag (CRecord _ _) = "CRecord"
ctag (CFieldAccess _ _ _) = "CFieldAccess"
ctag (CRecordUpdate _ _ _) = "CRecordUpdate"
ctag (CRangeList _ _ _) = "CRangeList"
ctag (CRangeArray _ _ _) = "CRangeArray"
ctag (CIndex _ _) = "CIndex"
ctag (CSlice _ _ _ _) = "CSlice"
ctag (CStringIndex _ _) = "CStringIndex"
ctag (CStringSlice _ _ _ _) = "CStringSlice"
ctag (CListIndex _ _) = "CListIndex"
ctag (CListSlice _ _ _ _) = "CListSlice"
ctag (CMethod _ _ _ _) = "CMethod (typeclass dispatch)"
ctag (CDict _ _) = "CDict (dictionary)"
ctag (CVariantUpdate _ _ _) = "CVariantUpdate (named-field variant update)"
ctag _ = "?"

-- ── D4 emitter-completeness inventory entry point ───────────────────────────
-- Mirrors emitProgram's setup but turns gap-recording ON, attributes each gap to
-- the top-level binding / impl group being emitted, and SKIPS the `@main` path (a
-- real prelude/compiler module carries no `main`).  Emits every fn-bind, value
-- bind, and impl group; returns the recorded gap events ("<binding>\t<reason>").
-- The emitted LLVM text is discarded by the probe — only the gap log matters.
export emitProgramGaps : CProgram -> List String
emitProgramGaps (CProgram groups ctorArs ctorTypes implEntries) =
  let e = Emit {
    counter = Ref 0,
    buf = Ref [],
    fnNames = Ref (collectFns groups),
    sigs = Ref (inferSigs (fnBinds groups)),
    ctors = Ref ctorArs,
    lams = Ref [],
    recFields = Ref (collectRecords groups),
    implEntries = Ref implEntries,
    ctorTypes = Ref ctorTypes,
    globalsReg = Ref (initGlobalsRegReady (valBinds groups)),
  }
  let _ = resetDistinctTypeNames ()
  let _ = setRef dictConstCacheRef []
  let _ = setRef dispCacheRef []
  let _ = installKnownFnMap (collectFns groups)
  let _ = installCtorMap ctorArs
  let _ = installSigMap (sigTable e)
  let _ = installDefArityMap e (fnBinds groups)
  let _ = setRef floatFwSetRef (collectFloatFw e (fnBinds groups))
  let _ = setRef floatClosureFnsRef (collectFloatClosureFns e (fnBinds groups))
  -- the gap census emits every bind ORDINARILY — reset the (b′) groups so a prior
  -- emitProgram's routing state can never leak into this path.
  let _ = setRef gDispGroupsRef []
  let _ = resetGaps ()
  let _ = enableGapRecord ()
  let _ = emitPreamble e
  let _ = emitFnsGaps e (fnBinds groups)
  let _ = emitImplsGaps e implEntries
  let _ = emitTopBindsGaps e [] (valBinds groups)
  gapEvents ()

emitFnsGaps : Emit -> List CBind -> Unit
emitFnsGaps _ [] = ()
emitFnsGaps e ((CBind name cs)::rest) =
  let _ = setGapBind ("fn " ++ name)
  let _ = emitFn e (CBind name cs)
  emitFnsGaps e rest

emitImplsGaps : Emit -> List CImplEntry -> Unit
emitImplsGaps e entries = emitGroupsGaps e (groupImpls entries)

emitGroupsGaps : Emit -> List ImplGroup -> Unit
emitGroupsGaps _ [] = ()
emitGroupsGaps e (g::rest) =
  let _ = setGapBind "impl \{groupMethodOf g}@\{groupTag g}"
  let _ = emitGroup e g
  emitGroupsGaps e rest

emitTopBindsGaps : Emit -> List (String, (String, LTy)) -> List CBind -> Unit
emitTopBindsGaps _ _ [] = ()
emitTopBindsGaps e env ((CBind name [CClause [] body])::rest) =
  let _ = setGapBind ("val " ++ name)
  let (v, ty) = emitExpr e env body
  emitTopBindsGaps e ((name, (v, ty))::env) rest
emitTopBindsGaps e env ((CBind name _)::rest) =
  let _ = setGapBind ("val " ++ name)
  let _ = gapU "non-nullary / multi-clause value binding (slice 1)"
  emitTopBindsGaps e env rest
# DESUGAR
(DUse false (UseGroup ("frontend" "ast") ((mem "Lit" true) (mem "Pat" true) (mem "Addr" true) (mem "Route" true))))
